name: Alexander disease
lab_findings: elevated astrocyte markers (gli fibrillary acidic protein and s100b)
clinical_summary: Alexander disease is a rare disorder characterized by macrocephaly, intellectual disability, seizures, and spasticity. It can affect individuals of all ages and may also present with agenesis of the corpus callosum and hyperreflexia, often following an autosomal dominant inheritance pattern.

name: Alpha-mannosidosis
clinical_summary: Alpha-mannosidosis is a rare autosomal recessive disease characterized by symptoms including macroglossia, intellectual disability, and global developmental delay. These symptoms often manifest in childhood, infancy, or neonatal stages, accompanied by features such as coarse facial features and hearing impairment.

name: Aspartylglucosaminuria
clinical_summary: Aspartylglucosaminuria is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by childhood onset of severe dental and facial anomalies, including gingival overgrowth and mandibular prognathia, often accompanied by intellectual disability and delayed speech and language development.

name: Multiple sulfatase deficiency
clinical_summary: Multiple sulfatase deficiency is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by variable age of onset from neonatal to adolescent periods and clinical manifestations including hydrocephalus, sensorineural hearing impairment, coarse facial features, and smooth philtrum. Additional common features are macrocephaly and anteverted nares in affected individuals.

name: Canavan disease
clinical_summary: Canavan disease is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by childhood-onset macrocephaly, hearing and visual impairment, ultimately progressing to blindness. Optic atrophy and abnormal visual evoked potentials are notable complications of this condition.

name: Cystinosis
clinical_summary: Cystinosis is a rare disease characterized by multisystem involvement, including renal tubular dysfunction, nephropathy, and extrarenal manifestations such as hypothyroidism and delayed puberty. It typically presents in infancy, childhood, adolescence, or adulthood with symptoms like proteinuria, nephropathy, and photophobia.

name: Fucosidosis
clinical_summary: Fucosidosis is a rare metabolic disorder characterized by a combination of severe physical and developmental abnormalities, including brachycephaly and dysostosis multiplex. It is typically marked by an early onset in infancy or childhood and follows an autosomal recessive inheritance pattern.

name: Krabbe disease
clinical_summary: Krabbe disease is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by hearing impairment, sensorineural hearing loss, and visual impairment, often presenting in infancy. It may also include symptoms such as atypical behavior, sensory neuropathy, and abnormal thumb morphology.

name: Mucopolysaccharidosis type 6
clinical_summary: Mucopolysaccharidosis type 6 is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by thick lower lip vermilion, coarse facial features, joint stiffness, and other systemic manifestations. It typically presents in childhood with symptoms including chronic otitis media, sinusitis, and abnormal metaphysis morphology.

name: Sialidosis type 1
clinical_summary: Sialidosis type 1 is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a combination of distinctive physical features, sensory impairments, and ocular abnormalities. Common symptoms include coarse facial features, thickened lower lip vermilion, and visual impairment, typically presenting in adolescence or early childhood.

name: Mucolipidosis type IV
clinical_summary: Mucolipidosis type IV is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a range of ophthalmological and behavioral symptoms, including strabismus, retinopathy, and photophobia. It typically presents in infancy, often accompanied by intellectual disability and atypical behavior.

name: Mucolipidosis type III
lab_findings: Abnormal form of the vertebral bodies
clinical_summary: Mucolipidosis type III is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by distinctive skeletal and ocular features. Affected individuals typically exhibit symptoms such as genu valgum, hyperlordosis, corneal opacity, and prominent occiput, often leading to visual impairment and joint stiffness starting in childhood.

name: Sandhoff disease
clinical_summary: Sandhoff disease is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a combination of neurological symptoms, including ataxia, seizures, and hearing impairment, often accompanied by macrocephaly and facial features such as full cheeks. The disease is typically present from infancy through adulthood, resulting in progressive neurological deterioration.

name: Mucopolysaccharidosis type 7
lab_findings: increased lysosomal storage of keratan sulfate
clinical_summary: Mucopolysaccharidosis type 7 is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by coarse facial features, intellectual disability, lymphedema, hypotonia, joint stiffness, and potentially inguinal hernia. Its age of onset can vary from infancy, neonatal, to childhood and adolescence.

name: Turner syndrome
key_symptoms: Enlargement of the distal femoral epiphysis
clinical_summary: Turner syndrome is a rare disease characterized by a variety of physical abnormalities, including elevated liver enzymes, cardiovascular anomalies, and skeletal irregularities. It is present at birth and can progress throughout childhood.

name: Beta-thalassemia
clinical_summary: Beta-thalassemia is a rare disease characterized by abnormalities of the skeletal system, anemia, and abnormal hemoglobin production, typically presenting in childhood or infancy. It is inherited in either an autosomal dominant or autosomal recessive pattern.

name: Facioscapulohumeral dystrophy
clinical_summary: Facioscapulohumeral dystrophy is a rare, inherited disorder characterized by progressive muscle weakness, skeletal muscle atrophy, and distinctive facial features such as a mask-like facies. It presents with various symptoms including scapular winging, hyperlordosis, and elevated creatine kinase levels across all age groups.

name: Emery-Dreifuss muscular dystrophy
inheritance: Autosomal dominant, Autosomal recessive, X-linked recessive
clinical_summary: Emery-Dreifuss muscular dystrophy is a rare disease characterized by symptoms such as pectus excavatum, reduced tendon reflexes, joint stiffness, myotonia, and myopathy. It often presents in childhood and has three known inheritance patterns: autosomal dominant, autosomal recessive, and X-linked recessive.

name: Kearns-Sayre syndrome
inheritance: Autosomal recessive, Mitochondrial inheritance, Not applicable
clinical_summary: Kearns-Sayre syndrome is a rare mitochondrial disorder characterized by a variety of systemic symptoms, including pigmentary retinopathy and third degree atrioventricular block. The age of onset can vary, typically affecting children, adolescents, adults, or infancy, and is associated with mitochondrial inheritance.

name: MERRF
clinical_summary: MERRF syndrome is a rare disease characterized by multiple systemic symptoms including sensorineural hearing impairment, optic atrophy, multiple lipomas, ataxia, generalized myoclonic seizures, and myopathy. It exhibits a variable age of onset, ranging from childhood to adulthood, and follows a mitochondrial inheritance pattern.

name: Central core disease
lab_findings: muscle biopsy shows core-like areas of atrophy
clinical_summary: Central core disease is a rare genetic disorder characterized by congenital joint hypermobility, hypotonia, and motor delay, often associated with musculoskeletal abnormalities. The condition typically manifests in childhood and follows an autosomal dominant inheritance pattern.

name: Fabry disease
clinical_summary: Fabry disease is a rare genetic disorder characterized by renal insufficiency, nephrotic syndrome, and other symptoms including hearing impairment, conjunctival telangiectasia, hematuria, and hyperkeratosis. It typically presents in adolescence, adulthood, or childhood and is inherited through X-linked dominant or recessive patterns.

name: Marfan syndrome
clinical_summary: Marfan syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a range of physical features, including disproportionate tall stature and a slender build, as well as cardiovascular complications such as aortic root aneurysm and aortic aneurysm. It can also be associated with respiratory issues like spontaneous pneumothorax and skeletal abnormalities like pes planus.

name: Ataxia-telangiectasia
clinical_summary: Ataxia-telangiectasia is a rare disease characterized by the combination of delayed puberty, polycystic ovaries, strabismus, and abnormal eye movement, typically presenting during infancy or childhood. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern, and individuals with the condition may also develop diabetes mellitus due to associated endocrine abnormalities.

name: Down syndrome
lab_findings: elevated free and total estriol levels
clinical_summary: Down syndrome is a rare disease characterized by brachycephaly, epicanthus, short neck, thickened nuchal skin fold, upslanted palpebral fissure, and brachydactyly. It presents at both antenatal and neonatal stages.

name: CHARGE syndrome
clinical_summary: CHARGE syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by a combination of abnormal pinna morphology, posteriorly rotated ears, cryptorchidism, hypogonadotropic hypogonadism, micropenis, and inner ear abnormalities. It typically manifests ante-natally or neonatally, and its inheritance pattern can be autosomal dominant or may be of unknown origin.

name: Oculocerebrorenal syndrome of Lowe
key_symptoms: Benign neoplasm of the central nervous system
clinical_summary: Oculocerebrorenal syndrome of Lowe is a rare X-linked recessive disorder characterized by joint hypermobility, feeding difficulties in infancy, and abnormality of calcium-phosphate metabolism, among other symptoms. Symptoms typically manifest ante- or neonatally and may include low levels of vitamin D and a neoplasm of the skin.

name: Fragile X syndrome
clinical_summary: Fragile X syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by symptoms including intellectual disability, joint hypermobility, and macroorchidism, typically presenting in childhood. This X-linked dominant disorder is associated with various physical and learning disabilities.

name: Mucopolysaccharidosis type 1
clinical_summary: Mucopolysaccharidosis type 1 is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by distinctive facial features and systemic complications, such as inguinal hernia, Gingival overgrowth, sinusitis, everted lower lip vermilion, and macrocephaly. This condition can manifest at any age due to its autosomal recessive inheritance pattern.

name: 22q11.2 deletion syndrome
clinical_summary: 22q11.2 deletion syndrome is a rare disease characterized by distinct facial features, including cleft palate, low-set ears, and bulbous nose, as well as conductive hearing impairment. It is associated with T cell physiology abnormalities and is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern.

name: Williams syndrome
clinical_summary: Williams syndrome is a rare genetic disorder associated with a range of physical abnormalities, including an enlarged mouth and inguinal hernia, and potential complications such as renal insufficiency and proteinuria. It is characterized by an abnormality of the bladder, a pelvic kidney, and renal issues, and is typically inherited in an autosomal dominant manner.

name: Wolf-Hirschhorn syndrome
clinical_summary: Wolf-Hirschhorn syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by severe physical and developmental abnormalities, including Downslanted palpebral fissures, Hypodontia, and Global developmental delay. Affected individuals often experience severe symptoms such as seizures, ataxia, and hypotonia from birth.

name: Ataxia with vitamin E deficiency
clinical_summary: Ataxia with vitamin E deficiency is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a combination of neurological symptoms including ataxia, areflexia, muscle weakness, and peripheral neuropathy. This disorder can manifest at any age.

name: Rheumatic fever
clinical_summary: Rheumatic fever is a rare disease characterized by systemic symptoms such as arthritis, subcutaneous nodules, sinusitis, and pallor, often presenting in childhood. It can lead to complications including abnormal mitral and aortic valve morphology.

name: X-linked agammaglobulinemia
clinical_summary: X-linked agammaglobulinemia is a rare disease characterized by symptoms such as glossoptosis, sinusitis, and conjunctivitis, particularly presenting in childhood. It follows an X-linked recessive inheritance pattern.

name: Wiskott-Aldrich syndrome
inheritance: Autosomal dominant, Autosomal recessive, Not applicable, X-linked recessive
clinical_summary: Wiskott-Aldrich syndrome is a rare disease characterized by a combination of symptoms, including sinusitis, otitis media, petechiae, bruising susceptibility, and purpura, typically presenting in infancy or neonatal age. The mode of inheritance is X-linked recessive.

name: Hypophosphatasia
key_symptoms: Skin dimple over apex of long bone angulation
clinical_summary: Hypophosphatasia is a rare disease characterized by abnormal skeletal development, including large fontanelles, abnormal rib and metaphysis morphology, and narrow chest. It presents with a range of symptoms, including irritability, and dental anomalies, affecting individuals of all ages with possible autosomal dominant or recessive inheritance patterns.

name: Ornithine transcarbamylase deficiency
clinical_summary: Ornithine transcarbamylase deficiency is a rare, X-linked recessive disorder characterized by symptoms of hepatic failure, severe metabolic disturbances, and neurologic features. Affected males present with a range of clinical manifestations, including splenomegaly, hypoglycemia, hyperammonemia, aminoaciduria, and seizures across all ages of onset.

name: Kennedy disease
clinical_summary: Kennedy disease is a rare X-linked recessive disorder affecting adult males, characterized by a range of symptoms including decreased fertility, gynecomastia, hypotonia, dysarthria, hyporeflexia, and gait disturbance. It appears to have a distinct presentation in adulthood.

name: Rubinstein-Taybi syndrome
lab_findings: hypomelanosis of the skin may show abnormal patterns
clinical_summary: Rubinstein-Taybi syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by distinct facial features including a high palate, hypertelorism, and convex nasal ridge, among others. This condition manifests at birth and is typically inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern.

name: X-linked retinoschisis
clinical_summary: X-linked retinoschisis is a rare genetic eye disorder characterized by abnormal vision, electroretinogram results, and retinoschisis, typically presenting in infancy, childhood, or adolescence. It often involves glaucoma and progressive visual loss.

name: Hypochondroplasia
lab_findings: Abnormality of pelvic girdle bone morphology
Abnormal form of the vertebral bodies
age_of_onset: Adolescent, Adult, Antenatal, Childhood, Infancy, Neonatal
clinical_summary: Hypochondroplasia is a rare genetic condition characterized by skeletal dysplasia and multiple bone abnormalities, presenting with various age of onset. It is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern.

name: X-linked hypohidrotic ectodermal dysplasia
clinical_summary: X-linked hypohidrotic ectodermal dysplasia is a rare genetic disorder characterized by symptoms including delayed tooth eruption, sparse body hair, and an inability to sweat. It presents at birth or in early childhood through features such as a prominent lower lip and a depressed nasal ridge.

name: Neurofibromatosis type 1
clinical_summary: Neurofibromatosis type 1 is a rare disease characterized by abnormalities of the nervous system, delayed puberty, and presence of skin manifestations such as melanocytic nevus and multiple lipomas. It typically presents in infancy or neonatal stage and is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern, often accompanied by mild intellectual disability and specific learning disabilities.

name: Non-acquired isolated growth hormone deficiency
lab_findings: Decreased response to growth hormone stimulation test
inheritance: Autosomal dominant, Autosomal recessive, X-linked recessive
clinical_summary: Non-acquired isolated growth hormone deficiency is a rare disease characterized by anterior hypopituitarism, growth delay, and short stature, typically presenting with symptoms in early infancy. It presents with a range of signs including doll-like facies and delayed puberty, with various modes of inheritance including autosomal dominant, autosomal recessive, and X-linked recessive patterns.

name: Chronic granulomatous disease
clinical_summary: Chronic granulomatous disease is a rare inherited disorder characterized by recurrent infections and inflammation due to neutrophil dysfunction, presenting with symptoms such as fever, sinusitis, and skin lesions. It is inherited in an autosomal or X-linked recessive pattern, typically manifesting in early childhood or adolescence.

name: Homocystinuria due to cystathionine beta-synthase deficiency
clinical_summary: Homocystinuria due to cystathionine beta-synthase deficiency is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by dental crowding, osteoporosis, ectopia lentis, arachnodactyly, intellectual disability, and disproportionate tall stature. This condition typically manifests in the adolescent, adult, or childhood stages of life.

name: Lesch-Nyhan syndrome
clinical_summary: Lesch-Nyhan syndrome presents with a range of symptoms, including renal insufficiency, atypical behavior, hematuria, mild intellectual disability, and spasticity, often manifesting at birth. It is inherited in an X-linked recessive pattern.

name: Cystinuria
clinical_summary: Cystinuria is a rare inherited disorder characterized by the abnormal accumulation of cystine in the urine, leading to a range of symptoms including hypertension, nausea, hyperuricemia, abnormal urinary odor, hypocitraturia, and flank pain. It affects individuals of all ages, with an inheritance pattern that can be either autosomal recessive or semi-dominant.

name: Monosomy 5p syndrome
clinical_summary: Monosomy 5p syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by microcephaly, epicanthus, microretrognathia, round face, wide nasal bridge, and hypotonia. This condition typically presents before birth and is not inherited, with the cause being unknown.

name: Norrie disease
clinical_summary: Norrie disease is a rare X-linked recessive disorder characterized by multiple congenital anomalies, including auditory and ocular abnormalities. Affected individuals often exhibit severe visual impairment due to the presence of cataract and chorioretinal abnormalities.

name: Alpha-1-antitrypsin deficiency
clinical_summary: Alpha-1-antitrypsin deficiency is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by reduced circulating alpha-1-antitrypsin concentration, which can lead to emphysema and other serious lung and hepatic manifestations. It presents with symptoms such as bronchiectasis, jaundice, and hepatic fibrosis in patients of all ages.

name: Hyperkalemic periodic paralysis
clinical_summary: Hyperkalemic periodic paralysis is a rare, inherited disorder characterized by episodes of muscle weakness or gait disturbance, hyperkalemia, and elevated creatine kinase concentration. It typically presents in childhood with symptoms including reduced tendon reflexes, fasciculations, and myotonia, following an autosomal dominant inheritance pattern.

name: Schwartz-Jampel syndrome
clinical_summary: Schwartz-Jampel syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by distinct facial features and symptoms present from infancy or birth, including narrow mouth and pursed lips, and an autosomal recessive inheritance pattern. Patients typically exhibit other facial abnormalities such as trismus, high palate, and eversion of the lower lip.

name: Diastrophic dysplasia
lab_findings: Abnormal form of the vertebral bodies
clinical_summary: Diastrophic dysplasia is a rare, genetic disorder with an autosomal recessive inheritance pattern, characterized by antenatal or neonatal onset. Key features include macrocephaly, abnormal rib and metaphysis morphology, clavicle abnormalities, intrauterine growth retardation, and scoliosis.

name: Noonan syndrome
clinical_summary: Noonan syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by joint hypermobility, abnormal pulmonary valve morphology, and other systemic anomalies, with age of onset ranging from antenatal to childhood. It can be inherited in an autosomal dominant or autosomal recessive pattern, affecting various bodily systems.

name: Gorlin syndrome
clinical_summary: Gorlin syndrome is a rare, autosomal dominant disorder characterized by various physical anomalies, including nevi, palmar and plantar pits, and macrocephaly, often presenting in adolescence or adulthood. Additional features may include cerebral calcification and neoplasms.

name: Fibrodysplasia ossificans progressiva
lab_findings: Abnormality of the first metatarsal bone
clinical_summary: Fibrodysplasia ossificans progressiva is a rare disease characterized by limitation of joint mobility, subcutaneous nodules, and spinal abnormalities, often resulting in spinal rigidity and fusion. It typically manifests in childhood with an autosomal dominant mode of inheritance.

name: Pfeiffer syndrome
key_symptoms: Symphalangism affecting the phalanges of the hand
lab_findings: microcephalic osteodysplastic dysplasia is the more specific name but lab findings for pfeiffer syndrome are scarce.
prolonged ptosis in infancy suggests possible oculomotor nerve palsy
clinical_summary: Pfeiffer syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by craniofacial abnormalities, including turricephaly, hypertelorism, and high forehead. It presents antenatally or at birth with associated features such as brachydactyly and ptosis.

name: Zellweger syndrome
clinical_summary: Zellweger syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by abnormal pinna morphology, respiratory insufficiency, hepatomegaly, and skeletal dysplasia, typically manifesting in the neonatal period. It is an autosomal recessive disorder with a uniformly fatal outcome, resulting in death in infancy.

name: WAGR syndrome
clinical_summary: WAGR syndrome is a rare condition, characterized by a combination of congenital anomalies, including cryptorchidism, microcephaly, micrognathia, hearing abnormalities, and visual impairment. It is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern and often presents in utero or shortly after birth.

name: Waardenburg syndrome type 3
clinical_summary: Waardenburg syndrome type 3 is a rare genetic disorder characterized by the neonatal onset of microcephaly, facial abnormalities, hearing impairment, and narrow nasal bridge, often accompanied by other distinctive facial features. It can be inherited in an autosomal dominant or autosomal recessive pattern.

name: Townes-Brocks syndrome
clinical_summary: Townes-Brocks syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a combination of craniofacial, genitourinary, and digital abnormalities. It is typically inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern and can manifest with symptoms such as abnormal pinna morphology, rectovaginal fistula, and preaxial hand polydactyly.

name: Waardenburg syndrome type 1
clinical_summary: Waardenburg syndrome type 1 is a rare genetic disorder characterized by mandibular prognathia, hearing impairment, and multiple ocular abnormalities, typically presenting at birth. It is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern.

name: Crouzon syndrome
clinical_summary: Crouzon syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by craniofacial abnormalities including brachycephaly, hypertelorism, and maxillary hypoplasia. It presents antenatally, at birth, or in infancy with conductive hearing impairment.

name: Cowden syndrome
clinical_summary: Cowden syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a range of anomalies including abnormal penis development, large tongue and thyroid, and goiter. It is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern and can affect individuals of all ages.

name: Coffin-Lowry syndrome
clinical_summary: Coffin-Lowry syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by joint hypermobility, distinctive facial features, and other physical anomalies primarily presenting in childhood, infancy, or neonatal stages. It follows an X-linked dominant inheritance pattern.

name: BOR syndrome
key_symptoms: Stenosis of the external auditory canal
Atresia of the external auditory canal
clinical_summary: BOR syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by multiple congenital anomalies of the ear and surrounding structures. It is an autosomal dominant condition typically manifesting at birth or in early childhood and features symptoms including hearing impairment and preauricular anomalies.

name: Hereditary hemorrhagic telangiectasia
clinical_summary: Hereditary hemorrhagic telangiectasia is a rare disorder characterized by the presence of telangiectasias and spontaneous, recurrent epistaxis, which can occur in individuals of all ages. It is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern, manifesting with symptoms such as lip and tongue telangiectasia, nasolacrimal mucosa involvement, and other telangiectatic lesions.

name: Saethre-Chotzen syndrome
clinical_summary: Saethre-Chotzen syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by craniosynostosis and various skeletal abnormalities including facial asymmetry, clinodactyly, and abnormal skull morphology. It is an autosomal dominant disorder present at birth, affecting the morphology of the pinna and the skull's development.

name: Beckwith-Wiedemann syndrome
clinical_summary: Beckwith-Wiedemann syndrome is a rare disease characterized by tall stature, large for gestational age, and an increased risk of developing neoplasms, often presenting with renal abnormalities such as enlarged kidneys, nephropathy, and nephrocalcinosis. It can be present at birth (antentatal or neonatal age) and tends to follow an autosomal dominant pattern of inheritance, although the mode of inheritance may be unknown in some cases.

name: Albers-Schönberg osteopetrosis
clinical_summary: Albers-Schönberg osteopetrosis is a rare genetic disorder characterized by bone manifestations such as macrocephaly, abnormal metaphysis morphology, frontal bossing, and bone pain, often presenting in childhood. This autosomal dominant disorder is also associated with symptoms of arthritis and joint dislocation.

name: Aicardi syndrome
clinical_summary: Aicardi syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a combination of craniofacial abnormalities, including microcephaly, short philtrum, and protruding ear, along with skeletal anomalies such as bifid and fused ribs. It is an X-linked dominant condition with onset typically occurring in the neonatal period.

name: Alagille syndrome
clinical_summary: Alagille syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by distinctive facial features and liver dysfunction, including cholestasis. It can manifest at any age and is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern.

name: Abetalipoproteinemia
clinical_summary: Abetalipoproteinemia is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by symptoms including abnormal nervous system function, Acanthocytosis, and low levels of vitamin E in childhood or infancy, often resulting in fat malabsorption and Steatorrhea.

name: Cat-eye syndrome
clinical_summary: Cat-eye syndrome is a rare congenital disorder characterized by a combination of preauricular skin tag, anal atresia, and other abnormalities. Key features include hydronephrosis, hypertelorism, and downslanted palpebral fissures, which present either antenatally or neonatally.

name: Ellis Van Creveld syndrome
clinical_summary: Ellis Van Creveld syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disease characterized by a range of physical abnormalities including narrow chest, hand polydactyly, and abnormality of the dentition. Early symptoms occur in utero and persist into neonatal life, often accompanied by failure to thrive and other associated features.

name: Jeune syndrome
lab_findings: Abnormality of pelvic girdle bone morphology
clinical_summary: Jeune syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by abnormalities in the sternum, rib, and clavicle development, narrow chest, and Brachydactyly. Symptoms include various skeletal abnormalities present since birth, often diagnosed antenatally or in the neonatal period.

name: Treacher-Collins syndrome
clinical_summary: Treacher-Collins syndrome is a rare congenital disorder characterized by multiple facial abnormalities, including malar flattening, retrognathia, and hypoplasia of the maxilla. It is inherited in an autosomal dominant or autosomal recessive pattern, presenting with notable facial features from birth.

name: Cornelia de Lange syndrome
key_symptoms: Atresia of the external auditory canal
inheritance: Autosomal dominant, Not applicable, X-linked recessive
clinical_summary: Cornelia de Lange syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by a range of physical anomalies including high palate, thin vermilion border, brachycephaly, microcephaly, low anterior hairline, and long philtrum. It is a congenital condition with symptoms apparent at birth or before birth.

name: Holoprosencephaly
inheritance: Autosomal recessive, Multigenic/multifactorial, Not applicable, Oligogenic, X-linked dominant
clinical_summary: Holoprosencephaly is a rare condition characterized by holoprosencephaly, a median cleft palate, and median cleft lip, often presenting with abnormal facial shape and a single median maxillary incisor. It is associated with nervous system abnormalities and can manifest from before birth through early infancy, with various inheritance patterns including autosomal recessive and X-linked dominant.

name: Apert syndrome
lab_findings: Morphological abnormality of the semicircular canal
clinical_summary: Apert syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by distinct morphological abnormalities, including conductive hearing impairment. It presents with autosomal dominant inheritance, manifesting antenatally or at birth.

name: Lamellar ichthyosis
clinical_summary: Lamellar ichthyosis is a rare skin disorder characterized by dry skin, hyperkeratosis, ectropion, pruritus, erythroderma, and abnormality of the nail. It typically presents in the neonatal period and can have an autosomal dominant or autosomal recessive inheritance pattern.

name: Menkes disease
clinical_summary: Menkes disease is a rare, X-linked recessive disorder characterized by early onset of joint hypermobility, microcephaly, and other physical anomalies, such as inguinal hernia, abnormal palate morphology, and pectus excavatum. Commonly associated with dry skin, symptoms manifest in the neonatal period.

name: Microphthalmia, Lenz type
clinical_summary: Microphthalmia, Lenz type is a rare X-linked recessive disorder characterized by multiple congenital anomalies presenting in neonates, including microphthalmia, abnormal ear morphology, and urinary tract malformations. Key features include posteriorly rotated ears, cryptorchidism, and hypospadias.

name: Meckel syndrome
clinical_summary: Meckel syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive genetic disorder characterized by severe prenatal abnormalities, including multiple congenital malformations. Affected individuals typically exhibit multiple anomalies, including posteriorly rotated ears, multicystic kidney dysplasia, and microcephaly.

name: Monosomy 18p syndrome
clinical_summary: Monosomy 18p syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by distinct facial features, intellectual disability, and delayed speech and language development. It is typically inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern and presents with various physical and developmental abnormalities.

name: Distal monosomy 7q36 syndrome
clinical_summary: Distal monosomy 7q36 syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a distinct set of physical features, including microcephaly, full cheeks, micrognathia, and an upslanted palpebral fissure, among others. The condition manifests antenatally or neonatally, and its inheritance pattern is currently unknown.

name: Distal deletion 9p syndrome
clinical_summary: Distal deletion 9p syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by physical anomalies such as posteriorly rotated ears, short nose, and midface retrusion. Affected infants typically exhibit these symptoms from infancy or neonatal periods.

name: Trisomy 13 syndrome
lab_findings: Abnormality of the fontanelles or cranial sutures
clinical_summary: Trisomy 13 syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by congenital anomalies including median cleft lip, cleft palate, and craniofacial abnormalities. Affected individuals often present with these symptoms antenatally or in the neonatal period.

name: Distal duplication 15q syndrome
clinical_summary: Distal duplication 15q syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a distinctive set of physical features affecting the face and palate, including cryptorchidism, high palate, sloping forehead, long philtrum, micrognathia, and prominent nasal bridge. The onset occurs in the fetus or shortly after birth, but the mode of inheritance remains unknown.

name: Trisomy 18 syndrome
clinical_summary: Trisomy 18 syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a range of physical abnormalities including cryptorchidism, narrow palate, dolichocephaly, prominent occiput, microretrognathia, and hypertelorism. It typically presents antenatally and continues into the neonatal period.

name: Distal duplication 18q syndrome
clinical_summary: Distal duplication 18q syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by cryptorchidism, dolichocephaly, and a range of craniofacial abnormalities including a round or triangular face and micrognathia. The syndrome is present at birth, with symptoms evident from an antenatal or neonatal age.

name: Bruck syndrome
lab_findings: normal to low alkaline phosphatase isoenzymes
clinical_summary: Bruck syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive disease characterized by distinctive facial features, bone fragility, and joint stiffness, often presenting in early life. The condition's manifestations include triangular face, osteoporosis, pterygium, joint stiffness, talipes equinovarus, and potentially life-threatening respiratory insufficiency.

name: Darier disease
clinical_summary: Darier disease is a rare skin disorder characterized by symptoms such as pruritus, hyperpigmentation, and nail abnormalities. It typically presents in adolescence or adulthood, although onsets in childhood have been reported, and is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern.

name: 46,XY difference of sex development due to 5-alpha-reductase 2 deficiency
clinical_summary: 46,XY difference of sex development due to 5-alpha-reductase 2 deficiency is characterized by cryptorchidism, ambiguous genitalia, and perineal hypospadias in individuals with an autosomal recessive inheritance pattern. Affected individuals typically present with physical signs such as small scrotum and bifid scrotum from infancy, childhood, or adolescence.

name: Isolated complex I deficiency
inheritance: Autosomal recessive, Mitochondrial inheritance, X-linked dominant
clinical_summary: Isolated complex I deficiency is a rare mitochondrial disorder characterized by a combination of proximal tubulopathy and other systemic manifestations including hearing and eye abnormalities. This condition has a variable inheritance pattern, including autosomal recessive, mitochondrial, and X-linked dominant forms.

name: Bethlem muscular dystrophy
clinical_summary: Bethlem muscular dystrophy is a rare disease characterized by childhood- or infancy-onset muscle weakness, flexion contracture, and reduced muscle collagen VI. It is associated with myopathic abnormalities and muscular dystrophy.

name: Pentasomy X syndrome
clinical_summary: Pentasomy X syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a complex clinical presentation of hypotonia, microcephaly, and facial dysmorphisms. Affected individuals typically exhibit these symptoms from a neonatal age and the inheritance pattern is unknown.

name: Congenital osteogenesis imperfecta-microcephaly-cataracts syndrome
clinical_summary: Congenital osteogenesis imperfecta-microcephaly-cataracts syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by antenatal and neonatal onset of symptoms including microcephaly, cataracts, intrauterine growth retardation, recurrent fractures, micromelia, and disproportionate short-limb short stature. This condition presents with multiple severe congenital anomalies from birth.

name: Trisomy 9p syndrome
clinical_summary: Trisomy 9p syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a range of physical abnormalities including craniofacial and skeletal anomalies. The symptoms, which are present at birth, are thought to be due to an unknown inheritance pattern.

name: 22q11.2 duplication syndrome
clinical_summary: 22q11.2 duplication syndrome is a rare condition characterized by distinct facial features including a cleft palate, narrow face, epicanthus, hypertelorism, high forehead, and depressed nasal ridge. It typically follows an autosomal dominant inheritance pattern, allowing for expression in individuals of all ages.

name: Incontinentia pigmenti
clinical_summary: Incontinentia pigmenti is a rare, X-linked dominant disorder characterized by a range of clinical symptoms primarily affecting the orofacial region. It manifests neonatally with symptoms including orofacial cleft, hearing abnormality, and visual impairment.

name: Hereditary orotic aciduria
clinical_summary: Hereditary orotic aciduria is a rare genetic disorder characterized by global developmental delay, anemia, and distinctive urinary abnormalities, including orotic acid crystalluria and aminoaciduria, presenting in infancy or neonatal onset. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern.

name: Mevalonic aciduria
clinical_summary: Mevalonic aciduria is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by childhood onset of large fontanelles, microcephaly, dolichocephaly, and distinctive facial features including a triangular face and downslanted palpebral fissures. This disorder often results in associated intellectual disability.

name: Neonatal adrenoleukodystrophy
clinical_summary: Neonatal adrenoleukodystrophy is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by distinct craniofacial features, including sensorineural hearing impairment. Affected individuals typically present at birth with symptoms of this condition, often with a wide range of severity and progression.

name: Alkaptonuria
clinical_summary: Alkaptonuria is a rare genetic disorder characterized by abnormal skin pigmentation, joint deformities including arthritis, joint dislocation, and joint swelling, often accompanied by hearing abnormalities and vision issues, typically presenting in infancy or adulthood. It inherits an autosomal recessive pattern.

name: Nager syndrome
inheritance: Autosomal dominant, Autosomal recessive, Not applicable
clinical_summary: Nager syndrome is a rare disease characterized by congenital skeletal dysplasia, maxillofacial abnormalities, and hearing impairment. Manifestations include hypoplasia of the maxilla, micrognathia, and delayed speech and language development.

name: Acromegaly
key_symptoms: Cortical diaphyseal thickening of the upper limbs
clinical_summary: Acromegaly is a rare disease characterized by symptoms including osteoarthritis, arthralgia, and cortical diaphyseal thickening of the upper limbs.

name: Smith-Magenis syndrome
clinical_summary: Smith-Magenis syndrome is a rare autosomal dominant disorder characterized by a range of physical anomalies including brachycephaly, broad forehead, and dental abnormalities. Additionally, recurrent otitis media and distinctive facial features such as a wide nasal bridge and deeply set eyes are also commonly observed in individuals with this condition.

name: Tetrasomy X syndrome
clinical_summary: Tetrasomy X syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by global developmental delay and specific learning disabilities, often accompanied by physical anomalies such as radioulnar synostosis and abnormal dentition. It typically manifests in infancy or at birth.

name: Adult Refsum disease
clinical_summary: Adult Refsum disease is a rare genetic disorder characterized by anosmia, visual impairments, and eye abnormalities. It is primarily inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern, affecting individuals across various age groups.

name: Retinitis pigmentosa-intellectual disability-deafness-hypogonadism syndrome
clinical_summary: This autosomal recessive disorder, characterized by a combination of visual and auditory impairments, is associated with coarse facial features, hypogonadism, and intellectual disability. Clinical manifestations of this disease include sensorineural hearing loss, visual impairment, nystagmus, cataract, and cryptorchidism.

name: Isovaleric acidemia
clinical_summary: Isovaleric acidemia is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by severe complications including global developmental delay, metabolic acidosis, and intellectual disability. Affected individuals may also experience seizures, hypotonia, and lethargy.

name: Aarskog-Scott syndrome
inheritance: Autosomal dominant, Autosomal recessive, X-linked recessive
clinical_summary: Aarskog-Scott syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by distinctive facial and physical features, including a shawl scrotum, everted lower lip vermilion, and hypertelorism. The condition can exhibit various inheritance patterns, including autosomal dominant, autosomal recessive, and X-linked recessive.

name: Chronic mucocutaneous candidiasis
clinical_summary: Chronic mucocutaneous candidiasis is a rare disease characterized by abnormalities of the vagina, mouth, skin, and lip morphology, often accompanied by skin rash and hyperkeratosis, typically presenting in infancy or childhood. It can be inherited in an autosomal dominant or autosomal recessive pattern.

name: Arthrogryposis-oculomotor limitation-electroretinal anomalies syndrome
clinical_summary: Arthrogryposis-oculomotor limitation-electroretinal anomalies syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by neonatal onset and symptoms including visual impairment, ptosis, inguinal hernia, and distinctive physical features such as triangular face and macrotia. This syndrome exhibits an autosomal dominant or autosomal recessive pattern of inheritance.

name: Sheldon-Hall syndrome
lab_findings: no laboratory findings are established for sheldon-hall syndrome
clinical_summary: Sheldon-Hall syndrome is a rare congenital condition characterized by a distinctive combination of facial and neck anomalies, including high palate, micrognathia, and webbed neck. It typically manifests at birth and is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern.

name: Argininemia
clinical_summary: Argininemia is a rare disease characterized by symptoms such as atypical behavior, seizures, and hyperammonemia, typically presenting in childhood or infancy. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern.

name: Lethal hemolytic anemia-genital anomalies syndrome
clinical_summary: Lethal hemolytic anemia-genital anomalies syndrome is a rare, neonatally-presenting disorder characterized by a combination of life-threatening anemia, hypospadias, and various congenital anomalies. Key features include ureteral abnormalities, microcephaly, micrognathia, and distinct facial dysmorphology.

name: Blue rubber bleb nevus
clinical_summary: Blue rubber bleb nevus is a rare disorder characterized by skin abnormalities and systemic involvement, including cavernous hemangiomas, subcutaneous nodules, and coagulation abnormalities. It typically presents in childhood and can lead to complications such as volvulus and intestinal bleeding.

name: Isolated Dandy-Walker malformation
lab_findings: * low cerebrospinal fluid (csf) pressure in the lumbar region
* abnormal mri with findings consistent with dandy-walker malformation
clinical_summary: Isolated Dandy-Walker malformation is a rare condition characterized by the development of hydrocephalus, macrocephaly, and cerebellar hypoplasia. It presents antenatally or neonatally with clinical features including a prominent occiput and platybasia.

name: Argininosuccinic aciduria
clinical_summary: Argininosuccinic aciduria is a rare disorder characterized by various neurological and developmental symptoms, including hypertension, intellectual disability, seizure, and ataxia. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern and can affect individuals of all ages, also leading to specific learning disabilities and lethargy.

name: Craniosynostosis-Dandy-Walker malformation-hydrocephalus syndrome
clinical_summary: This rare syndrome presents with a combination of craniosynostosis, Dandy-Walker malformation, and hydrocephalus at infancy, and is characterized by clinical features including dolichocephaly, hypertelorism, micrognathia, strabismus, and optic atrophy. It follows an autosomal dominant inheritance pattern.

name: Cutis marmorata telangiectatica congenita
clinical_summary: Cutis marmorata telangiectatica congenita is a rare congenital disorder characterized by retinal detachment, leukocoria, and a range of skin abnormalities, including cutis marmorata. Symptoms may also include seizures and subcutaneous hemorrhage, typically presenting at a neonatal age.

name: Cohen syndrome
clinical_summary: Cohen syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a combination of musculoskeletal, dental, and craniofacial abnormalities. Affected individuals often present with symptoms such as joint hypermobility, dental abnormalities, microcephaly, and distinctive facial features including an open mouth.

name: Cooper-Jabs syndrome
key_symptoms: Atresia of the external auditory canal
clinical_summary: Cooper-Jabs syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by congenital malformations, including brachycephaly and conductive hearing impairment. Affected individuals also exhibit characteristic facial abnormalities.

name: Charlie M syndrome
clinical_summary: Charlie M syndrome is a rare condition characterized by a combination of craniofacial and digital anomalies, including narrow mouth, thin vermilion border, hypertelorism, micrognathia, brachydactyly, and split hand. It typically presents in neonates with no established inheritance pattern.

name: Cholestasis-lymphedema syndrome
clinical_summary: Cholestasis-lymphedema syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by jaundice, lymphedema, and biliary tract abnormalities. Clinical manifestations often appear in infancy or neonatal periods, accompanied by skin pigmentation abnormalities, multiple lipomas, and splenomegaly.

name: Cleidocranial dysplasia
clinical_summary: Cleidocranial dysplasia is a rare genetic disorder characterized by delayed eruption of teeth and abnormal bone morphology, typically presenting at birth. It is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern, and symptoms include chronic otitis media, osteoporosis, brachydactyly, abnormality of the dentition, and abnormal rib morphology.

name: Fetal alcohol syndrome
clinical_summary: Fetal alcohol syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by intellectual disability, global developmental delay, and other physical and cognitive impairments. It is typically evident at birth or prenatally, with symptoms persisting into later life.

name: Ebstein malformation of the tricuspid valve
key_symptoms: Ebstein anomaly of the tricuspid valve
clinical_summary: Ebstein malformation of the tricuspid valve is a rare condition characterized by premature birth, atrial septal defect, respiratory insufficiency, and Ebstein anomaly of the tricuspid valve, with imperforate tricuspid valve presenting fatigue. It can affect individuals of all ages due to an autosomal dominant inheritance pattern.

name: Postaxial acrofacial dysostosis
clinical_summary: Postaxial acrofacial dysostosis is a rare genetic disorder characterized by various congenital anomalies including camptodactyly, facial flattening, and ocular abnormalities. It presents antenatally or neonatally and has an autosomal recessive inheritance pattern.

name: Dyskeratosis congenita
lab_findings: Abnormal morphology of female internal genitalia
inheritance: Autosomal dominant, Autosomal recessive, X-linked recessive
clinical_summary: Dyskeratosis congenita is a rare disease characterized by a range of physical abnormalities including abnormal genitalia, dental issues, and pharyngeal abnormalities. It may present at various ages, and its inheritance pattern is multifactorial.

name: XY type gonadal dysgenesis-associated anomalies syndrome
key_symptoms: Atresia of the external auditory canal
clinical_summary: XY type gonadal dysgenesis-associated anomalies syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a range of congenital anomalies, including gonadal dysgenesis and ureteral abnormalities. Symptoms also include polycystic ovaries, atresia of the external auditory canal, and skeletal features such as brachydactyly and enlarged hands, presenting with a neonatal onset.

name: Familial dysautonomia
clinical_summary: Familial dysautonomia is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by multi-systemic dysfunction, including alacrima, abnormal pupil morphology, atypical behavior, and complex vasomotor and thermoregulatory disturbances. Affected individuals present with symptoms typically apparent in infancy, childhood, or the neonatal period, including hypertension, hypohidrosis, and hyperhidrosis.

name: Dubowitz syndrome
clinical_summary: Dubowitz syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a combination of distinctive facial features and delayed cranial suture closure, often evident at birth. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern and typically presents antenatally or in the neonatal period.

name: Diencephalic syndrome
clinical_summary: Diencephalic syndrome is a rare disease characterized by a complex presentation of various symptoms, including long penis, everted lower lip vermilion, hydrocephalus, macrotia, nystagmus, and atypical behavior. Its onset can occur in infancy, adolescence, adulthood, or childhood.

name: Encephalopathy due to sulfite oxidase deficiency
clinical_summary: Encephalopathy due to sulfite oxidase deficiency is a rare genetic disorder characterized by multiple congenital anomalies, including microcephaly, full cheeks, and eye abnormalities such as deeply set eyes and ectopia lentis. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive manner and presents with variable ages of onset, from antenatal through infancy and neonatal periods.

name: Pyruvate dehydrogenase deficiency
inheritance: Autosomal recessive, Not applicable, X-linked dominant
clinical_summary: Pyruvate dehydrogenase deficiency is a rare disease characterized by a range of symptoms including microcephaly, abnormal eye movement, seizures, and hypotonia, often presenting in childhood, infancy, or at birth. It exhibits an autosomal recessive inheritance pattern.

name: Isolated glycerol kinase deficiency
clinical_summary: Isolated glycerol kinase deficiency is a rare X-linked recessive disease characterized by a range of symptoms including cryptorchidism, osteoporosis, intellectual disability, seizures, hypotonia, and global developmental delay. This condition can affect individuals of all ages, presenting with varying degrees of severity.

name: Neonatal severe primary hyperparathyroidism
clinical_summary: Neonatal severe primary hyperparathyroidism is a rare disease characterized by neonatal onset of symptoms, including abnormal calcium-phosphate metabolism and recurrent fractures, often resulting in narrowing of the chest and abnormal bone morphology.

name: Hypogonadism-mitral valve prolapse-intellectual disability syndrome
clinical_summary: This rare syndrome is characterized by hypogonadism, testicular abnormalities, and associated physical features such as high palate and short neck in infancy. Associated symptoms include decreased fertility and gynecomastia, with intellectual disability contributing to overall cognitive impairment.

name: Congenital hydrocephalus
clinical_summary: Congenital hydrocephalus is a rare condition characterized by the abnormal accumulation of cerebrospinal fluid leading to increased intracranial pressure. Clinical manifestations typically include hydrocephalus and associated physical features such as macrocephaly and facial asymmetry.

name: Neonatal hemochromatosis
clinical_summary: Neonatal hemochromatosis is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by neonatal onset of multiple congenital anomalies, including micrognathia, hypoglycemia, and liver pathology. These symptoms often occur at birth and can include congenital hepatic fibrosis.

name: Cutaneous mastocytosis-deafness-microtia syndrome
clinical_summary: Cutaneous mastocytosis-deafness-microtia syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a range of symptoms including high palate, triangular face, optic atrophy, and skin abnormalities, typically presenting in infancy or at birth. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern and is associated with significant clinical manifestations.

name: Hartnup disease
clinical_summary: Hartnup disease is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by symptoms such as emotional lability, cutaneous photosensitivity, and ataxia, among others. It can manifest at any age with various neurological and cutaneous manifestations.

name: Congenital fiber-type disproportion myopathy
inheritance: Autosomal dominant, Autosomal recessive, X-linked recessive
clinical_summary: Congenital fiber-type disproportion myopathy is a rare disease characterized by early onset hypotonia and progressive muscle weakness. The disease is typically identified in infancy or neonatal period and may present with associated features such as high palate, dental crowding, and motor delay.

name: Freeman-Sheldon syndrome
clinical_summary: Freeman-Sheldon syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a range of physical abnormalities including narrow mouth, abnormal dentition, and hypertelorism, often accompanied by hearing impairment and cryptorchidism. The condition exhibits autosomal dominant or autosomal recessive inheritance patterns.

name: Mitochondrial DNA depletion syndrome, encephalomyopathic form with methylmalonic aciduria
clinical_summary: Mitochondrial DNA depletion syndrome, encephalomyopathic form with methylmalonic aciduria is a rare disease characterized by infancy-onset microcephaly, sensorineural hearing and visual impairments, along with other associated ocular abnormalities. This disease exhibits mitochondrial inheritance.

name: Moebius syndrome
clinical_summary: Moebius syndrome is a rare condition characterized by congenital anomalies of facial muscles and extraocular muscles, presenting with symptoms such as open mouth, mask-like facies, and strabismus. It can manifest in utero or at birth with associated ptosis, ophthalmoplegia, and abnormal voice.

name: MASA syndrome
lab_findings: reduced corpus callosum size on mri
clinical_summary: MASA syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by delayed speech and language development, intellectual disability, and physical symptoms including spastic paraplegia and gait disturbance. It typically presents at birth with symptoms worsening over time, due to its X-linked recessive inheritance pattern.

name: Marshall syndrome
clinical_summary: Marshall syndrome is a rare condition characterized by neonatal onset of abnormalities including thick lower lip vermilion, brachycephaly, malar flattening, and hypertelorism. It also presents with abnormal dentition in affected individuals.

name: Neuroblastoma
clinical_summary: Neuroblastoma is a rare, malignant neoplasm of the nervous system, often presenting with elevated urinary catecholamines, weight loss, thrombocytopenia, and anemia. It can occur in infants, neonates, children, adolescents, and is not associated with a familial inheritance pattern.

name: Thanatophoric dysplasia
clinical_summary: Thanatophoric dysplasia is a rare, lethal genetic disorder characterized by abnormal bone development, including macrocephaly, narrow chest, platyspondyly, and abnormal metaphysis morphology. It is typically present at birth, with symptoms including hypotonia and redundant skin.

name: Metatropic dysplasia
clinical_summary: Metatropic dysplasia is a rare genetic disorder characterized by skeletal abnormalities, including high forehead, rib and metaphysis morphology irregularities, and joint stiffness. These symptoms typically present antenatally or in early infancy, often manifesting as a narrow chest and scoliosis.

name: Proximal myotonic myopathy
clinical_summary: Proximal myotonic myopathy is a rare autosomal dominant disorder characterized by a combination of skeletal muscle weakness, myotonia, and atrophic changes, often presenting with various age of onset in individuals with a family history of the disease.

name: Opsismodysplasia
clinical_summary: Opsismodysplasia is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by neonatal onset of symptoms including macrocephaly, abnormal bone morphology, and hypotonia. Notable physical features include large fontanelles, brachydactyly, and tapered fingers, often accompanied by an abnormal metaphysis.

name: Horizontal gaze palsy with progressive scoliosis
clinical_summary: Horizontal gaze palsy with progressive scoliosis is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by progressive scoliosis, kyphosis, and impaired rotational gaze. Common physical features include microcephaly, short neck, and strabismus, typically presenting in adolescence or childhood.

name: Linear nevus sebaceus syndrome
clinical_summary: Linear nevus sebaceus syndrome is a rare disease characterized by various congenital anomalies including prominent occiput, facial asymmetry, and abnormalities affecting the eye and vision. It typically presents in early life.

name: Jacobsen syndrome
clinical_summary: Jacobsen syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by intellectual disability, growth delay, and thrombocytopenia, among other symptoms. Symptoms often begin during fetal development and may persist through infancy and beyond.

name: Joubert syndrome with oculorenal defect
clinical_summary: Joubert syndrome with oculorenal defect is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by intellectual disability, ataxia, hypotonia, and global developmental delay, often presenting with nephropathy, retinal dystrophy in infancy or neonatal onset. This condition typically involves a combination of multisystem abnormalities.

name: Larsen-like osseous dysplasia-short stature syndrome
lab_findings: Abnormality of pelvic girdle bone morphology
Abnormal form of the vertebral bodies
clinical_summary: Larsen-like osseous dysplasia-short stature syndrome is a rare disease characterized by skeletal dysplasia with symptoms such as abnormally shaped metaphyses, hip dysplasia, and scoliosis, typically presenting at birth. The cause and inheritance pattern of this syndrome are not currently understood.

name: Trichorhinophalangeal syndrome type 2
clinical_summary: Trichorhinophalangeal syndrome type 2 is a rare disorder characterized by joint hypermobility, distinctive facial features, and an autosomal dominant inheritance pattern. It typically presents in childhood, infancy, or at birth, with features including a thin upper lip vermilion, long philtrum, protruding ear, bulbous nose, and deep philtrum.

name: Congenital pulmonary lymphangiectasia
clinical_summary: Congenital pulmonary lymphangiectasia is a rare disease characterized by congenital abnormalities in lymphatic vessels of the lungs, presenting with symptoms including cyanosis and congestive heart failure at neonatal age. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern.

name: Laurence-Moon syndrome
clinical_summary: Laurence-Moon syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by neonatal onset of intellectual disability, obesity, and various physical abnormalities including hand polydactyly and cryptorchidism. Affected individuals may also exhibit other distinctive features such as finger syndactyly and abnormality of the antitragus.

name: Schizencephaly
clinical_summary: Schizencephaly is a rare cerebral malformation characterized by a cleft in the cerebral hemispheres, presenting with symptoms such as strabismus, intellectual disability, seizure, spasticity, global developmental delay, and hemiparesis. It can manifest at any age with a possible autosomal dominant or autosomal recessive inheritance pattern.

name: Silver-Russell syndrome
lab_findings: elevated insulin-like growth factor binding protein 3 (igfbp-3)
clinical_summary: Silver-Russell syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by intrauterine growth retardation, low-set ears, triangular face, and blue sclerae, typically presenting at birth and accompanied by short stature and cachexia. It can occur through an autosomal dominant inheritance pattern.

name: Multiple sclerosis-ichthyosis-factor VIII deficiency syndrome
clinical_summary: Multiple sclerosis-ichthyosis-factor VIII deficiency syndrome is characterized by a combination of neurological symptoms in childhood, including optic atrophy, sensory neuropathy, and ataxia. The syndrome is further complicated by abnormalities in leukocyte morphology and an underlying factor VIII deficiency.

name: Sjögren-Larsson syndrome
lab_findings: very long-chain fatty acid levels elevated
clinical_summary: Sjögren-Larsson syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by retinal abnormalities, including retinopathy, myopia, and macular degeneration, often accompanied by dry skin and hyperkeratosis. This autosomal recessive condition typically manifests in infancy or neonatal period.

name: Sirenomelia
clinical_summary: Sirenomelia is a rare congenital disorder characterized by a spectrum of severe anomalies including ambiguous genitalia, urinary abnormalities, and spinal anomalies. It presents with a complex combination of antenatal and neonatal anomalies.

name: Infantile spasms-broad thumbs syndrome
lab_findings: Abnormality of the fontanelles or cranial sutures
clinical_summary: Infantile spasms-broad thumbs syndrome is a rare condition characterized by seizures and physical abnormalities present at birth, including cranial deformities, eye issues, and hypertelorism. The exact inheritance pattern for this condition is currently unknown.

name: Sturge-Weber syndrome
clinical_summary: Sturge-Weber syndrome is a rare disease characterized by the presence of a facial capillary hemangioma, often accompanied by seizures, strabismus, glaucoma, and optic atrophy. It typically manifests antenatally or in the neonatal period, and its cause is not related to inheritance.

name: Stormorken-Sjaastad-Langslet syndrome
clinical_summary: Stormorken-Sjaastad-Langslet syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by physical abnormalities including a high forehead, deeply set eyes, and miosis, accompanied by hematological features such as purpura, asplenia, and abnormal thrombocyte function. It is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern, affecting individuals of all ages.

name: Thrombocytopenia-absent radius syndrome
clinical_summary: Thrombocytopenia-absent radius syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by antenatal/neonatal onset of thrombocytopenia, coagulation abnormalities, and characteristic physical anomalies. Common physical features include absent radius, broad forehead, high forehead, and coxa valga.

name: VACTERL/VATER association
clinical_summary: VACTERL/VATER association is a rare congenital disease characterized by a complex combination of anomalies including gastrointestinal, respiratory, and genitourinary defects. The disease presents with symptoms occurring antenatally and neonatally, including polyhydramnios, premature birth, and various structural abnormalities.

name: Juvenile Paget disease
clinical_summary: Juvenile Paget disease is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by various developmental abnormalities including macrocephaly, dental anomalies, and other congenital features. Clinical manifestations include hypertension, hearing impairment, optic atrophy, and pectus carinatum.

name: Pallister-Killian syndrome
clinical_summary: Pallister-Killian syndrome is characterized by a range of distinctive facial features including a thick upper lip, thin upper lip, everted lower lip, long philtrum, and short neck, often accompanied by ptosis. Symptoms of this rare disorder typically appear before or shortly after birth.

name: Proteus syndrome
clinical_summary: Proteus syndrome is a rare condition characterized by a range of distinctive vascular and cutaneous lesions, including capillary hemangioma, epidermal nevus, and vascular skin abnormality, which typically present in infancy and manifest as a combination of skin abnormalities. The condition is not believed to be heritable or inherited.

name: Prune belly syndrome
inheritance: Autosomal dominant, Not applicable, X-linked recessive
clinical_summary: Prune belly syndrome is a rare congenital disorder characterized by multicystic kidney dysplasia, recurrent urinary tract infections, and various genitourinary anomalies. It presents antenatally or in the neonatal period with symptoms including cryptorchidism, abnormality of the bladder, and hydroureter.

name: Peroxisomal acyl-CoA oxidase deficiency
clinical_summary: Peroxisomal acyl-CoA oxidase deficiency is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by multiple congenital anomalies including epicanthus, hypertelorism, low-set ears, strabismus, and sensorineural hearing impairment. Affected individuals typically present with these symptoms at birth.

name: Difference of sex development-intellectual disability syndrome
clinical_summary: Difference of sex development-intellectual disability syndrome is a rare congenital disorder characterized by hypogonadism, small scrotum, and intellectual disability. Clinical features also include thin vermilion border, abnormality of the face, short philtrum, and short neck, typically presenting at neonatal age.

name: Costello syndrome
clinical_summary: Costello syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by macrocephaly, narrow palate, short neck, strabismus, ptosis, and skin abnormalities, often present before birth or at birth. It typically follows an autosomal dominant pattern of inheritance.

name: Congenital rubella syndrome
lab_findings: Abnormality of the fontanelles or cranial sutures
clinical_summary: Congenital rubella syndrome is a condition characterized by a range of systemic and multisystemic abnormalities, including abnormalities of the cranial structure, hearing impairment, and visual impairment. It presents at birth due to infection with the rubella virus during pregnancy.

name: Free sialic acid storage disease
clinical_summary: Free sialic acid storage disease is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by symptoms including nystagmus, oculomotor apraxia, abnormal skin pigmentation, intellectual disability, seizures, and ataxia. It typically presents from the antenatal period through infancy and neonatal stages.

name: Osteogenesis imperfecta
inheritance: Autosomal dominant, Autosomal recessive, X-linked recessive
clinical_summary: Osteogenesis imperfecta is a rare disease characterized by various craniofacial abnormalities including Brachycephaly, Macrocephaly, Prominent occiput, and Micrognathia, often accompanied by mixed hearing impairment. The disease manifestations can occur in all ages and are influenced by autosomal dominant, autosomal recessive, and X-linked recessive modes of inheritance.

name: Mucopolysaccharidosis type 3
clinical_summary: Mucopolysaccharidosis type 3 is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a range of symptoms that include chronic otitis media, delayed speech and language development, malabsorption, coarse hair, and generalized hirsutism. Symptoms typically manifest in childhood and progress to significant progressive neurologic deterioration.

name: Hirschsprung disease
lab_findings: Functional abnormality of the gastrointestinal tract
inheritance: Autosomal dominant, Autosomal recessive, Multigenic/multifactorial, Not applicable
clinical_summary: Hirschsprung disease is characterized by an absence of ganglionic cells in the distal colon, leading to symptoms such as intestinal obstruction, abdominal distention, and weight loss. Clinical presentation typically occurs in childhood, infancy, or the neonatal period.

name: Glycogen storage disease due to glucose-6-phosphatase deficiency
clinical_summary: Glycogen storage disease due to glucose-6-phosphatase deficiency is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by full cheeks, hypoglycemia, and hyperuricemia, among other symptoms. Symptoms manifest in infancy or neonatally and include seizure, hypotonia, and recurrent respiratory infections.

name: Gaucher disease
clinical_summary: Gaucher disease is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by splenomegaly, hepatomegaly, anemia, decreased beta-glucocerebrosidase level, and symptoms such as fatigue and strabismus. It can manifest at any age due to its autosomal recessive inheritance pattern.

name: GM1 gangliosidosis
clinical_summary: GM1 gangliosidosis is a rare, childhood-onset disease characterized by a range of physical abnormalities, including inguinal hernia, macroglossia, and coarse facial features. The condition is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern.

name: Waardenburg syndrome
clinical_summary: Waardenburg syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by congenital hearing impairment, distinctive facial features, and skin pigmentation abnormalities. It may present at infancy or neonatal age, with inheritance patterns following both autosomal dominant and autosomal recessive patterns.

name: Usher syndrome
clinical_summary: Usher syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by progressive sensorineural hearing impairment and visual impairment, ultimately leading to blindness. Symptoms typically begin in infancy, childhood, or neonatal periods, often with nyctalopia (night blindness) initially.

name: Retinitis pigmentosa
inheritance: Autosomal dominant, Autosomal recessive, Mitochondrial inheritance, X-linked recessive
clinical_summary: Retinitis pigmentosa is a rare inherited disorder characterized by progressive visual impairment and potentially associated sensory hearing impairment. This disease manifests across various age groups, including childhood, adolescence, and adulthood, through different modes of inheritance.

name: Pelizaeus-Merzbacher disease
clinical_summary: Pelizaeus-Merzbacher disease is a rare neurological disorder characterized by symptoms such as microcephaly, hearing and visual impairment, and nystagmus. It exhibits variable inheritance patterns, including X-linked dominant and X-linked recessive modes of transmission.

name: X-linked recessive ocular albinism
clinical_summary: X-linked recessive ocular albinism is a rare genetic disorder characterized by multiple ocular manifestations, typically presenting in infancy or neonatally. It is marked by symptoms such as astigmatism, strabismus, photophobia, and abnormal macular morphology.

name: Phenylketonuria
clinical_summary: Phenylketonuria is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by phenylalaninuria, microcephaly, atypical behavior, osteopenia, eczematoïd dermatitis, and hypopigmentation of the skin. It typically presents in infancy.

name: Chondrodysplasia-difference of sex development syndrome
lab_findings: Abnormality of pelvic girdle bone morphology
clinical_summary: Chondrodysplasia-difference of sex development syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by microcephaly, macrotia, telecanthus, narrow chest, intellectual disability, and intrauterine growth retardation. This condition presents with severe symptoms that begin in the antenatal or neonatal stages.

name: Weaver syndrome
clinical_summary: Weaver syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by accelerated skeletal maturation and multiple associated physical abnormalities. Key features include facial dysmorphia, large hands, and joint stiffness.

name: Fanconi anemia
lab_findings: Abnormality of blood and blood-forming tissues
clinical_summary: Fanconi anemia is a rare disorder characterized by physical anomalies including hypopigmented skin patches and abnormal thumb morphology, as well as hematological abnormalities such as thrombocytopenia and leukopenia. It is a childhood-onset condition that can exhibit both autosomal recessive and X-linked recessive inheritance patterns.

name: Eosinophilic fasciitis
clinical_summary: Eosinophilic fasciitis is a rare disease characterized by eosinophilia, abnormality of eosinophils, and various systemic symptoms including edema, acrocyanosis, arthritis, and subcutaneous nodules. It predominantly affects adults with unknown inheritance pattern.

name: Granulomatosis with polyangiitis
clinical_summary: Granulomatosis with polyangiitis is a rare disease characterized by the presence of granulomas and vasculitis, manifesting with symptoms such as sinusitis and epistaxis. It affects individuals across various age groups, including adolescents, adults, children, and the elderly.

name: Immunoglobulin A vasculitis
clinical_summary: Immunoglobulin A vasculitis is a rare disease characterized by symptoms including hematuria, bruising susceptibility, and purpura, typically presenting in childhood. The disorder often manifests with a combination of gastrointestinal symptoms such as nausea and vomiting, and abdominal pain.

name: Anti-glomerular basement membrane disease
clinical_summary: Anti-glomerular basement membrane disease is a rare condition characterized by kidney damage resulting in symptoms such as proteinuria, hematuria, anemia, and respiratory insufficiency. The disease typically presents in individuals of various ages, including adolescence, adulthood, and old age, without a familial inheritance pattern.

name: Microscopic polyangiitis
clinical_summary: Microscopic polyangiitis is a rare systemic vasculitis characterized by kidney damage, including renal insufficiency and Glomerulonephritis, as well as various extrarenal manifestations. Common symptoms include Hematuria, Skin rash, Fever, and Hemoptysis, affecting individuals across the lifespan from childhood to adulthood.

name: Locked-in syndrome
clinical_summary: Locked-in syndrome is a rare neurological disorder characterized by symptoms including abnormal eye and vision irregularities, diplopia, and spasticity or hypertonia. Onset occurs across all ages.

name: Takayasu arteritis
clinical_summary: Takayasu arteritis is a rare disease characterized by symptoms such as abnormal heart valve morphology, weight loss, fever, dilatation, vasculitis, and arteritis in individuals who have reached adolescence or adulthood. It does not appear to follow a specific inheritance pattern.

name: Whipple disease
clinical_summary: Whipple disease is characterized by a range of systemic symptoms including uveitis, depression, myoclonus, arthritis, splenomegaly, and fever. The exact clinical presentation can vary between cases.

name: Kawasaki disease
clinical_summary: Kawasaki disease is a rare, multifactorial, condition characterized by a combination of fever, leukocytosis, thrombocytosis, pericarditis, abnormal heart valve morphology, and arthritis, primarily affecting individuals in infancy, childhood, and adolescence. Its multifactorial inheritance pattern suggests a complex interplay of genetic and environmental factors.

name: Multiminicore myopathy
key_symptoms: Respiratory insufficiency due to muscle weakness
clinical_summary: Multiminicore myopathy is a rare genetic disorder characterized by neonatal to childhood onset of myopathy, joint hypermobility, and generalized hypotonia, often accompanied by strabismus and joint stiffness. It exhibits autosomal dominant and autosomal recessive inheritance patterns.

name: Polymyositis
clinical_summary: Polymyositis is a rare adult-onset inflammatory muscle disease characterized by proximal muscle weakness, elevated circulating creatine kinase concentration, and abnormal EMG findings. It presents with symptoms such as hypotonia, arthralgia, and autoimmunity.

name: Dermatomyositis
key_symptoms: Diffuse reticular or finely nodular infiltrations
Respiratory insufficiency due to muscle weakness
clinical_summary: Dermatomyositis is a rare disease characterized by weight loss, respiratory insufficiency, and muscle weakness leading to respiratory complications. It predominantly affects adults and the elderly.

name: Behçet disease
clinical_summary: Behçet disease is a rare, multifactorial disorder characterized by a constellation of systemic symptoms including oral ulcers, photophobia, meningitis, arthritis, subcutaneous nodules, and fever. It may present at any age, from childhood through adulthood.

name: Oculopharyngeal muscular dystrophy
clinical_summary: Oculopharyngeal muscular dystrophy is a rare disease characterized by symptoms including ptosis, ophthalmoplegia, dysphagia, and myopathy in adults and elderly individuals. The condition is associated with autosomal dominant and autosomal recessive patterns of inheritance.

name: Alpha-N-acetylgalactosaminidase deficiency
clinical_summary: Alpha-N-acetylgalactosaminidase deficiency is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by the presence of various symptoms including coarse facial features, hearing impairment, cataracts, blindness, and nystagmus, often associated with intellectual disability. It can present at any age, including infancy, childhood, or adulthood.

name: Inclusion body myositis
clinical_summary: Inclusion body myositis is a rare adult-onset muscle disorder characterized by progressive skeletal muscle atrophy, weakness, and elevated circulating creatine kinase concentration, often accompanied by electromyography abnormalities and ragged-red muscle fibers. This disease predominantly affects the elderly.

name: Eosinophilic granulomatosis with polyangiitis
clinical_summary: Eosinophilic granulomatosis with polyangiitis is a rare disease characterized by respiratory symptoms such as asthma, along with systemic manifestations including sinusitis, purpura, urticaria, weight loss, and eosinophilia. The disease can occur at various stages of life, including adolescence, adulthood, and old age.

name: Maple syrup urine disease
clinical_summary: Maple syrup urine disease is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by symptoms including abnormality of the pharynx, intellectual disability, seizure, ataxia, and hypotonia. Onset typically occurs in childhood, infancy, or neonatal stages, often resulting in global developmental delay.

name: Methylmalonic acidemia with homocystinuria
