clinical_summary: Methylmalonic acidemia with homocystinuria is a rare disease characterized by symptoms including microcephaly, retinopathy, amblyopia, intellectual disability, seizure, and hypotonia. It has variable inheritance patterns, including autosomal recessive and X-linked recessive.

name: Glutathione synthetase deficiency
lab_findings: Increased level of L-pyroglutamic acid in urine
clinical_summary: Glutathione synthetase deficiency is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by severe abnormalities in the nervous and immune systems, along with significant metabolic complications. It is typically diagnosed in the neonatal period and presents with symptoms such as hemolytic anemia and chronic metabolic acidosis.

name: X-linked alpha-thalassemia-intellectual disability syndrome
clinical_summary: X-linked alpha-thalassemia-intellectual disability syndrome is a rare, X-linked recessive disorder characterized by severe neurodevelopmental disabilities and distinctive physical anomalies, which become apparent in infancy or at birth. Symptoms include cryptorchidism, male pseudohermaphroditism, ambiguous genitalia, microcephaly, abnormal faces, and hypertelorism.

name: Familial Mediterranean fever
clinical_summary: Familial Mediterranean fever is a rare disease characterized by symptoms of constipation, abdominal pain, arthralgia, myalgia, proteinuria, and irritability across various age groups. It is inherited through an autosomal dominant or autosomal recessive pattern.

name: Simpson-Golabi-Behmel syndrome
clinical_summary: Simpson-Golabi-Behmel syndrome is a rare X-linked recessive disorder characterized by tall stature, wide mouth, macroglossia, and macrocephaly, often presenting with multicystic kidney dysplasia and cryptorchidism. It manifests in individuals from the antenatal period through childhood and infancy, often persisting to neonatal stages.

name: Multiple symmetric lipomatosis
inheritance: Autosomal dominant, Autosomal recessive, Mitochondrial inheritance, Not applicable
clinical_summary: Multiple symmetric lipomatosis is a rare disease characterized by the development of multiple lipomas accompanied by symptoms such as insulin resistance, gait disturbance, and joint stiffness. The exact inheritance pattern is not applicable, with onset occurring across various age groups, from childhood to adulthood.

name: Dermatitis herpetiformis
clinical_summary: Dermatitis herpetiformis is a rare skin condition characterized by intense pruritus and urticaria. It is associated with systemic symptoms including microcytic anemia, malabsorption, and recurrent fractures, indicative of autoimmunity.

name: Mucopolysaccharidosis type 4
clinical_summary: Mucopolysaccharidosis type 4 is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by symptoms such as abnormality of the dentition, hearing impairment, short neck, and skeletal abnormalities including pectus carinatum and abnormal rib and metaphysis morphology. This condition primarily manifests in childhood or infancy.

name: Giant cell arteritis
clinical_summary: Giant cell arteritis is a rare disease characterized by inflammation of the blood vessels, presenting with symptoms such as vasculitis, joint stiffness, headache, and fever. It primarily affects adults and has a multifactorial inheritance pattern.

name: Budd-Chiari syndrome
clinical_summary: Budd-Chiari syndrome is a rare disease characterized by portal hypertension, ascites, splenomegaly, cirrhosis, fever, and abdominal pain, affecting individuals of all ages. It is a multigenic/multifactorial condition with no specific age of onset defined.

name: Niemann-Pick disease type C
clinical_summary: Niemann-Pick disease type C is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by vertical supranuclear gaze palsy, jaundice, and progressive organ dysfunction, including abnormal liver function, leading to mental deterioration and gait disturbance. This disorder affects individuals of all ages, manifesting through various neurological and hepatic symptoms.

name: Rabies
clinical_summary: Rabies is a rare disease characterized by symptoms including depression, hallucinations, anxiety, fever, diarrhea, nausea, and vomiting that can affect individuals of any age. The disease is not inherited, but its exact cause is not specified within the given information.

name: Botulism
clinical_summary: Botulism is a rare disease characterized by a range of symptoms including urinary retention, xerostomia, diplopia, dysarthria, dysphagia, nausea and vomiting. It affects individuals of all ages and is not inherited.

name: Wolfram syndrome
clinical_summary: Wolfram syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by multiple systemic symptoms, including sensorineural hearing impairment and endocrinopathy, presenting from adolescence to adulthood. Common symptoms include optic atrophy, diabetes mellitus, diabetes insipidus, polydipsia, and recurrent urinary tract infections.

name: Pemphigus vulgaris
clinical_summary: Pemphigus vulgaris is a rare autoimmune disorder characterized by atypical scarring of the skin, which can manifest at various ages including adulthood, childhood, or old age. Symptoms may also include urticaria, weight loss, and recurrent infections, with feeding difficulties possible in infancy.

name: Autosomal dominant hyper-IgE syndrome due to STAT3 deficiency
clinical_summary: Autosomal dominant hyper-IgE syndrome due to STAT3 deficiency is a rare genetic disorder characterized by eczematoid dermatitis, recurrent respiratory and infections, and elevated IgE levels. It typically presents in infancy or at birth and is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern.

name: Stickler syndrome
clinical_summary: Stickler syndrome is a rare genetic condition characterized by craniofacial dysmorphisms, including malar flattening, epicanthus, microretrognathia, and hypoplasia of the maxilla, often associated with visual impairment. It may have an autosomal dominant or autosomal recessive pattern of inheritance, and typically presents at or before birth.

name: Tetralogy of Fallot
clinical_summary: Tetralogy of Fallot is a rare congenital heart defect with variable expression, characterized by multiple craniofacial abnormalities. It manifests prenatally and is associated with an autosomal dominant and multigenic/multifactorial inheritance pattern.

name: Bazex-Dupré-Christol syndrome
lab_findings: abnormal facial and scalp hair pattern histopathology
clinical_summary: Bazex-Dupré-Christol syndrome is a rare X-linked dominant disorder characterized by sparse or coarse hair, milia, and basal cell carcinoma, typically presenting in infancy or at birth. The syndrome also includes other skin and hair-related abnormalities, such as subcutaneous nodules and pili torti.

name: 46,XX gonadal dysgenesis
inheritance: Autosomal dominant, Autosomal recessive, Not applicable, X-linked recessive
clinical_summary: 46,XX gonadal dysgenesis is a rare condition characterized by decreased fertility, delayed puberty, and hormonal imbalances, presenting in adulthood. It is typically associated with an abnormal gonadal development in individuals with typical female karyotype.

name: ICF syndrome
clinical_summary: ICF syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by macrocephaly, micrognathia, intellectual disability, global developmental delay, communicating hydrocephalus, and abnormality of neutrophils, typically presenting in childhood. It follows an autosomal recessive inheritance pattern.

name: Isolated Joubert syndrome
clinical_summary: Isolated Joubert syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by severe intellectual disability, global developmental delay, and physical symptoms including hypotonia, ataxia, and oculomotor apraxia that begin before birth. Cerebellar vermis hypoplasia is a defining feature of this disease.

name: Holt-Oram syndrome
clinical_summary: Holt-Oram syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by abnormal cardiovascular system morphology, skeletal abnormalities including split hands and triphalangeal thumbs, and joint stiffness. This condition often presents with a variable severity of symptoms at birth, and is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern.

name: Roberts syndrome
clinical_summary: Roberts syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by brachycephaly, microcephaly, and other craniofacial anomalies. It is associated with underdeveloped nasal alae and abnormality of the upper limb.

name: Lujan-Fryns syndrome
clinical_summary: Lujan-Fryns syndrome is a rare X-linked recessive disorder characterized by distinctive facial features including high palate, macrocephaly, micrognathia, and a high forehead. It is often associated with intellectual disability and atypical behavior, typically presenting in infancy or neonatal stages.

name: Seckel syndrome
clinical_summary: Seckel syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by the combination of microcephaly, narrow face, micrognathia, convex nasal ridge, and intellectual disability, typically presenting prenatally. It follows an autosomal recessive inheritance pattern.

name: Caudal regression syndrome
lab_findings: Abnormality of pelvic girdle bone morphology
Abnormality of the wing of the ilium
clinical_summary: Caudal regression syndrome is a rare condition characterized by a combination of congenital abnormalities, including bowel incontinence, abnormal vertebral segmentation and fusion, and sacral aplasia/hypoplasia. The condition is typically identified prenatally or in the neonatal period, and its etiology is attributed to a multigenic/multifactorial mechanism.

name: Werner syndrome
clinical_summary: Werner syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a combination of physical and hormonal abnormalities, including hypogonadism and a slender build. Symptoms typically present during adolescence or adulthood and may include a wide range of developmental and degenerative changes.

name: Waardenburg-Shah syndrome
clinical_summary: Waardenburg-Shah syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by neonatal onset of hearing impairment and associated with multiple congenital anomalies, including abnormalities of the nose, eye, and potentially other features. It follows an autosomal dominant or autosomal recessive inheritance pattern.

name: Peters plus syndrome
clinical_summary: Peters plus syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a range of congenital anomalies including hypospadias, cleft palate, and hypertelorism. Symptoms of Peters plus syndrome include hypospadias, cleft palate, cleft upper lip, hydrocephalus, and preauricular skin tags, often presenting antenatally or in the neonatal period.

name: Neurofibromatosis-Noonan syndrome
clinical_summary: Neurofibromatosis-Noonan syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by abnormalities of the face, learning disabilities, and other distinctive physical features such as hypertelorism and a webbed neck. It is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern and typically manifests from infancy or at birth.

name: Campomelic dysplasia
clinical_summary: Campomelic dysplasia is a rare autosomal dominant disorder characterized by a combination of skeletal malformations, including macrocephaly, micrognathia, short neck, and 11 pairs of ribs, often presenting with cleft palate. The condition manifests prenatally or in the neonatal period.

name: Nance-Horan syndrome
clinical_summary: Nance-Horan syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a constellation of physical abnormalities, including dentition anomalies, distinctive facial features, and ear abnormalities. It is a neonatally presenting X-linked dominant disorder.

name: Netherton syndrome
clinical_summary: Netherton syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a combination of cutaneous, hair, and systemic symptoms that manifest in infancy or neonatal period. Symptoms include eczematoid dermatitis, malabsorption, urticaria, asthma, and abnormal hair, often accompanied by acanthosis nigricans.

name: Hereditary sensory and autonomic neuropathy type 4
clinical_summary: Hereditary sensory and autonomic neuropathy type 4 is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by self-mutilation, anhidrosis, intellectual disability, recurrent infections, and abnormal nerve conduction. It typically presents at a young age, including in infancy and childhood.

name: Non-distal duplication 10q syndrome
clinical_summary: Non-distal duplication 10q syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by multiple congenital anomalies, including joint hypermobility, microcephaly, and micrognathia. The exact age of onset is neonatal, and the inheritance pattern is unknown.

name: Trisomy 12p syndrome
clinical_summary: Trisomy 12p syndrome is a rare genetic condition characterized by distinct facial features such as inverted vermilion of the lower lip. It presents with a neonatal onset of symptoms, including turricephaly, malar flattening, epicanthus, full cheeks, and hypertelorism.

name: 3q13 microdeletion syndrome
lab_findings: Abnormality of the fontanelles or cranial sutures
clinical_summary: 3q13 microdeletion syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by antenatal to neonatal onset of macrocephaly, epicanthus, and hypertelorism among other symptoms such as cryptorchidism, urinary system abnormalities, and issues with cranial sutures. This condition typically does not follow a familial inheritance pattern.

name: Xp22.3 microdeletion syndrome
clinical_summary: Xp22.3 microdeletion syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by neonatal onset of hypogonadotropic hypogonadism, decreased fertility, and other symptoms including myopia and polycystic ovaries. This condition presents secondary amenorrhea and an abnormality of chromosome segregation in affected individuals.

name: Distal deletion 17q syndrome
clinical_summary: Distal deletion 17q syndrome is characterized by a distinct set of facial features including hypertelorism, narrow mouth, and abnormal philtrum, often present from antenatal and neonatal life due to the rare and genetic condition.

name: Distal deletion 3p syndrome
clinical_summary: Distal deletion 3p syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by distinctive facial features, including posteriorly rotated ears, hypertelorism, long philtrum, micrognathia, telecanthus, and ptosis. The age of onset is typically antenatal or neonatal, with inheritance pattern remaining unknown.

name: Monosomy 13q14 syndrome
clinical_summary: Monosomy 13q14 syndrome is a rare genetic condition characterized by microcephaly, intellectual disability, and distinctive facial features. The onset typically occurs in infancy or neonatal period.

name: Distal deletion 13q syndrome
clinical_summary: Distal deletion 13q syndrome is a rare condition characterized by a combination of physical abnormalities, including ambiguous genitalia, microcephaly, and hand anomalies, which can present at birth or in infancy. The inherited pattern of this condition is currently unknown.

name: Distal deletion 10p syndrome
clinical_summary: Distal deletion 10p syndrome is a rare disease characterized by physical and developmental abnormalities, including intellectual disability and severe global developmental delay. Its symptoms, such as posteriorly rotated ears and wide nasal bridge, are typically present from childhood.

name: Ring chromosome 8 syndrome
clinical_summary: Ring chromosome 8 syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by distinct physical abnormalities and renal anomalies. It presents with a range of congenital features including abnormal ureter morphology and hydronephrosis from birth.

name: Currarino syndrome
clinical_summary: Currarino syndrome is a rare condition characterized by aplasia/hypoplasia of the sacrum, often associated with sacrococcygeal teratoma, anal atresia/stenosis, and abdominal distention. Affected individuals may experience low back pain, and the condition can manifest at any age.

name: Ring chromosome 6 syndrome
clinical_summary: Ring chromosome 6 syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by clinical features including microcephaly, epicanthus, hypertelorism, macrotia, a wide nasal bridge, and a short neck. It manifests from birth or in infancy with unknown inheritance pattern.

name: Ring chromosome 1 syndrome
clinical_summary: Ring chromosome 1 syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by distinct facial features and growth restriction, including microcephaly, a round face, and anteverted nares. The disease typically presents neonatally and its inheritance pattern is currently unknown.

name: Ring chromosome 10 syndrome
clinical_summary: Ring chromosome 10 syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by distinct facial features, including thin vermilion border, hypertelorism, long philtrum, micrognathia, and low-set ears, with a wide nasal bridge. Onset occurs in utero or at birth.

name: Progressive familial intrahepatic cholestasis
clinical_summary: Progressive familial intrahepatic cholestasis is a rare autosomal recessive disease characterized by jaundice, cholestasis, and other severe symptoms, leading to significant morbidity. The disease presents in various age ranges, including infancy, childhood, and adolescence, with failure to thrive, splenomegaly, thrombocytopenia, and coagulopathy common features.

name: Carey-Fineman-Ziter syndrome
clinical_summary: Carey-Fineman-Ziter syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by neonatal onset of multiple congenital anomalies. Symptoms include glossoptosis, cleft palate, high palate, thin vermilion border, microcephaly, and features of Pierre-Robin sequence.

name: Heart defects-limb shortening syndrome
lab_findings: Abnormal form of the vertebral bodies
clinical_summary: Heart defects-limb shortening syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive condition primarily affecting infants, characterized by congenital heart defects and skeletal abnormalities, often lethal in infancy. Primary symptoms include abnormal rib morphology, narrow chest, and various cardiac defects such as ventricular and atrial septal defects.

name: C syndrome
clinical_summary: C syndrome is a rare disease characterized by a combination of physical abnormalities, including posteriorly rotated ears, cryptorchidism, gingival overgrowth, high palate, trigonocephaly, and microcephaly, which begin to manifest in the antenatal or neonatal period. The cause of C syndrome is currently unknown and not inherited.

name: 48,XXYY syndrome
clinical_summary: 48,XXYY syndrome is characterized by symptoms such as joint hypermobility, infertility, and intellectual disability, which often manifest during adolescence, childhood, infancy, or neonatal periods. The underlying cause of this syndrome remains unknown, and its inheritance pattern is not applicable.

name: Fraser syndrome
clinical_summary: Fraser syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a combination of congenital abnormalities, including abnormal pinna morphology, urinary system abnormalities, sight loss, and finger syndactyly. It often presents with symptoms such as posteriorly rotated ears and cryptophthalmos, typically from an antenatal or neonatal age.

name: Léri-Weill dyschondrosteosis
lab_findings: Abnormality of pelvic girdle bone morphology
clinical_summary: Léri-Weill dyschondrosteosis is a rare genetic condition characterized by abnormalities in bone morphology, including the humerus, carpal bones, and metaphysis. It manifests at various ages, from infancy to adolescence, with an autosomal dominant inheritance pattern.

name: Autosomal recessive spondylocostal dysostosis
lab_findings: Abnormal form of the vertebral bodies
clinical_summary: Autosomal recessive spondylocostal dysostosis is a rare disease characterized by antenatal onset of short neck, abnormal rib morphology, and associated complications such as scoliosis and respiratory insufficiency. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern.

name: Duane retraction syndrome
inheritance: Autosomal dominant, Autosomal recessive, Not applicable
clinical_summary: Duane retraction syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by strabismus, abnormality of eye movement, and short palpebral fissure, typically manifesting in infancy or neonatal age. It can be inherited in an autosomal dominant or autosomal recessive pattern, or may not be applicable.

name: Noonan syndrome with multiple lentigines
clinical_summary: Noonan syndrome with multiple lentigines is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a combination of physical abnormalities, including multiple lentigines, hypertelorism, and skin hyperextensibility, among others. It typically exhibits autosomal dominant inheritance and symptoms present at birth.

name: 3C syndrome
lab_findings: Abnormality of the fontanelles or cranial sutures
clinical_summary: 3C syndrome is a rare disease characterized by the presence of multiple craniofacial and neurological anomalies, including cleft palate, abnormal skull development, and hydrocephalus. It can present prenatally, at birth, or in infancy due to either an autosomal recessive or X-linked recessive inheritance pattern.

name: Aase-Smith syndrome type 1
clinical_summary: Aase-Smith syndrome type 1 is a rare autosomal dominant disorder characterized by a combination of prenatal onset symptoms, including cleft palate, abnormal pinna morphology, Dandy-Walker malformation, joint stiffness, and talipes equinovarus. Additionally, symptoms such as trismus may be present in the neonatal period.

name: Ablepharon macrostomia syndrome
clinical_summary: Ablepharon macrostomia syndrome is a rare condition characterized by a combination of features including ambiguous genitalia, wide mouth, hypoplasia of the maxilla, and hearing impairment, among others. It presents in the antenatal or neonatal period and is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern.

name: Trisomy X syndrome
clinical_summary: Trisomy X syndrome is characterized by abnormalities in chromosome segregation, and is marked by symptoms including tall stature, epicanthus, delayed speech and language development, and global and motor delays. Onset typically occurs in childhood or infancy.

name: Triploidy syndrome
clinical_summary: Triploidy syndrome is a rare condition characterized by intrauterine growth retardation, abnormal chromosome segregation, and various congenital anomalies including posteriorly rotated ears and hypoplasia of the penis. It presents antenatally and in the neonatal period.

name: Mitochondrial myopathy and sideroblastic anemia
clinical_summary: Mitochondrial myopathy and sideroblastic anemia is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by systemic symptoms including high palate, microcephaly, long philtrum, micrognathia, glaucoma, and delayed puberty. It manifests in individuals during infancy, childhood, or adolescence.

name: Carnitine palmitoyl transferase 1A deficiency
clinical_summary: Carnitine palmitoyl transferase 1A deficiency is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by infancy or neonatal onset of symptoms including lethargy, seizures, and hypotonia. Clinical manifestations of this disorder may also include atypical behavior, coma, and reduced tendon reflexes.

name: Amelocerebrohypohidrotic syndrome
clinical_summary: Amelocerebrohypohidrotic syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by hypohidrosis, neurological symptoms such as seizures, spasticity, and mental deterioration, often presenting in childhood or infancy. This syndrome may also be accompanied by developmental regression and abnormal EEG findings.

name: Griscelli syndrome
clinical_summary: Griscelli syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by hypopigmented skin patches, thrombocytopenia, and abnormalities in neutrophils and platelet count. Symptoms typically manifest in early childhood or infancy, involving reduced tendon reflexes, premature graying of hair, and leukopenia.

name: Epilepsy-telangiectasia syndrome
clinical_summary: Epilepsy-telangiectasia syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by intellectual disability, seizures, and distinctive facial features including a long philtrum and anteverted nares, often accompanied by conjunctival telangiectasia and abnormal dentition. It typically manifests in childhood.

name: Oculogastrointestinal muscular dystrophy
clinical_summary: Oculogastrointestinal muscular dystrophy is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a combination of gastrointestinal and muscular symptoms that manifest in childhood, including malabsorption, gastroparesis, and skeletal muscle atrophy. Its presentation is marked by systemic involvement, including abdominal distention, cachexia, and myopathy.

name: Proximal Xq28 duplication syndrome
clinical_summary: Proximal Xq28 duplication syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by various developmental abnormalities, including gait disturbance, speech or vocalization issues, and congenital malformations such as cryptorchidism, hypospadias, and pectus excavatum. Symptoms typically manifest at birth or before birth.

name: Trisomy 5p syndrome
lab_findings: normal to increased height for age
clinical_summary: Trisomy 5p syndrome is a rare chromosomal disorder characterized by macrocephaly, dolichocephaly, and associated physical features such as a round face and protruding ears. It typically manifests in infancy, with symptoms present antenatally or at birth.

name: Trisomy 4p syndrome
clinical_summary: Trisomy 4p syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a range of symptoms including abnormal ears, dentition, palate, and facial features. Affected individuals often exhibit symptoms from birth, with abnormalities detectable antennatally or in the neonatal period.

name: Trisomy 8q syndrome
clinical_summary: Trisomy 8q syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by distinctive physical features that typically present neonatally. Symptoms may include posteriorly rotated ears, everted lower lip vermilion, and other craniofacial abnormalities.

name: Distal duplication 6p syndrome
clinical_summary: Distal duplication 6p syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by distinctive facial features, including narrow mouth, pointed chin, micrognathia, prominent nasal bridge, and short neck. It is associated with antenatal and neonatal age of onset, with unknown inheritance pattern.

name: Mosaic trisomy 14 syndrome
lab_findings: aneuploid marker chromosomes in metaphase spreads
clinical_summary: Mosaic trisomy 14 syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by distinct physical features such as posteriorly rotated ears, wide mouth, and prominent nasal bridge, typically evident at birth. The inheritance pattern of this condition remains unknown.

name: Non-distal duplication 13q syndrome
clinical_summary: Non-distal duplication 13q syndrome is a rare condition characterized by a combination of congenital abnormalities, including cryptorchidism, dental, and facial anomalies, manifesting antenatally or in the neonatal period. The exact inheritance pattern of this condition remains unknown.

name: 17p11.2 microduplication syndrome
clinical_summary: 17p11.2 microduplication syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by intellectual disability, global developmental delay, and associated features such as hypotonia, dysarthria, and pharyngeal abnormalities. It typically presents in childhood, infancy, or neonatal period with symptoms including autism and moderate developmental delays.

name: Distal duplication 14q syndrome
lab_findings: Abnormality of the upper urinary tract
clinical_summary: Distal duplication 14q syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by pre- and/or perinatal manifestation of hearing impairment, glaucoma, cardiovascular abnormalities, and lung anomalies. The inherited pattern of this disorder is currently unknown.

name: PAGOD syndrome
lab_findings: Abnormal morphology of female internal genitalia
clinical_summary: PAGOD syndrome is a rare condition characterized by abnormal internal genitalia, respiratory, and urinary anomalies, primarily manifesting in neonates. Affected individuals often exhibit a combination of abnormalities including pulmonary hypoplasia, cardiovascular morphology issues, and multicystic kidney dysplasia.

name: Aicardi-Goutières syndrome
clinical_summary: Aicardi-Goutières syndrome is a rare, progressive neurodegenerative disorder characterized by global developmental delay, spasticity, and hypertonia, often accompanied by gait disturbance, porencephaly, and a convex nasal ridge. It manifests either antenatally, in infancy, or at birth, and is typically inherited in an autosomal dominant and autosomal recessive pattern.

name: Hypoglossia-hypodactyly syndrome
clinical_summary: Hypoglossia-hypodactyly syndrome is a rare condition characterized by a combination of narrow mouth, cleft palate, micrognathia, wide nasal bridge, telecanthus, and hypodontia. The exact cause of this condition remains unknown and is thought to occur either before birth or shortly after.

name: Agnathia-holoprosencephaly-situs inversus syndrome
inheritance: Autosomal dominant, Autosomal recessive, Not applicable
clinical_summary: Agnathia-holoprosencephaly-situs inversus syndrome is a rare multisystem disorder characterized by multiple congenital anomalies, including craniofacial and neural abnormalities, often present at birth. The condition is thought to be caused by a combination of genetic mutations, with possible inheritance patterns including autosomal dominant and autosomal recessive.

name: Testicular regression syndrome
lab_findings: Abnormal morphology of female internal genitalia
clinical_summary: Testicular regression syndrome is a rare condition characterized by absent testis, abnormal internal genitalia morphology, and decreased fertility, typically presenting in neonatal age. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive manner.

name: Adrenomyodystrophy
clinical_summary: Adrenomyodystrophy is a rare disease characterized by infancy or neonatal onset symptoms that include megacystis, abnormality of the urinary system, and seizures, often accompanied by hypotonia and other systemic manifestations such as megalocornea and hepatic steatosis.

name: ADULT syndrome
clinical_summary: Adult syndrome is a rare autosomal dominant disorder characterized by a range of symptoms including abnormal dentition, nasal and eye abnormalities, dry and thin skin, and skin markings such as melanocytic nevus and freckling. It presents antenatally or in the neonatal period.

name: Alopecia-epilepsy-pyorrhea-intellectual disability syndrome
clinical_summary: Alopecia-epilepsy-pyorrhea-intellectual disability syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by abnormalities of the dentition, intellectual disability, seizures, and specific ocular features. This condition presents in early infancy, with onset occurring in neonatal age and an autosomal dominant pattern of inheritance.

name: Alopecia-contractures-dwarfism-intellectual disability syndrome
clinical_summary: Alopecia-contractures-dwarfism-intellectual disability syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a combination of alopecia, ear anomalies, joint stiffness, hypohidrosis, and developmental delays. This condition is marked by intellectual disability and physical features such as kyphosis, which present either before or shortly after birth.

name: Fetal akinesia deformation sequence
clinical_summary: Fetal akinesia deformation sequence is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by congenital anomalies including cryptorchidism, cleft palate, and micrognathia. Symptoms may appear in utero and persist into the neonatal period, often accompanied by additional features such as posteriorly rotated ears and hypertelorism.

name: 2q37 microdeletion syndrome
clinical_summary: 2q37 microdeletion syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by physical and developmental delays from birth, including joint hypermobility, intellectual disability, and midface retrusion. Key features of the condition also include a round face and generalized hypotonia, often leading to global developmental delay.

name: Enamel-renal syndrome
clinical_summary: Enamel-renal syndrome is a rare disease characterized by a combination of dental enamel abnormalities and renal dysfunction, most often presenting in childhood. Its autosomal recessive inheritance pattern suggests that affected individuals inherit two mutated copies of the responsible gene.

name: Amelo-onycho-hypohidrotic syndrome
clinical_summary: Amelo-onycho-hypohidrotic syndrome is a rare disease characterized by dental enamel abnormalities, delayed tooth eruption, and various cutaneous manifestations. The condition is associated with symptoms including dry skin and abnormalities in skin moisture, fingernail morphology, and skin thickening.

name: Amaurosis-hypertrichosis syndrome
clinical_summary: Amaurosis-hypertrichosis syndrome is a rare, neonatally inherited condition characterized by visual impairment and distinctive physical features, including abnormal eyelash morphology and thick eyebrows. It is caused by cone/cone-rod dystrophy and retinal dystrophy, often accompanied by photophobia.

name: Mosaic variegated aneuploidy syndrome
clinical_summary: Mosaic variegated aneuploidy syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by multiple congenital abnormalities resulting from widespread aneuploidy in somatic cells. Common clinical features include antenatal or neonatal onset of epicanthus, micrognathia, glaucoma, cataract, microphthalmia, and Dandy-Walker malformation.

name: Beta-mercaptolactate cysteine disulfiduria
clinical_summary: Beta-mercaptolactate cysteine disulfiduria is a rare disease characterized by intellectual disability, joint hypermobility, high forehead, posteriorly rotated ears, seizures, and short stature. The inheritance pattern and age of onset for this condition are unknown.

name: Achondrogenesis
lab_findings: prenatal ultrasound findings of vertebral and sacral anomalies
triphosphate and nucleotide pattern on amniotic fluid analysis
clinical_summary: Achondrogenesis is a rare disease characterized by prenatal and neonatal symptoms of macrocephaly, long philtrum, micrognathia, anteverted nares, short neck, and thickened nuchal skin fold. It can be inherited through either an autosomal dominant or autosomal recessive pattern.

name: Oxoglutaric aciduria
clinical_summary: Oxoglutaric aciduria is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by neonatal onset, leading to severe neurological symptoms such as hydrocephalus, global developmental delay, and skeletal muscle atrophy. Affected individuals also exhibit ataxia and hypertonia due to impaired Krebs cycle metabolism.

name: Abruzzo-Erickson syndrome
clinical_summary: Abruzzo-Erickson syndrome is a rare X-linked recessive disorder characterized by distinctive craniofacial abnormalities, including hypospadias, abnormal palate morphology, cleft palate, and malar flattening, often accompanied by sensorineural hearing impairment. This condition typically manifests antenatally.

name: Triple A syndrome
clinical_summary: Triple A syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a combination of symptoms including palmoplantar keratoderma, failure to thrive in infancy, and hypoglycemia. The condition can affect individuals of all ages and may also present with other systemic symptoms such as hypernasal speech, weight loss, and vomiting.

name: Acrocraniofacial dysostosis
clinical_summary: Acrocraniofacial dysostosis is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by multiple congenital anomalies affecting the craniofacial region, including prominent physical abnormalities. Symptoms include a range of facial and ear deformities present at birth.

name: Acrodermatitis enteropathica
clinical_summary: Acrodermatitis enteropathica is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by abnormality of the tongue, eyelid morphology, and other skin-related symptoms in infancy or neonatal onset. Symptoms include glossitis, furrowed tongue, blepharitis, and conjunctivitis.

name: Acrodysostosis
lab_findings: Abnormal form of the vertebral bodies
clinical_summary: Acrodysostosis is a rare genetic disorder characterized by intellectual disability and multiple skeletal abnormalities, including hypoplasia of the maxilla and brachydactyly, present at birth. It is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern and may also be apparent antenatally.

name: Vitamin B12-unresponsive methylmalonic acidemia
clinical_summary: Vitamin B12-unresponsive methylmalonic acidemia is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by severe neurological impairment, including intellectual disability and developmental delay. Symptoms include hypotonia, lethargy, coma, and thrombocytopenia, typically presenting at birth or during childhood.

name: Acro-renal-ocular syndrome
key_symptoms: Short distal phalanx of the thumb
clinical_summary: Acro-renal-ocular syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by various congenital abnormalities including renal, skeletal, and possibly ocular anomalies. Key signs include horseshoe kidney, anomalous thumb and radius morphology, and crossed fused renal ectopia, inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern.

name: Acro-renal-mandibular syndrome
clinical_summary: Acro-renal-mandibular syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by multiple malformations, including posteriorly rotated ears, split hand, split foot, hypoplasia of the radius, hypoplasia of the ulna, and a rudimentary fibula. This condition is present from birth, with symptoms evident in the antenatal and neonatal periods.

name: Hajdu-Cheney syndrome
clinical_summary: Hajdu-Cheney syndrome is a rare autosomal dominant disorder characterized by joint hypermobility and associated craniofacial abnormalities. Onset typically occurs in childhood or infancy, with signs including hypertelorism, long philtrum, micrognathia, downslanted palpebral fissures, and thick eyebrows.

name: Acrofacial dysostosis, Weyers type
clinical_summary: Acrofacial dysostosis, Weyers type is a rare congenital disorder characterized by a neonatal onset of abnormalities including abnormal dentition, hypodontia, and polydactyly. It is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern.

name: Adams-Oliver syndrome
clinical_summary: Adams-Oliver syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by cutis marmorata, aplasia cutis congenita, skull defect, failure to thrive, and abnormalities of the lower and upper limbs, often presenting at birth or in utero. It can be inherited in an autosomal dominant or autosomal recessive manner.

name: Acromesomelic dysplasia, Maroteaux type
clinical_summary: Acromesomelic dysplasia, Maroteaux type is a rare genetic disorder characterized by joint hypermobility, dolichocephaly, and other skeletal abnormalities. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive manner and typically presents in infancy or at birth.

name: Acromesomelic dysplasia, Hunter-Thompson type
lab_findings: Abnormality of pelvic girdle bone morphology
clinical_summary: Acromesomelic dysplasia, Hunter-Thompson type is a rare autosomal recessive disease characterized by brachydactyly, joint stiffness, abnormalities in speech and vocalization, and skeletal deformities. Affected individuals typically present with antenatal or neonatal symptoms, including scoliosis and hip dislocation.

name: Hereditary sensory and autonomic neuropathy type 2
clinical_summary: Hereditary sensory and autonomic neuropathy type 2 is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by symptoms including hyperhidrosis, dystrophic toenail, and acroosteolysis. It presents with distinctive physical characteristics such as a tapered finger and Wormian bones, typically appearing from infancy or neonatally.

name: Acromicric dysplasia
clinical_summary: Acromicric dysplasia is a rare, neonatally onset condition characterized by distinct facial features including a narrow mouth, thick lower lip vermilion, round face, long philtrum, bulbous nose, and anteverted nares. It is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern.

name: Cerebellar ataxia-ectodermal dysplasia syndrome
clinical_summary: Cerebellar ataxia-ectodermal dysplasia syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by developmental anomalies including inguinal hernia, cryptorchidism, ectodermal dysplasias, and neurodevelopmental impairments due to cerebellar ataxia in neonates. The physical manifestations include a triangular face, broad forehead, hypodontia, and microdontia, reflecting its distinct ectodermal dysplastic features.

name: Congenital unilateral hypoplasia of depressor anguli oris
clinical_summary: Congenital unilateral hypoplasia of depressor anguli oris is a rare genetic disorder characterized by various congenital anomalies, including abnormality of the lower lip and asymmetric crying face. It typically presents in the neonatal period with a high likelihood of autosomal dominant inheritance.

name: Cerebellar ataxia-hypogonadism syndrome
clinical_summary: Cerebellar ataxia-hypogonadism syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by hypogonadotropic hypogonadism, decreased fertility, and various neurological manifestations including ataxia and optic atrophy. Its symptoms can appear across a wide age range, from childhood to adulthood.

name: Spinocerebellar ataxia-dysmorphism syndrome
lab_findings: delayed speech development on psychometric testing
clinical_summary: Spinocerebellar ataxia-dysmorphism syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a combination of physical anomalies, including joint hypermobility and distinctive facial features. It typically presents in infancy, often with associated posteriorly rotated ears, dolichocephaly, epicanthus, broad forehead, and anteverted nares.

name: Ataxia-photosensitivity-short stature syndrome
clinical_summary: Ataxia-photosensitivity-short stature syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by a combination of ataxia, cutaneous photosensitivity, and short stature, typically presenting in infancy or childhood. Symptoms include dry skin, urticaria, high palate, and reduced tendon reflexes.

name: Atkin-Flaitz syndrome
clinical_summary: Atkin-Flaitz syndrome is a rare X-linked dominant disorder characterized by a distinct set of physical features, including macroorchidism and macrocephaly. Affected individuals typically present with these abnormalities from neonatal age.

name: Ataxia-deafness-intellectual disability syndrome
clinical_summary: Ataxia-deafness-intellectual disability syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by sensorineural hearing impairment, intellectual disability, and ataxia, typically presenting in childhood or infancy. Symptoms may also include strabismus, nystagmus, and abnormal palate morphology.

name: Baller-Gerold syndrome
clinical_summary: Baller-Gerold syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by multiple congenital anomalies, including finger aplasia, large fontanelles, brachyturricephaly, and proptosis, often presenting in antenatal or neonatal stages. It follows an autosomal recessive pattern of inheritance, resulting in growth delay.

name: Bannayan-Riley-Ruvalcaba syndrome
clinical_summary: Bannayan-Riley-Ruvalcaba syndrome is a rare autosomal dominant disorder characterized by a combination of macrocephaly, hamartomatous polyposis, and nevus, often appearing in infancy or neonatal stage, along with other features like abnormal large intestine morphology and short stature. It is also associated with capillary hemangioma.

name: Bangstad syndrome
clinical_summary: Bangstad syndrome is a rare disease characterized by a combination of abnormal testis morphology, polycystic ovaries, and other facial abnormalities, including microcephaly and a narrow face. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern and presents from birth.

name: Severe microbrachycephaly-intellectual disability-athetoid cerebral palsy syndrome
clinical_summary: Severe microbrachycephaly-intellectual disability-athetoid cerebral palsy syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by a range of facial and cranial abnormalities including brachycephaly, microcephaly, and hypertelorism. The condition typically presents in neonatal age and is associated with significant developmental delays and neurological impairments.

name: Bartsocas-Papas syndrome
clinical_summary: Bartsocas-Papas syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by hypoplastic male external genitalia, ambiguous genitalia, and various craniofacial features. It presents with a range of congenital anomalies, including antenatal or neonatal onset median cleft lip, cleft palate, microcephaly, and toe syndactyly.

name: Barber-Say syndrome
inheritance: Autosomal dominant, Autosomal recessive, Not applicable
clinical_summary: Barber-Say syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by distinct facial features, including a wide mouth, abnormal face, hypertelorism, hearing impairment, bulbous nose, and wide nasal bridge. It typically presents at birth (neonatal age) and may be inherited in an autosomal dominant or recessive pattern.

name: Aniridia-renal agenesis-psychomotor retardation syndrome
lab_findings: microphthalmia is not confirmed but ocular hypotony is sometimes found
clinical_summary: Aniridia-renal agenesis-psychomotor retardation syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a combination of congenital eye abnormalities, renal anomaly, and developmental delays. Affected infants typically exhibit symptoms from birth, including aniridia, bilateral renal anomalies, and strabismus.

name: Ankyloblepharon-ectodermal defects-cleft lip/palate syndrome
clinical_summary: Ankyloblepharon-ectodermal defects-cleft lip/palate syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by conductive hearing impairment, ectodermal defects, and facial anomalies, with onset in neonates. This autosomal dominant condition presents with a range of symptoms including hypohidrosis and dystrophic toenails.

name: Microphthalmia with limb anomalies
clinical_summary: Microphthalmia with limb anomalies is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by congenital anomalies including microphthalmia, blepharophimosis, finger aplasia, and craniofacial abnormalities. It presents with symptoms such as hypoplasia of the maxilla, posteriorly rotated ears, and abnormal eyebrow morphology starting antenatally or in early infancy.

name: Antley-Bixler syndrome
clinical_summary: Antley-Bixler syndrome is a rare condition characterized by distinctive facial features, such as posteriorly rotated ears and anteverted nares, combined with skeletal abnormalities including abnormal rib morphology and narrow chest. The disorder is typically present at birth, with a variable inheritance pattern potentially following an autosomal dominant or autosomal recessive pattern.

name: Aortic arch anomaly-facial dysmorphism-intellectual disability syndrome
clinical_summary: Aortic arch anomaly-facial dysmorphism-intellectual disability syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by distinctive facial features and intellectual disability. It is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern, typically presenting in infancy or at birth with symptoms such as posteriorly rotated ears and broad forehead.

name: Aphalangy-syndactyly-microcephaly syndrome
key_symptoms: Aplasia/Hypoplasia of the distal phalanges of the toes
clinical_summary: Aphalangy-syndactyly-microcephaly syndrome is a rare autosomal dominant disorder characterized by a combination of microcephaly, skeletal abnormalities, and developmental anomalies. Clinical features typically become apparent at birth and may include short stature, finger and toe abnormalities, and various degrees of developmental impairment.

name: Aphalangy-hemivertebrae-urogenital-intestinal dysgenesis syndrome
key_symptoms: Aplasia/Hypoplasia of the phalanges of the hand
Aplasia/Hypoplasia of the phalanges of the toes
clinical_summary: Aphalangy-hemivertebrae-urogenital-intestinal dysgenesis syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by multiple congenital anomalies, including skeletal, genital, and urogenital abnormalities. It typically presents in the neonatal period with symptoms such as splited foot, hemivertebrae, and aplasia/hypoplasia of the phalanges.

name: AREDYLD syndrome
clinical_summary: AREDYLD syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by distinct facial features, including posteriorly rotated ears, narrow mouth, mandibular prognathia, and smooth philtrum, often accompanied by an abnormality of the ureter. This condition is typically present from birth.

name: X-linked mandibulofacial dysostosis
clinical_summary: X-linked mandibulofacial dysostosis is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a distinct set of facial abnormalities, including posteriorly rotated ears and micrognathia, typically presenting at birth. It is inherited in an X-linked recessive pattern, primarily affecting males.

name: Arthrogryposis multiplex congenita-whistling face syndrome
clinical_summary: Arthrogryposis multiplex congenita-whistling face syndrome is a rare disease characterized by a combination of physical abnormalities, including posteriorly rotated ears, narrow mouth, and blepharophimosis. This autosomal recessive condition typically presents antenatally or in the neonatal period.

name: Atrioventricular defect-blepharophimosis-radial and anal defect syndrome
clinical_summary: Atrioventricular defect-blepharophimosis-radial and anal defect syndrome is a rare disease characterized by a combination of congenital malformations, including abnormality of the genitourinary system and microcephaly. The symptoms typically present in infancy or at birth.

name: Heart-hand syndrome type 2
clinical_summary: Heart-hand syndrome type 2 is a rare disease characterized by various congenital skeletal abnormalities, including clavicle, shoulder, and limb abnormalities, typically manifesting in infancy or neonatally. Key features include abnormalities of the radius and brachydactyly, often associated with joint stiffness and micromelia.

name: Cardiomyopathy-cataract-hip spine disease syndrome
clinical_summary: Cardiomyopathy-cataract-hip spine disease syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive condition characterized by cataract, platyspondyly, joint stiffness, cardiomyopathy, and congenital heart defects. Patients typically present with these symptoms in adolescence or adulthood.

name: Cardiofaciocutaneous syndrome
clinical_summary: Cardiofaciocutaneous syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by distinctive facial features, dry skin, and varying degrees of vision impairment, often presenting from birth. The condition is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern.

name: Camurati-Engelmann disease
key_symptoms: Cortical thickening of long bone diaphyses
clinical_summary: Camurati-Engelmann disease is a rare, autosomal dominant disorder characterized by skeletal dysplasia and bone pain. It manifests as abnormal vertebral column, skull, radius, and diaphysis morphology.

name: Camptodactyly syndrome, Guadalajara type 1
clinical_summary: Camptodactyly syndrome, Guadalajara type 1 is a rare autosomal recessive condition characterized by features that present since birth, including intrauterine growth retardation, spina bifida, and other structural anomalies. Common physical features include toe syndactyly, hallux valgus, and a distinctive facial appearance with downturned corners of mouth.

name: Camptodactyly syndrome, Guadalajara type 2
clinical_summary: Camptodactyly syndrome, Guadalajara type 2 is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a combination of congenital anomalies, including labial hypoplasia and intrauterine growth retardation. This autosomal recessive disorder typically manifests in the neonatal period with symptoms such as microcephaly, pectus excavatum, and talipes equinovarus.

name: Camptodactyly-joint contractures-facial skeletal defects syndrome
clinical_summary: Camptodactyly-joint contractures-facial skeletal defects syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a combination of facial, skeletal, and muscular abnormalities, presenting with symptoms such as narrow mouth and palate, facial asymmetry, micrognathia, and high forehead starting in the neonatal period. It can be inherited in either an autosomal dominant or autosomal recessive pattern.

name: Night blindness-skeletal anomalies-dysmorphism syndrome
clinical_summary: Night blindness-skeletal anomalies-dysmorphism syndrome is a rare disease characterized by a combination of visual impairment, skeletal anomalies, and distinctive dysmorphic features in childhood. The specific clinical features include joint hypermobility, abnormal palate morphology, malar flattening, retrognathia, epicanthus, and ptosis, although the cause of the disease is currently unknown.

name: Cerebrocostomandibular syndrome
key_symptoms: Atresia of the external auditory canal
lab_findings: micrognathic jaw morphology on ct scan
inheritance: Autosomal dominant, Autosomal recessive, Not applicable
clinical_summary: Cerebrocostomandibular syndrome is a rare condition characterized by a combination of facial and skeletal abnormalities, including cleft palate, micrognathia, and posternal rib gap, often presenting with neonatal respiratory distress. It can manifest antenatally or in the neonatal period, with various inheritance patterns potentially contributing to its development.

name: Catel-Manzke syndrome
clinical_summary: Catel-Manzke syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a combination of glossoptosis, cleft palate, and craniofacial abnormalities in infancy. Clinical presentation includes failure to thrive, with associated physical anomalies such as malar flattening, micrognathia, and clinodactyly of the 5th finger.

name: Cortical blindness-intellectual disability-polydactyly syndrome
clinical_summary: Cortical blindness-intellectual disability-polydactyly syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a combination of cortical blindness, intellectual disability, and polydactyly, presenting from antenatal to neonatal age. Clinical features include various craniofacial abnormalities, such as abnormal palate morphology, microretrognathia, and long philtrum.

name: Cataract-intellectual disability-hypogonadism syndrome
clinical_summary: Cataract-intellectual disability-hypogonadism syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by hypogonadotropic hypogonadism, intellectual disability, and specific physical anomalies. It presents with a range of congenital abnormalities, including microcephaly, malar flattening, and a distinctive facial appearance.

name: Cataract-aberrant oral frenula-growth delay syndrome
clinical_summary: Cataract-aberrant oral frenula-growth delay syndrome is a rare condition characterized by symptoms such as cataract, accessory oral frenulum, and growth delays. It typically presents in childhood and its inheritance pattern is unknown.

name: Autosomal recessive palmoplantar keratoderma and congenital alopecia
clinical_summary: This rare disease is characterized by neonatal onset of visual impairment, palmoplantar keratoderma, and alopecia, with features including cataract, joint stiffness, and subcutaneous nodule formation. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern and associated with atypical scarring of the skin.

name: Cataract-ataxia-deafness syndrome
clinical_summary: Cataract-ataxia-deafness syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by neonatal onset of sensorineural hearing impairment, visual impairment, and developmental cataract, often accompanied by nystagmus and progressive ataxia. This syndrome also involves decreased nerve conduction velocity.

name: Congenital heart defect-round face-developmental delay syndrome
clinical_summary: This rare syndrome presents with a combination of congenital heart defects, distinctive facial features including a round face, and developmental delay, often manifesting in infancy or at birth. The exact cause and inheritance pattern of this syndrome remain unknown.

name: Boomerang dysplasia
lab_findings: Decreased response to growth hormone stimulation test
clinical_summary: Boomerang dysplasia is a rare disease characterized by abnormal bone morphology, including micromelia, narrow chest, and tibia/fibula abnormalities, with symptoms present from an early age (antenatal/neonatal onset).

name: Böök syndrome
clinical_summary: Böök syndrome is a rare condition characterized by the combination of abnormal tooth structure, narrow palate, and other specific morphological abnormalities. It typically presents in adulthood with manifestations including abnormal eyebrow morphology, hypodontia, hyperhidrosis, and hypoplastic fingernails.

name: Borjeson-Forssman-Lehmann syndrome
clinical_summary: Borjeson-Forssman-Lehmann syndrome is a rare X-linked recessive disorder characterized by neonatal onset and various physical anomalies including coarse facial features, hypogonadism, and tapered fingers. Key physical features include cryptorchidism, small scrotum, and gynecomastia in affected males.

name: Blepharonasofacial malformation syndrome
clinical_summary: Blepharonasofacial malformation syndrome is characterized by distinctive facial features, including a long philtrum and underdeveloped nasal alae, among others. This rare condition typically presents in childhood with symptoms like joint hypermobility and abnormal pinna morphology.

name: Maxillonasal dysplasia
clinical_summary: Maxillonasal dysplasia is a rare condition characterized by cleft palate, maxillary hypoplasia, depressed nasal ridge, and other distinct facial features, with neonatal onset. It exhibits an autosomal dominant, autosomal recessive, or multifactorial inheritance pattern.

name: Congenital contractural arachnodactyly
clinical_summary: Congenital contractural arachnodactyly is a rare genetic disorder characterized by high palate, arachnodactyly, flexion contracture, joint stiffness, and slender build, often presenting in the neonatal period. It is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern.

name: Beemer-Ertbruggen syndrome
clinical_summary: Beemer-Ertbruggen syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a distinct set of congenital malformations. Affected individuals present with a complex phenotype including posteriorly rotated ears, cryptorchidism, micrognathia, and facial dysmorphia.

name: Distal limb deficiencies-micrognathia syndrome
clinical_summary: Distal limb deficiencies-micrognathia syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a combination of anomalies including microretrognathia, hypoplasia of the maxilla, and distal limb abnormalities. The condition presents during the neonatal period with distinctive features.

name: Symmetrical thalamic calcifications
clinical_summary: This is a rare condition characterized by symmetrical thalamic calcifications presenting in neonates with symptoms of ataxia, spasticity, hypertonia, reduced tendon reflexes, weak cry, and respiratory insufficiency. It has a neonatal age of onset and is not inherited.

name: Campomelia, Cumming type
clinical_summary: Campomelia, Cumming type is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by the presence of multiple congenital anomalies, primarily affecting the kidneys, palate, skull, and limbs. Affected individuals may present with multicystic kidney dysplasia, cleft palate, and other skeletal abnormalities.

name: Lambert syndrome
clinical_summary: Lambert syndrome is a rare congenital disorder characterized by the combination of inguinal hernia, hypospadias, wide mouth, malar flattening, preauricular skin tag, and jaundice, typically presenting at birth. The inheritance pattern of Lambert syndrome remains unclear.

name: Branchio-oculo-facial syndrome
clinical_summary: Branchio-oculo-facial syndrome is a rare autosomal dominant disorder characterized by features such as posteriorly rotated ears, conductive hearing impairment, and coloboma. It presents antenatal and neonatal onset, with physical characteristics including everted lower lip vermilion and abnormal pinna morphology.

name: Autosomal dominant popliteal pterygium syndrome
clinical_summary: Autosomal dominant popliteal pterygium syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a combination of congenital anomalies including genital and oral abnormalities. It is an autosomal dominant disorder with an antenatal to neonatal age of onset.

name: Feingold syndrome
clinical_summary: Feingold syndrome is a rare autosomal dominant disorder characterized by distinctive craniofacial and limb abnormalities. Symptoms include microcephaly, short palpebral fissure, and varied limb anomalies such as abnormal pinna morphology and clinodactyly.

name: Brachydactyly-preaxial hallux varus syndrome
clinical_summary: Brachydactyly-preaxial hallux varus syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by multiple craniofacial anomalies and brachydactyly. It is an autosomal dominant condition presenting symptoms in infancy.

name: Brachymorphism-onychodysplasia-dysphalangism syndrome
clinical_summary: Brachymorphism-onychodysplasia-dysphalangism syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by distinctive facial features, including a wide mouth, microcephaly, and epicanthus, among others. It is an autosomal dominant disorder, meaning it is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern and typically presents at birth.

name: Brachytelephalangy-dysmorphism-Kallmann syndrome
clinical_summary: Brachytelephalangy-dysmorphism-Kallmann syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by a combination of physical abnormalities including thin upper lip vermilion, hypertelorism, and brachydactyly. It presents in the neonatal period and follows an autosomal dominant pattern of inheritance.

name: Bowen-Conradi syndrome
clinical_summary: Bowen-Conradi syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by microcephaly, sloping forehead, micrognathia, prominent nose, and joint stiffness, often resulting in death in infancy. This condition is present from birth, manifesting antenatally or in the neonatal period.

name: Brachydactyly-elbow wrist dysplasia syndrome
lab_findings: Abnormal distal phalanx morphology of finger
clinical_summary: Brachydactyly-elbow wrist dysplasia syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by abnormal humerus morphology, joint stiffness, and increased risk of elbow dislocation, often accompanied by macrocephaly, brachydactyly, and abnormal fingernail morphology. It is an autosomal dominant condition with antenatal, infancy, or neonatal onset.

name: Craniofrontonasal dysplasia
clinical_summary: Craniofrontonasal dysplasia is a rare genetic disorder characterized by brachycephaly, joint hypermobility, and facial anomalies including hypertelorism and a depressed nasal ridge. It is an X-linked dominant disorder with an antenatal or neonatal age of onset.

name: SPECC1L-related hypertelorism syndrome
clinical_summary: SPECC1L-related hypertelorism syndrome is a rare autosomal dominant disorder characterized by neonatal onset of hypertelorism, round face, everted lower lip vermilion, thin vermilion border, shawl scrotum, and cryptorchidism. These distinguishing features often present together in a unique clinical profile.

name: Cantú syndrome
clinical_summary: Cantú syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by distinctive craniofacial features and macrocephaly. It is an autosomal dominant condition that typically presents in the neonatal period.

name: Non-syndromic bilambdoid and sagittal craniosynostosis
key_symptoms: Stenosis of the external auditory canal
clinical_summary: Non-syndromic bilambdoid and sagittal craniosynostosis is a rare condition characterized by cranial deformities including open mouth, hydrocephalus, and dolichocephaly, presenting neonatally. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern.

name: Craniotelencephalic dysplasia
lab_findings: normal to low 18p12.2-p11.22 copy number variation
abnormalities in karyotype, specifically chr8 p 23.1 24.1
clinical_summary: Craniotelencephalic dysplasia is a rare disease characterized by global developmental delay, craniosynostosis, and multiple neurological abnormalities. Key features include frontal bossing, hydrocephalus, microcephaly, and visual impairment, with onset typically occurring in the neonatal period.

name: Cranio-osteoarthropathy
clinical_summary: Cranio-osteoarthropathy is a rare, inherited disease characterized by large fontanelles, abnormal skull morphology, mottled pigmentation, arthritis, joint swelling, and joint stiffness typically presenting in childhood. This condition follows an autosomal recessive inheritance pattern.

name: Autosomal recessive Robinow syndrome
clinical_summary: Autosomal recessive Robinow syndrome is a rare condition characterized by unique facial abnormalities, including wide mouth, hypertelorism, and a wide nasal bridge, often presenting in infancy or neonatally. It is also associated with abnormal dentition, anteverted nares, and brachydactyly.

name: Cranioectodermal dysplasia
clinical_summary: Cranioectodermal dysplasia is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by joint hypermobility, skeletal abnormalities, and facial features such as anteverted nares and hypotelorism. It manifests prenatally and is evident at birth, affecting multiple systems in the body.

name: Craniodigital-intellectual disability syndrome
lab_findings: low to normal serum 25(oh) vitamin d
clinical_summary: Craniodigital-intellectual disability syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by intellectual disability and distinct physical features such as a short skull shape and narrow nasal bridge. It presents in the neonatal period with varying inheritance patterns, including autosomal recessive and X-linked recessive forms.

name: Craniodiaphyseal dysplasia
key_symptoms: Stenosis of the external auditory canal
inheritance: Autosomal dominant, Autosomal recessive, Not applicable
clinical_summary: Craniodiaphyseal dysplasia is a rare genetic disorder characterized by childhood onset of macrocephaly, coarse facial features, and conductive hearing impairment. It is inherited in an autosomal dominant or autosomal recessive pattern.

name: Crane-Heise syndrome
clinical_summary: Crane-Heise syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive genetic disorder characterized by a combination of cleft palate, hypertelorism, and other distinct physical features. It is typically identified antenatally or shortly after birth with symptoms including intrauterine growth retardation.

name: Dahlberg-Borer-Newcomer syndrome
clinical_summary: Dahlberg-Borer-Newcomer syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by the combination of nephropathy, wide nasal bridge, and telecanthus, which typically presents in childhood. It may also involve ocular abnormalities such as cataract and endocrine abnormalities including hypothyroidism and hypoparathyroidism.

name: Curry-Jones syndrome
clinical_summary: Curry-Jones syndrome is a rare condition characterized by neonatal onset of hypertelorism, facial asymmetry, microphthalmia, hypopigmented skin patches, and intellectual disability. Agenesis of the corpus callosum may also be present in this disorder.

name: Cutis gyrata-acanthosis nigricans-craniosynostosis syndrome
clinical_summary: Cutis gyrata-acanthosis nigricans-craniosynostosis syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a combination of craniofacial abnormalities, including turricephaly, dolichocephaly, and malar flattening. It presents antenatally or in the neonatal period, with notable physical findings such as bifid scrotum and a narrow palate.

name: Knobloch syndrome
clinical_summary: Knobloch syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a combination of severe eye abnormalities, including retinal detachment, myopia, and macular degeneration. Complications also include non-ocular anomalies, such as skull defects and potential cranial malformations.

name: Craniofacial-deafness-hand syndrome
clinical_summary: Craniofacial-deafness-hand syndrome is a rare congenital disorder characterized by a combination of craniofacial abnormalities and sensorineural hearing impairment. It is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern and present from birth.

name: Gómez-López-Hernández syndrome
clinical_summary: Gómez-López-Hernández syndrome is a rare condition characterized by distinct facial features, including a thin vermilion border, and associated with neurological symptoms such as hydrocephalus. It presents at birth with symptoms like brachycephaly, turricephaly, and mask-like facies with hypertelorism.

name: Cryptorchidism-arachnodactyly-intellectual disability syndrome
clinical_summary: Cryptorchidism-arachnodactyly-intellectual disability syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by abnormalities in testicular development, dental configuration, facial proportions, and eye movement. Additional features include hypospadias, and skeletal abnormalities such as pectus carinatum, typically presenting at birth or in infancy.

name: Jackson-Weiss syndrome
clinical_summary: Jackson-Weiss syndrome is a rare autosomal dominant disorder characterized by a range of craniofacial abnormalities. The symptoms, including abnormal palate morphology and hypertelorism, typically become apparent in infancy.

name: Crisponi syndrome
clinical_summary: Crisponi syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by distinct facial features and hypohidrosis, presenting at a neonatal age. Symptoms include a high palate, full cheeks, long philtrum, wide nose, and anteverted nares.

name: Greenberg dysplasia
lab_findings: Abnormal form of the vertebral bodies
clinical_summary: Greenberg dysplasia is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by multiple congenital anomalies, including micrognathia, narrow chest, platyspondyly, lymphedema, brachydactyly, and skull defects. The disease becomes apparent in the antenatal or neonatal period.

name: Desbuquois syndrome
lab_findings: Abnormality of the femoral neck or head region
clinical_summary: Desbuquois syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by joint hypermobility, short neck, glaucoma, proptosis, and abnormal metaphysis morphology, associated with intellectual disability. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern and typically presents from birth.

name: Cerebrofaciothoracic dysplasia
clinical_summary: Cerebrofaciothoracic dysplasia is a rare disease characterized by a range of developmental anomalies, including wide mouth, brachycephaly, and short neck. It is present at birth, with an autosomal recessive inheritance pattern.

name: Isolated cerebellar agenesis
clinical_summary: Isolated cerebellar agenesis is a rare condition characterized by a range of neurological symptoms including microcephaly, macrocephaly, abnormal eye movement, atypical behavior, seizures, and ataxia, which typically present in childhood. The inheritance pattern of isolated cerebellar agenesis is unknown.

name: Lethal congenital contracture syndrome type 1
clinical_summary: Lethal congenital contracture syndrome type 1 is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by hypertelorism, micrognathia, pulmonary hypoplasia, skeletal muscle atrophy, hip bone abnormalities, and short stature, affecting individuals from birth. This condition is often diagnosed prenatally or shortly after birth.

name: Vici syndrome
clinical_summary: Vici syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by severe symptoms including hypopigmentation, intellectual disability, and agenesis of the corpus callosum. Affected individuals often suffer from global developmental delay and hypotonia, with a high mortality rate in infancy.

name: Intellectual disability-hypoplastic corpus callosum-preauricular tag syndrome
lab_findings: low density in cerebral white matter
increased n-acetyl aspartate in corpus callosum
clinical_summary: This rare autosomal recessive disorder, also known as Intellectual disability-hypoplastic corpus callosum-preauricular tag syndrome, presents at birth with symptoms of microcephaly, preauricular skin tag, protruding ear, hypertonia, growth delay, and hypoplasia of the corpus callosum. Affected individuals exhibit intellectual disability.

name: Xq21 microdeletion syndrome
lab_findings: Decreased response to growth hormone stimulation test
clinical_summary: Xq21 microdeletion syndrome is a rare X-linked recessive disorder characterized by sensorineural hearing impairment and ocular abnormalities, primarily presenting in infancy and neonatal stages. Common findings include chorioretinal abnormalities, choroideremia, and abnormal cochlea morphology.

name: Joubert syndrome with hepatic defect
clinical_summary: Joubert syndrome with hepatic defect is a rare disease characterized by neonatal onset of nephropathy, visual impairment, and structural eye abnormalities, including chorioretinal coloboma and iris coloboma. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern.

name: CODAS syndrome
clinical_summary: CODAS syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a specific set of congenital anomalies, including epicanthus, overfolded helix, anteverted nares, cataract, abnormality of dental enamel, and delayed eruption of teeth. It typically manifests in infancy or neonatal period.

name: COFS syndrome
clinical_summary: COFS syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by antenatal or neonatal onset of hypogonadism, microcephaly, micrognathia, sensorineural hearing impairment, everted lower lip vermilion, and wide nasal bridge. It follows an autosomal recessive inheritance pattern.

name: Bone dysplasia, lethal Holmgren type
clinical_summary: Bone dysplasia, lethal Holmgren type, is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by antenatal-onset skeletal dysplasia and significant postnatal complications, including respiratory insufficiency. Key physical abnormalities include short ribs, a narrow chest, and abnormal femur morphology, leading to severe weight loss and early mortality.

name: Mesomelic dysplasia, Kantaputra type
clinical_summary: Mesomelic dysplasia, Kantaputra type is a rare condition characterized by abnormalities in bone morphology, including short stature and unusual shapes of the humerus and fibula. It is a neonatal onset condition, typically presenting with autosomal dominant inheritance.

name: Axial mesodermal dysplasia spectrum
lab_findings: Abnormal morphology of female internal genitalia
clinical_summary: Axial mesodermal dysplasia spectrum is a rare genetic disorder characterized by abnormalities in the urinary and genital systems of affected females, including abnormal morphology of internal genitalia. This disorder presents with symptoms such as renal cysts and hydronephrosis.

name: Hereditary mucoepithelial dysplasia
lab_findings: Abnormal morphology of female internal genitalia
clinical_summary: Hereditary mucoepithelial dysplasia is a rare, autosomal dominant disorder presenting in childhood, characterized by multiple congenital anomalies including abnormal morphology of female internal genitalia, genitourinary system abnormalities, and systemic features such as gingival overgrowth and cataract. The condition also features abnormality of the bladder.

name: Cone rod dystrophy
key_symptoms: Bone spicule pigmentation of the retina
inheritance: Autosomal dominant, Autosomal recessive, X-linked recessive
clinical_summary: Cone rod dystrophy is a rare genetic disorder characterized by progressive visual loss and photophobia, often presenting with nyctalopia, abnormal retinal pigmentation, optic disc pallor, and color vision defects. Its age of onset and mode of inheritance can vary, affecting individuals in adolescence, adulthood, or childhood and manifesting as autosomal dominant, autosomal recessive, or X-linked recessive patterns.

name: Thanatophoric dysplasia type 1
clinical_summary: Thanatophoric dysplasia type 1 is a rare, autosomal dominant disease characterized by macrocephaly, wide anterior fontanel, hearing impairment, proptosis, narrow chest, and platyspondyly, typically presenting antenatally or in the neonatal period.

name: Skeletal dysplasia-epilepsy-short stature syndrome
clinical_summary: This rare genetic disorder, also known as skeletal dysplasia-epilepsy-short stature syndrome, is characterized by various congenital abnormalities including abnormal dentition and intellectual disability, along with epilepsy and short stature. It manifests neonatally and is inherited in an unknown pattern.

name: Thoracic dysplasia-hydrocephalus syndrome
clinical_summary: Thoracic dysplasia-hydrocephalus syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by conductive hearing impairment, depressed nasal ridge, narrow chest, abnormal metaphysis morphology, intellectual disability, and seizure, with onset occurring antenatally. The inheritance pattern of this syndrome is currently unknown.

name: Kyphomelic dysplasia
clinical_summary: Kyphomelic dysplasia is a rare disease characterized by a constellation of skeletal abnormalities, including micrognathia, narrow chest, lateral clavicle hook, anterior rib cupping, and missing ribs. It begins in utero or at birth, with an unknown inheritance pattern.

name: Ghosal hematodiaphyseal dysplasia
lab_findings: Abnormality of pelvic girdle bone morphology
Abnormal form of the vertebral bodies
clinical_summary: Ghosal hematodiaphyseal dysplasia is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a range of skeletal abnormalities, including anemia. Affected individuals typically present in infancy or neonatally with distinctive morphological changes to various bones in the body.

name: Thoracomelic dysplasia
lab_findings: Abnormality of pelvic girdle bone morphology
clinical_summary: Thoracomelic dysplasia is a rare condition characterized by joint hypermobility, abnormal metaphysis morphology, and skeletal abnormalities visible in infancy. Symptoms include a bell-shaped thorax, low posterior hairline, abnormal pelvic girdle bone morphology, and genu valgum.

name: Acrofacial dysostosis, Rodríguez type
lab_findings: Abnormality of pelvic girdle bone morphology
clinical_summary: Acrofacial dysostosis, Rodríguez type is a rare disease characterized by distinct facial and skeletal anomalies, including malar flattening, microretrognathia, and microtia. It can present with various levels of severity from antenatal to neonatal onset, and has a variable inheritance pattern that is either autosomal dominant or autosomal recessive.

name: Hypomandibular faciocranial dysostosis
lab_findings: no lab findings available for this disease
clinical_summary: Hypomandibular faciocranial dysostosis is a rare condition characterized by multiple craniofacial abnormalities including a narrow mouth, cleft palate, and brachycephaly. It presents with various symptoms such as bifid uvula, low-set ears, and choanal stenosis at an antenatal or neonatal age.

name: Oculomaxillofacial dysostosis
clinical_summary: Oculomaxillofacial dysostosis is a rare condition characterized by a combination of facial abnormalities, including median cleft lip and palate. Affected individuals experience neonatal onset of symptoms, which include anomalies of the teeth and nose.

name: Craniofacial dysostosis-diaphyseal hyperplasia syndrome
