clinical_summary: Craniofacial dysostosis-diaphyseal hyperplasia syndrome is characterized by neonatal onset of macroglossia, abnormal craniofacial features, and dental abnormalities. This rare autosomal dominant disorder primarily affects craniofacial development.

name: Ectodermal dysplasia-intellectual disability-central nervous system malformation syndrome
clinical_summary: Ectodermal dysplasia-intellectual disability-central nervous system malformation syndrome is a rare X-linked recessive disorder characterized by hydrocephalus, macrocephaly, and other distinct craniofacial features. Onset typically occurs ante- or neonatally, with manifestations including intellectual disability and central nervous system malformations.

name: Epiphyseal dysplasia-hearing loss-dysmorphism syndrome
clinical_summary: Epiphyseal dysplasia-hearing loss-dysmorphism syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by neonatal onset of wide mouth, epicanthus, hypertelorism, facial asymmetry, and long philtrum, accompanied by sensorineural hearing impairment. The inheritance pattern of this syndrome is currently unknown.

name: Ectodermal dysplasia-blindness syndrome
clinical_summary: Ectodermal dysplasia-blindness syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by neonatal onset of multiple ectodermal abnormalities. It presents with a constellation of symptoms including abnormal teeth, protruding ears, narrow nasal bridge, microcornea, microphthalmia, and subsequent blindness.

name: Hidrotic ectodermal dysplasia, Halal type
clinical_summary: Hidrotic ectodermal dysplasia, Halal type is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a combination of congenital anomalies, including abnormal genital system development and ectodermal system abnormalities. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern and typically presents in infancy or neonatal period.

name: Caudal duplication
clinical_summary: Caudal duplication is a rare condition characterized by congenital abnormalities affecting the genital system and nervous system, including spina bifida and vertebral segmentation defects. It presents with symptoms such as ureteral duplication, abnormality of the penis, and myelomeningocele, often identified prenatally or at birth.

name: Wolcott-Rallison syndrome
clinical_summary: Wolcott-Rallison syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive condition characterized by severe symptoms including neonatal insulin-dependent diabetes mellitus, abnormal liver function, skeletal dysplasia, and decreased neutrophil count. It presents in infancy with growth delay, short stature, and other systemic manifestations.

name: Acrofacial dysostosis, Catania type
clinical_summary: Acrofacial dysostosis, Catania type is a rare condition characterized by distinct facial and dental abnormalities, including microcephaly and microretrognathia. It presents with antenatal or neonatal onset and can exhibit autosomal dominant or X-linked dominant inheritance patterns.

name: Acrofrontofacionasal dysostosis
clinical_summary: Acrofrontofacionasal dysostosis is a rare genetic disorder characterized by cleft palate, high and broad forehead, and craniofacial abnormalities. It manifests in the neonatal period and is inherited in an autosomal recessive manner.

name: Dysosteosclerosis
clinical_summary: Dysosteosclerosis is a rare disease characterized by macrocephaly, hypertelorism, hearing impairment, nystagmus, and optic atrophy often presenting in infancy or neonatal onset. It is inherited in either an autosomal recessive or X-linked recessive pattern, with dental enamel abnormalities also observed in some cases.

name: Thakker-Donnai syndrome
clinical_summary: Thakker-Donnai syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive condition, characterized by a range of congenital anomalies including hydronephrosis, rectovaginal fistula, and other dysmorphic features. The disorder presents with severe symptoms in infancy, evident before or shortly after birth.

name: Temtamy syndrome
clinical_summary: Temtamy syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by congenital and neonatal onset of hypertelorism, chorioretinal and iris coloboma, brachydactyly, intellectual disability, and global developmental delay. This condition is present from birth or shortly after.

name: Dysequilibrium syndrome
lab_findings: normal to low copper levels in serum
clinical_summary: Dysequilibrium syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by a combination of symptoms including strabismus, intellectual disability, seizures, ataxia, hypotonia, and gait disturbances, typically presenting in neonates. It follows an autosomal recessive inheritance pattern.

name: Alpers-Huttenlocher syndrome
clinical_summary: Alpers-Huttenlocher syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a range of neurodegenerative features, including microcephaly and abnormalities of the eye and vision. The syndrome may present in infancy, childhood, adolescence, or adulthood, with symptoms also including ataxia, hypotonia, and spasticity.

name: Transcobalamin deficiency
clinical_summary: Transcobalamin deficiency is a rare autosomal recessive disorder presenting in infancy or neonatally with symptoms including thrombocytopenia, pancytopenia, and megaloblastic bone marrow.Affected individuals may also experience decreased total neutrophil count, lymphopenia, and acute kidney injury.

name: Lipodystrophy due to peptidic growth factors deficiency
clinical_summary: Lipodystrophy due to peptidic growth factors deficiency is a rare disease characterized by a combination of craniofacial abnormalities and palmoplantar keratoderma. Affected individuals typically present with these symptoms from a neonatal age and may display abnormalities including a narrow mouth, micrognathia, and other skeletal manifestations such as a convex nasal ridge and pectus excavatum.

name: Prolidase deficiency
clinical_summary: Prolidase deficiency is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a variety of physical abnormalities. Symptoms of the condition may be present from infancy to adolescence, including features such as abnormal facial structures and hearing impairment.

name: Dermoodontodysplasia
clinical_summary: Dermoodontodysplasia is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a combination of mandibular prognathia, ptosis, microdontia, and cutaneous abnormalities. Symptoms typically manifest during childhood and are inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern.

name: Dermatoosteolysis, Kirghizian type
clinical_summary: Dermatoosteolysis, Kirghizian type is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a spectrum of skeletal and ocular anomalies including keratitis, nyctalopia, oligodontia, and abnormal diaphysis and metaphysis morphology. It typically presents in infancy and involves hand malformations and other associated anomalies.

name: 1p36 deletion syndrome
clinical_summary: 1p36 deletion syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by distinct facial features, such as a pointed chin, long philtrum, and brachydactyly, alongside delayed speech and language development. It is typically apparent at birth or prenatally and has a complex inheritance pattern.

name: Oculocerebrocutaneous syndrome
clinical_summary: Oculocerebrocutaneous syndrome is a rare congenital disorder characterized by a combination of physical and neurological symptoms. The condition is marked by preauricular skin tags, ptosis, intellectual disability, seizures, cerebellar hypoplasia, and polymicrogyria.

name: Blepharo-cheilo-odontic syndrome
clinical_summary: Blepharo-cheilo-odontic syndrome is a rare autosomal dominant disorder characterized by congenital abnormalities, including eyelid, dental, and auditory impairments. It presents with features such as abnormal eyelid morphology, hypertelorism, and conductive hearing impairment from birth.

name: Cleft lip/palate-deafness-sacral lipoma syndrome
clinical_summary: Cleft lip/palate-deafness-sacral lipoma syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by a combination of craniofacial and neurological anomalies, including facial asymmetry, cleft lip and palate, and ataxia. It presents with a range of complex features, such as depressed nasal ridge, sparse scalp hair, meningocele, and hip dislocation, often manifesting in infancy or at birth.

name: Laryngotracheoesophageal cleft
clinical_summary: Laryngotracheoesophageal cleft is a rare disease characterized by respiratory symptoms such as cyanosis, laryngomalacia, dyspnea, and recurrent respiratory infections, often presenting antenatally or at birth. Affected individuals may also experience voice abnormalities and hoarse crying.

name: Acrocardiofacial syndrome
clinical_summary: Acrocardiofacial syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a range of developmental anomalies, primarily affecting the ears, hands, and genitalia. Affected individuals often present with intellectual disability and may experience feeding difficulties in infancy.

name: Cleft palate-large ears-small head syndrome
clinical_summary: Cleft palate-large ears-small head syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by a combination of craniofacial anomalies including cleft palate, large ears, and small head size, often presenting in infancy or at birth. Common features also include hypospadias, gingival overgrowth, micrognathia, and other distinctive physical characteristics.

name: Fibrochondrogenesis
clinical_summary: Fibrochondrogenesis is a rare congenital disorder characterized by a range of craniofacial abnormalities, including narrow mouth, cleft palate, and wide anterior fontanel, often presenting at birth. The condition can be inherited in an autosomal dominant or autosomal recessive pattern.

name: Femur-fibula-ulna complex
clinical_summary: Femur-fibula-ulna complex is a rare disease characterized by multiple congenital anomalies, including abnormal lower limb morphology and upper limb abnormalities such as humeroradial synostosis. It presents with symptoms like micromelia, short humerus, and split hand, among others.

name: Endocardial fibroelastosis
clinical_summary: Endocardial fibroelastosis is a rare disease characterized by neonatal onset and symptoms including posteriorly rotated ears, abnormal palate morphology, micrognathia, telecanthus, congestive heart failure, and restrictive cardiomyopathy. The disease is currently of unknown inheritance pattern.

name: Epiphyseal stippling-osteoclastic hyperplasia syndrome
clinical_summary: Epiphyseal stippling-osteoclastic hyperplasia syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive, and often lethal skeletal dysplasia with symptoms manifesting before or shortly after birth. Affected individuals display a range of physical abnormalities including hypotelorism, patent ductus arteriosus, and other skeletal malformations.

name: Spinocerebellar ataxia type 34
clinical_summary: Spinocerebellar ataxia type 34 is a rare disorder characterized by symptoms including nystagmus, dry skin, hypohidrosis, urticaria, dysarthria, and hyporeflexia. It is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern, affecting individuals of various ages from adolescence to neonatal onset.

name: Extrasystoles-short stature-hyperpigmentation-microcephaly syndrome
clinical_summary: Extrasystoles-short stature-hyperpigmentation-microcephaly syndrome is a rare autosomal dominant disorder characterized by microcephaly, delayed skeletal maturation, and short stature. It is also associated with other physical manifestations including micrognathia, hypertelorism, and strabismus.

name: Flat face-microstomia-ear anomaly syndrome
lab_findings: increased nuchal fold thickness in utero
clinical_summary: Flat face-microstomia-ear anomaly syndrome is a rare condition characterized by neonatal onset of narrow mouth, long face, broad forehead, long philtrum, and micrognathia, with malar flattening also present. The inheritance pattern of the syndrome is currently unknown.

name: Facial dysmorphism-anorexia-cachexia-eye and skin anomalies syndrome
clinical_summary: Facial dysmorphism-anorexia-cachexia-eye and skin anomalies syndrome is a rare disease characterized by facial dysmorphism, including anteverted nares and ptosis, as well as various physical anomalies. Common physical manifestations include pectus excavatum, abnormality of the thyroid gland, melanocytic nevus, and asymmetry of the thorax.

name: Facial dysmorphism-macrocephaly-myopia-Dandy-Walker malformation syndrome
clinical_summary: Facial dysmorphism-macrocephaly-myopia-Dandy-Walker malformation syndrome is a rare genetic disorder presenting with neonatal onset and characteristic craniofacial features such as macrocephaly and coarse facial features. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern and often associated with dental and palatal abnormalities.

name: Lethal faciocardiomelic dysplasia
clinical_summary: Lethal faciocardiomelic dysplasia is a rare, neonatally expressed disorder characterized by multiple congenital anomalies, including intrauterine growth retardation and characteristic facial features. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive manner, resulting in severe symptoms in affected individuals.

name: Faciocardiorenal syndrome
clinical_summary: Faciocardiorenal syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by multiple congenital anomalies, including Horseshoe kidney, cleft palate, and craniofacial abnormalities, presenting in neonates. The condition involves a complex combination of facial, renal, and possibly cardiac anomalies.

name: Autosomal recessive faciodigitogenital syndrome
clinical_summary: Autosomal recessive faciodigitogenital syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by joint hypermobility, distinctive facial features and physical anomalies, presenting since neonatal age. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern, meaning carriers are not typically affected but may pass the mutated gene to offspring.

name: Femoral-facial syndrome
lab_findings: chromosomal analysis normal but with increased breaks and telomeric fusions
clinical_summary: Femoral-facial syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by a combination of cleft palate, micrognathia, and short femur, evident from birth. These physical anomalies result in distinct orofacial and skeletal features, including thin upper lip vermilion and a long philtrum.

name: Pai syndrome
clinical_summary: Pai syndrome is a rare condition characterized by a combination of craniofacial abnormalities including median cleft lip, cleft palate, and bifid uvula, with hypertelorism and telecanthus also present. The condition is typically evident antennatally or in the neonatal period, with unknown inheritance pattern.

name: Fetal trimethadione syndrome
clinical_summary: Fetal trimethadione syndrome is a rare condition presenting with a combination of congenital anomalies, including hypospadias, ambiguous genitalia, high palate, and microcephaly. It is characterized by an antenatal or neonatal onset without a discernible familial inheritance pattern.

name: Fetal hydantoin syndrome
lab_findings: Abnormality of the fontanelles or cranial sutures
clinical_summary: Fetal hydantoin syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by a cluster of physical abnormalities present at birth, including hearing abnormalities, pinna anomalies, and facial deformities. It manifests ante- and neonatally, with dermatoglyphic and genital abnormalities also observed.

name: Phenobarbital embryopathy
clinical_summary: Phenobarbital embryopathy is a rare disease characterized by multiple congenital anomalies, including epicanthus, mandibular prognathia, and intellectual disability, which result from prenatal exposure to phenobarbital. Symptoms include brachydactyly, low-set ears, and hypertelorism, typically presenting at birth.

name: Fetal cytomegalovirus syndrome
clinical_summary: Fetal cytomegalovirus syndrome is a rare disease characterized by sensorineural hearing impairment and intellectual disability. It presents with symptoms such as splenomegaly, thrombocytopenia, anemia, and abnormal coagulation, typically occurring prenatally or in the neonatal period.

name: Vitamin K antagonist embryofetopathy
clinical_summary: Vitamin K antagonist embryofetopathy is a rare disease characterized by distinct physical anomalies including anteverted nares, short nose, depressed nasal bridge, punctate vertebral calcifications, epiphyseal stippling, and short neck, typically detected in-utero or in the neonatal period. This condition is often associated with maternal anticoagulant therapy.

name: Emery-Nelson syndrome
key_symptoms: Contractures of the interphalangeal joint of the thumb
clinical_summary: Emery-Nelson syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by a combination of facial abnormalities, including high palate, long philtrum, and high forehead, alongside brachydactyly and abnormal thumb morphology. Infants with this condition also present with neonatal hypotonia.

name: Diabetic embryopathy
lab_findings: Abnormal morphology of female internal genitalia
clinical_summary: Diabetic embryopathy is a rare disease characterized by various congenital anomalies, including abnormal morphology of female internal genitalia, cryptorchidism, and micropenis. It presents with a range of symptoms from antenatal to neonatal onset, including ureteral duplication, hydronephrosis, and cleft palate.

name: Methimazole embryofetopathy
clinical_summary: Methimazole embryofetopathy is a rare condition characterized by a constellation of congenital malformations, including hypospadias, choanal atresia, and skull defects. Affected newborns may also experience intrauterine growth retardation, hypothyroidism, and abnormalities of the thyroid gland.

name: Toluene embryopathy
clinical_summary: Toluene embryopathy is a rare prenatal condition characterized by a distinctive combination of congenital malformations, including cryptorchidism, hydronephrosis, microcephaly, epicanthus, smooth philtrum, and micrognathia. It presents in the antenatal or neonatal period.

name: Lowry-Wood syndrome
clinical_summary: Lowry-Wood syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by microcephaly, nystagmus, intellectual disability, and skeletal abnormalities, including epiphyseal dysplasia and coxa vara, with symptoms typically appearing in the neonatal period.

name: Dysplasia epiphysealis hemimelica
clinical_summary: Dysplasia epiphysealis hemimelica is a rare disease characterized by joint stiffness, bone pain, and developmental abnormalities such as Pes planus, Genu valgum, and Genu varum, often leading to osteoarthritis. It typically manifests in adolescence or childhood and may follow an autosomal dominant pattern of inheritance.

name: Hypohidrotic ectodermal dysplasia-hypothyroidism-ciliary dyskinesia syndrome
clinical_summary: This rare disease is characterized by a combination of symptoms including atypical behavior, hypothyroidism, hypohidrosis, alopecia, dystrophic toenail, and recurrent respiratory infections in individuals of all ages presenting with this syndrome since childhood. The condition is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern.

name: Ectodermal dysplasia-sensorineural deafness syndrome
clinical_summary: Ectodermal dysplasia-sensorineural deafness syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by sensorineural hearing impairment and ectodermal abnormalities. Clinical features include carious teeth, skin thickening, arachnodactyly, and brittle, coarse hair that manifests in infancy or childhood.

name: Congenital muscular dystrophy-infantile cataract-hypogonadism syndrome
clinical_summary: Congenital Muscular Dystrophy-Infantile Cataract-Hypogonadism syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by neonatal onset of muscular dystrophy, hypotonia, and gait disturbance. Affected individuals also exhibit hypogonadism, abnormal ovary, and distinct facial features, including mask-like facies.

name: Focal facial dermal dysplasia type III
clinical_summary: Focal facial dermal dysplasia type III is a rare condition characterized by multiple dysmorphic facial features, including epicanthus, short philtrum, wide nasal bridge, and depressed nasal ridge, alongside potential associated conditions such as anal atresia. It typically manifests antenatally or neonatally, with varying inheritance patterns suggested to be both autosomal dominant and autosomal recessive.

name: Leukomelanoderma-infantilism-intellectual disability-hypodontia-hypotrichosis syndrome
clinical_summary: Leukomelanoderma-infantilism-intellectual disability-hypodontia-hypotrichosis syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disease characterized by a range of physical and developmental abnormalities, including hypospadias, hypodontia, and delayed eruption of teeth. It becomes apparent at birth (neonatal onset) and is associated with decreased fertility, among other features.

name: Ectodermal dysplasia, trichoodontoonychial type
clinical_summary: Ectodermal dysplasia, trichoodontoonychial type is a rare condition characterized by sparse hair, abnormal dental morphology, and unusual skin and nail manifestations, including irregular hyperpigmentation and abnormal toenail morphology. It presents with variable age of onset, including antenatal, infancy, and neonatal periods, with an unknown inheritance pattern.

name: EEC syndrome
clinical_summary: EEC syndrome is a rare condition characterized by distinct facial features including a thick eyebrow, lacrimation abnormalities, dental anomalies such as carious teeth, taurodontia, and microdontia. It exhibits antenatal or neonatal onset and follows an autosomal dominant pattern of inheritance.

name: EEM syndrome
clinical_summary: EEM syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by neonatal onset of ocular and dental abnormalities, including retinopathy, abnormal vision, carious teeth, widely spaced or absent teeth, and selective tooth agenesis, as well as an absent eyebrow. These characteristic features occur due to the genetic mutation inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern.

name: Edinburgh malformation syndrome
clinical_summary: Edinburgh malformation syndrome is a rare condition characterized by a distinctive combination of facial and cranial defects including thin vermilion border, hydrocephalus, and low-set ears, typically presenting in the neonatal period. The exact mechanism and inheritance pattern of the disease remain unknown.

name: Aminopterin/methotrexate embryofetopathy
clinical_summary: Aminopterin/methotrexate embryofetopathy is a rare condition characterized by congenital malformations, with symptoms including hydrocephalus, epicanthus, and other facial anomalies. It has an antenatal onset and is not inherited.

name: Diaphragmatic defect-limb deficiency-skull defect syndrome
clinical_summary: Diaphragmatic defect-limb deficiency-skull defect syndrome is a rare congenital disorder characterized by a combination of diaphragmatic defects, limb abnormalities, and skull defects. It is an antenatal condition with an unknown inheritance pattern.

name: Donnai-Barrow syndrome
clinical_summary: Donnai-Barrow syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by antenatal and neonatal onset of macrocephaly, wide anterior fontanel, hypertelorism, broad forehead, and widow's peak. Affected individuals may also present with proteinuria.

name: Craniosynostosis, Herrmann-Opitz type
clinical_summary: Craniosynostosis, Herrmann-Opitz type is a rare condition characterized by cleft palate, brachycephaly, and other craniofacial abnormalities. The symptoms are present antenatally and include cleft palate, brachycephaly, turricephaly, malar flattening, and hypertelorism.

name: 46,XX ovotesticular difference of sex development
lab_findings: Abnormal morphology of female internal genitalia
clinical_summary: 46,XX ovotesticular difference of sex development is a rare genetic disorder characterized by abnormalities in both female and male internal genitalia, often presenting with cryptorchidism, a small scrotum, hypospadias, and a bifid scrotum. It can manifest at various ages: antenatal, neonatal, or during adolescence.

name: Hernández-Aguirre Negrete syndrome
clinical_summary: Hernández-Aguirre Negrete syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder presenting with a range of distinctive facial features and developmental delays. Key clinical symptoms include posteriorly rotated ears, wide mouth, round face, and bulbous nose, often accompanied by seizures and significant global developmental delay.

name: Short stature-craniofacial anomalies-genital hypoplasia syndrome
clinical_summary: This rare disease is characterized by short stature, craniofacial anomalies, and genital hypoplasia, presenting with symptoms such as posteriorly rotated ears, trigonocephaly, epicanthus, and microretrognathia. Affected individuals also experience delayed puberty and intellectual disability, often starting in infancy or even at birth.

name: HEC syndrome
clinical_summary: HEC syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by a neonatal onset of developmental cataract, communicating hydrocephalus, and abnormalities affecting the pharynx and pupil morphology. The condition also often presents with premature birth and polyhydramnios, with unknown inheritance patterns.

name: Multifocal infantile hemangioma with extracutenous involvement
clinical_summary: Multifocal infantile hemangioma with extracutaneous involvement is a rare condition characterized by a broad range of symptoms. Affected infants may present with systemic complications beginning in the neonatal period.

name: Harrod syndrome
clinical_summary: Harrod syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by a combination of facial and cranial abnormalities, including a narrow mouth, high palate, microcephaly, narrow face, long face, and pointed chin, typically present from infancy or neonatal stage. The inheritance pattern of Harrod syndrome remains unknown.

name: Hartsfield syndrome
lab_findings: i am not aware of the clinical laboratory findings for hartsfield syndrome as it seems to be an obscure or rare condition.
clinical_summary: Hartsfield syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by cleft palate, facial anomalies such as hypertelorism, Downslanted palpebral fissures, and microphthalmia. It can exhibit autosomal dominant and autosomal recessive inheritance patterns, typically presenting symptoms antenatally.

name: Hall-Riggs syndrome
clinical_summary: Hall-Riggs syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by distinctive craniofacial features, including a wide mouth, microcephaly, epicanthus, and a prominent nose, present since birth. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern.

name: Dysmorphism-pectus carinatum-joint laxity syndrome
clinical_summary: This rare disease, Dysmorphism-pectus carinatum-joint laxity syndrome, is characterized by a distinct set of physical features evident from infancy, including joint hypermobility, facial dysmorphism, and nasal ridge anomalies, among others. The exact inheritance pattern is currently unknown.

name: Hallermann-Streiff syndrome
clinical_summary: Hallermann-Streiff syndrome is a rare condition characterized by multiple congenital abnormalities, including those affecting the dentition, skull, eyes, and limbs. These abnormalities often become apparent at birth or shortly after infancy.

name: Greig cephalopolysyndactyly syndrome
clinical_summary: Greig cephalopolysyndactyly syndrome is a rare autosomal dominant disorder characterized by macrocephaly, hypertelorism, and hand polydactyly. These distinguishing features present antenatally and are notable during birth.

name: Acromesomelic dysplasia, Grebe type
clinical_summary: Acromesomelic dysplasia, Grebe type is a rare autosomal recessive condition characterized by skeletal dysplasia, brachydactyly, and polydactyly, which typically presents prenatally or at birth. It is marked by features such as joint stiffness, short foot, and short toe.

name: Grubben-de Cock-Borghgraef syndrome
clinical_summary: Grubben-de Cock-Borghgraef syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by abnormal dentition, facial features, and delayed speech and language development. Key physical findings include a round face, short neck, and eye abnormalities, as well as blue sclerae, often present at birth.

name: Focal dermal hypoplasia
clinical_summary: Focal dermal hypoplasia is a rare X-linked dominant disorder characterized by a range of congenital anomalies, including kidney abnormalities and facial features. Symptoms typically manifest antenatally or in the neonatal period.

name: Grant syndrome
clinical_summary: Grant syndrome is a rare condition characterized by neonatal onset of several distinctive physical features, including large fontanelles, micrognathia, blue sclerae, joint dislocation, frontal bossing, and Wormian bones. The inheritance pattern of Grant syndrome is currently unknown.

name: Gorlin-Chaudhry-Moss syndrome
clinical_summary: Gorlin-Chaudhry-Moss syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by a neonatal onset of craniofacial and dental abnormalities, including brachycephaly, low anterior hairline, and hypertelorism. It also involves additional features such as conductive hearing impairment and abnormality of the eye, with an autosomal recessive inheritance pattern.

name: GMS syndrome
clinical_summary: GMS syndrome is a rare condition characterized by neonatal onset of microcephaly, epicanthus, low-set ears, downslanted palpebral fissures, Rieger anomaly, and intellectual disability. The inheritance pattern of GMS syndrome is currently unknown.

name: Prominent glabella-microcephaly-hypogenitalism syndrome
clinical_summary: This syndrome is characterized by microcephaly, dolichocephaly, and hypogenitalism in association with additional anomalies including hypertrophic glabella, cryptorchidism, small scrotum, and hydronephrosis. The condition has antenatal to neonatal onset and an unknown mode of inheritance.

name: German syndrome
clinical_summary: German syndrome is a rare congenital disorder characterized by distinct orofacial abnormalities, including an open mouth and high palate, presenting at birth. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern, suggesting its manifestation is influenced by genetic mutations.

name: Geroderma osteodysplastica
lab_findings: Abnormal form of the vertebral bodies
clinical_summary: Geroderma osteodysplastica is a rare disorder characterized by neonatal onset of joint hypermobility, osteoporosis, thin skin, hyperextensible skin, and redundant skin, often resulting in recurrent fractures. This condition follows an autosomal recessive inheritance pattern.

name: Gemignani syndrome
clinical_summary: Gemignani syndrome is a rare condition characterized by a combination of symptoms including abnormal testis morphology, sensorineural hearing impairment, delayed puberty, hypopigmented skin patches, and intellectual disability. It presents in adulthood with an autosomal recessive inheritance pattern.

name: Genitopalatocardiac syndrome
key_symptoms: Non-midline cleft of the upper lip
clinical_summary: Genitopalatocardiac syndrome is a rare condition characterized by its association with male pseudohermaphroditism, cleft palate, and cardiac anomalies. The condition presents with a variable range of symptoms including micrognathia, low-set ears, and intrauterine growth retardation from antenatal or neonatal onset.

name: Galloway-Mowat syndrome
clinical_summary: Galloway-Mowat syndrome is a rare condition characterized by the combination of renal impairment, including nephropathy, nephrotic syndrome, and proteinuria, alongside non-renal features such as seizures and congenital malformations. It presents in infancy or childhood, typically with autosomal recessive inheritance, although the mode of inheritance may also be X-linked recessive in some families.

name: GAPO syndrome
clinical_summary: GAPO syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by neonatal onset and features of joint hypermobility, distinctive facial traits, and micrognathia. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern.

name: Splenogonadal fusion-limb defects-micrognathia syndrome
clinical_summary: Splenogonadal fusion-limb defects-micrognathia syndrome is a rare condition characterized by a combination of prenatal and neonatal abnormalities, including inguinal hernia, hip dysplasia, and upper limb anomalies. The condition's symptoms are apparent at birth and often associated with premature birth.

name: Fryns syndrome
clinical_summary: Fryns syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by multiple congenital anomalies. Key features include antenatal or neonatal onset with physical characteristics such as high palate, broad forehead, long philtrum, micrognathia, and posteriorly rotated ears.

name: Frontofacionasal dysplasia
clinical_summary: Frontofacionasal dysplasia is a rare congenital disorder characterized by a combination of craniofacial abnormalities, including cleft palate, brachycephaly, and bifid nasal tip. It presents with symptoms such as hypertelorism, broad forehead, and preauricular skin tag in the neonatal period, with inheritance patterns remaining unknown.

name: Cole-Carpenter syndrome
lab_findings: Abnormal form of the vertebral bodies
inheritance: Autosomal dominant, Autosomal recessive, Not applicable
clinical_summary: Cole-Carpenter syndrome is a rare disease characterized by neonatal onset of multiple systemic and skeletal abnormalities, including micrognathia, proptosis, and abnormal skeletal morphology. Its inheritance pattern is variable, with cases reported in an autosomal dominant and autosomal recessive fashion.

name: Flynn-Aird syndrome
clinical_summary: Flynn-Aird syndrome is a rare autosomal dominant disorder characterized by progressive sensorineural hearing impairment and multiple systemic complications. It typically presents in childhood with a combination of sensorineural hearing loss and other severe symptoms including vision impairment and neurological deterioration.

name: Paraplegia-intellectual disability-hyperkeratosis syndrome
clinical_summary: Paraplegia-intellectual disability-hyperkeratosis syndrome is a rare, X-linked recessive disorder characterized by intellectual disability, lower limb spasticity, and distinctive skin symptoms during infancy.

name: Scalp-ear-nipple syndrome
clinical_summary: Scalp-ear-nipple syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by neonatal onset of small earlobe, scalp and skin abnormalities, sparse hair, microtia, and aplasia/hypoplasia of the nipples. It is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern.

name: Hepatic fibrosis-renal cysts-intellectual disability syndrome
clinical_summary: Hepatic fibrosis-renal cysts-intellectual disability syndrome is a rare disease characterized by a combination of liver, kidney, and developmental abnormalities, including intellectual disability. Key physical features of this syndrome include posteriorly rotated ears, underdeveloped nasal alae, and abnormal eyes, often evident from infancy or birth.

name: Gingival fibromatosis-facial dysmorphism syndrome
clinical_summary: Gingival fibromatosis-facial dysmorphism syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by the onset of symptoms at neonatal age, including gingival fibromatosis, macrocephaly, and distinctive facial features such as hypertelorism and an everted lower lip vermilion. Affected individuals may also present with high palate and gingival overgrowth.

name: Macrosomia-microphthalmia-cleft palate syndrome
lab_findings: Abnormality of the fontanelles or cranial sutures
clinical_summary: Macrosomia-microphthalmia-cleft palate syndrome is a rare condition characterized by multiple congenital abnormalities, including large size at birth, abnormal cranial features, microphthalmia, and vision impairments such as microcornea. The exact inheritance pattern is unknown, although the condition has an antenatal and neonatal age of onset.

name: Ollier disease
clinical_summary: Ollier disease is a rare bone disorder characterized by the presence of multiple enchondromas and associated abnormalities in bone morphology and cartilage growth. It often presents with symptoms such as osteolysis, abnormal metaphysis morphology, hemangiomas, and micromelia.

name: Hand-foot-genital syndrome
key_symptoms: Shortening of all middle phalanges of the fingers
clinical_summary: Hand-foot-genital syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by recurrent urinary tract infections, genitourinary abnormalities, and an association with congenital malformations. It is an autosomal dominant condition with an antenatal or neonatal age of onset.

name: McDonough syndrome
clinical_summary: McDonough syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by a distinct facial morphology, including posteriorly rotated ears, prominent supraorbital ridges, macrotia, and protruding nose, often presenting in infancy or neonatally. The inheritance pattern of this condition is unknown.

name: Laurin-Sandrow syndrome
clinical_summary: Laurin-Sandrow syndrome is a rare autosomal dominant disorder characterized by various polydactyly and syndactyly abnormalities, including preaxial hand polydactyly and toe syndactyly. Common manifestations include triphalangeal thumb and abnormal metacarpal morphology, often present at birth.

name: Cystic fibrosis-gastritis-megaloblastic anemia syndrome
lab_findings: measuring 20% or higher in fecal elastase
clinical_summary: Cystic fibrosis-gastritis-megaloblastic anemia syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a distinct combination of congenital anomalies and extrapulmonary manifestations. Onset typically occurs in infancy.

name: Dislocation of the hip-dysmorphism syndrome
clinical_summary: Dislocation of the hip-dysmorphism syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by joint hypermobility, characteristic facial dysmorphia, and dislocation of the hip, manifesting in infancy or neonatally. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern.

name: Macrocephaly-spastic paraplegia-dysmorphism syndrome
clinical_summary: Macrocephaly-spastic paraplegia-dysmorphism syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by macrocephaly and various dysmorphic facial features. Symptoms typically become apparent in childhood, including coarse facial features, a short philtrum, and specific lip characteristics.

name: Sanjad-Sakati syndrome
clinical_summary: Sanjad-Sakati syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by distinct facial features and growth restrictions. Key symptoms include abnormal ears, thin lip edges, and microcephaly, which are typically apparent in infancy or even at birth.

name: Kabuki syndrome
clinical_summary: Kabuki syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by congenital abnormalities including ptosis, hypodontia, and microdontia. Key features include long palpebral fissures, Peters anomaly, and widely spaced teeth often presenting at birth or in early infancy.

name: Epidermolysis bullosa simplex with anodontia/hypodontia
clinical_summary: Epidermolysis bullosa simplex with anodontia/hypodontia is a rare disease characterized by congenital facial abnormalities, dental issues, and vision problems. Symptoms, including delayed eruption of teeth and mandibular prognathia, typically present in infancy or neonatally.

name: Familial partial lipodystrophy, Dunnigan type
clinical_summary: Familial partial lipodystrophy, Dunnigan type is a rare autosomal dominant disorder characterized by various symptoms including a round face, diabetes mellitus, insulin resistance, secondary amenorrhea, thin skin, and xanthomatosis, typically presenting in adolescence, adulthood, childhood, or old age.

name: Isotretinoin syndrome
clinical_summary: Isotretinoin syndrome is a rare condition characterized by a complex combination of congenital anomalies, including cleft palate, micrognathia, and abnormal outer ear development. It can present with various physical abnormalities, such as hypotonia, reduced tendon reflexes, and hypoplastic toenails, often manifesting at birth or in infancy.

name: Jung syndrome
clinical_summary: Jung syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by various congenital anomalies, including hypothyroidism, microcephaly, and midfacial abnormalities. It typically presents in infancy or neonatal stage with distinct facial and genitourinary manifestations.

name: Johanson-Blizzard syndrome
clinical_summary: Johanson-Blizzard syndrome is a rare Autosomal recessive disorder characterized by various systemic malformations including abnormal dentition, underdeveloped nasal alae, and pancreatic abnormalities, often presenting with intrauterine growth retardation and failure to thrive. Onset of this syndrome typically begins before, during, or after birth.

name: Harlequin ichthyosis
clinical_summary: Harlequin ichthyosis is a rare, congenital disorder characterized by severe skin scaling, joint mobility limitations, and distinctive facial features, including a depressed nasal ridge and ectropion. It presents with significant morbidity in the neonatal period, due to its autosomal recessive inheritance pattern.

name: Hypospadias-intellectual disability, Goldblatt type syndrome
clinical_summary: Hypospadias-intellectual disability, Goldblatt type syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by intellectual disability, physical anomalies such as posteriorly rotated ears and hypospadias, and joint stiffness, typically presenting in infancy or at birth. The exact cause of this disorder is unknown.

name: Neuronal intranuclear inclusion disease
clinical_summary: Neuronal intranuclear inclusion disease is a rare, autosomal dominant disorder characterized by a range of neurological symptoms, including ophthalmoplegia, nystagmus, and ataxia. Affected individuals often experience atypical behavior, dementia, and seizure activity, which can manifest at various ages.

name: Familial isolated hypoparathyroidism
inheritance: Autosomal dominant, Autosomal recessive, X-linked recessive
clinical_summary: Familial isolated hypoparathyroidism is a rare disease characterized by symptoms including hypoparathyroidism, hypocalcemia, myopathy, seizure, abnormalities in calcium-phosphate metabolism, and cataract. It can occur in individuals of all ages and is inherited through autosomal dominant, autosomal recessive, and X-linked recessive patterns.

name: Megacystis-microcolon-intestinal hypoperistalsis syndrome
clinical_summary: Megacystis-microcolon-intestinal hypoperistalsis syndrome is a rare disease characterized by severe gastrointestinal dysmotility, resulting in megacystis, microcolon, and hypoperistalsis. It often presents at birth or shortly after with symptoms including nausea, vomiting, abdominal distention, and multicystic kidney dysplasia.

name: Hypodontia-dysplasia of nails syndrome
clinical_summary: Hypodontia-dysplasia of nails syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by abnormalities in the dentition and nail morphology. It follows an autosomal dominant inheritance pattern, typically presenting in childhood with symptoms such as hypodontia and delayed tooth eruption.

name: Fibulo-ulnar hypoplasia-renal anomalies syndrome
clinical_summary: Fibulo-ulnar hypoplasia-renal anomalies syndrome is a rare congenital disorder characterized by a distinct set of craniofacial features and polyhydramnios. This condition is associated with antenatal and neonatal onset.

name: Primary hypomagnesemia with hypercalciuria and nephrocalcinosis with severe ocular involvement
clinical_summary: Primary hypomagnesemia with hypercalciuria and nephrocalcinosis is a rare disease characterized by severe ocular involvement, including myopia and chorioretinal coloboma. This condition typically presents with symptoms such as inguinal hernias, nephropathy, nystagmus, and nephrolithiasis in affected children.

name: Maternal hyperthermia-induced birth defects
clinical_summary: Maternal hyperthermia-induced birth defects is a rare disease characterized by various congenital anomalies, including cleft palate, microcephaly, and intrauterine growth retardation. Symptoms also include seizures, hypotonia, and joint stiffness, often presenting in the antenatal or neonatal periods.

name: Histidinuria-renal tubular defect syndrome
clinical_summary: Histidinuria-renal tubular defect syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by physical abnormalities such as long philtrum, macrotia, wide nasal bridge, and hypoplastic toenails, along with sensorineural hearing impairment and intellectual disability. The exact inheritance pattern of this condition is unknown, with symptoms typically manifesting in childhood or infancy.

name: Holzgreve syndrome
clinical_summary: Holzgreve syndrome is a rare condition characterized by a spectrum of congenital anomalies, including posteriorly rotated ears, lung, heart, kidney, and cranial morphological abnormalities. The condition presents antenatally or in the neonatal period, with an unclear mode of inheritance.

name: Holoprosencephaly-postaxial polydactyly syndrome
clinical_summary: Holoprosencephaly-postaxial polydactyly syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by antenatal and neonatal onset of microphthalmia, hypotelorism, postaxial hand polydactyly, and genital abnormalities. It is marked by the combined features of holoprosencephaly and postaxial polydactyly.

name: Holoprosencephaly-caudal dysgenesis syndrome
clinical_summary: Holoprosencephaly-caudal dysgenesis syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by antenatal and neonatal onset of multiple congenital anomalies, including abnormal genital development, renal insufficiency, facial clefts, and microcephaly. The mode of inheritance is unknown, and the disorder is associated with a range of severe systemic complications.

name: Holoprosencephaly-craniosynostosis syndrome
lab_findings: reversed frontal posterior skull height ratio on mri
clinical_summary: Holoprosencephaly-craniosynostosis syndrome is a rare condition characterized by various craniofacial and ocular abnormalities, including brachycephaly, microcephaly, and strabismus. Its exact age of onset and inheritance pattern remain unknown.

name: Orofaciodigital syndrome type 1
clinical_summary: Orofaciodigital syndrome type 1 is a rare congenital disorder characterized by a combination of symptoms including cleft lip, tongue, and facial abnormalities, with variable presentation in infancy. It exhibits an X-linked dominant inheritance pattern with antenatal onset.

name: Ophthalmomandibulomelic dysplasia
clinical_summary: Ophthalmomandibulomelic dysplasia is a rare condition characterized by its complex set of symptoms including blindness, joint mobility limitation, radioulnar synostosis, and limb abnormalities such as micromelia and mesomelia, often accompanied by elbow dislocation. The exact inheritance pattern and age of onset of this disease are currently unknown.

name: Oculocerebral hypopigmentation syndrome, Preus type
clinical_summary: Oculocerebral hypopigmentation syndrome, Preus type is a rare condition characterized by neonatal onset of symptoms including high palate, hydrocephalus, hearing impairment, nystagmus, ocular albinism, and arachnodactyly, with an unknown inheritance pattern. Its specific combination of symptoms distinguishes it from other disorders.

name: Odonto-onycho dysplasia-alopecia syndrome
clinical_summary: Odonto-onycho dysplasia-alopecia syndrome is a rare neonatal disorder characterized by dental abnormalities, including microdontia and tooth malposition, along with cutaneous and nail anomalies. The condition also presents with alopecia and keratoderma, affecting nails and fingers/palms.

name: Severe oculo-renal-cerebellar syndrome
clinical_summary: Severe oculo-renal-cerebellar syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by onset in infancy with symptoms including joint hypermobility, optic atrophy, and renal insufficiency. Clinical manifestations also include strabismus, proteinuria, and hypotonia, indicating complex effects on multiple body systems.

name: Oculocerebral hypopigmentation syndrome, Cross type
clinical_summary: Oculocerebral hypopigmentation syndrome, Cross type is a rare disease characterized by neonatal onset and various physical anomalies including cryptorchidism, narrow mouth, and abnormal eye morphology, among others. The inheritance pattern of this disease is unknown.

name: Papillon-Lefèvre syndrome
clinical_summary: Papillon-Lefèvre syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by severe periodontal disease and skin hyperkeratosis in infancy or childhood. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern.

name: Otofaciocervical syndrome
clinical_summary: Otofaciocervical syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a distinct set of facial and ear abnormalities, including high palate, full cheeks, macrotia, and protruding ear, often accompanied by conductive hearing impairment. It can manifest at an early age, both antenatally and neonatally, and is inherited through both autosomal dominant and autosomal recessive patterns.

name: Pachydermoperiostosis
clinical_summary: Pachydermoperiostosis is a rare inherited disorder characterized by thickened skin, bone pain, and abnormal cortical bone morphology. It typically presents in childhood, often in conjunction with hyperhidrosis, seborrheic dermatitis, and an increased risk of osteomyelitis.

name: Otoonychoperoneal syndrome
clinical_summary: Otoonychoperoneal syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder presenting with craniofacial features such as dolichocephaly, macrotia, and upslanted palpebral fissure, alongside systemic abnormalities like abnormal diaphysis morphology and flexion contracture. It is characterized by intellectual disability, typically of mild severity.

name: Autosomal recessive malignant osteopetrosis
clinical_summary: Autosomal recessive malignant osteopetrosis is a rare disease characterized by symptoms including hydrocephalus, macrocephaly, hearing impairment, otitis media, visual impairment, and nystagmus, typically presenting in infancy or neonatal age. This disorder is inherited in an autosomal recessive manner.

name: Multicentric carpo-tarsal osteolysis with or without nephropathy
clinical_summary: Multicentric carpo-tarsal osteolysis with or without nephropathy is a rare disease characterized by a combination of osteolysis in the carpal and tarsal bones, and nephropathy, often presenting with symptoms such as proteinuria. It is an autosomal dominant disorder with a childhood onset.

name: Nasu-Hakola disease
clinical_summary: Nasu-Hakola disease is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a combination of oculomotor apraxia and neurological abnormalities, including atypical behavior and frontal lobe dementia. It is typically manifested in adolescence or adulthood and is associated with symptoms such as disinhibition, irritability, and personality changes.

name: Short stature-valvular heart disease-characteristic facies syndrome
clinical_summary: Short stature-valvular heart disease-characteristic facies syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by congenital anomalies including abnormality of the dentition, high palate, ptosis, dental crowding, mitral valve prolapse, and pulmonic stenosis. This condition is typically inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern, presenting symptoms in infancy or the neonatal period.

name: Phakomatosis pigmentovascularis
clinical_summary: Phakomatosis pigmentovascularis is a rare congenital disorder characterized by ocular and cutaneous manifestations, including glaucoma, nevi flammeus, and hypopigmented skin patches. It typically presents neonatally with neurological complications such as seizures and cerebral cortical atrophy.

name: Short stature-wormian bones-dextrocardia syndrome
clinical_summary: Short stature-wormian bones-dextrocardia syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by a combination of craniofacial abnormalities, including micrognathia, broad alveolar ridges, and low-set ears, among other symptoms. Its inheritance pattern is unknown, and it typically presents in infancy or at birth.

name: Alopecia-intellectual disability syndrome
lab_findings: normal to low serum zinc levels
clinical_summary: Alopecia-intellectual disability syndrome is a rare condition characterized by neonatal onset of microcephaly, hearing impairment, intellectual disability, hypotonia, alopecia, and sparse scalp hair. This autosomal recessive disorder manifests in infancy and is associated with significant developmental and physical deficits.

name: Pelvic dysplasia-arthrogryposis of lower limbs syndrome
clinical_summary: Pelvic dysplasia-arthrogryposis of lower limbs syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by skeletal abnormalities and neuromuscular impairment. Clinical features include blue sclerae, gait disturbance, hip dislocation, and skeletal muscle atrophy, typically presenting in utero or at birth.

name: Penoscrotal transposition
inheritance: Autosomal recessive, Not applicable, X-linked recessive
clinical_summary: Penoscrotal transposition is a rare disease characterized by an abnormal arrangement of the penoscrotal area, often accompanied by other congenital anomalies such as hypospadias, renal agenesis, and renal dysplasia. Symptoms typically present at birth or in infancy.

name: PEHO syndrome
clinical_summary: PEHO syndrome is a rare condition characterized by a combination of craniofacial anomalies, including abnormal pinna morphology, abnormal palate morphology, abnormal upper lip, open mouth, malar flattening, and epicanthus. It typically presents in infancy or neonatal onset with possible autosomal dominant or autosomal recessive inheritance patterns.

name: Spastic paraparesis-deafness syndrome
clinical_summary: Spastic paraparesis-deafness syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by hypogonadism, sensorineural hearing impairment, visual impairment, cataract, gait disturbance, and hyperreflexia. It presents in childhood, with an unknown inheritance pattern.

name: Renal agenesis, bilateral
clinical_summary: Bilateral renal agenesis is a rare autosomal recessive congenital disorder characterized by the absence of both kidneys. Clinical manifestations may include oligohydramnios, epicanthus, hypertelorism, low-set ears, and a depressed nasal ridge.

name: Cronkhite-Canada syndrome
clinical_summary: Cronkhite-Canada syndrome is a rare disease characterized by abnormal skin pigmentation, lymphedema, and nail abnormalities, including fingernail and toenail alterations. It typically affects adults and the elderly, with no known familial inheritance pattern.

name: Polyneuropathy-intellectual disability-acromicria-premature menopause syndrome
clinical_summary: Polyneuropathy-intellectual disability-acromicria-premature menopause syndrome is a rare disease characterized by decreased nerve conduction velocity, intellectual disability, and various neurological symptoms, including decreased tendon reflexes and muscle weakness. The condition typically presents in adults without a known inheritance pattern.

name: Postaxial polydactyly-dental and vertebral anomalies syndrome
lab_findings: Abnormal form of the vertebral bodies
clinical_summary: Postaxial polydactyly-dental and vertebral anomalies syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by multiple congenital anomalies including thickened nuchal skin fold and vertebral fusion. It typically presents in the neonatal period with a complex combination of symptoms such as brachydactyly and macrodontia.

name: Poland syndrome
key_symptoms: Aplasia of the pectoralis major muscle
inheritance: Autosomal dominant, Autosomal recessive, Multigenic/multifactorial, Not applicable
clinical_summary: Poland syndrome is a rare condition characterized by thoracic asymmetry, aplasia/hypoplasia of the nipples and breasts, and absence of subcutaneous fat due to aplasia of the pectoralis major muscle. It can involve renal hypoplasia and has a complex and multifactorial inheritance pattern.

name: Hereditary acrokeratotic poikiloderma
clinical_summary: Hereditary acrokeratotic poikiloderma is a rare genetic disorder with infancy or neonatal onset, characterized by various skin manifestations including thin skin, eczematoid dermatitis, gingivitis, urticaria, hypopigmented skin patches, and dystrophic toenail. The mode of inheritance is currently unknown.

name: Pili torti-developmental delay-neurological abnormalities syndrome
clinical_summary: Pili torti-developmental delay-neurological abnormalities syndrome is a rare condition characterized by global developmental delay, ataxia, growth delay, and joint hypermobility, often presenting with pili torti and abnormal hair texture in infancy. The exact mode of inheritance is currently unknown.

name: Piebald trait-neurologic defects syndrome
clinical_summary: Piebald trait-neurologic defects syndrome is characterized by hearing impairment, abnormal eyebrow morphology, cutaneous photosensitivity, skin pigmentation abnormalities, and intellectual disability. The inheritance pattern and age of onset for this syndrome are unknown.

name: Phocomelia, Schinzel type
clinical_summary: Phocomelia, Schinzel type is a rare disease characterized by antenatal onset of severe limb abnormalities, including finger aplasia, foot oligodactyly, micromelia, fibular aplasia, and tibia morphology abnormalities. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern.

name: Lower limb malformation-hypospadias syndrome
clinical_summary: Lower limb malformation-hypospadias syndrome is a rare condition characterized by a combination of hypospadias, abnormal cardiovascular system morphology, ureter abnormality, posturally rotated ears, macrotia, and short neck. Onset typically occurs in the neonatal period, with little information available regarding the inheritance pattern of the disease.

name: Upper limb defect-eye and ear abnormalities syndrome
clinical_summary: Upper limb defect-eye and ear abnormalities syndrome is a rare condition associated with infantile-onset hearing impairment and chorioretinal coloboma. Affected individuals may also experience developmental delays and physical abnormalities such as abnormal antihelix formation.

name: Müllerian duct anomalies-limb anomalies syndrome
clinical_summary: Müllerian duct anomalies-limb anomalies syndrome is a rare condition characterized by a combination of congenital malformations including microcephaly, limb abnormalities, and Müllerian duct anomalies. It is characterized by neonatal onset, including split hand, micromelia, and abnormality of the wrist, in addition to short stature and uterus didelphys.

name: FATCO syndrome
clinical_summary: FATCO syndrome is a rare condition characterized by antenatal and neonatal onset of symptoms including split hand, premature birth, and cardiovascular abnormalities. Associated physical anomalies include abnormal morphology of the fibula and tibia.

name: Acrogeria
clinical_summary: Acrogeria is a rare disease characterized by childhood or infantile onset of joint hypermobility, thin skin, fine hair, short stature, and irregular hyperpigmentation. The cause and inheritance pattern of Acrogeria are unknown.

name: Metaphyseal chondrodysplasia, Spahr type
clinical_summary: Metaphyseal chondrodysplasia, Spahr type is a rare, autosomal recessive disease characterized by abnormality of the dentition, hip dysplasia, scoliosis, abnormal metaphysis morphology, and gait disturbance. It typically presents in childhood with symptoms such as carious teeth.

name: White forelock with malformations
clinical_summary: White forelock with malformations is a rare condition presenting with a combination of joint hypermobility, specific physical malformations, and craniofacial abnormalities predominantly evident from infancy. The inheritance pattern is unknown, complicating diagnosis and understanding of the condition's progression.

name: Isolated megalencephaly
lab_findings: Abnormality of the fontanelles or cranial sutures
clinical_summary: Isolated megalencephaly is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by cranial and genital abnormalities, including macrocephaly and macroorchidism. Symptoms appear antenatally or in the neonatal period and include features such as dolichocephaly and prominent occiput.

name: Megalocornea-intellectual disability syndrome
clinical_summary: Megalocornea-intellectual disability syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by intellectual disability, joint hypermobility, and distinctive physical features including micrognathia and megalocornea. Affected individuals typically present with global developmental delay from infancy or childhood.

name: Neurocutaneous melanocytosis
clinical_summary: Neurocutaneous melanocytosis is a rare condition characterized by the presence of melanocytic nevi and thickened skin, often accompanied by intellectual disability, seizures, generalized hirsutism, and increased intracranial pressure, typically presenting in childhood.

name: Melkersson-Rosenthal syndrome
clinical_summary: Melkersson-Rosenthal syndrome is a rare disease characterized by recurrent episodes of orofacial edema, cranial nerve paralysis, and dermatological abnormalities. It typically presents in childhood, but the inheritance pattern is unknown.

name: Microcephaly-deafness-intellectual disability syndrome
clinical_summary: Microcephaly-deafness-intellectual disability syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by the presence of microcephaly, facial asymmetry, and other distinct physical features such as abnormal palate morphology and epicanthus. The onset of this syndrome typically occurs in infancy or neonatally.

name: Oculoauriculovertebral spectrum with radial defects
clinical_summary: Oculoauriculovertebral spectrum with radial defects is a rare condition characterized by a combination of facial asymmetry, inner ear abnormalities, and limb malformations. It presents with symptoms from birth, including short mandibular rami, laryngeal stridor, microtia, and aplasia or hypoplasia of the thumb.

name: Microcornea-glaucoma-absent frontal sinuses syndrome
clinical_summary: Microcornea-glaucoma-absent frontal sinuses syndrome is a rare disease characterized by epicanthus, microcornea, glaucoma, and visual impairment. It typically presents in childhood or infancy with associated abnormal skull morphology and a round face.

name: Spastic ataxia-corneal dystrophy syndrome
clinical_summary: Spastic ataxia-corneal dystrophy syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a combination of developmental cataract, myopia, optic atrophy, corneal dystrophy, ataxia, and gait disturbance, often presenting in childhood. This condition is marked by the simultaneous manifestation of neurological and ocular impairments.

name: Lethal intrauterine growth restriction-cortical malformation-congenital contractures syndrome
clinical_summary: Lethal intrauterine growth restriction-cortical malformation-congenital contractures syndrome is a rare X-linked recessive disorder characterized by antenatal onset of microcephaly, sloping forehead, low-set ears, short neck, holoprosencephaly, and limitation of joint mobility. This syndrome results in severe congenital anomalies and is typically lethal.

name: Corpus callosum agenesis-abnormal genitalia syndrome
clinical_summary: Corpus callosum agenesis-abnormal genitalia syndrome is a rare X-linked recessive disorder characterized by microcephaly, coarse facial features, and neurological symptoms such as strabismus and seizure. It typically presents with these features in infancy or the neonatal period.

name: Micro syndrome
lab_findings: i couldn't verify the lab findings for micro syndrome as it's not a widely recognized or established medical condition.
microcephaly is more commonly associated with other conditions such as primordial dwarfism, and i found the following general findings for that condition:
clinical_summary: Micro syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a combination of distinct facial features and physical anomalies. It typically presents in childhood and is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern.

name: Microcephaly-cervical spine fusion anomalies syndrome
clinical_summary: Microcephaly-cervical spine fusion anomalies syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by severe congenital anomalies, including microcephaly, micrognathia, and sloping forehead in addition to other physical abnormalities. The age of onset is typically antenatal, with some manifestations present at birth.

name: Autosomal recessive chorioretinopathy-microcephaly syndrome
clinical_summary: Autosomal recessive chorioretinopathy-microcephaly syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a combination of congenital physical abnormalities and microcephaly, with symptoms typically appearing in the neonatal period. The condition is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern, meaning affected individuals must inherit two copies of the mutated gene, one from each parent.

name: Microcephaly-microcornea syndrome, Seemanova type
clinical_summary: Microcephaly-microcornea syndrome, Seemanova type: a rare condition characterized by microcephaly, brachycephaly, narrow high palate, and retrognathia presenting in infancy or at birth. Other symptoms may include hypogonadism and ocular abnormalities including microcornea.

name: Langer mesomelic dysplasia
clinical_summary: Langer mesomelic dysplasia is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by severe short stature, limb shortening, and abnormal carpal morphology. Its antenatal onset is marked by symptoms including high palate, mesomelic/rhizomelic limb shortening, and Madelung deformity.

name: Mesomelic dysplasia, Nievergelt type
clinical_summary: Mesomelic dysplasia, Nievergelt type is a rare condition characterized by limited joint mobility, skeletal abnormalities, and short limbs. It demonstrates pre- and perinatal onset and is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern.

name: Microcephalic primordial dwarfism, Montreal type
clinical_summary: Microcephalic primordial dwarfism, Montreal type is a rare disorder characterized by a combination of physical anomalies, including posteriorly rotated ears, hypertonia, and low posterior hairline, typically presenting at birth or in infancy. The inheritance pattern of this condition is currently unknown.

name: Mesomelic dwarfism-cleft palate-camptodactyly syndrome
clinical_summary: Mesomelic dwarfism-cleft palate-camptodactyly syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by developmental abnormalities, including cleft palate, abnormal long bone formation, and hip and elbow deformities. Symptoms of this disorder are typically present at birth and include features such as retrognathia, mesomelia, and bowing of the long bones.

name: Microcephalic primordial dwarfism, Toriello type
clinical_summary: Microcephalic primordial dwarfism, Toriello type is a rare disorder characterized by microcephaly, intellectual disability, and multiple other congenital anomalies. It is distinguished by its complex and unique presentation of microcephaly in conjunction with Downslanted palpebral fissures, cataract, abnormal rib morphology, and brachydactyly.

name: Osteoglosphonic dysplasia
lab_findings: Abnormal form of the vertebral bodies
clinical_summary: Osteoglosphonic dysplasia is a rare, hereditary condition characterized by a spectrum of prenatal and neonatal features, including craniosynostosis, abnormal clavicle, and distinctive facial anomalies. It is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern.

name: Mesomelic dwarfism, Reinhardt-Pfeiffer type
clinical_summary: Mesomelic dwarfism, Reinhardt-Pfeiffer type is a rare autosomal dominant disorder characterized by micromelia, malformations of long bones, and joint dislocations. Clinical manifestations occur prenatally, in infancy, or at birth.

name: Fibular aplasia-complex brachydactyly syndrome
clinical_summary: Fibular aplasia-complex brachydactyly syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a combination of skeletal abnormalities and joint mobility limitations, present at birth. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern and manifests with symptoms such as brachydactyly, abnormal thumb morphology, and micromelia.

name: Mayer-Rokitansky-Küster-Hauser syndrome type 2
clinical_summary: Mayer-Rokitansky-Küster-Hauser syndrome type 2 is a rare disorder characterized by azoospermia, ectopic kidney, and other congenital anomalies such as short neck and abnormal rib morphology. It can manifest in varying stages from antenatal, neonatal, or adolescence.

name: Muckle-Wells syndrome
lab_findings: normal or decreased complement levels (c3, c4)
clinical_summary: Muckle-Wells syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by nephrotic syndrome and nephropathy, as well as other symptoms including progressive sensorineural hearing impairment and skin rash. It typically presents in infancy, childhood, or the neonatal period and is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern.

name: 3M syndrome
clinical_summary: 3M syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by distinct facial features present at birth. Clinical manifestations include an everted lower lip vermilion, a triangular face, broad forehead, bulbous nose, anteverted nares, and a short neck.

name: Ataxia-pancytopenia syndrome
clinical_summary: Ataxia-pancytopenia syndrome is a rare autosomal dominant disorder characterized by symptoms including ataxia, cerebellar atrophy, and splenomegaly. The condition presents in adolescence, adulthood, or childhood with associated features of nystagmus, gait disturbance, and hyperreflexia.

name: Noonan syndrome-like disorder with loose anagen hair
clinical_summary: Noonan syndrome-like disorder with loose anagen hair is a rare condition characterized by physical features including webbed neck, low posterior hairline, and sparse scalp hair. It is marked by delayed skeletal maturation, short nose, and short stature, typically presenting antenatally or in infancy.

name: Oculofaciocardiodental syndrome
clinical_summary: Oculofaciocardiodental syndrome is a rare X-linked dominant disorder characterized by multiple congenital anomalies, including dental and facial abnormalities, often presenting in infancy or at birth. It is distinguished by an array of systemic manifestations including those affecting the skin, eyes, face, teeth, muscles, and cardiovascular and palatal anatomy.

name: Oculodentodigital dysplasia
clinical_summary: Oculodentodigital dysplasia is a rare disorder characterized by various congenital abnormalities, including microcornea, abnormal pinna morphology, and nasal anomalies, typically presenting in infancy or at birth. The disorder is inherited in an autosomal dominant and autosomal recessive pattern.

name: Keipert syndrome
key_symptoms: Aplasia/Hypoplasia of the distal phalanges of the toes
clinical_summary: Keipert syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by unique physical features, including hypertelorism, broad forehead, and abnormalities of the hands and facial structure, associated with sensorineural hearing impairment. This X-linked recessive disorder typically becomes apparent at birth or in infancy.

name: Neurofaciodigitorenal syndrome
key_symptoms: Atresia of the external auditory canal
clinical_summary: Neurofaciodigitorenal syndrome is a rare condition characterized by a combination of abnormalities in the pinna, facial shape, nasal bridge, and limbs, including a prominent nasal bridge and triphalangeal thumb. Symptoms typically present in-utero or at birth, although the mode of inheritance is currently unknown.

name: Tetraamelia-multiple malformations syndrome
clinical_summary: Tetraamelia-multiple malformations syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a spectrum of congenital malformations, including nasal aplasia/hypoplasia, kidney anomalies, and genitourinary abnormalities. This syndrome typically presents antenatally, in infancy, or neonatally and has been linked to both autosomal recessive and X-linked recessive inheritance patterns.

name: Thalidomide embryopathy
clinical_summary: Thalidomide embryopathy is a rare disease characterized by split hand and variable limb abnormalities, including preaxial hand polydactyly and triphalangeal thumb. Common physical findings include radial club hand and fibula abnormalities.

name: Radioulnar synostosis-microcephaly-scoliosis syndrome
clinical_summary: Radioulnar synostosis-microcephaly-scoliosis syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by abnormalities of the dentition, facial features, and skeletal system, often presenting antenatally or in early neonatal life. The exact inheritance pattern is unknown, although it appears to manifest in multiple systems starting before or at birth.

name: Radioulnar synostosis-developmental delay-hypotonia syndrome
clinical_summary: Radioulnar synostosis-developmental delay-hypotonia syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by a combination of multicystic kidney dysplasia, dental abnormalities, and facial anomalies including macrocephaly, dolichocephaly, and a narrow face. The exact mechanism of inheritance is unknown.

name: Weismann-Netter syndrome
clinical_summary: Weismann-Netter syndrome is a rare disease characterized by severe short stature, tibial bowing, and abnormal long bone morphology. The condition presents in the neonatal period with unknown inheritance pattern.

name: Trichodermodysplasia-dental alterations syndrome
clinical_summary: Trichodermodysplasia-dental alterations syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by delayed eruption of teeth, palmoplantar keratoderma, and sparse scalp hair with features of fine and brittle hair, often associated with scoliosis. This condition typically manifests in neonates, although a clear inheritance pattern is unknown.

name: Thomas syndrome
clinical_summary: Thomas syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a combination of congenital anomalies, including multicystic kidney dysplasia, cleft palate, and cardiac morphological abnormalities. Symptoms present antenatally or postnatally.

name: Hoyeraal-Hreidarsson syndrome
inheritance: Autosomal dominant, Autosomal recessive, X-linked recessive
clinical_summary: Hoyeraal-Hreidarsson syndrome is a rare disease characterized by microcephaly, intellectual disability, and failure to thrive, typically manifesting in infancy or neonatally. Key clinical manifestations also include global developmental delay, hypertonia, and cerebellar hypoplasia.

name: Urban-Rogers-Meyer syndrome
clinical_summary: Urban-Rogers-Meyer syndrome is a rare disease characterized by multiple congenital anomalies, including cryptorchidism, hypogonadism, and prominent nasal bridge, typically presenting in infancy. The exact inheritance pattern of this condition is unknown.

name: Trichodysplasia-xeroderma syndrome
clinical_summary: Trichodysplasia-xeroderma syndrome is a rare condition characterized by neonatal onset of dry skin, coarse and brittle hair, sparse scalp hair, and trichodysplasia presented with pili torti. The inheritance pattern of this disorder is currently unknown.

name: Unilateral ocular duplication
lab_findings: Abnormality of the fontanelles or cranial sutures
clinical_summary: Unilateral ocular duplication is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a complex combination of craniofacial and ocular anomalies, including median cleft lip and microcornea. It typically presents with a distinctive set of symptoms at birth, following an autosomal dominant inheritance pattern.

name: Autoimmune polyendocrinopathy type 1
clinical_summary: Autoimmune polyendocrinopathy type 1 is a rare, autosomal recessive disease characterized by various endocrine dysfunctions and autoimmune manifestations. It typically presents with a combination of symptoms including primary adrenal insufficiency, hypo-parathyroidism, and chronic mucocutaneous candidiasis in adolescence or childhood.

name: Wieacker-Wolff syndrome
clinical_summary: Wieacker-Wolff syndrome is a rare condition characterized by neonatal onset of ocular motor dysfunction, intellectual disability, and developmental delay, often accompanied by joint mobility limitations and speech abnormalities. The condition is primarily diagnosed as X-linked recessive, without inheritance.

name: Worster-Drought syndrome
clinical_summary: Worster-Drought syndrome is a rare, autosomal dominant disorder characterized by abnormal cranial nerve morphology, impaired speech or vocalization, and multiple neurological symptoms. It primarily affects individuals in childhood, with symptoms including tongue muscle weakness, hyperreflexia, and specific learning disabilities.

name: Velo-facial-skeletal syndrome
clinical_summary: Velo-facial-skeletal syndrome is a rare disease characterized by a combination of distinct craniofacial abnormalities, including narrowed and lengthened face configurations, with symptoms typically present at birth. The cause of this syndrome is unknown and its mode of inheritance remains unclear.

name: Verloove Vanhorick-Brubakk syndrome
key_symptoms: Atresia of the external auditory canal
lab_findings: Abnormality of pelvic girdle bone morphology
Abnormal form of the vertebral bodies
clinical_summary: Verloove Vanhorick-Brubakk syndrome is a rare condition manifesting with various congenital anomalies in the neonatal period, including cryptorchidism, cleft palate, and other ear and parathyroid abnormalities. The inheritance pattern of this condition is currently unknown.

name: MMEP syndrome
clinical_summary: MMEP syndrome is a rare condition characterized by the combination of cryptorchidism, median cleft lip, orofacial cleft, microcephaly, and mandibular prognathia, often accompanied by severe visual impairment. Its onset typically occurs before or at birth.

name: Weill-Marchesani syndrome
clinical_summary: Weill-Marchesani syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by its onset in infancy or neonatal stages, presenting with symptoms such as glaucoma, brachydactyly, short stature, high myopia, and microspherophakia. It is inherited in both autosomal dominant and autosomal recessive patterns.

name: Thoraco-abdominal enteric duplication
clinical_summary: Thoraco-abdominal enteric duplication is a rare disease characterized by the presence of extra intestinal tissue that can be associated with multiple congenital anomalies, such as missing ribs, heart defects, and respiratory insufficiency, typically presenting in infancy. The age of onset is infancy, and the inheritance pattern is currently unknown.

name: Acromelic frontonasal dysplasia
clinical_summary: Acromelic frontonasal dysplasia is a rare genetic disorder characterized by distinct craniofacial abnormalities presenting at birth, including large fontanelles and brachycephaly. It is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern, typically evident in the antenatal or neonatal periods.

name: Young syndrome
clinical_summary: Young syndrome is a rare condition characterized by obstructive azoospermia and recurrent respiratory infections, including bronchitis, sinopulmonary infections, and otitis media, with decreased fertility. It typically presents in adulthood.

name: Facial dysmorphism-shawl scrotum-joint laxity syndrome
clinical_summary: Facial dysmorphism-shawl scrotum-joint laxity syndrome is a rare condition characterized by a combination of distinct physical features, including cryptorchidism, shawl scrotum, and facial dysmorphisms, typically observed at birth.
