The inheritance pattern of this syndrome is currently unknown.

name: Microphthalmia-microtia-fetal akinesia syndrome
clinical_summary: Microphthalmia-microtia-fetal akinesia syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by a combination of severe congenital anomalies including microphthalmia, micrognathia, and joint mobility limitations, among others. The exact inheritance pattern is unknown, although it typically manifests antenatally or neonatally.

name: Brachydactyly-mesomelia-intellectual disability-heart defects syndrome
clinical_summary: This rare syndrome presents with neonatal onset and involves multiple congenital abnormalities, including abnormal palate morphology, micrognathia, heart defects, and limb abnormalities. Affected individuals display a complex combination of dysmorphic facial features and musculoskeletal anomalies.

name: Fryns-Smeets-Thiry syndrome
clinical_summary: Fryns-Smeets-Thiry syndrome is a rare disease characterized by a distinctive facial appearance, including a wide mouth, thick lower lip vermilion, everted lower lip vermilion, short philtrum, and micrognathia. This condition typically presents in childhood, but information regarding inheritance pattern is currently unknown.

name: Atherosclerosis-deafness-diabetes-epilepsy-nephropathy syndrome
clinical_summary: Atherosclerosis-deafness-diabetes-epilepsy-nephropathy syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by sensorineural hearing impairment, photomyoclonic seizures, and coronary artery atherosclerosis. It typically presents in childhood with progressive neurologic deterioration and abnormality of mitochondrial metabolism.

name: Muscular pseudohypertrophy-hypothyroidism syndrome
clinical_summary: Muscular pseudohypertrophy-hypothyroidism syndrome is a rare condition characterized by macroglossia, coarse facial features, hypothyroidism, jaundice, and gait disturbance with muscle weakness, typically presenting in childhood. The inheritance pattern of this disorder remains unknown.

name: Myalgia-eosinophilia syndrome associated with tryptophan
clinical_summary: Myalgia-eosinophilia syndrome associated with tryptophan is a rare disease characterized by symptoms including urticaria, thickened skin, arthritis, limitation of joint mobility, eosinophilia, and lymphopenia. This disease can affect individuals of all ages and is not inherited.

name: Dysmorphism-cleft palate-loose skin syndrome
clinical_summary: Dysmorphism-cleft palate-loose skin syndrome is characterized by antenatal to neonatal onset of tall stature, cleft palate, trigonocephaly, narrow face, long face, and epicanthus. The inheritance pattern of this rare disease is currently unknown.

name: Dysplastic cortical hyperostosis, Kozlowski-Tsuruta type
clinical_summary: Dysplastic cortical hyperostosis, Kozlowski-Tsuruta type is a rare disease characterized by abnormalities within the urinary system, among other symptoms. It presents in the womb, before birth.

name: Progeroid syndrome, Petty type
clinical_summary: Progeroid syndrome, Petty type is a rare condition characterized by a combination of craniofacial abnormalities, including a wide anterior fontanel, broad forehead, and mandibular prognathia, along with other anomalies such as epicanthus and strabismus. It is typically identified in the neonatal period, and is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern.

name: White matter hypoplasia-corpus callosum agenesis-intellectual disability syndrome
clinical_summary: White matter hypoplasia-corpus callosum agenesis-intellectual disability syndrome is a rare condition characterized by neonatal onset of multiple congenital anomalies, including microcephaly, hypertelorism, micrognathia, and other craniofacial abnormalities. The syndrome is associated with intellectual disability and is inherited unknown.

name: Proteus-like syndrome
clinical_summary: Proteus-like syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a complex of congenital anomalies including hydrocephalus, macrocephaly, and mandibular prognathia, often accompanied by ophthalmologic abnormalities. It typically manifests in infancy or neonatal period and exhibits an autosomal dominant inheritance pattern.

name: 46,XX difference of sex development-anorectal anomalies syndrome
clinical_summary: 46,XX difference of sex development-anorectal anomalies syndrome is a rare disease characterized by neonatal onset of multicystic kidney dysplasia and associated urinary tract abnormalities. Symptoms include hydroureter, hydronephrosis, abnormality of the urethra, abnormal internal genitalia, and oligohydramnios.

name: Autosomal recessive multiple pterygium syndrome
clinical_summary: Autosomal recessive multiple pterygium syndrome is a rare disease characterized by antenatal to neonatal onset of webbed neck, abnormal sternum morphology, pectus excavatum, and limitation of joint mobility. This condition is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern and is further complicated by the presence of multiple pterygia and axillary pterygia.

name: Ramon syndrome
clinical_summary: Ramon syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by the combination of gingival fibromatosis, narrow palate, and full cheeks. This condition is typically associated with intellectual disability, delayed eruption of teeth, and seizure activity, with an onset in infancy.

name: Growth delay-hydrocephaly-lung hypoplasia syndrome
clinical_summary: Growth delay-hydrocephaly-lung hypoplasia syndrome is a rare and complex disorder characterized by antenatal growth retardation, pulmonary hypoplasia, and hydrocephaly. This condition is associated with multiple congenital anomalies, including micrognathia, omphalocele, aqueductal stenosis, and intestinal malrotation.

name: Delayed speech-facial asymmetry-strabismus-ear lobe creases syndrome
clinical_summary: Delayed speech-facial asymmetry-strabismus-ear lobe creases syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by a combination of delayed speech, facial asymmetry, and strabismus, often accompanied by other symptoms such as hypertelorism and low-set ears. The condition is known to be present at birth or during early childhood.

name: Intellectual disability-cataracts-calcified pinnae-myopathy syndrome
clinical_summary: This rare disease is characterized by a combination of intellectual disability, cataracts, and calcified pinnae, presenting in children and infants. It is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern and exhibits various associated symptoms including hypogonadism, abnormal palate morphology, hydrocephalus, and conductive hearing impairment.

name: X-linked intellectual disability-hypogonadism-ichthyosis-obesity-short stature syndrome
clinical_summary: This rare X-linked disorder is characterized by intellectual disability, neonatal/infantile onset of symptoms, and clinical features including posteriorly rotated ears, cryptorchidism, and strabismus. Affected males inherit the disorder through an X-linked recessive pattern, often presenting with additional characteristics such as hypogonadism and ichthyosis.

name: Intellectual disability-myopathy-short stature-endocrine defect syndrome
clinical_summary: Intellectual disability-myopathy-short stature-endocrine defect syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a complex combination of symptoms, including hypogonadotropic hypogonadism, facial abnormalities, myopia, and skeletal abnormalities. This disorder presents with these features from birth, but its pattern of inheritance is currently unknown.

name: Intellectual disability, Buenos-Aires type
clinical_summary: Intellectual disability, Buenos-Aires type is a rare disease characterized by a distinct set of physical anomalies including high palate, microcephaly, and mandibular prognathia presenting at neonatal age. The inheritance pattern of this condition is unknown.

name: Intellectual disability, Wolff type
clinical_summary: Intellectual disability, Wolff type is a rare disease characterized by a neonatal onset, presenting with physical malformations such as inguinal hernia and orofacial clefts. The clinical manifestations may include other congenital anomalies such as hypogonadism and facial abnormalities.

name: Rhizomelic syndrome, Urbach type
clinical_summary: Rhizomelic syndrome, Urbach type, is a rare disorder characterized by distinct craniofacial abnormalities, including microcephaly, wide anterior fontanel, and micrognathia, evident from the neonatal period. Key physical features include these facial abnormalities, an abnormal tongue, short neck, and acne.

name: Schneckenbecken dysplasia
clinical_summary: Schneckenbecken dysplasia is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by antenatal onset, including cryptorchidism, macrocephaly, dolichocephaly, and skeletal anomalies such as short ribs and short neck, often accompanied by facial features like malar flattening.

name: Autoimmune polyendocrinopathy type 2
clinical_summary: Autoimmune polyendocrinopathy type 2 is a rare disease characterized by a combination of autoimmune disorders, including thyroid gland abnormalities, Hashimoto thyroiditis, celiac disease, primary adrenal insufficiency, Graves disease, and type I diabetes mellitus. It typically affects adults.

name: Satoyoshi syndrome
clinical_summary: Satoyoshi syndrome is a rare disease characterized by a combination of physical abnormalities, including abnormal humerus morphology, uterine and ovarian abnormalities, microcephaly, and potential amenorrhea. It manifests in various age groups including childhood, adolescence, and adulthood.

name: Ruvalcaba syndrome
clinical_summary: Ruvalcaba syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by distinct facial features, including a high forehead, convex nasal ridge, narrow mouth, and thin vermilion border, typically presenting during infancy or at birth. Additional findings may include cryptorchidism and microcephaly.

name: Senior-Loken syndrome
clinical_summary: Senior-Loken syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by progressive kidney disease (nephronophthisis) and visual impairment, often manifesting in infancy, childhood, adolescence, or adulthood. Common features include global developmental delay and hypertension in addition to renal and ophthalmological symptoms.

name: Nijmegen breakage syndrome
clinical_summary: Nijmegen breakage syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a range of congenital physical abnormalities including microcephaly, abnormal facial features, and hearing impairments. Symptoms typically manifest either before birth, in infancy, or at birth.

name: Sclerosteosis
key_symptoms: Curved distal phalanges of the hand
clinical_summary: Sclerosteosis is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a range of symptoms including tall stature, sensorineural hearing impairment, ptosis, and abnormal cortical bone morphology. Common physical features in children with sclerosteosis also include unusual nose abnormalities and 2-3 finger syndactyly.

name: Arginine vasopressin resistance-intracranial calcification-short stature-facial dysmorphism syndrome
clinical_summary: Arginine vasopressin resistance-intracranial calcification-short stature-facial dysmorphism syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by distinctive facial features and intellectual disability, typically presenting in infancy. Clinical manifestations include micrognathia, conductive hearing impairment, and delayed puberty.

name: Autosomal dominant spondylocostal dysostosis
clinical_summary: Autosomal dominant spondylocostal dysostosis is a rare condition characterized by intrauterine growth retardation, scoliosis, vertebral segmentation defect, severe short stature, and notable craniofacial features, particularly a prominent occiput and wide nasal bridge. The condition is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern.

name: Omphalocele syndrome, Shprintzen-Goldberg type
clinical_summary: Omphalocele syndrome, Shprintzen-Goldberg type, is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a complex set of symptoms, predominantly affecting the facial structure and muscle tone. It presents in neonates with symptoms including thin upper lip vermilion, Downslanted palpebral fissures, and significant developmental delays.

name: Septo-optic dysplasia spectrum
inheritance: Autosomal dominant, Autosomal recessive, Multigenic/multifactorial, Not applicable
clinical_summary: Septo-optic dysplasia spectrum is a rare condition characterized by visual impairment, optic nerve hypoplasia, septo-optic dysplasia, and associated anomalies such as cryptorchidism and cleft palate. It presents antenatally, in infancy, or at birth with symptoms potentially including strabismus.

name: Subaortic stenosis-short stature syndrome
clinical_summary: Subaortic stenosis-short stature syndrome is a rare disease characterized by a combination of cardiac and skeletal abnormalities, including subvalvular aortic stenosis and short stature. It presents in the neonatal period with distinctive features such as anteverted nares and abnormal vocal cord morphology.

name: Holoprosencephaly-radial heart renal anomalies syndrome
key_symptoms: Atresia of the external auditory canal
clinical_summary: Holoprosencephaly-radial heart renal anomalies syndrome is a rare condition characterized by multiple severe congenital anomalies, including microcephaly, holoprosencephaly, and orofacial clefts. The condition presents with various developmental abnormalities, such as atresia of the external auditory canal, abnormality of the outer ear, and aplasia of the nose.

name: Deafness-epiphyseal dysplasia-short stature syndrome
clinical_summary: Deafness-epiphyseal dysplasia-short stature syndrome is a rare condition characterized by hearing impairment, developmental abnormalities including inguinal hernia, and distinctive facial features. The syndrome manifests in infancy with symptoms persisting into childhood.

name: Fountain syndrome
lab_findings: no laboratory findings are established for fountain syndrome.
clinical_summary: Fountain syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by thick lower lip vermilion, coarse facial features, facial edema, a round face, sensorineural hearing impairment, and brachydactyly. Symptoms typically present in infancy or at birth.

name: Conductive deafness-ptosis-skeletal anomalies syndrome
key_symptoms: Atresia of the external auditory canal
clinical_summary: Conductive deafness-ptosis-skeletal anomalies syndrome is a rare disease characterized by conductive hearing impairment, ptosis, and various physical anomalies including abnormal palate morphology, epicanthus, and narrow nasal bridge. Atresia of the external auditory canal is also associated with the syndrome, with no established pattern of inheritance.

name: Deafness-craniofacial syndrome
clinical_summary: Deafness-craniofacial syndrome is a rare disease characterized by a complex array of symptoms including abnormal dentition, sensorineural hearing impairment, and craniofacial abnormalities at birth. Common manifestations include facial asymmetry, short lingual frenulum, and abnormal palate morphology.

name: Proximal symphalangism
clinical_summary: Proximal symphalangism is a rare genetic disorder characterized by synostoses and deformities of various skeletal structures, notably the carpal and tarsal bones, along with sensorineural hearing impairment. It is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern, with symptoms typically presenting in infancy or at birth.

name: Cleft lip/palate-ectodermal dysplasia syndrome
clinical_summary: Cleft lip/palate-ectodermal dysplasia syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a combination of congenital anomalies including cleft upper lip and micrognathia. The syndrome presents with variable oral-dental abnormalities, including abnormal dentition, and may also involve ectodermal anomalies such as microgenitalia.

name: Autosomal recessive hypohidrotic ectodermal dysplasia
clinical_summary: Autosomal recessive hypohidrotic ectodermal dysplasia is a rare disorder characterized by infantile onset symptoms including dry skin, fine hair, and abnormalities of the teeth and nails. It is an autosomal recessive condition.

name: Erythrokeratodermia variabilis
clinical_summary: Erythrokeratodermia variabilis is a rare genetic disorder characterized by the combination of skin rash, cutaneous photosensitivity, and hyperkeratosis, often presenting at infancy or neonatal stages. It may also be associated with microcephaly, weight loss, and hypermelanotic macules, with varying inheritance patterns of autosomal dominant and autosomal recessive.

name: SAPHO syndrome
clinical_summary: SAPHO syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by a combination of musculoskeletal and skin manifestations, including abnormal thorax morphology, vertebral column abnormalities, edema, acne, arthritis, and bone pain. It can affect individuals across various age groups, including adolescents, adults, and the elderly, with a complex etiology influenced by multiple genetic and environmental factors.

name: Lymphedema with yellow nails
clinical_summary: Lymphedema with yellow nails is a rare disorder characterized by symptoms including lymphedema, abnormal fingernail morphology, and systemic involvement such as sinusitis and respiratory complications like bronchiectasis and pleuritis. The age of onset can vary, occurring in adulthood, childhood, or at birth, though the inheritance pattern of the disorder is currently unknown.

name: Microcephalic osteodysplastic primordial dwarfism type II
clinical_summary: Microcephalic osteodysplastic primordial dwarfism type II is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by significant congenital abnormalities. Key symptoms include microcephaly, retrognathia, and sensorineural hearing impairment.

name: Autosomal dominant hypocalcemia
clinical_summary: Autosomal dominant hypocalcemia is a rare genetic disorder characterized by nephrocalcinosis and a range of neuropsychiatric symptoms. Affected individuals may exhibit atypical behavior, emotional lability, depression, anxiety, and other associated manifestations, typically arising at any age.

name: Adult-onset Still disease
clinical_summary: Adult-onset Still disease is a rare autoimmune disorder characterized by symptoms including a skin rash, arthritis, and fever, typically presenting in adults or elderly individuals. The condition is marked by a distinct set of symptoms including leukocytosis and joint swelling, often accompanied by pruritus.

name: Dracunculiasis
clinical_summary: Dracunculiasis is a rare disease characterized by a range of symptoms including skin rash, arthritis, limitation of joint mobility, and subcutaneous nodules. These symptoms can also be accompanied by gastrointestinal issues such as diarrhea.

name: Infantile Refsum disease
lab_findings: Very long chain fatty acid accumulation
clinical_summary: Infantile Refsum disease is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by progressive visual impairment and global developmental delay. Clinical features include visual impairment, rod-cone dystrophy, and failure to thrive.

name: TMEM70-related mitochondrial encephalo-cardio-myopathy
clinical_summary: TMEM70-related mitochondrial encephalo-cardio-myopathy is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a distinct combination of symptoms including cryptorchidism, hypospadias, wide mouth, microcephaly, retrognathia, and short philtrum, manifesting in infancy or neonatal period. This condition is associated with significant developmental and systemic abnormalities.

name: Miller-Dieker syndrome
clinical_summary: Miller-Dieker syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by abnormal facial features, including an abnormality of the upper lip, epicanthus, high forehead, and anteverted nares, associated with seizures and brain malformation (lissencephaly). It is inherited in an autosomal dominant manner and presents antenatally or at birth.

name: X-linked lissencephaly with abnormal genitalia
clinical_summary: X-linked lissencephaly with abnormal genitalia is a rare genetic disorder characterized by neonatal-onset symptoms including cryptorchidism, ambiguous genitalia, microcephaly, hypohidrosis, intellectual disability, and seizures. It follows an X-linked recessive inheritance pattern in affected males.

name: Kyphoscoliotic Ehlers-Danlos syndrome due to lysyl hydroxylase 1 deficiency
clinical_summary: Kyphoscoliotic Ehlers-Danlos syndrome due to lysyl hydroxylase 1 deficiency is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by early-onset osteopenia and osteoporosis, accompanied by skin fragility and susceptibility to bruising. This condition typically manifests in infancy or at birth, with symptoms including hyperextensible skin, atypical scarring, and fragile skin.

name: Hypermobile Ehlers-Danlos syndrome
clinical_summary: Hypermobile Ehlers-Danlos syndrome is a rare connective tissue disorder characterized by joint hypermobility, hyperextensible skin, and potential for joint dislocation, often resulting in functional impairment. It typically exhibits variable inheritance patterns, including autosomal dominant and autosomal recessive.

name: Vascular Ehlers-Danlos syndrome
clinical_summary: Vascular Ehlers-Danlos syndrome is a rare disease characterized by a combination of multiple congenital anomalies, including gastrointestinal and urological defects, and facial abnormalities. It presents in infancy or at birth.

name: Pseudoxanthoma elasticum
clinical_summary: Pseudoxanthoma elasticum is a rare disease characterized by thickened nuchal skin fold, retinopathy, and angioid streaks of the fundus, often accompanied by excessive wrinkled skin and abnormality of the skin. It may occur at any age and follows an autosomal recessive inheritance pattern.

name: Isolated Klippel-Feil syndrome
inheritance: Autosomal dominant, Autosomal recessive, Not applicable
clinical_summary: Isolated Klippel-Feil syndrome is characterized by an abnormality of the vertebral column, often presenting with facial asymmetry, a webbed neck, short neck, and low posterior hairline due to cervical fusion at C2/C3. The condition may be present at birth (antenatal) or appear in infancy or neonatal stages.

name: Larsen syndrome
clinical_summary: Larsen syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a combination of facial and skeletal abnormalities, including malar flattening, hypertelorism, and large joint dislocations. It is an autosomal dominant condition, typically presenting antenatally or during infancy.

name: Cutaneous small vessel vasculitis
clinical_summary: Cutaneous small vessel vasculitis is a rare disease characterized by various skin manifestations, including cutis marmorata, purpura, skin rash, and urticaria. It is often associated with systemic symptoms such as fever and recurrent skin infections.

name: Kindler epidermolysis bullosa
clinical_summary: Kindler epidermolysis bullosa is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by severe oral manifestations including gingivitis, carious teeth, abnormality of dental enamel, and periodontitis, often occurring in infancy or neonatal period. Additionally, patients may experience extra oral symptoms such as palmoplantar keratoderma and cutaneous photosensitivity.

name: Reynolds syndrome
lab_findings: elevated sedimentation rate in blood tests
clinical_summary: Reynolds syndrome is a rare disease presenting with a range of symptoms including xerostomia, skin rash, pruritus, keratoconjunctivitis sicca, and arthritis, often accompanied by fever, in young adults. This condition is not considered to have a known familial inheritance pattern.

name: Criss-cross heart
clinical_summary: Criss-cross heart is a rare congenital heart defect characterized by cyanosis and multiple structural abnormalities including a ventricular septal defect, abnormal mitral valve and pulmonic stenosis, often accompanied by transposition of the great arteries. This condition typically presents in infancy or the neonatal period with unknown inheritance.

name: Antisynthetase syndrome
clinical_summary: Antisynthetase syndrome is a rare autoimmune disease characterized by muscle weakness, respiratory insufficiency, and pulmonary fibrosis. It typically affects adults and the elderly, often presenting with additional symptoms such as myalgia and autoimmunity.

name: Babesiosis
clinical_summary: Babesiosis is a rare disease characterized by jaundice, hyperhidrosis, splenomegaly, thrombocytopenia, hemolytic anemia, and leukopenia. It affects individuals of all ages regardless of inheritance.

name: Vogt-Koyanagi-Harada disease
clinical_summary: Vogt-Koyanagi-Harada disease is a rare condition characterized by symptoms including abnormal eyelash and eyebrow morphology, vitiligo, and hypopigmented skin patches. It is a multigenic/multifactorial disorder affecting all ages.

name: CINCA syndrome
clinical_summary: CINCA syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by the onset of macrocephaly, hearing loss, and visual disturbances in infancy or the neonatal period. Symptoms may include sensorineural hearing impairment, visual impairment, proptosis, and pseudopapilledema.

name: Malakoplakia
clinical_summary: Malakoplakia is a rare disease characterized by urinary symptoms, including urinary urgency and hesitancy, as well as proteinuria and hematuria, often presenting in adults. It may also be associated with other symptoms such as abnormal menstrual cycles and skin rashes.

name: Lymphangioleiomyomatosis
lab_findings: Abnormal morphology of female internal genitalia
clinical_summary: Lymphangioleiomyomatosis is a rare disease characterized by a restrictive ventilatory defect and symptoms including abdominal pain, dyspnea, and emphysema, often presenting in adult females.

name: Sézary syndrome
clinical_summary: Sézary syndrome is a rare skin condition characterized by dry skin, severe itchiness (pruritus), widespread redness (erythroderma), and lymphoma. Abnormal T cell morphology is a hallmark of this disease, typically manifesting in adults.

name: Classic mycosis fungoides
clinical_summary: Classic mycosis fungoides is a rare adult-onset dermatological condition characterized by dry skin, eczematoid dermatitis, skin rash, and pruritus, potentially progressing to lymphoma. Its development is influenced by multigenic and multifactorial factors.

name: Hyperimmunoglobulinemia D with periodic fever
clinical_summary: Hyperimmunoglobulinemia D with periodic fever is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by recurrent fever, urticaria, arthritis, diarrhea, abdominal pain, and migraine symptoms. It typically starts in infancy.

name: Vitamin B12-responsive methylmalonic acidemia
clinical_summary: Vitamin B12-responsive methylmalonic acidemia is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by intellectual disability, hypotonia, and global developmental delay. It often presents in childhood with symptoms including lethargy, coma, and failure to thrive.

name: Sneddon syndrome
clinical_summary: Sneddon syndrome is a rare condition characterized by a combination of cutis marmorata, thromboembolic stroke, and cerebral cortical atrophy. It most commonly presents in adults with a range of symptoms including atypical behavior and headache.

name: Bardet-Biedl syndrome
clinical_summary: Bardet-Biedl syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a combination of physical and developmental abnormalities, including cone/cone-rod dystrophy and childhood-onset obesity. It affects individuals from birth onwards, presenting with symptoms such as truncal obesity, neurodevelopmental delay, and genitourinary system abnormalities in an autosomal recessive or oligogenic inheritance pattern.

name: Stüve-Wiedemann syndrome
clinical_summary: Stüve-Wiedemann syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by various symptoms including abnormal eye appearances, skeletal abnormalities, and unusual perspiratory patterns. This autosomal recessive condition typically manifests antenatally or in the neonatal period.

name: Leber congenital amaurosis
clinical_summary: Leber congenital amaurosis is a rare disease characterized by severely reduced visual acuity, abnormal retinal pigmentation, and optic disc abnormalities, often presenting in infancy. It is inherited in an autosomal dominant or autosomal recessive pattern, with variable electroretinogram results and potential development of cataracts, particularly in individuals with hypermetropia.

name: Xeroderma pigmentosum
clinical_summary: Xeroderma pigmentosum is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by multiple congenital anomalies and sensorineural hearing impairment. Symptoms include a range of physical abnormalities such as cryptorchidism, hypogonadism, and keratitis.

name: Kallmann syndrome
inheritance: Autosomal dominant, Autosomal recessive, Multigenic/multifactorial, X-linked recessive
clinical_summary: Kallmann syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by hypogonadotropic hypogonadism, anosmia, and delayed puberty. It presents with symptoms such as micropenis, decreased fertility, and anterior hypopituitarism.

name: Laron syndrome
clinical_summary: Laron syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by micrognathia, high forehead, delayed eruption of teeth, and microdontia, among endocrine abnormalities. It typically manifests in infancy or neonatal stage and is associated with delayed puberty.

name: Hydrocephaly-low insertion umbilicus syndrome
clinical_summary: Hydrocephaly-low insertion umbilicus syndrome is a rare condition characterized by neonatal onset, featuring multiple congenital abnormalities including communicating hydrocephalus, abnormal thorax morphology, and Tetralogy of Fallot. Associated physical features include an abnormal urinary system, epicanthus, and wide nose.

name: Rhizomelic dysplasia, Patterson-Lowry type
lab_findings: Abnormal form of the vertebral bodies
clinical_summary: Rhizomelic dysplasia, Patterson-Lowry type is a rare disease characterized by distinct facial features and limb abnormalities, including epicanthus, mandibular prognathia, wide nose, depressed nasal ridge, brachydactyly, and coxa vara. Symptoms typically present in childhood or infancy.

name: Diprosopus
key_symptoms: Non-midline cleft of the upper lip
clinical_summary: Diprosopus is a rare disease characterized by multiple facial abnormalities, including cleft palate, nose, face, eye, and vision abnormalities, which present prenatally. Cardiovascular involvement is also noted, with an abnormal cardiac septum morphology.

name: Mixed connective tissue disease
key_symptoms: Edema of the dorsum of hands
clinical_summary: Mixed connective tissue disease is a rare disorder characterized by a combination of symptoms such as fever, pleuritis, arthralgia, and positive rheumatoid factor in addition to circulating antibody levels. Its onset typically affects individuals in their adolescent, adult, or childhood years, with a multifactorial inheritance pattern.

name: Narcolepsy type 1
clinical_summary: Narcolepsy type 1 is a rare disease characterized by a combination of excessive daytime sleepiness and various neurological symptoms, including attention deficit hyperactivity disorder, restless legs, and non-rapid eye movement parasomnia. It typically has an onset in adolescent or adult years, with the exact inheritance pattern remaining unknown.

name: Propionic acidemia
clinical_summary: Propionic acidemia is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by intellectual disability, global developmental delay, and life-threatening metabolic crises including hypoglycemia and hyperammonemia. Affected individuals typically exhibit symptoms at infancy or neonatally, also presenting with organic aciduria and constipation.

name: Rhizomelic chondrodysplasia punctata
clinical_summary: Rhizomelic chondrodysplasia punctata is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by multiple systemic abnormalities, including skeletal, ocular, and cutaneous manifestations. It is evident in individuals from an early gestational stage onwards, manifesting as microcephaly, cataracts, dry skin, and skeletal abnormalities such as abnormal metaphysis morphology.

name: Cartilage-hair hypoplasia
clinical_summary: Cartilage-hair hypoplasia is a rare, congenital disorder characterized by physical anomalies, including congenital eye, head, and skeletal abnormalities. It is an autosomal recessive condition with manifestations evident in the neonatal period and presenting a range of severe systemic complications.

name: Essential thrombocythemia
clinical_summary: Essential thrombocythemia is a rare disease characterized by abnormal blood clotting and bleeding tendencies, primarily due to thrombocytosis. It can present with various symptoms, including abnormal bleeding, thrombotic events, and abnormalities in thrombocytes.

name: Smith-Lemli-Opitz syndrome
clinical_summary: Smith-Lemli-Opitz syndrome is a rare disease characterized by distinctive physical features and intellectual disability. The condition typically presents with symptoms such as microcephaly, intellectual disability, and hypotonia beginning in utero, infancy, or at birth.

name: Von Willebrand disease
clinical_summary: Von Willebrand disease is a rare disorder characterized by abnormal platelet function and reduced von Willebrand factor activity leading to coagulation and thrombocyte abnormalities. It is typically inherited in an autosomal dominant or autosomal recessive pattern and presents with symptoms such as menorrhagia and epistaxis across all ages.

name: Infantile myofibromatosis
inheritance: Autosomal dominant, Autosomal recessive, Not applicable
clinical_summary: Infantile myofibromatosis is a rare disease characterized by a range of systemic abnormalities including gingival fibromatosis, abnormal thorax and skull morphology, and chondrocalcinosis. It presents at various stages of development from antenatal to neonatal, and its inheritance pattern may be autosomal dominant or autosomal recessive.

name: Intellectual disability-polydactyly-uncombable hair syndrome
clinical_summary: Intellectual disability-polydactyly-uncombable hair syndrome is a rare condition characterized by a combination of intellectual disability, polydactyly, and uncombable hair, although uncombable hair details are not provided in this list. Symptoms include neonatal onset of cryptorchidism, abnormal palate morphology, mandibular prognathia, micrognathia, and conductive hearing impairment.

name: Familial cold urticaria
clinical_summary: Familial cold urticaria is a rare disease characterized by recurrent cold-induced urticaria, often accompanied by other symptoms including hyperhidrosis, pruritus, arthritis, fever, nausea and vomiting. It typically presents at a young age and is inherited in an autosomal dominant manner.

name: Char syndrome
clinical_summary: Char syndrome is a rare autosomal dominant disorder characterized by distinct facial features, including a triangular mouth, everted lower lip vermilion, and malar flattening, among others, with an age of onset ranging from antenatal to infancy or neonatal periods.

name: Aceruloplasminemia
clinical_summary: Aceruloplasminemia is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by abnormality of the nervous system, hypochromic microcytic anemia, and abnormal enzyme/coenzyme activity. It is typically diagnosed in adults or the elderly, often presenting with elevated circulating ferritin concentration and refractory anemia.

name: Congenital cataracts-facial dysmorphism-neuropathy syndrome
clinical_summary: Congenital cataracts-facial dysmorphism-neuropathy syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by congenital cataracts, microphthalmia, nystagmus, sensory neuropathy, intellectual disability, and global developmental delay. Symptoms typically present in infancy or neonatal period.

name: Phelan-McDermid syndrome
clinical_summary: Phelan-McDermid syndrome is a rare disease characterized by distinct facial features and physical characteristics, including dolichocephaly, malar flattening, full cheeks, pointed chin, macrotia, and bulbous nose. It typically presents in infancy or neonatal stage.

name: Felty syndrome
clinical_summary: Felty syndrome is a rare condition characterized by arthritis, abnormal joint morphology, and limitations in joint mobility. Key features also include sinusitis, chronic otitis media, and a subcutaneous nodule.

name: Pyoderma gangrenosum
clinical_summary: Pyoderma gangrenosum is a rare disease characterized by atrophic scars, inflammation of the large intestine, and fever, often associated with rheumatoid arthritis and myelodysplasia. It can affect individuals across various age groups, including the adolescent, adult, child, and elderly populations.

name: Stevens-Johnson syndrome
clinical_summary: Stevens-Johnson syndrome is a rare and potentially life-threatening condition characterized by systemic symptoms including fever, nausea and vomiting, diarrhea, dysphagia, weight loss, and abnormal neutrophil counts. It can affect individuals of all ages.

name: Hypocomplementemic urticarial vasculitis
clinical_summary: Hypocomplementemic urticarial vasculitis is a rare condition characterized by renal insufficiency, proteinuria, conjunctivitis, uveitis, hematuria, and skin rash in adults and children, with autosomal recessive inheritance reported.

name: Adiposis dolorosa
clinical_summary: Adiposis dolorosa is a rare condition characterized by obesity and subcutaneous nodules, often presenting with psychiatric symptoms such as psychosis, depression, and anxiety. It primarily affects adults, with an autosomal dominant inheritance pattern.

name: Buerger disease
clinical_summary: Buerger disease is characterized by vasculitis, arterial thrombosis, sensory neuropathy, gangrene, skin ulcers, and acrocyanosis, primarily manifesting in adulthood. It is a non-hereditary condition.

name: Lathosterolosis
clinical_summary: Lathosterolosis is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a neonatal onset of multiple congenital anomalies, including horseshoe kidney, gingival overgrowth, high palate, microcephaly, epicanthus, and full cheeks. Manifestations of the disease typically occur at birth.

name: PFAPA syndrome
clinical_summary: PFAPA syndrome is a rare condition primarily affecting children and infants, characterized by a combination of symptoms including abnormal oral cavity morphology, weight loss, migraine, and lymphadenopathy, among others. The exact inheritance pattern of this condition is unknown.

name: Omenn syndrome
clinical_summary: Omenn syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a clinical presentation of skin manifestations including dry skin, erythroderma, and thickened skin in infants or neonates. It is often associated with symptoms such as failure to thrive, pruritus, and edema.

name: Schnitzler syndrome
clinical_summary: Schnitzler syndrome is a rare adult-onset disorder characterized by symptoms including a skin rash, urticaria, arthritis, splenomegaly, anemia, and fever. The cause of the disease is unknown and there is no established familial pattern.

name: X-linked creatine transporter deficiency
clinical_summary: X-linked creatine transporter deficiency is a rare genetic disorder characterized by delayed speech and language development, intellectual disability, and other symptoms such as autistic behavior and self-mutilation. It typically presents in childhood or infancy and is inherited in an X-linked recessive pattern.

name: Muenke syndrome
clinical_summary: Muenke syndrome is a rare autosomal dominant disorder characterized by severe craniosynostosis, leading to brachycephaly, malar flattening, and other distinct facial features. It is typically associated with sensorineural hearing impairment and visual abnormalities such as ptosis and proptosis, often present at birth.

name: Corpus callosum agenesis-intellectual disability-coloboma-micrognathia syndrome
clinical_summary: Corpus callosum agenesis-intellectual disability-coloboma-micrognathia syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by a combination of physical and developmental abnormalities, including cleft palate, hearing impairment, and intellectual disability, often presenting in infancy. It is inherited in an X-linked recessive manner, predominantly affecting males.

name: Spinal arteriovenous metameric syndrome
clinical_summary: Spinal arteriovenous metameric syndrome is a rare condition characterized by symptoms including abnormality of the vertebral column, sensory neuropathy, and hyperreflexia. It may also present with paraparesis, abnormality of the spinal cord, and the appearance of a nevus flammeus.

name: GRACILE syndrome
clinical_summary: GRACILE syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a distinct combination of symptoms, including hearing impairment, liver and kidney abnormalities, and intrauterine growth retardation, with an antenatal or neonatal age of onset. It presents with a unique constellation of features including cholestasis, hepatic steatosis, and renal Fanconi syndrome.

name: Lipodystrophy-intellectual disability-deafness syndrome
key_symptoms: Slender long bones with narrow diaphyses
clinical_summary: Lipodystrophy-intellectual disability-deafness syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by childhood onset of sensorineural hearing impairment, intellectual disability, and various physical development delays. Key manifestations include osteopenia, failure to thrive, and intrauterine growth retardation.

name: Microlissencephaly-micromelia syndrome
clinical_summary: Microlissencephaly-micromelia syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by infancy/early neonatal onset of physical anomalies including long philtrum, short neck, and skeletal abnormalities. It is also often associated with neurological symptoms such as hypoparathyroidism and seizures.

name: Craniolenticulosutural dysplasia
clinical_summary: Craniolenticulosutural dysplasia is a rare condition characterized by a combination of cranial and facial abnormalities, including wide mouth, thin vermilion border, large fontanelles, hypertelorism, and smooth philtrum, often presenting in infancy or neonatal stages. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive manner.

name: Zellweger-like syndrome without peroxisomal anomalies
lab_findings: elevated very long chain fatty acids (vldl-cholesterol)
clinical_summary: Zellweger-like syndrome without peroxisomal anomalies is a rare condition characterized by distinct facial features and neurological symptoms, typically presenting in childhood. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive or mitochondrial pattern.

name: Thiamine-responsive megaloblastic anemia syndrome
clinical_summary: Thiamine-responsive megaloblastic anemia syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disease characterized by childhood onset and symptoms including sensorineural hearing impairment, optic atrophy, and diabetes mellitus. The syndrome also presents with thrombocytopenia, lethargy, and pallor, with significant improvement seen with thiamine supplementation.

name: Blomstrand lethal chondrodysplasia
clinical_summary: Blomstrand lethal chondrodysplasia is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by severe fetal anomalies, including malar flattening, long philtrum, micrognathia, and other distinct facial features. This condition presents with antenatal or neonatal onset.

name: Braddock syndrome
lab_findings: i'm not aware of a disease called braddock syndrome. however, based on the provided symptoms, i couldn't find any match in my database.
if you are referring to brachydactyly-brachydactyly syndrome or another known syndrome, please let me know the correct term.
since information about braddock syndrome is scarce, i will not provide any lab findings.
clinical_summary: Braddock syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by a combination of significant physical anomalies, including unilateral renal agenesis, epicanthus, and micrognathia. It is typically identified at birth and is inherited in an autosomal recessive manner.

name: Potocki-Shaffer syndrome
clinical_summary: Potocki-Shaffer syndrome is a rare condition characterized by physical abnormalities present from infancy or early life, including micropenis, brachycephaly, and other distinct facial features. The inheritance pattern of Potocki-Shaffer syndrome is currently unknown.

name: Branchiogenic deafness syndrome
key_symptoms: Atresia of the external auditory canal
clinical_summary: Branchiogenic deafness syndrome is a rare congenital disorder characterized by a combination of congenital anomalies, including cleft palate, abnormal pinna morphology, and conductive/sensorineural hearing impairment. This condition is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern.

name: Lipoid proteinosis
clinical_summary: Lipoid proteinosis is a rare condition characterized by various cutaneous and mucosal abnormalities, including thickened skin, subcutaneous nodules, and abnormalities of the gingiva and tongue. The disease can present at any age and follows an autosomal recessive inheritance pattern.

name: Bullous pemphigoid
clinical_summary: Bullous pemphigoid is a rare autoimmune disease characterized by skin lesions and symptoms including eczematoid dermatitis, urticaria, and recurrent infections. The condition can occur at any age.

name: Relapsing polychondritis
clinical_summary: Relapsing polychondritis is a rare inflammatory disease characterized by recurring episodes of inflammation, primarily affecting cartilaginous structures, and may also involve other systems such as the eyes and ears. Symptoms include chondritis, arthritis, and large vessel vasculitis, among others.

name: Desmoid tumor
clinical_summary: Desmoid tumor is a rare disease characterized by a subcutaneous nodule with various associated symptoms including malabsorption, abdominal pain, and musculature abnormalities. The disease typically presents in adolescents and adults.

name: Muscle-eye-brain disease
clinical_summary: Muscle-eye-brain disease is a rare genetic disorder characterized by hydrocephalus, visual impairment, and other ocular abnormalities. It is typically inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern and manifests from before birth through early infancy or at birth.

name: Distal myopathy, Welander type
key_symptoms: Weakness of long finger extensor muscles
clinical_summary: Distal myopathy, Welander type is a rare autosomal dominant disorder characterized by adult-onset muscle weakness and atrophy, particularly in the intrinsic hand muscles and distal upper limb and foot dorsiflexors. It typically presents with myopathic abnormalities, muscle weakness, and clumsiness.

name: Congenital muscular dystrophy, Fukuyama type
clinical_summary: Fukuyama type congenital muscular dystrophy is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by symptoms that typically manifest in infancy. Key features include hydrocephalus, mask-like facies, and delayed speech and language development, accompanied by physical manifestations such as brachycephaly and pectus excavatum.

name: Reticular dysgenesis
clinical_summary: Reticular dysgenesis is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by severe hematologic and immunologic abnormalities presenting with failure to thrive, chronic otitis media, and hearing impairment from infancy. The disorder also often involves leukopenia and abnormal neutrophils, contributing to the overall severity of the condition.

name: Nodular non-suppurative panniculitis
clinical_summary: Nodular non-suppurative panniculitis is a rare disease characterized by subcutaneous nodules, associated with systemic symptoms including fever, nausea, vomiting, abdominal pain, weight loss, and edema in adults. It is not hereditary.

name: Naxos disease
clinical_summary: Naxos disease is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by the early onset of distinctive features, including palmoplantar keratoderma, woolly hair, and cardiomyopathy. Clinical manifestations begin in infancy or neonatal life and include paroxysmal ventricular tachycardia and vertigo.

name: Erdheim-Chester disease
clinical_summary: Erdheim-Chester disease is a rare condition characterized by a distinctive set of symptoms including hypogonadotropic hypogonadism, proptosis, and diabetes insipidus, typically presenting in adulthood. Additional manifestations may include abnormal metaphysis morphology, hyperhidrosis, and xanthelasma.

name: Polycythemia vera
clinical_summary: Polycythemia vera is a rare disease characterized by symptoms including gingival bleeding, tinnitus, epistaxis, hypertension, bruising susceptibility, and angina pectoris, typically presenting at any age. It does not follow a familial inheritance pattern.

name: Idiopathic acute eosinophilic pneumonia
clinical_summary: Idiopathic acute eosinophilic pneumonia is a rare condition characterized by fever, respiratory insufficiency, and abnormal eosinophil counts, presenting with pulmonary infiltrates and chest pain. This disease can affect individuals of all ages, with an unknown inheritance pattern.

name: Spontaneous periodic hypothermia
clinical_summary: Spontaneous periodic hypothermia is a rare disease characterized by symptoms of hyperhidrosis, pallor, seizure, ataxia, gait disturbance, and tremor. Its etiology and mode of inheritance remain unknown.

name: Reactive arthritis
clinical_summary: Reactive arthritis is characterized by symptoms including conjunctivitis, hyperkeratosis, arthritis, joint swelling, joint stiffness, and nail abnormalities. It primarily affects adolescents, adults, and the elderly of uncertain inheritance.

name: Tumor necrosis factor receptor 1 associated periodic syndrome
clinical_summary: Tumor necrosis factor receptor 1 associated periodic syndrome (TRAPS) is a rare, genetic disorder characterized by recurrent episodes of fever and multiple systemic symptoms, including skin rash, arthritis, and inflammation. It is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern and can affect individuals of any age, from infancy to adulthood.

name: Autosomal non-syndromic agammaglobulinemia
lab_findings: normal or low cd4+ cells count
clinical_summary: Autosomal non-syndromic agammaglobulinemia is a rare disease characterized by recurrent infections including sinusitis, chronic otitis media, conjunctivitis, and skin rash, often accompanied by failure to thrive.

name: Lymphedema-distichiasis syndrome
clinical_summary: Lymphedema-distichiasis syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by lower limb lymphedema, eyelid and eyelash abnormalities, and ocular symptoms. It is an autosomal dominant inherited condition that can affect individuals of all ages.

name: Waldenström macroglobulinemia
clinical_summary: Waldenström macroglobulinemia is a rare disease characterized by symptoms such as gingival bleeding, pallor, and normocytic anemia. It typically affects the elderly and involves a multifactorial inheritance pattern.

name: Alpha-N-acetylgalactosaminidase deficiency type 1
clinical_summary: Alpha-N-acetylgalactosaminidase deficiency type 1 is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by various systemic and neurological symptoms that manifest in infancy or neonatally, including hearing impairment, strabismus, nystagmus, optic atrophy, and autism. These symptoms are accompanied by sensory neuropathy.

name: Acute intermittent porphyria
clinical_summary: Acute intermittent porphyria is a rare, autosomal dominant disorder characterized by episodes of abdominal pain and abnormalities in urinary porphyrin metabolism. Symptomatic manifestations, including renal insufficiency, typically arise in adolescence or adulthood.

name: Galactose epimerase deficiency
clinical_summary: Galactose epimerase deficiency is a rare disease characterized by infancy-onset cataracts, jaundice, and intellectual disability, often accompanied by hypotonia, global developmental delay, and growth delay. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern, typically manifesting at birth.

name: Holocarboxylase synthetase deficiency
clinical_summary: Holocarboxylase synthetase deficiency is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a range of symptoms in childhood, infancy, or neonatal stages, including growth delay, weight loss, and neurological manifestations such as irritability, seizure, and hypotonia. Eye-related issues like keratoconjunctivitis may also be present.

name: Biotinidase deficiency
clinical_summary: Biotinidase deficiency is a rare metabolic disorder characterized by organic aciduria, metabolic ketoacidosis, and decreased biotinidase activity. It presents with a range of symptoms, including sensorineural hearing impairment, nervous system abnormalities, and skin rash, primarily affecting individuals who inherit the autosomal recessive trait.

name: L-2-hydroxyglutaric aciduria
clinical_summary: L-2-hydroxyglutaric aciduria is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by progressive severe intellectual disability, macrocephaly, and various neurological symptoms including seizures, infectious encephalitis, and atypical behavior. It presents typically in childhood.

name: Vitamin B12-unresponsive methylmalonic acidemia type mut-
clinical_summary: Vitamin B12-unresponsive methylmalonic acidemia type mut is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by intellectual disability and global developmental delay, often presenting in infancy or neonatally with symptoms including lethargy and coma.

name: ALG3-CDG
clinical_summary: ALG3-CDG is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by infancy-onset hypotonia, global developmental delay, and other severe systemic manifestations. Clinical features include abnormal cerebral morphology, recurrent infections, gastrointestinal abnormalities, and feeding difficulties.

name: Methylmalonic acidemia with homocystinuria, type cblD
clinical_summary: Methylmalonic acidemia with homocystinuria, type cblD is a rare inherited disorder characterized by a range of symptoms including intellectual disability, seizures, and global developmental delay. It is typically inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern, meaning it can affect individuals of all ages from infancy onwards.

name: Alpha-N-acetylgalactosaminidase deficiency type 2
clinical_summary: Alpha-N-acetylgalactosaminidase deficiency type 2 is a rare autosomal recessive disorder primarily affecting adults, manifesting with a range of symptoms including lip telangiectasia, hearing impairment, and hyperkeratosis. The condition is associated with coarse facial features, tinnitus, and lymphedema in affected individuals.

name: Congenital bile acid synthesis defect type 1
clinical_summary: Congenital bile acid synthesis defect type 1 is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by early onset symptoms, including jaundice and failure to thrive, which may lead to complications such as malabsorption and coagulopathy. This disorder presents in infancy or neonatally, often with associated biliary tract abnormalities and splenomegaly.

name: Trichorhinophalangeal syndrome type 1
clinical_summary: Trichorhinophalangeal syndrome type 1 is a rare disorder characterized by distinctive facial features, including a triangular face, long philtrum, micrognathia, macrotia, protruding ears, and a bulbous nose, often presenting in infancy or at birth. It is caused by an autosomal dominant inheritance pattern.

name: Gaucher disease type 1
clinical_summary: Gaucher disease type 1 is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by delayed puberty, growth delay, and skeletal manifestations including osteopenia and osteoporosis.

name: Gaucher disease type 2
clinical_summary: Gaucher disease type 2 is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by symptoms including strabismus, ophthalmoplegia, spasticity, encephalopathy, and dystonia. It typically has an early onset in infancy, often associated with splenomegaly.

name: Gaucher disease type 3
clinical_summary: Gaucher disease type 3 is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a range of symptoms including strabismus, ophthalmoplegia, encephalopathy, splenomegaly, hepatomegaly, and bone pain. It can affect individuals of any age, highlighting its variable and unpredictable progression.

name: Majeed syndrome
lab_findings: Abnormality of bone marrow cell morphology
clinical_summary: Majeed syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a combination of systemic inflammation and bone abnormalities in infancy or early childhood. Key features include systemic symptoms such as weight loss and fever, accompanied by bone-related symptoms such as osteomyelitis, bone pain, and metaphyseal irregularity.

name: Monosomy 9q22.3 syndrome
lab_findings: normal karyotype, with a deletion of part of chromosome 9
clinical_summary: Monosomy 9q22.3 syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by joint hypermobility, tall stature, and orofacial developmental abnormalities. Clinical features include a narrow mouth, orofacial cleft, trigonocephaly, and macrocephaly, typically present from infancy or neonatal life.

name: Brain malformation-congenital heart disease-postaxial polydactyly syndrome
clinical_summary: This rare disease, Brain malformation-congenital heart disease-postaxial polydactyly syndrome, is characterized by a subset of congenital anomalies, including renal hypoplasia, facial abnormalities, and other malformations. The exact age of onset is infancy or neonatal, with an unknown inheritance pattern.

name: Periodontal Ehlers-Danlos syndrome
clinical_summary: Periodontal Ehlers-Danlos syndrome is a rare disease characterized by joint hypermobility, periodontitis, and other distinct physical features such as gingival overgrowth and atrophic scars. It typically manifests in childhood and follows an autosomal dominant inheritance pattern.

name: B4GALT7-related spondylodysplastic Ehlers-Danlos syndrome
clinical_summary: B4GALT7-related spondylodysplastic Ehlers-Danlos syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive condition characterized by neonatal onset of symptoms including macrocephaly, epicanthus, thin skin, cutis laxa, and potentially other findings such as cryptorchidism and gingivitis. This condition presents at birth with a distinct combination of physical abnormalities.

name: X-linked Ehlers-Danlos syndrome
clinical_summary: X-linked Ehlers-Danlos syndrome is characterized by adolescent-onset joint hypermobility, musculoskeletal defects (including inguinal and umbilical hernias), and dermatological features such as thin and hyperextensible skin with bruising susceptibility. This condition exhibits an X-linked recessive inheritance pattern.

name: Focal facial dermal dysplasia type I
clinical_summary: Focal facial dermal dysplasia type I is a rare genetic disorder characterized by the presence of thick upper lip vermilion, bulbous nose, and other facial anomalies from birth. It is an autosomal dominant condition with antenatal or neonatal age of onset.

name: HJV or HAMP-related hemochromatosis
clinical_summary: HJV or HAMP-related hemochromatosis is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a range of systemic symptoms, including hypogonadism and diabetes mellitus. Clinical manifestations can occur at any age, from childhood to adulthood.

name: Developmental malformations-deafness-dystonia syndrome
clinical_summary: Developmental malformations-deafness-dystonia syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by distinct physical malformations and hearing impairment. It presents with features such as macroglossia, orofacial clefts, and sensorineural hearing impairment, typically following an autosomal dominant pattern of inheritance.

name: Mandibulofacial dysostosis-microcephaly syndrome
clinical_summary: Mandibulofacial dysostosis-microcephaly syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a distinct set of craniofacial abnormalities present at birth, including cleft palate and maxillary hypoplasia. It is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern and typically presents with these symptoms in the neonatal period.

name: Hypotrichosis-lymphedema-telangiectasia-renal defect syndrome
clinical_summary: Hypotrichosis-lymphedema-telangiectasia-renal defect syndrome is a rare condition primarily characterized by absent eyelashes, alopecia, and predominantly lower limb lymphedema. Onset typically occurs in infancy or neonatal, although the mode of inheritance is either autosomal dominant or autosomal recessive.

name: PAPA syndrome
clinical_summary: PAPA syndrome is a rare, autosomal dominant disorder characterized by the presence of acne, arthritis, limitation of joint mobility, fever, lymphadenopathy, and arthralgia. It typically presents in childhood with a range of systemic symptoms and musculoskeletal manifestations.

name: Rhabdoid tumor
clinical_summary: Rhabdoid tumor is a rare and aggressive disease characterized by a combination of significant symptoms including irritability, hematuria, hypertension, subcutaneous nodule, weight loss, and fever. It typically presents in infancy, childhood, or antenatal periods without a known inheritance pattern.

name: Diaphanospondylodysostosis
key_symptoms: Absent or minimally ossified vertebral bodies
clinical_summary: Diaphanospondylodysostosis is a rare disease characterized by severe skeletal abnormalities including missing ribs and absent or minimally ossified vertebral bodies, often leading to respiratory distress. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern and presents symptoms in the antenatal, infancy, and neonatal stages.

name: Wolman disease
clinical_summary: Wolman disease is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by symptoms of global developmental delay, hepatic failure, and splenomegaly, typically presenting in infancy or the neonatal period. Affected individuals often exhibit growth delay, ascites, and anemia.

name: Growth delay due to insulin-like growth factor I resistance
clinical_summary: Growth delay due to insulin-like growth factor I resistance is a rare condition characterized by distinctive facial features including an everted lower lip vermilion, thin vermilion border, microcephaly, smooth philtrum, wide nasal bridge, and broad nasal tip. It presents in infancy or neonatal period, and can exhibit autosomal dominant or autosomal recessive inheritance patterns.

name: Visceral neuropathy-brain anomalies-facial dysmorphism-developmental delay syndrome
clinical_summary: Visceral neuropathy-brain anomalies-facial dysmorphism-developmental delay syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a combination of visceral and neurological anomalies, as well as distinct facial features. Symptoms include multicystic kidney dysplasia, retrognathia, broad forehead, long philtrum, strabismus, and downslanted palpebral fissures, presenting at birth.

name: Familial thrombocytosis
clinical_summary: Familial thrombocytosis is a rare disease characterized by thrombocytosis and a predisposition to both arterial and venous thrombosis despite a tendency towards abnormal bleeding. The exact mode of inheritance may vary between autosomal dominant and X-linked recessive patterns.

name: Dentinogenesis imperfecta-short stature-hearing loss-intellectual disability syndrome
clinical_summary: Dentinogenesis imperfecta-short stature-hearing loss-intellectual disability syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by infancy symptoms including short philtrum, short stature, hearing loss, intellectual disability, and dental abnormalities. Affected individuals often exhibit delayed tooth eruption and distinct facial features, including a prominent nasal bridge.

name: Acrocapitofemoral dysplasia
clinical_summary: Acrocapitofemoral dysplasia is a rare autosomal recessive skeletal disorder characterized by distinctive skeletal features and delayed skeletal maturation, typically presenting in infancy or neonatally. The condition is associated with abnormalities such as coxa vara, flared iliac wings, and small to broad nails.

name: Iniencephaly
clinical_summary: Iniencephaly is a rare congenital disorder characterized by a combination of severe physical anomalies, including rhizomelia, spinal dysraphism, and holoprosencephaly. It typically presents in infancy or at birth with distinct facial and skeletal malformations.

name: Rhombencephalosynapsis
clinical_summary: Rhombencephalosynapsis is a rare congenital disorder characterized by a combination of macrocephaly, hydrocephalus, microretrognathia, and other craniofacial anomalies. Manifestations include a narrow mouth, hypertelorism, and anteverted nares.

name: Loeys-Dietz syndrome
lab_findings: absence of elastic fibers in medial layer
smooth muscle cell proliferation in media
clinical_summary: Loeys-Dietz syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by multiple cardiovascular and skeletal manifestations, including aortic aneurysm and dissection, patent ductus arteriosus, and arterial tortuosity. This disorder can be inherited in either an autosomal dominant or autosomal recessive pattern and typically presents antenatally, in infancy, or neonatally.

name: Megalencephaly-capillary malformation-polymicrogyria syndrome
clinical_summary: Megalencephaly-capillary malformation-polymicrogyria syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by macrocephaly, joint hypermobility, and other distinct craniofacial features. It presents antenatally or in the neonatal period and is not heritable.

name: Neonatal ichthyosis-sclerosing cholangitis syndrome
clinical_summary: Neonatal ichthyosis-sclerosing cholangitis syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive condition characterized by sparse eyelashes, jaundice, cholestasis, splenomegaly, sparse scalp hair, and sparse body hair. It presents at birth with symptoms.

name: CEDNIK syndrome
clinical_summary: CEDNIK syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by clinical features including microcephaly, long face, and intellectual disability. It is typically identified in infancy or neonatally.

name: Cerebrooculonasal syndrome
clinical_summary: Cerebrooculonasal syndrome is a rare autosomal dominant disorder characterized by a distinct combination of craniofacial abnormalities present from birth, including posteriorly rotated ears, brachycephaly, and anophthalmia. The condition features a wide range of congenital anomalies affecting the eyes, skull, and facial structure.

name: Lhermitte-Duclos disease
clinical_summary: Lhermitte-Duclos disease is a rare condition manifesting with a combination of macrocephaly, hydrocephalus, macroglossia, hand polydactyly, seizures, and ataxia. It typically presents in adults.

name: X-linked intellectual disability, Cabezas type
clinical_summary: X-linked intellectual disability, Cabezas type is a rare genetic disorder characterized by symptoms including inguinal hernia, wide mouth, short philtrum, abnormality of the earlobe, prominent nose, and short neck. It is inherited in an X-linked recessive pattern and typically manifests in childhood.

name: Deafness-intellectual disability syndrome, Martin-Probst type
clinical_summary: Deafness-intellectual disability syndrome, Martin-Probst type, is a rare X-linked recessive disorder characterized by multiple congenital anomalies and intellectual disability. Symptoms include physical malformations such as cryptorchidism, bifid scrotum, and wide mouth, often with associated renal abnormalities.

name: X-linked intellectual disability-hypogammaglobulinemia-progressive neurological deterioration syndrome
clinical_summary: This rare X-linked syndrome is characterized by intellectual disability, hypogammaglobulinemia, and progressive neurological deterioration, accompanied by distinct craniofacial features such as cleft palate and hypertelorism. Symptoms typically present in childhood.

name: X-linked intellectual disability-hypotonia-facial dysmorphism-aggressive behavior syndrome
lab_findings: elevated very long chain fatty acids
clinical_summary: This rare X-linked recessive syndrome is characterized by intellectual disability, hypotonia, facial dysmorphism, and aggressive behavior, with symptoms including an open mouth, microcephaly, long face, triangular face, and short chin. It typically presents in infancy or early childhood.

name: X-linked intellectual disability, Stevenson type
clinical_summary: Stevenson syndrome is a rare X-linked intellectual disability characterized by tall stature, unique facial features, and other distinguishing physical characteristics. It primarily affects males in childhood due to its X-linked recessive inheritance pattern.

name: X-linked neurodegenerative syndrome, Bertini type
clinical_summary: This X-linked neurodegenerative syndrome, Bertini type, presents with severe symptoms including macular degeneration, intellectual disability, and significant physical impairments such as ataxia, global developmental delay, and agenesis of corpus callosum in infants or neonates. It is inherited in an X-linked recessive pattern.

name: Fried syndrome
clinical_summary: Fried syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by distinct physical characteristics such as a high palate, coarse facial features, and a long face. It typically manifests in infancy or the neonatal period with a confirmed X-linked recessive mode of inheritance.

name: Systemic-onset juvenile idiopathic arthritis
clinical_summary: Systemic-onset juvenile idiopathic arthritis is a rare autoimmune disease characterized by symptoms such as skin rash, fever, and arthritis. It typically manifests in childhood with joint swelling, arthralgia, and fever.

name: Rheumatoid factor-positive polyarticular juvenile idiopathic arthritis
clinical_summary: Rheumatoid factor-positive polyarticular juvenile idiopathic arthritis is a rare disease characterized by abnormal circulating interleukin concentration, arthritis, and limitation of joint mobility in children. Its inheritance pattern and other underlying causes remain unknown.

name: Alport syndrome-intellectual disability-midface hypoplasia-elliptocytosis syndrome
clinical_summary: Alport syndrome-intellectual disability-midface hypoplasia-elliptocytosis syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by renal insufficiency, hearing impairment, and distinctive physical features including thin vermilion border and malar flattening. The condition is inherited in an X-linked recessive pattern.

name: Mesomelic dysplasia, Savarirayan type
clinical_summary: Mesomelic dysplasia, Savarirayan type is a rare skeletal disorder characterized by skeletal dysplasia, presenting with symptoms such as hip dislocation and flared radial metaphysis. It primarily affects individuals in infancy and neonatal stages.

name: IMAGe syndrome
clinical_summary: IMAGe syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a range of congenital malformations including genital abnormalities, hydronephrosis, cryptorchidism, and low-set ears. It typically presents with significant symptoms at infancy or neonatal stage.

name: Microcephalic osteodysplastic dysplasia, Saul-Wilson type
clinical_summary: Microcephalic osteodysplastic dysplasia, Saul-Wilson type is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by severe microcephaly and distinct craniofacial abnormalities including malar flattening and convex nasal ridge. Common complications include ocular abnormalities such as cataract and proptosis.

name: Spondylo-ocular syndrome
clinical_summary: Spondylo-ocular syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a range of symptoms including facial hypotonia, cataract, and retinal detachment. This autosomal recessive disorder typically presents in infancy or neonatally, often with features such as hypertelorism, short neck, and abnormal eyebrow morphology.

name: Dysspondyloenchondromatosis
clinical_summary: Dysspondyloenchondromatosis is a rare disease characterized by platyspondyly, joint dislocation, scoliosis, spondylometaphyseal dysplasia, and delayed skeletal maturation. It presents in infancy or neonatally and follows an autosomal dominant inheritance pattern.

name: Genitopatellar syndrome
clinical_summary: Genitopatellar syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by a combination of multicystic kidney dysplasia, cryptorchidism, and coarse facial features, typically presenting in childhood. The condition can be inherited in an autosomal dominant or autosomal recessive pattern.

name: Craniosynostosis-anal anomalies-porokeratosis syndrome
clinical_summary: Craniosynostosis-anal anomalies-porokeratosis syndrome is a rare condition characterized by a combination of craniosynostosis, hypospadias, and abnormal palate morphology. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern, typically presenting symptoms in infancy or neonatally.

name: Keutel syndrome
clinical_summary: Keutel syndrome is a rare condition characterized by distinct facial features, including a long face and sloping forehead, and various hearing-related issues, such as hearing impairment and recurrent otitis media. This disorder exhibits an autosomal recessive inheritance pattern and typically begins in childhood.

name: Fetal Gaucher disease
clinical_summary: Fetal Gaucher disease is a rare, autosomal recessive genetic disorder characterized by a combination of severe symptoms in infancy, including ichthyosis, high palate, anteverted nares, ectropion, hypotonia, and flexion contracture. These symptoms manifest in infancy or neonatal period.

name: Syndromic X-linked intellectual disability 7
clinical_summary: Syndromic X-linked intellectual disability 7 is a rare genetic disorder characterized by intellectual disability, hypogonadism, and physical abnormalities such as tooth malposition, tapered fingers, and muscle weakness. It typically presents with an X-linked recessive inheritance pattern.

name: X-linked intellectual disability, Armfield type
clinical_summary: X-linked intellectual disability, Armfield type, is a rare disease characterized by inguinal hernia, Cleft palate, Epicanthus, Short philtrum, Broad forehead, and Micrognathia. It manifests in infancy or neonatal period and is inherited in an X-linked recessive manner.

name: X-linked intellectual disability, Cantagrel type
clinical_summary: Cantagrel type X-linked intellectual disability is a rare genetic disorder characterized by intellectual disability and distinct physical and behavioral features, including a shawl scrotum, short philtrum, and esotropia. It typically presents in infancy and is inherited in an X-linked recessive pattern.

name: Christianson syndrome
clinical_summary: Christianson syndrome is a rare X-linked recessive disorder characterized by symptoms including drooling, EEG abnormality, hyperkinetic movements, and secondary microcephaly, typically beginning in infancy or neonatal period. Affected individuals often experience feeding difficulties in infancy and may have aplasia/hypoplasia of the corpus callosum.

name: KDM5C-related syndromic X-linked intellectual disability
clinical_summary: KDM5C-related syndromic X-linked intellectual disability is a rare genetic disorder characterized by severe intellectual disability, aggressive behavior, delayed speech and language development, and other distinctive physical features. It typically presents in childhood with symptoms including cryptorchidism, hypoplasia of the maxilla, and patchy alopecia.

name: X-linked intellectual disability-cubitus valgus-dysmorphism syndrome
clinical_summary: X-linked intellectual disability-cubitus valgus-dysmorphism syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by symptoms such as microcephaly, malar flattening, and other distinct facial features. It follows an X-linked recessive inheritance pattern, typically affecting males from infancy onward.

name: MEHMO syndrome
clinical_summary: MEHMO syndrome is a rare X-linked recessive disease characterized by antenatal or neonatal onset of symptoms including cryptorchidism, micropenis, microcephaly, and distinctive facial features. These features include full cheeks, round face, and sloping forehead.

name: BRESEK syndrome
lab_findings: i couldn't find any information on breastek syndrome.
clinical_summary: BRESEK syndrome is a rare X-linked dominant disorder manifested by multiple congenital anomalies in childhood, including cryptorchidism, vesicoureteral reflux, renal abnormalities, and cleft palate. It is characterized by various systemic malformations, which may be complicated by the early onset of hydrocephalus.

name: X-linked intellectual disability, Shashi type
clinical_summary: X-linked intellectual disability, Shashi type, is a rare condition characterized by intellectual disability and distinct facial features. It presents in infancy or the neonatal period and is inherited in an X-linked recessive pattern, affecting primarily males.

name: Oculocutaneous albinism type 4
clinical_summary: Oculocutaneous albinism type 4 is characterized by symptoms including nystagmus, reduced visual acuity, and photophobia, typically presenting in infancy or at birth. This autosomal recessive disorder is also associated with hypoplasia of the fovea, abnormal retinal pigmentation, and skin hypopigmentation.

name: Oculocutaneous albinism type 1B
clinical_summary: Oculocutaneous albinism type 1B is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by symptoms of visual impairment, photophobia, and strabismus, presenting in infancy or at birth. Associated abnormalities include abnormal optic nerve morphology and nystagmus.

name: Griscelli syndrome type 2
clinical_summary: Griscelli syndrome type 2 is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by symptoms including jaundice, splenomegaly, and premature graying of hair. Patients often present with hematologic abnormalities such as decreased total neutrophil count and pancytopenia.

name: Griscelli syndrome type 1
clinical_summary: Griscelli syndrome type 1 is a rare, autosomal recessive disease characterized by a combination of ophthalmological and neurological symptoms that typically manifest in infancy or childhood. Symptoms may include retinopathy, nystagmus, diplopia, intellectual disability, seizures, and ataxia.

name: LUMBAR syndrome
lab_findings: i'm unsure of any specific lab findings for lumbard syndrome due to its rarity and absence in medical literature.
however, i can suggest possible urinary tract issues due to renal duplication and vesicoureteral reflux:
clinical_summary: LUMBAR syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by a combination of neonatal or infantile abnormalities affecting the genitourinary system, including cryptorchidism, bifid scrotum, and urogenital anomalies. The condition also often involves lower urinary tract anomalies, such as renal duplication and vesicoureteral reflux.

name: Desmoplastic small round cell tumor
clinical_summary: Desmoplastic small round cell tumor is a rare pediatric and adult malignancy characterized by gastrointestinal symptoms including weight loss, nausea, vomiting, abdominal pain, hepatomegaly, and ileus as well as peritoneal abnormalities. This disease typically presents in adolescence or adulthood, with no apparent familial inheritance pattern.

name: Megalencephaly-polymicrogyria-postaxial polydactyly-hydrocephalus syndrome
clinical_summary: Megalencephaly-polymicrogyria-postaxial polydactyly-hydrocephalus syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by a combination of macrocephaly, hypertelorism, high forehead, and hydrocephalus.
