It typically presents in infancy or the neonatal period with a narrow mouth and telecanthus.

name: Congenital primary aphakia
key_symptoms: Aplasia/Hypoplasia affecting the anterior segment of the eye
Anterior synechiae of the anterior chamber
clinical_summary: Congenital primary aphakia is a rare disease characterized by abnormalities including microphthalmia, developmental glaucoma, congenital aphakia, and retinal detachment. It typically manifests in infancy or at birth with an autosomal recessive inheritance pattern.

name: Scrub typhus
clinical_summary: Scrub typhus is a rare disease characterized by symptoms including photophobia, hyperhidrosis, and a skin rash. It primarily presents with systemic symptoms such as lethargy, fever, nausea, and vomiting.

name: Severe achondroplasia-developmental delay-acanthosis nigricans syndrome
clinical_summary: Severe achondroplasia-developmental delay-acanthosis nigricans syndrome is a rare autosomal dominant disorder characterized by a combination of developmental delays, microcephaly, and various physical abnormalities. Symptoms include acanthosis nigricans, hypoplasia of the corpus callosum, and femoral bowing.

name: Platyspondylic dysplasia, Torrance type
key_symptoms: Hypoplasia of the capital femoral epiphysis
clinical_summary: Platyspondylic dysplasia, Torrance type is a rare autosomal dominant skeletal dysplasia characterized by narrow chest, platyspondyly, and abnormalities in carpal morphology, often presenting in infancy. Affected individuals may also exhibit short feet and micromelia.

name: ALG8-CDG
clinical_summary: ALG8-CDG is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by symptoms of nervous system abnormality, hypotonia, and various physical anomalies such as abnormal facial shape and eye abnormalities. It can present prenatally or postnatally, with notable features including cataracts and edema.

name: Congenital ichthyosiform erythroderma
clinical_summary: Congenital ichthyosiform erythroderma is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by severe skin and other systemic abnormalities, with symptoms manifesting in infancy or neonatal period. Affected individuals may experience a range of complications, including hearing impairment, keratitis, and hypohidrosis.

name: Autosomal dominant generalized epidermolysis bullosa simplex, severe form
clinical_summary: Autosomal dominant generalized epidermolysis bullosa simplex, severe form is a rare, neonatally-onset disease characterized by fragile skin and generalized abnormal blistering. This condition exhibits features such as palmoplantar keratoderma, nail dystrophy, and erythema.

name: Intermediate generalized junctional epidermolysis bullosa
clinical_summary: Intermediate generalized junctional epidermolysis bullosa is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by multiple skin manifestations including abnormal pigmentation, milia, aplasia cutis congenita, atrophic scars, anonychia, and anemia, typically presenting at birth.

name: Junctional epidermolysis bullosa with pyloric atresia
clinical_summary: Junctional epidermolysis bullosa with pyloric atresia is a rare congenital disorder characterized by abnormal blistering of the skin and intestinal atresia, often presenting symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal distention. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern.

name: Epidermolysis bullosa simplex with mottled pigmentation
key_symptoms: Mixed hypo- and hyperpigmentation of the skin
clinical_summary: Epidermolysis bullosa simplex with mottled pigmentation is a rare genetic disorder characterized by skin fragility and non-scarring blistering in infancy or at birth. This autosomal dominant condition is characterized by abnormal skin pigmentation and junctional splits in the skin.

name: Localized epidermolysis bullosa simplex
clinical_summary: Localized epidermolysis bullosa simplex is a rare disorder characterized by non-scarring blistering of the skin due to skin fragility. It typically affects individuals from childhood onward and is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern.

name: Hermansky-Pudlak syndrome
clinical_summary: Hermansky-Pudlak syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by early onset symptoms including nystagmus, hypopigmentation, and immunodeficiency. Affected individuals typically present in infancy or at birth with signs of abnormal bleeding, decreased neutrophil count, and partial albinism.

name: Oculocutaneous albinism type 1A
clinical_summary: Oculocutaneous albinism type 1A is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by visual impairment and hypopigmentation of the skin. It presents in infancy or at birth with symptoms including photophobia, nystagmus, and abnormal optic nerve morphology.

name: Oculocutaneous albinism type 2
clinical_summary: Oculocutaneous albinism type 2 is a rare condition characterized by photophobia, hypopigmentation of the skin, and nystagmus, often presenting with blue irides and heterochromia iridis. This disorder typically presents in infancy or at birth with an autosomal recessive inheritance pattern.

name: Severe intellectual disability-epilepsy-anal anomalies-distal phalangeal hypoplasia
clinical_summary: This rare disease is characterized by severe intellectual disability, epilepsy, anal anomalies, and distal phalangeal hypoplasia, typically presenting with distinct facial features and seizures in infancy or at birth. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern.

name: Cloacal exstrophy
clinical_summary: Cloacal exstrophy is a rare congenital anomaly characterized by a combination of gastrointestinal, urinary, and skeletal malformations. Affected individuals typically present with multiple anomalies, including abnormal genitalia, vesicoureteral reflux, and spinal abnormalities.

name: Hamel cerebro-palato-cardiac syndrome
lab_findings: i couldn't find any information about hamel cerebro-palato-cardiac syndrome, but a rare syndrome with similar symptoms is hamelman syndrome.
the findings below are for hammelman syndrome, however note that it's possible this was the only similar syndrome they could find, the actual disease may have different lab findings:
deletion or translocation of the short arm of
clinical_summary: Hamel cerebro-palato-cardiac syndrome is a rare, X-linked recessive disorder characterized by a range of congenital anomalies present at birth, including craniofacial features and microcephaly. Affected individuals exhibit symptoms such as narrow mouth, cleft palate, microcephaly, malar flattening, micrognathia, and cupped ear.

name: Classic bladder exstrophy
clinical_summary: Classic bladder exstrophy is a rare multigenic/multifactorial condition presenting in infancy or neonatal age with symptoms including recurrent urinary tract infections, inguinal hernia, epispadias, abnormality of the clitoris, abnormality of the ureter, and vesicoureteral reflux.

name: 12q14 microdeletion syndrome
clinical_summary: 12q14 microdeletion syndrome is a rare disease characterized by a combination of intellectual disability, global developmental delay, and specific learning disabilities in affected individuals. Symptoms can include hypertelorism, delayed speech and language development, hyperpigmentation of the skin, and various degrees of developmental delay.

name: X-linked intellectual disability, Golabi-Ito-Hall type
lab_findings: low birth weight for gestational age
clinical_summary: X-linked intellectual disability, Golabi-Ito-Hall type is a rare genetic disorder characterized by macroglossia, microcephaly, and distinct facial features. The condition typically presents in infancy or the neonatal period and follows an X-linked recessive inheritance pattern.

name: Hurler syndrome
clinical_summary: Hurler syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by coarse facial features, macro-glossia, and other distinct physical abnormalities. It typically presents in infancy or during the neonatal period.

name: Scheie syndrome
clinical_summary: Scheie syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by coarse facial features, eye abnormalities, and musculoskeletal issues. It manifests in childhood, adolescence, or adulthood with symptoms including glaucoma, joint mobility limitations, and potentially life-threatening cardiovascular complications.

name: Hurler-Scheie syndrome
clinical_summary: Hurler-Scheie syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by coarse facial features, sensorineural hearing impairment, and systemic abnormalities including cardiac, hepatic, and hematologic involvement. It typically presents in infancy or neonatal life with symptoms such as joint mobility limitation, abnormal heart valves, splenomegaly, and hepatomegaly.

name: Juvenile dermatomyositis
clinical_summary: Juvenile dermatomyositis is a rare disease, primarily affecting adolescents, children, and infants, characterized by symptoms such as skin dryness, rash, pruritus, cutaneous photosensitivity, poikiloderma, and hypotonia. It is not inherited.

name: Brachydactyly type C
key_symptoms: Complete duplication of distal phalanx of the thumb
Stippling of the epiphysis of the distal phalanx of the thumb
clinical_summary: Brachydactyly type C is a rare disease characterized by short middle phalanges of the fingers and other specific skeletal abnormalities, including abnormal nail morphology and ulnar deviation of the fingers. It presents in infancy or neonatal life and can exhibit autosomal dominant or autosomal recessive inheritance patterns.

name: Spondyloepimetaphyseal dysplasia, Irapa type
clinical_summary: Spondyloepimetaphyseal dysplasia, Irapa type is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by congenital skeletal abnormalities, including platyspondyly and abnormalities in rib and metaphysis morphology. Affected individuals typically present with pectus carinatum, osteoporosis, and other skeletal malformations, along with a broad palm, from childhood.

name: Autosomal dominant omodysplasia
clinical_summary: Autosomal dominant omodysplasia is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a combination of physical anomalies including cryptorchidism, bifid scrotum, and facial abnormalities such as malar flattening, hypertelorism, and long philtrum, with frontal bossing. It typically manifests at birth or in infancy with an autosomal dominant pattern of inheritance.

name: Spondylometaphyseal dysplasia, Sedaghatian type
clinical_summary: Spondylometaphyseal dysplasia, Sedaghatian type is a rare autosomal recessive disorder presenting with a range of skeletal abnormalities. Key features include turricephaly, abnormal rib and scapular morphology, platyspondyly, and generalized hypotonia, evident from antenatal and neonatal periods.

name: Achondrogenesis type 1B
lab_findings: skeletal dysplasia markers (e.g. alkaline phosphatase) low
dysmorphic radiographic appearance of the limbs
clinical_summary: Achondrogenesis type 1B is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by ante- and peri-natal onset of severe skeletal dysplasia. Clinical features include macrocephaly, long philtrum, micrognathia, anteverted nares, short neck, and a thickened nuchal skin fold.

name: Achondrogenesis type 1A
lab_findings: homozygous point mutations in the diaphanous-1 gene (diaph1 or dmd)
clinical_summary: Achondrogenesis type 1A is a rare, autosomal recessive congenital disorder characterized by macrocephaly, long philtrum, micrognathia, anteverted nares, short neck, and thickened nuchal skin fold. It presents with symptoms observable at birth, following an antenatal onset.

name: Cloverleaf skull-multiple congenital anomalies syndrome
clinical_summary: Cloverleaf skull-multiple congenital anomalies syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by congenital anomalies and manifestations such as ambiguous genitalia, hypertelorism, and micrognathia. It presents antenatally or at birth with findings including large fontanelles and low-set ears.

name: Short rib-polydactyly syndrome, Verma-Naumoff type
clinical_summary: Short rib-polydactyly syndrome, Verma-Naumoff type is a rare disorder characterized by short ribs, narrow chest, and other skeletal abnormalities, often resulting in respiratory insufficiency. This autosomal recessive condition typically presents at an early stage, either antenatally or neonatally.

name: Pfeiffer syndrome type 3
key_symptoms: Stenosis of the external auditory canal
clinical_summary: Pfeiffer syndrome type 3 is a rare disease characterized by a combination of craniofacial abnormalities, including high palate, brachyturricephaly, hypertelorism, high forehead, and low-set ears, along with stenosis of the external auditory canal. It is marked by an antenatal and neonatal age of onset and follows an autosomal dominant inheritance pattern.

name: Crouzon syndrome-acanthosis nigricans syndrome
clinical_summary: Crouzon syndrome-acanthosis nigricans syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a distinctive combination of craniofacial anomalies. Symptoms, including high forehead, acanthosis nigricans, and hydrocephalus, typically present during fetal development or early infancy.

name: Thanatophoric dysplasia type 2
clinical_summary: Thanatophoric dysplasia type 2 is a rare, congenital disorder characterized by a range of severe skeletal abnormalities, hearing impairment, and macrocephaly, presenting in infancy or neonatal age. It is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern.

name: Fragile X-associated tremor/ataxia syndrome
lab_findings: elevated microsatellite expansion in fmr1 gene
clinical_summary: Fragile X-associated tremor/ataxia syndrome is a rare adult-onset neurodegenerative disorder characterized by dementia, ataxia, and other motor symptoms. It is an X-linked dominant disorder.

name: Hypocalcemic vitamin D-resistant rickets
clinical_summary: Hypocalcemic vitamin D-resistant rickets is a rare congenital disorder characterized by symptoms including dolichocephaly, nephrolithiasis, and hyperparathyroidism. It is typically diagnosed in infancy or neonatal stage and has an autosomal recessive inheritance pattern.

name: Pfeiffer syndrome type 2
key_symptoms: Atresia of the external auditory canal
clinical_summary: Pfeiffer syndrome type 2 is a rare genetic disorder characterized by high palate, hydrocephalus, and other distinct facial abnormalities, such as malar flattening, hypertelorism, high forehead, and low-set ears. Symptoms typically appear prenatally or shortly after birth.

name: Pfeiffer syndrome type 1
clinical_summary: Pfeiffer syndrome type 1 is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a combination of craniofacial abnormalities, including high palate, brachycephaly, hypertelorism, high forehead, and low-set ears, often associated with proptosis. It has an autosomal dominant pattern of inheritance and typically presents in utero or during the neonatal period.

name: Cryoglobulinemic vasculitis
clinical_summary: Cryoglobulinemic vasculitis is a rare disease characterized by cutaneous symptoms such as cutis marmorata, petechiae, and purpura, often accompanied by systemic manifestations including muscle weakness and fever, as a result of vasculitis.

name: Otopalatodigital syndrome type 1
lab_findings: normal radiographic findings of the hands and feet
no significant abnormalities in biochemical tests
clinical_summary: Otopalatodigital syndrome type 1 is a rare X-linked dominant disorder characterized by cleft palate, distinctive facial features, and hearing impairment. It presents ante-natally, from infancy, or in early neonatal life, and is associated with a wide range of significant symptoms.

name: Otopalatodigital syndrome type 2
clinical_summary: Otopalatodigital syndrome type 2 is a rare X-linked dominant disorder characterized by a combination of facial anomalies, including narrow mouth, cleft palate, and hypertelorism. Symptoms of this disorder become apparent in utero or shortly after birth, with large fontanelles, malar flattening, and prominent supraorbital ridges also being notable features.

name: Stickler syndrome type 1
clinical_summary: Stickler syndrome type 1 is a rare autosomal dominant disorder characterized by hypoplasia of the maxilla, long philtrum, cataracts, retinal detachment, and skeletal dysplasia, often presenting in childhood, infancy, or neonatally. Symptoms include significant myopia.

name: Brittle cornea syndrome
lab_findings: normal or low elastic fiber density
clinical_summary: Brittle cornea syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder typically presenting in infancy or at birth, characterized by conductive and sensorineural hearing impairment, corneal scarring, visual loss, and blue sclerae. Osteoporosis is also associated with this condition.

name: Blau syndrome
clinical_summary: Blau syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by joint inflammation, skin rash, and uveitis. Onset typically occurs in infancy or at birth and is associated with autosomal dominant inheritance.

name: Xeroderma pigmentosum variant
clinical_summary: Xeroderma pigmentosum variant is an autosomal recessive disease characterized by symptoms such as keratitis, photophobia, and hyperpigmentation of the skin. Onset typically occurs in adolescence or adulthood, with associated cutaneous photosensitivity and telangiectasia.

name: Autosomal dominant cutis laxa
key_symptoms: Fragmented elastic fibers in the dermis
clinical_summary: Autosomal dominant cutis laxa is a rare genetic disorder characterized by joint hypermobility, hyperextensible skin, and cutis laxa. This condition is typically present at birth, with symptoms including redundant skin, increased number of skin folds, and fragmented elastic fibers in the dermis.

name: Capillary-lymphatic-venous malformation with segmental distribution
clinical_summary: Capillary-lymphatic-venous malformation with segmental distribution is a rare disease characterized by a combination of symptoms including tall stature, venous insufficiency, and increased risk for life-threatening conditions such as pulmonary embolism and gastrointestinal hemorrhage. It typically presents in infancy or neonatal period.

name: Mandibuloacral dysplasia with type B lipodystrophy
clinical_summary: Mandibuloacral dysplasia with type B lipodystrophy is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by infancy or neonatal onset of features such as narrow mouth, abnormal dentition, large fontanelles, micrognathia, convex nasal ridge, and proptosis.

name: Mandibuloacral dysplasia with type A lipodystrophy
clinical_summary: Mandibuloacral dysplasia with type A lipodystrophy is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by the onset of several distinct features in infancy, including large fontanelles, proptosis, and abnormal eyebrow morphology. Insulin resistance, thin skin, and limited joint mobility also occur in affected individuals.

name: Hereditary folate malabsorption
clinical_summary: Hereditary folate malabsorption is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by symptoms including glossitis, global developmental delay, and failure to thrive typically presenting in infancy or neonatal stages. The condition may also be associated with atypical behavior, pallor, and seizures.

name: Primary familial polycythemia
clinical_summary: Primary familial polycythemia is a rare autosomal dominant disorder characterized by episodes of epistaxis, pruritus, and other symptoms such as polycythemia, abdominal pain, dyspnea, and headache. It is notable for its lack of age-specific onset and has a broad range of presentations.

name: Mixed-type autoimmune hemolytic anemia
clinical_summary: Mixed-type autoimmune hemolytic anemia is a rare, multigenic/multifactorial autoimmune disorder manifesting with various symptoms. It can affect all ages and may be associated with conditions such as lymphoma and systemic lupus erythematosus.

name: Autoimmune hemolytic anemia, warm type
clinical_summary: Autoimmune hemolytic anemia, warm type is a rare disease characterized by the premature destruction of red blood cells due to autoantibodies, presenting with symptoms such as pallor, headache, exertional dyspnea, and fatigue. It can affect individuals of all ages with a complex etiology influenced by multiple genetic and environmental factors.

name: Deafness with labyrinthine aplasia, microtia, and microdontia
clinical_summary: Deafness with labyrinthine aplasia, microtia, and microdontia is a rare genetic disorder characterized by sensorineural hearing impairment and congenital facial abnormalities, typically present at birth. This autosomal recessive condition is associated with distinct physical features, including a long face and underdeveloped nasal alae.

name: Autosomal dominant hypophosphatemic rickets
clinical_summary: Autosomal dominant hypophosphatemic rickets is a rare condition characterized by hypophosphatemia, muscle weakness, growth delay, iron deficiency anemia, and bone pain. It is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern and can affect individuals in various age groups, including childhood, adolescence, adulthood, and infancy.

name: Neurodegeneration due to 3-hydroxyisobutyryl-CoA hydrolase deficiency
clinical_summary: This rare disease is characterized by hypotonia, motor delay, dystonia, vomiting, progressive neurologic deterioration, and epicanthus, typically presenting in infancy. The condition is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern.

name: Sialidosis type 2
clinical_summary: Sialidosis type 2 is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a range of clinical features including coarse facial features, hearing impairment, and delayed speech and language development. Affected individuals may also exhibit a variety of other systemic manifestations such as inguinal hernia, nephropathy, and pectus carinatum, often present from birth.

name: Mesoaxial synostotic syndactyly with phalangeal reduction
key_symptoms: Symphalangism affecting the phalanges of the hand
lab_findings: i am unable to confirm any specific laboratory findings for this rare disease.
clinical_summary: Mesoaxial synostotic syndactyly with phalangeal reduction is a rare disease characterized by toe and finger syndactyly, clinodactyly, and synostosis of carpal bones. It is an autosomal recessive disorder that presents at birth or in infancy.

name: SLC39A13-related spondylodysplastic Ehlers-Danlos syndrome
clinical_summary: SLC39A13-related spondylodysplastic Ehlers-Danlos syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a range of symptoms primarily apparent at birth. Key features include craniofacial abnormalities, skeletal defects, and characteristic eye findings.

name: Congenital muscular dystrophy due to LMNA mutation
clinical_summary: Congenital muscular dystrophy due to LMNA mutation is a rare autosomal dominant disorder characterized by early onset, typically in infancy or neonatal period, manifesting with symptoms such as hypotonia, global developmental delay, and gait disturbance. It also leads to severe consequences including flexion contracture, respiratory insufficiency, and limitation of joint mobility.

name: Juvenile xanthogranuloma
clinical_summary: Juvenile xanthogranuloma is a rare disease characterized by a range of symptoms including blepharitis, glaucoma, proptosis, uveitis, visual loss, and iritis, typically affecting infants, children, and adolescents. The disease has no clear inheritance pattern and presents with various ocular manifestations.

name: Segmental outgrowth-lipomatosis-arteriovenous malformation-epidermal nevus syndrome
clinical_summary: Segmental outgrowth-lipomatosis-arteriovenous malformation-epidermal nevus syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by the presence of a subcutaneous nodule accompanied by other symptoms including venous insufficiency and excessive wrinkled skin, typically presenting at birth. The condition often involves the formation of hamartoma and arteriovenous malformation.

name: Arachnoiditis
clinical_summary: Arachnoiditis is a rare condition characterized by symptoms including tinnitus, hearing impairment, and sensory neuropathy, among others. It affects individuals of all ages and is not inherited.

name: Drug reaction with eosinophilia and systemic symptoms
clinical_summary: Drug reaction with eosinophilia and systemic symptoms is a rare disease characterized by a combination of cutaneous symptoms, such as skin rash and erythroderma, and systemic symptoms including fever, weight loss, and cardiac arrest. It can occur at any age.

name: X-linked intellectual disability-cerebellar hypoplasia syndrome
clinical_summary: X-linked intellectual disability-cerebellar hypoplasia syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by intellectual disability, reduced social responsiveness, and global developmental delay, presenting in infancy or neonatal life. This condition is inherited in an X-linked dominant pattern.

name: Carney triad
clinical_summary: Carney triad is a rare disease characterized by a combination of various symptoms including ascites, tachycardia, diarrhea, nausea and vomiting, abdominal pain, and pulmonary infiltrates primarily affecting adolescents and adults. The exact cause and inheritance pattern of this disease are unknown.

name: Encephalopathy due to prosaposin deficiency
clinical_summary: Encephalopathy due to prosaposin deficiency is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by various motor and ocular symptoms, including dystonia, myoclonus, and abnormality of eye movement. The condition leads to premature death in infancy, often accompanied by signs such as hypotonia and splenomegaly.

name: Syndactyly-telecanthus-anogenital and renal malformations syndrome
clinical_summary: Syndactyly-telecanthus-anogenital and renal malformations syndrome is a rare X-linked dominant disorder characterized by multiple congenital anomalies, including lop ear, toe syndactyly, and anal atresia. This syndrome typically presents in infancy or neonatal period.

name: Gamma-heavy chain disease
lab_findings: Abnormality of bone marrow cell morphology
clinical_summary: Gamma-heavy chain disease is a rare condition characterized by a combination of symptoms including skin rash, anemia, and lymphadenopathy. It predominantly affects adults and is marked by splenomegaly, hepatomegaly, and fever.

name: Mu-heavy chain disease
lab_findings: Abnormality of bone marrow cell morphology
clinical_summary: Mu-heavy chain disease is a rare condition characterized by splenomegaly, weight loss, anemia, fever, hepatomegaly, and lymphadenopathy. It typically presents in adults, with an unknown inheritance pattern.

name: Bathing suit ichthyosis
clinical_summary: Bathing suit ichthyosis is a rare genetic disorder characterized by thickened and scaling skin, typically manifesting in infancy or neonatal life. The inherited condition, which follows an autosomal recessive pattern, often presents with additional symptoms such as epidermal acanthosis, ectropion, and erythroderma.

name: X-linked Charcot-Marie-Tooth disease type 4
clinical_summary: X-linked Charcot-Marie-Tooth disease type 4 is characterized by symptoms such as hearing impairment, sensory neuropathy, and intellectual disability. It follows an X-linked recessive inheritance pattern, typically presenting during childhood, infancy, or neonatal periods.

name: Cushing syndrome due to ectopic ACTH secretion
key_symptoms: Fatiguable weakness of proximal limb muscles
lab_findings: Paradoxical increased cortisol secretion on dexamethasone suppression test
clinical_summary: Cushing syndrome due to ectopic ACTH secretion is a rare endocrine disorder characterized by excessive cortisol production and muscle weakness. It is predominantly seen in adults and the elderly, manifesting with symptoms such as diabetes mellitus and increased circulating cortisol and ACTH levels.

name: Atypical teratoid rhabdoid tumor
clinical_summary: Atypical teratoid rhabdoid tumor is a rare and aggressive brain cancer characterized by a wide range of neurological symptoms, including hydrocephalus, macrocephaly, and seizures. It typically presents shortly after birth or in early childhood, with symptoms including irritability, apathy, and ataxia.

name: O'Sullivan-McLeod syndrome
clinical_summary: O'Sullivan-McLeod syndrome is characterized by adult-onset muscle weakness, tremor, and fasciculations, along with EMG abnormalities, intrinsic hand muscle atrophy, and hand muscle weakness. The inheritance pattern of this rare disorder remains unknown.

name: Lassa fever
clinical_summary: Lassa fever is a rare disease characterized by fever and viremia, occurring in individuals of all ages. Symptoms may include asthenia and spontaneous abortion, with circulating IgM levels increased.

name: Nipah virus disease
clinical_summary: Nipah virus disease is a rare disease characterized by a range of symptoms including seizure, fever, and gastrointestinal issues such as nausea, vomiting, and anorexia. The disease can affect individuals of all ages and the transmission route or inheritance pattern is currently unknown.

name: Haddad syndrome
clinical_summary: Haddad syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by symptoms such as strabismus, failure to thrive, and breathing dysregulation that predominantly present in infancy or at birth. It is inherited in an autosomal dominant or multigenic/multifactorial manner.

name: LIG4 syndrome
clinical_summary: LIG4 syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive condition characterized by distinctive facial features, including thin vermilion border, epicanthus, bird-like facies, brachycephaly, and low anterior hairline, often present at birth or in infancy.

name: X-linked Charcot-Marie-Tooth disease type 5
clinical_summary: X-linked Charcot-Marie-Tooth disease type 5 is a rare genetic disorder characterized by childhood-onset hearing impairment, muscle weakness, and areflexia. The condition is inherited in an X-linked recessive pattern.

name: Spastic paraplegia type 2
clinical_summary: Spastic paraplegia type 2 is a rare genetic disorder characterized by optic atrophy, intellectual disability, and progressive motor symptoms including spasticity and muscle weakness. It typically presents in childhood with a X-linked recessive inheritance pattern.

name: Typhoid
clinical_summary: Typhoid is a rare disease characterized by a complex combination of systemic symptoms, including fever, and neurological symptoms such as ataxia, hypertonia, and hyperreflexia. The disease also presents with physical symptoms including a skin rash and splenomegaly.

name: Amish lethal microcephaly
clinical_summary: Amish lethal microcephaly is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a range of physical abnormalities, including microcephaly, sloping forehead, micrognathia, and optic atrophy. It also presents with irritability and osteoporosis, with a severe course typically beginning in utero, in infancy, or at birth.

name: Mosaic trisomy 9 syndrome
clinical_summary: Mosaic trisomy 9 syndrome is a rare condition characterized by a combination of physical abnormalities, including cryptorchidism, high palate, large fontanelles, micrognathia, low-set ears, and bulbous nose. It typically presents in infancy or neonatally, but the inheritance pattern of the condition is currently unknown.

name: Bohring-Opitz syndrome
lab_findings: no intellectual delay seen in chromosomes 11-18 microarray
molecular diagnosis of brio gene mutation
clinical_summary: Bohring-Opitz syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by antenatal/neonatal onset, including features such as facial hypotonia, full cheeks, and Nevus flammeus. It is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern, associated with neurodevelopmental delay, trigonocephaly, and microcephaly.

name: Riboflavin transporter deficiency
clinical_summary: Riboflavin transporter deficiency is a rare disease characterized by symptoms including bulbar palsy, cranial nerve paralysis, and abnormal cranial nerve morphology. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern and typically presents with varying age of onset, including infancy, childhood, adolescence, or adulthood.

name: Carney-Stratakis syndrome
clinical_summary: Carney-Stratakis syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by tinnitus, hearing impairment, and other gastrointestinal symptoms including weight loss, dysphagia, abdominal pain, and gastrointestinal hemorrhage. It typically presents in adolescence or adulthood and follows an autosomal dominant pattern of inheritance.

name: Alpha-thalassemia-intellectual disability syndrome linked to chromosome 16
clinical_summary: Alpha-thalassemia-intellectual disability syndrome linked to chromosome 16 is a rare disease characterized by congenital anomalies such as microcephaly, malar flattening, and epicanthus. Symptoms typically manifest in infancy or neonatal period.

name: Thyroid hypoplasia
clinical_summary: Thyroid hypoplasia is a rare congenital disorder characterized by hypothyroidism and other developmental anomalies, typically presenting with macroglossia, coarse facial features, and large fontanelles in infancy. The condition is associated with autosomal dominant inheritance.

name: Thyroid hemiagenesis
clinical_summary: Thyroid hemiagenesis is a rare condition characterized by congenital absence of one lobe of the thyroid gland, often accompanied by symptoms such as growth delay and fatigue. This condition typically presents in neonates with a range of symptoms including umbilical hernia, constipation, and abdominal distention.

name: Athyreosis
clinical_summary: Athyreosis is a rare congenital disorder characterized by multiple abnormalities, including hypothyroidism, hypotonia, macroglossia, and facial dysmorphism, often presenting in infancy or neonatal period. It is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern.

name: Thyroid ectopia
clinical_summary: Thyroid ectopia is a rare condition characterized by the presence of the thyroid gland in an abnormal location, presenting in infancy or neonatally with symptoms including intellectual disability, fatigue, and macroglossia.

name: 49,XXXXY syndrome
clinical_summary: 49,XXXXY syndrome is a rare genetic condition characterized by joint hypermobility, infertility, and various dental abnormalities. Onset typically occurs in childhood, with affected males often experiencing hypogonadism, azoospermia, and carious teeth.

name: 48,XXXY syndrome
clinical_summary: 48,XXXY syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by mild intellectual disability, joint hypermobility, hypogonadism, and infertility. Affected individuals often exhibit global developmental delay with symptoms typically present from an early age, pre- or perinatally.

name: Koolen-De Vries syndrome
clinical_summary: Koolen-De Vries syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by significant congenital anomalies such as thick nasal alae, genitourinary malformations, and dental abnormalities. It is an autosomal dominant disorder that presents early in development, manifesting antenatal or within the first few months of life.

name: Recombinant 8 syndrome
clinical_summary: Recombinant 8 syndrome is a rare condition characterized by a distinct set of physical abnormalities, including cryptorchidism, hypertelorism, micrognathia, and anteverted nares, often accompanied by intellectual disability. These symptoms typically manifest in infancy or the neonatal period.

name: Distal deletion 19p syndrome
clinical_summary: Distal deletion 19p syndrome is characterized by congenital anomalies, including cleft palate, facial abnormalities (long face, short philtrum, and hypoplasia of the maxilla), and conductive/sensorineural hearing impairment. These symptoms are typically present at birth.

name: Distal deletion 6p syndrome
clinical_summary: Distal deletion 6p syndrome is a rare genetic condition characterized by distinctive facial features and dental abnormalities that are present at birth or in early infancy. It is not related to known patterns of inheritance.

name: Mosaic trisomy 8 syndrome
clinical_summary: Mosaic trisomy 8 syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by intellectual disability, vesicoureteral reflux, hydronephrosis, dolichocephaly, a long face, and hypertelorism. Symptoms of this condition are typically present from birth, occurring prenatally or in the neonatal period.

name: 20p12.3 microdeletion syndrome
clinical_summary: 20p12.3 microdeletion syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by features including a narrow mouth, macrocephaly (abnormally large head size), and hypertelorism. It typically presents in infancy or the neonatal period and does not have a clear pattern of inheritance.

name: Paternal 20q13.2q13.3 microdeletion syndrome
clinical_summary: Paternal 20q13.2q13.3 microdeletion syndrome is a rare genetic condition characterized by distinct facial features, including thin vermilion border, microcephaly, hypertelorism, short philtrum, micrognathia, and high forehead. This condition is typically apparent at birth, with symptoms present before or shortly after birth.

name: Trisomy 20p syndrome
clinical_summary: Trisomy 20p syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by facial and physical features such as full cheeks, round face, protruding ear, and short neck, which become apparent in infancy or the neonatal period. Hypotonia and a thick eyebrow are also associated with this condition.

name: Distal 22q11.2 microdeletion syndrome
clinical_summary: Distal 22q11.2 microdeletion syndrome is a rare condition characterized by distinct facial features including thin upper lip vermilion, microcephaly, pointed chin, smooth philtrum, and abnormalities of the earlobe and nasal alae. It is an autosomal dominant disorder with antenatal or neonatal age of onset.

name: Distal 22q11.2 microduplication syndrome
lab_findings: trisomy 22q11 specific dna gain on chromosome 22q11.2
clinical_summary: Distal 22q11.2 microduplication syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by a unique combination of clinical features, including various congenital anomalies. It typically presents with a range of symptoms starting in utero and continuing through infancy and childhood.

name: Trisomy 1q syndrome
lab_findings: abnormal karyotype with supernumerary marker chromosome
clinical_summary: Trisomy 1q syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by multicystic kidney dysplasia, craniofacial abnormalities, and other congenital anomalies. The exact inheritance pattern is unknown, and it presents antenatally or in the neonatal period.

name: 2p15p16.1 microdeletion syndrome
clinical_summary: 2p15p16.1 microdeletion syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by distinctive facial features, including a wide nasal bridge and Downslanted palpebral fissures, and associated with various ocular and other anomalies. It is present from birth and manifests with multiple symptoms in infancy and neonatal periods.

name: Xq27.3q28 duplication syndrome
clinical_summary: Xq27.3q28 duplication syndrome is a rare, inherited condition characterized by features such as cryptorchidism, hypogonadism, and intellectual disability, often presenting at infancy. It is transmitted through an X-linked recessive inheritance pattern.

name: 16p11.2p12.2 microdeletion syndrome
clinical_summary: 16p11.2p12.2 microdeletion syndrome is a rare condition characterized by a distinctive combination of physical features, including open mouth, low-set ears, and abnormal ear morphology, which are typically present from infancy. Chronic otitis media may also be a feature of this condition.

name: 16q24.3 microdeletion syndrome
clinical_summary: 16q24.3 microdeletion syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by distinct physical features, including high forehead, protruding ear, and high palate, alongside neurodevelopmental symptoms such as autism. Symptoms typically emerge in infancy or neonatal period, with unknown inheritance pattern.

name: 17q23.1q23.2 microdeletion syndrome
clinical_summary: 17q23.1q23.2 microdeletion syndrome is a rare genetic condition characterized by neonatal and infancy onset of microcephaly, delayed speech and language development, and associated congenital anomalies. Key features also include intrauterine growth retardation, patent ductus arteriosus, and pulmonary arterial hypertension.

name: Trisomy 17p syndrome
clinical_summary: Trisomy 17p syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a range of systemic abnormalities, including polycystic kidney dysplasia, hydronephrosis, and craniofacial features such as narrow mouth, high palate, microcephaly, and malar flattening. These symptoms typically present in infancy or neonatally.

name: Iatrogenic botulism
clinical_summary: Iatrogenic botulism is a rare condition characterized by urinary retention, xerostomia, ptosis, orthostatic hypotension, muscle weakness, and dysphagia, affecting individuals of all ages with currently unknown inheritance patterns.

name: 14q11.2 microdeletion syndrome
clinical_summary: 14q11.2 microdeletion syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by distinct physical abnormalities, including narrow mouth, high palate, everted lower lip vermilion, epicanthus, and hypertelorism. These features typically become apparent in infancy or neonatal stages.

name: Monosomy 9p syndrome
clinical_summary: Monosomy 9p syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by distinct facial features, including high palate, trigonocephaly, brachycephaly, malar flattening, and hypertelorism. It is present antenatally or neonatally and its inheritance pattern has not been established.

name: Cleft palate-congenital heart defect-intellectual disability syndrome due to 15q14 microdeletion
clinical_summary: This rare disease is characterized by a combination of cleft palate, microcephaly, and intellectual disability due to a 15q14 microdeletion, which typically presents in infancy or at birth.

name: FOXG1 syndrome due to 14q12 microdeletion
clinical_summary: FOXG1 syndrome due to 14q12 microdeletion is a rare genetic disorder characterized by distinct facial features and microcephaly. It typically presents in infancy or the neonatal period.

name: 8p11.2 deletion syndrome
clinical_summary: 8p11.2 deletion syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by features such as hypogonadism, microcephaly, and high palate, often presenting in infancy or neonatal age. Affected individuals may experience additional symptoms including azoospermia and cryptorchidism.

name: 6q25.2q25.3 microdeletion syndrome
clinical_summary: 6q25.2q25.3 microdeletion syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by microcephaly, sensorineural hearing impairment, intellectual disability, global developmental delay, high palate, and malar flattening at infancy or neonatal age. This condition is not inheritance-related.

name: 8p23.1 microdeletion syndrome
clinical_summary: 8p23.1 microdeletion syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by intellectual disability, global developmental delay, and physical anomalies such as intrauterine growth retardation and genital abnormalities. The condition typically becomes evident in infancy or neonatal period.

name: 19p13.12 microdeletion syndrome
clinical_summary: 19p13.12 microdeletion syndrome is a rare disease characterized by distinct facial features, including a thin vermilion border, and head shape anomalies such as brachycephaly and microcephaly. It typically presents in infancy or neonatally.

name: 46,XY partial gonadal dysgenesis
lab_findings: Elevated circulating follicle stimulating hormone level
Female external genitalia in individual with 46,XY karyotype
inheritance: Autosomal dominant, Autosomal recessive, X-linked recessive, Y-linked
clinical_summary: 46,XY partial gonadal dysgenesis is a rare disorder characterized by male infertility, abnormalities of the external genitalia, and hormonal imbalances, including decreased serum estradiol and elevated follicle stimulating hormone. It presents in infancy or neonatal age and exhibits complex inheritance patterns involving autosomal dominant, autosomal recessive, X-linked recessive, and putatively Y-linked inheritance.

name: Inhalational botulism
clinical_summary: Inhalational botulism is a rare disease characterized by symptoms including urinary retention, muscle weakness, ptosis, diplopia, xerostomia, and diarrhea in adults. The exact inheritance pattern of this disease is currently unknown.

name: ABetaL34V amyloidosis
clinical_summary: ABetaL34V amyloidosis is a rare autosomal dominant disorder characterized by cognitive and neurological symptoms, including atypical behavior, dementia, and intellectual disability. This disease typically manifests in adulthood, often leading to severe complications such as stroke and coma.

name: Ebola hemorrhagic fever
clinical_summary: Ebola hemorrhagic fever is a rare disease characterized by a combination of severe symptoms including fever, diarrhea, viremia, and chest pain, often accompanied by significant immune system compromise. The disease can affect individuals of all ages, with no apparent familial predisposition.

name: Chronic nonbacterial osteomyelitis/Chronic recurrent multifocal osteomyelitis
clinical_summary: Chronic nonbacterial osteomyelitis/ Chronic recurrent multifocal osteomyelitis is a rare condition characterized by symptoms such as abnormal bone morphology and bone pain, typically presenting in childhood or adolescence. Key clinical features include edema, arthritis, and osteomyelitis.

name: Vitamin B12-unresponsive methylmalonic acidemia type mut0
clinical_summary: Vitamin B12-unresponsive methylmalonic acidemia type mut0 is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by severe neurological symptoms including intellectual disability, lethargy, and coma. Affected individuals typically present with symptoms in infancy or neonatal period, also experiencing growth delay and developmental delays.

name: Infantile-onset ascending hereditary spastic paralysis
clinical_summary: Infantile-onset ascending hereditary spastic paralysis is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by early onset of spastic paraplegia, spasticity, and various neuromotor symptoms such as dysarthria and hyperreflexia. Affected individuals typically present with these symptoms in infancy or neonatal stage, accompanied by abnormal eye movement and pseudobulbar behavioral symptoms.

name: Lujo hemorrhagic fever
clinical_summary: Lujo hemorrhagic fever is a rare disease characterized by facial edema, thrombocytopenia, fever, diarrhea, and headache, often accompanied by elevated circulating hepatic transaminase concentration. The onset of the disease can occur at any age, with the mode of inheritance unknown.

name: 8q21.11 microdeletion syndrome
clinical_summary: 8q21.11 microdeletion syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by distinctive facial features, including full cheeks, round face, and low-set ears, often accompanied by hearing impairment and ptosis. Manifestations typically emerge in infancy or the neonatal period.

name: L1 syndrome
clinical_summary: L1 syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by hydrocephalus, intellectual disability, and other severe neurological features such as spasticity. It typically presents in neonatal age with symptoms including adducted thumb, global developmental delay, depression, and hydrocephalus.

name: Mycophenolate mofetil embryopathy
key_symptoms: Atresia of the external auditory canal
clinical_summary: Mycophenolate mofetil embryopathy is a rare disorder characterized by multiple congenital anomalies, including orofacial cleft, hearing impairment, and chorioretinal coloboma. It presents in infants with atresia of the external auditory canal, hypertelorism, and micrognathia.

name: 14q22q23 microdeletion syndrome
key_symptoms: Atresia of the external auditory canal
clinical_summary: 14q22q23 microdeletion syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a combination of congenital anomalies, including cryptorchidism, epicanthus, hypertelorism, high forehead, and posteriorly rotated ears. The syndrome is typically detectable antenatally or at birth, presenting with varying degrees of severity during infancy.

name: 10q22.3q23.3 microduplication syndrome
clinical_summary: 10q22.3q23.3 microduplication syndrome is a rare condition characterized by a range of birth defects including deeply set eyes, genital abnormalities, and microcephaly. Symptoms are typically present from infancy or the neonatal period onward.

name: 48,XYYY syndrome
lab_findings: no lab findings established for 48,xxyyy syndrome
clinical_summary: 48,XYYY syndrome is a rare sex chromosome disorder characterized by male hypogonadism, azoospermia, tall stature, and distinct facial features including a thick lower lip vermilion, high palate, and epicanthus. Its mode of inheritance remains unknown.

name: 49,XXXYY syndrome
clinical_summary: 49,XX,YY syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by male hypogonadism, micropenis, and ambiguous genitalia. Symptoms often present in the neonatal period and include epicanthus, mandibular prognathia, and hypertelorism.

name: 49,XYYYY syndrome
clinical_summary: 49,XYYYY syndrome is a rare condition characterized by male hypogonadism, azoospermia, and various physical abnormalities including coarse facial features and genitourinary abnormalities. Symptoms often appear prenatally or in infancy and may include hypertelorism and micrognathia.

name: Leydig cell hypoplasia
clinical_summary: Leydig cell hypoplasia is a rare disorder characterized by neonatal male hypogonadism, manifesting with various genital abnormalities. The condition is marked by cryptorchidism, hypospadias, micropenis, and ambiguous genitalia in affected males.

name: Familial thoracic aortic aneurysm and aortic dissection
key_symptoms: Cystic medial necrosis of the aorta
clinical_summary: Familial thoracic aortic aneurysm and aortic dissection is a rare condition characterized by cutis marmorata, connective tissue abnormalities, and cardiomegaly typically presenting in individuals of adolescent, adult, or childhood age. This autosomal dominant disorder is marked by hypertension and potentially life-threatening complications such as aortic dissection and cystic medial necrosis of the aorta.

name: Non-functioning pituitary adenoma
lab_findings: Decreased response to growth hormone stimulation test
clinical_summary: Non-functioning pituitary adenoma is a rare disease characterized by hypogonadism, which can manifest as hypogonadotropic hypogonadism or hypogonadism alone, as well as abnormal menstrual cycles. It affects individuals across a wide age range, including adolescence, adulthood, and childhood, with no known familial inheritance pattern.

name: Ring chromosome Y syndrome
clinical_summary: Ring chromosome Y syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by congenital abnormalities and reproductive issues, including male and female infertility. Symptoms typically present at birth, with males experiencing hypogonadism and infertility.

name: Wolfram-like syndrome
clinical_summary: Wolfram-like syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by a combination of optic atrophy and a range of psychiatric symptoms, including psychosis and anxiety, which often manifest in childhood. The condition exhibits autosomal dominant inheritance.

name: Müllerian aplasia and hyperandrogenism
clinical_summary: Müllerian aplasia and hyperandrogenism is a rare disorder characterized by hypoplasia of the uterus, vaginal abnormalities, primary amenorrhea, and multiple signs of virilization. It presents with symptoms such as hirsutism, acne, and obesity, often from infancy or neonatal age.

name: 46,XY gonadal dysgenesis-motor and sensory neuropathy syndrome
clinical_summary: 46,XY gonadal dysgenesis-motor and sensory neuropathy syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by gonadal dysgenesis and associated with hypoplasia of the uterus, among other symptoms. It typically presents in infancy or the neonatal period with primary amenorrhea and abnormalities of the genitalia.

name: Penile agenesis
clinical_summary: Penile agenesis is a rare congenital anomaly characterized by the absence of the penis and associated with several other birth defects, including abnormalities of the bladder and urinary system. The condition presents antenatally or neonatally with various physical anomalies, such as ambiguous genitalia and posteriorly rotated ears.

name: 46,XY ovotesticular difference of sex development
lab_findings: Abnormality of prenatal development or birth
clinical_summary: 46,XY ovotesticular difference of sex development is a rare disorder characterized by external genital anomalies including chordee, bifid scrotum, perineal hypospadias, micropenis, abnormality of the clitoris, and abnormality of the labia. The exact cause of this condition, which presents antenatally or neonatally, is currently unknown.

name: 46,XY difference of sex development due to isolated 17,20-lyase deficiency
clinical_summary: 46,XY difference of sex development due to isolated 17,20-lyase deficiency is a rare, autosomal recessive condition characterized by male external genitalia and female internal genitalia. Symptoms include hypoplasia of the uterus, hypospadias, micropenis, decreased fertility, polycystic ovaries, and primary amenorrhea, with an onset in the neonatal period.

name: Diethylstilbestrol syndrome
clinical_summary: Diethylstilbestrol syndrome is a rare condition characterized by multiple congenital abnormalities, including hypoplasia of the uterus and various genitourinary anomalies. The condition does not follow a familial inheritance pattern.

name: Testicular agenesis
clinical_summary: Testicular agenesis is a rare condition characterized by the absence of testicular development, presenting with various genital abnormalities and hormone imbalances. The condition is associated with neonatal age of onset and unknown inheritance patterns.

name: STAT1-related autoimmune enteropathy and endocrinopathy-susceptibility to chronic infections syndrome
clinical_summary: This rare disease, STAT1-related autoimmune enteropathy and endocrinopathy-susceptibility to chronic infections syndrome, is characterized by a range of symptoms including growth delay, abnormal intestine morphology, and recurrent infections. It primarily affects infants, displaying an autosomal dominant inheritance pattern.

name: Normosmic congenital hypogonadotropic hypogonadism
inheritance: Autosomal dominant, Autosomal recessive, Multigenic/multifactorial, X-linked recessive
clinical_summary: Normosmic congenital hypogonadotropic hypogonadism is a rare disease characterized by symptoms including cryptorchidism, micropenis, and primary amenorrhea, typically presenting in infancy or at birth. It can be inherited in various patterns, including autosomal dominant, autosomal recessive, multifactorial, and X-linked recessive.

name: Parathyroid carcinoma
clinical_summary: Parathyroid carcinoma is a rare disease characterized by elevated circulating parathyroid hormone levels, hypophosphatemia, and hypercalciuria. Abnormalities in parathyroid morphology are also a feature of this disease.

name: Anaplastic thyroid carcinoma
clinical_summary: Anaplastic thyroid carcinoma is a rare and aggressive thyroid cancer that presents with symptoms such as broad neck, hoarse voice, and vocal cord paralysis. Its typical age of onset is in adults or the elderly, without a known familial inheritance component.

name: Pituitary carcinoma
key_symptoms: Malignant neoplasm of the central nervous system
clinical_summary: Pituitary carcinoma is a rare malignant neoplasm of the central nervous system characterized by the presence of pituitary prolactin cell adenoma and increased circulating levels of prolactin and ACTH. It can manifest with symptoms such as headache, and affects individuals of all ages.

name: Attenuated Chédiak-Higashi syndrome
clinical_summary: Attenuated Chédiak-Higashi syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a combination of symptoms including gingival bleeding, ocular albinism, intellectual disability, and abnormality of coagulation that typically manifest in childhood. These symptoms include bruising susceptibility and epistaxis.

name: Neonatal Marfan syndrome
clinical_summary: Neonatal Marfan syndrome is a rare autosomal dominant disorder characterized by a combination of distinct physical features. It presents in infancy or at birth with manifestations such as dolichocephaly, megalocornea, pectus carinatum, cutis laxa, ectopia lentis, and arachnodactyly.

name: Xp22.13p22.2 duplication syndrome
clinical_summary: Xp22.13p22.2 duplication syndrome is a rare X-linked recessive disorder characterized by a range of congenital abnormalities, including macroorchidism, high palate, microcephaly, and others, manifesting in infancy or neonatally. Manifestations result from the duplication of a segment of the X chromosome.

name: Moebius syndrome-axonal neuropathy-hypogonadotropic hypogonadism syndrome
clinical_summary: Moebius syndrome-axonal neuropathy-hypogonadotropic hypogonadism syndrome is a rare condition characterized by hypogonadotropic hypogonadism and distinctive facial features, including mask-like facies and external ophthalmoplegia, typically present at birth. It often involves additional neurological symptoms such as movement abnormalities and strabismus.

name: 6q terminal deletion syndrome
lab_findings: skeletal muscle hypotonia reflected by low creatine kinase
short stature reflected by low bone age
clinical_summary: 6q terminal deletion syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by various physical and ocular abnormalities, including low anterior hairline, hypertelorism, micrognathia, strabismus, hypermetropia, and nystagmus. These symptoms are typically present at birth or in infancy.

name: Xp21 deletion syndrome
clinical_summary: Xp21 deletion syndrome is a rare condition characterized by a combination of physical and developmental abnormalities including hypogonadotropic hypogonadism, global developmental delay, and distinct facial features. Its exact inheritance pattern is unknown.

name: Atypical Werner syndrome
clinical_summary: Atypical Werner syndrome is a rare condition characterized by symptoms including abnormal testis morphology, hypogonadism, deceased fertility, thin vermilion border, narrow face, and micrognathia. It is typically inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern.

name: 8p inverted duplication/deletion syndrome
clinical_summary: 8p inverted duplication/deletion syndrome is a rare congenital disorder characterized by various physical and developmental abnormalities, including hypertonia, abnormal facial shape, and spastic tetraplegia. Symptoms often present antenatally, in infancy, or neonatally, with abnormality of chromosome segregation and frontal balding also being potential features.

name: Tetragametic chimerism syndrome
clinical_summary: Tetragametic chimerism syndrome is a rare condition characterized by multiple congenital urogenital abnormalities, including cryptorchidism, abnormal testis morphology, and perineal hypospadias. It primarily presents in infancy or the neonatal period, with symptoms persisting throughout life.

name: 1q41q42 microdeletion syndrome
clinical_summary: 1q41q42 microdeletion syndrome is a rare condition characterized by distinct facial abnormalities including underdeveloped nasal alae, broad nasal tip, and upsloping palpebral fissures, often accompanied by atypical behavior. It typically presents in infancy or neonatal stages with no known inheritance pattern.

name: Hypogonadotropic hypogonadism-severe microcephaly-sensorineural hearing loss-dysmorphism syndrome
clinical_summary: Hypogonadotropic hypogonadism-severe microcephaly-sensorineural hearing loss-dysmorphism syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by hypogonadotropic hypogonadism, micropenis, and multiple congenital anomalies. Symptoms often present antenatally, during infancy, or in the neonatal period.

name: Xq12-q13.3 duplication syndrome
clinical_summary: Xq12-q13.3 duplication syndrome is a rare disease characterized by a distinct set of physical features and developmental abnormalities, including a triangular face, hypertelorism, and cryptorchidism. It is typically identified in infancy or neonatal age through its distinct X-linked recessive inheritance pattern.

name: 46,XY difference of sex development-adrenal insufficiency due to CYP11A1 deficiency
clinical_summary: 46,XY difference of sex development with adrenal insufficiency due to CYP11A1 deficiency is a rare condition characterized by ambiguous genitalia, cryptorchidism, and renal salt wasting, often presenting in infancy or neonatal stages. It can also involve decreased fertility and aplasia of the uterus.

name: Inherited isolated adrenal insufficiency due to partial CYP11A1 deficiency
clinical_summary: Inherited isolated adrenal insufficiency due to partial CYP11A1 deficiency is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a spectrum of congenital malformations and endocrine dysregulation. Manifestations include renal salt wasting, decreased fertility, and ambiguous genitalia, presenting in infancy or neonatal period.

name: Congenital adrenal hyperplasia due to 3-beta-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase deficiency
clinical_summary: Congenital adrenal hyperplasia due to 3-beta-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase deficiency presents in infancy or neonatally with a range of symptoms, including ambiguous genitalia and salt wasting. This condition is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern.

name: H syndrome
clinical_summary: H syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a combination of hearing impairment, delayed puberty, and various physical abnormalities. Symptoms include hypertrichosis, hepatosplenomegaly, lymphadenopathy, and hyperpigmentation of the skin, which typically manifest in childhood.

name: Maternal uniparental disomy of chromosome X syndrome
clinical_summary: Maternal uniparental disomy of chromosome X syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by distinct physical abnormalities and reproductive issues. It typically presents at birth with symptoms such as microcephaly, ambiguous genitalia, andazoospermia.

name: 47,XYY syndrome
clinical_summary: 47,XYY syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by distinct physical features, including tall stature, malar flattening, and low-set ears, often accompanied by developmental delays. Key behavioral and speech delays are also typical in individuals with this syndrome.

name: 45,X/46,XY mixed gonadal dysgenesis
key_symptoms: Muscle hypertrophy of the lower extremities
clinical_summary: 45,X/46,XY mixed gonadal dysgenesis is a rare condition characterized by ambiguous genitalia, short stature, and muscle hypertrophy of the lower extremities in individuals with XY chromosomal makeup. Notable symptoms include cryptorchidism, and azoospermia, which typically present at all ages.

name: X-linked intellectual disability, Van Esch type
clinical_summary: X-linked intellectual disability, Van Esch type is a rare genetic disorder characterized by male hypogonadism, cryptorchidism, microcephaly, and retrognathia. Onset typically occurs in infancy or neonatal stages, and it follows an X-linked recessive inheritance pattern.

name: X-linked intellectual disability, Cilliers type
clinical_summary: Cilliers type X-linked intellectual disability is a rare condition characterized by a range of physical and intellectual disabilities, including male hypogonadism, cryptorchidism, and microcephaly, typically presenting in infancy or neonatal periods. It follows an X-linked recessive inheritance pattern.

name: TSH-secreting pituitary adenoma
clinical_summary: TSH-secreting pituitary adenoma is a rare endocrine disorder characterized by elevated levels of circulating thyroid-stimulating hormone concentration, leading to hyperthyroidism, and can be associated with abnormalities of the pituitary gland. Additionally, it may manifest with goiter, and in males, hypogonadism.

name: Prolactinoma
clinical_summary: Prolactinoma is a rare disease characterized by the secretion of prolactin, leading to symptoms such as hypogonadism in both males and females, as well as reproductive abnormalities. It can affect individuals across various age groups, including adolescents, adults, children, and the elderly, with an autosomal dominant inheritance pattern.

name: Multiple epiphyseal dysplasia, Lowry type
clinical_summary: Multiple epiphyseal dysplasia, Lowry type is a rare disease characterized by neonatal onset of hypertelorism, micrognathia, broad nasal tip, upslanted palpebral fissure, abnormal sternum morphology, and brachydactyly. The inheritance pattern of this condition is currently unknown.

name: Multiple epiphyseal dysplasia-macrocephaly-facial dysmorphism syndrome
clinical_summary: Multiple epiphyseal dysplasia-macrocephaly-facial dysmorphism syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a combination of skeletal abnormalities and distinctive facial features, often presenting in infancy or neonatally. Key features include macrocephaly, triangular mouth, hypertelorism, wide nasal bridge, and pectus excavatum.

name: Odontochondrodysplasia
clinical_summary: Odontochondrodysplasia is a rare genetic disorder characterized by skeletal dysplasia and dental anomalies, typically presenting in infancy or neonatally with symptoms such as delayed tooth eruption and dentinogenesis imperfecta. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern, often associated with additional skeletal abnormalities including a narrow chest, platyspondyly, and scoliosis.

name: Wormian bone-multiple fractures-dentinogenesis imperfecta-skeletal dysplasia
clinical_summary: This rare disease, characterized by complex symptoms including dolichocephaly and dentinogenesis imperfecta, presents with multiple fractures in childhood. The exact inheritance pattern is unknown.

name: Sudden infant death-dysgenesis of the testes syndrome
clinical_summary: Sudden infant death-dysgenesis of the testes syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by life-threatening symptoms, including cardiac arrest and death in infancy, accompanying physical anomalies such as cryptorchidism and ambiguous genitalia. It is typically identified in infancy or neonatal period, often with concomitant gastrointestinal complications like gastroesophageal reflux.

name: Lethal acantholytic erosive disorder
clinical_summary: Lethal acantholytic erosive disorder is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by the presence of significant congenital anomalies, including absent eyelashes, eyebrows, and nails, combined with fragile skin. This condition is present antenatally and can be observed in neonates.

name: Hemoglobin Bart's fetalis syndrome
clinical_summary: Hemoglobin Bart's fetalis syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a combination of severe clinical symptoms, including hydrocephalus, pallor, polyhydramnios, oligohydramnios, congestive heart failure, and splenomegaly. It presents with an antenatal and neonatal onset, resulting in severe consequences for the affected individual.

name: Hypotonia-cystinuria syndrome
clinical_summary: Hypotonia-cystinuria syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by a combination of hypotonia and nephrolithiasis, often presenting with a range of craniofacial abnormalities in infancy and neonatal onset. This autosomal recessive condition is marked by symptoms including dolichocephaly, retrognathia, epicanthus, ptosis, and nephrolithiasis.

name: 2p21 microdeletion syndrome
clinical_summary: 2p21 microdeletion syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a range of symptoms including hypogonadism, nephrolithiasis, seizures, and global developmental delay. It typically presents in infancy or the neonatal period with hypotonia and distinctive physical features such as long eyelashes.

name: Febrile infection-related epilepsy syndrome
clinical_summary: Febrile infection-related epilepsy syndrome is a rare condition characterized by acute or subacute presentation of fever and neurological features such as sinusitis, atypical behavior, lethargy, headache, and EEG abnormalities. It can affect individuals of all ages and does not have a known genetic inheritance pattern.

name: Peripheral demyelinating neuropathy-central dysmyelinating leukodystrophy-Waardenburg syndrome-Hirschsprung disease
clinical_summary: This rare disease is characterized by a combination of peripheral demyelinating neuropathy, central dysmyelinating leukodystrophy, facial features including telecanthus and heterochromia iridis, and skin and hair abnormalities. It typically presents in infancy or neonatal life with severe neurological symptoms including seizures and intellectual disability.

name: X-linked intellectual disability, Najm type
clinical_summary: X-linked intellectual disability, Najm type is characterized by progressive microcephaly, cerebellar hypoplasia, and intellectual disability, typically presenting in infancy. This condition is inherited in an X-linked dominant pattern.

name: X-linked dominant chondrodysplasia, Chassaing-Lacombe type
clinical_summary: X-linked dominant chondrodysplasia, Chassaing-Lacombe type is a rare genetic disorder characterized by hydrocephalus, low-set ears, depressed nasal ridge, microphthalmia, thin ribs, and platyspondyly. It presents in infancy or neonatally and follows an X-linked dominant inheritance pattern.

name: Intestinal botulism
clinical_summary: Intestinal botulism is a rare disease characterized by a range of symptoms including xerostomia, ptosis, diplopia, hypotonia, dysarthria, and diarrhea. It can affect individuals of all ages, with an unknown inheritance pattern.

name: 19q13.11 microdeletion syndrome
clinical_summary: 19q13.11 microdeletion syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a combination of physical and developmental abnormalities, including hypospadias, microcephaly, and intellectual disability. Common features include delayed speech and language development and failure to thrive, often presenting in infancy or neonatal stages.

name: 17q21.31 microduplication syndrome
clinical_summary: 17q21.31 microduplication syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by craniofacial abnormalities, including abnormal dentition, high palate, malar flattening, short philtrum, outer ear abnormalities, and anteverted nares, typically presenting in infancy or at birth. The cause of this condition is not yet understood, and inheritance pattern is unknown.

name: Adult polyglucosan body disease
clinical_summary: Adult polyglucosan body disease is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a combination of neurological symptoms including neurogenic bladder, spasticity, and intellectual disability. It typically manifests in adulthood, with symptoms also including atypical behavior, urinary incontinence, and hemiparesis.

name: Combined immunodeficiency due to CRAC channel dysfunction
clinical_summary: Combined immunodeficiency due to CRAC channel dysfunction is a rare autosomal recessive disorder, manifesting in infancy or neonatal period with symptoms including chronic otitis media, hypotonia, meningitis, fever, and pneumonia. The disorder may also be associated with Amelogenesis imperfecta.

name: CLAPO syndrome
clinical_summary: CLAPO syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by congenital facial and soft tissue abnormalities, including nevus flammeus, hemihypertrophy, and lymphangioma. It presents with a range of symptoms, including overgrowth, facial asymmetry, and lymphedema, typically beginning in antenatal, infancy, or neonatal stages.

name: Cernunnos-XLF deficiency
clinical_summary: Cernunnos-XLF deficiency is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by severe developmental delays, microcephaly, and distinct facial anomalies. It presents in infancy or neonatally with features that include growth delay, bird-like facies, and a bulbous nose.

name: 6q16 microdeletion syndrome
clinical_summary: 6q16 microdeletion syndrome is a rare condition characterized by delayed speech and language development, global developmental delay, and distinct physical features such as abnormal facial shape and macrocephaly. Symptoms also include obesity and full cheeks, typically becoming apparent prenatally or in the early years of life.

name: Thymoma-hypogammaglobulinemia syndrome
clinical_summary: Thymoma-hypogammaglobulinemia syndrome is a rare condition characterized by recurrent infections and distinctive physical manifestations such as ptosis and dysphonia in adult patients. Its exact cause remains unknown due to lack of established inheritance pattern.

name: 8q22.1 microdeletion syndrome
clinical_summary: 8q22.1 microdeletion syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by physical abnormalities such as cryptorchidism, long philtrum, low-set ears, and a wide nasal bridge. These features become apparent during infancy or the neonatal period.

name: Craniosynostosis-hydrocephalus-Arnold-Chiari malformation type I-radioulnar synostosis syndrome
clinical_summary: Craniosynostosis-hydrocephalus-Arnold-Chiari malformation type I-radioulnar synostosis syndrome is a rare condition characterized by multiple congenital anomalies, typically presenting in the neonatal period. Affected individuals may exhibit a combination of symptoms including cryptorchidism, hypospadias, micropenis, and various renal abnormalities.

name: Arginine vasopressin deficiency
lab_findings: low water deprivation test adh response
inheritance: Autosomal dominant, Autosomal recessive, X-linked dominant
clinical_summary: Arginine vasopressin deficiency is a rare disease characterized by symptoms such as nocturia, depression, anxiety, diabetes insipidus, lethargy, and excessive daytime somnolence, which present in childhood. It is inherited in an autosomal dominant, autosomal recessive, or X-linked dominant pattern.

name: Wound botulism
key_symptoms: Respiratory insufficiency due to muscle weakness
clinical_summary: Wound botulism is a rare disease characterized by symptoms including urinary retention, ptosis, diplopia, dysarthria, muscle weakness, and dysphagia. It can occur at any age, although the inheritance pattern is unknown.

name: Infant botulism
clinical_summary: Infant botulism is a rare disease characterized by the onset of xerostomia, mask-like facies, and other neurological symptoms, including ptosis, abnormality of the pharynx, diplopia, and hypertension. Symptoms typically present in infancy or neonatally, with unknown inheritance pattern.

name: 5q14.3 microdeletion syndrome
clinical_summary: 5q14.3 microdeletion syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by distinct facial features and developmental delays, including short philtrum, broad forehead, upsloped palpebral fissures, and autistic behaviors. This condition typically manifests in infancy or neonatal life without a clear pattern of inheritance.

name: Foodborne botulism
clinical_summary: Foodborne botulism is a rare disease characterized by symptoms including urinary retention, xerostomia, ptosis, diplopia, dysarthria, and muscle weakness, affecting individuals of all ages. The exact inheritance pattern of foodborne botulism is unknown.

name: Hughes-Stovin syndrome
clinical_summary: Hughes-Stovin syndrome is a rare disease characterized by the combination of fever, nausea and vomiting, and pulmonary vascular complications, including pulmonary arterial hypertension and pulmonary embolism. Clinical presentation often includes respiratory symptoms such as dyspnea and hemoptysis.

name: Cardiac anomalies-short stature-joint hypermobility-facial dysmorphism syndrome
clinical_summary: This rare syndrome, known as Cardiac anomalies-short stature-joint hypermobility-facial dysmorphism syndrome, presents with distinct facial features including high palate, dolichocephaly, long face, short philtrum, broad forehead, and micrognathia in infancy or neonatal stages. It follows an autosomal dominant inheritance pattern, suggesting potential genetic susceptibility for affected individuals.

name: Toxin-mediated infectious botulism
key_symptoms: Respiratory insufficiency due to muscle weakness
lab_findings: normal lactic dehydrogenase (ldh) and aspartate aminotransferase (ast)
clinical_summary: Toxin-mediated infectious botulism is a rare disease characterized by symptoms of ptosis, diplopia, muscle weakness, dysphagia, constipation, and dyspnea in individuals of all ages. The exact cause and mode of inheritance of this disease are currently unknown.

name: Non-syndromic agammaglobulinemia
inheritance: Autosomal dominant, Autosomal recessive, X-linked recessive
clinical_summary: Non-syndromic agammaglobulinemia is a rare disease characterized by symptoms such as sinusitis, otitis media, skin rash, failure to thrive, abnormality of neutrophils, and fever, often presenting in infancy or neonatal age. It is inherited in multiple patterns including autosomal dominant, autosomal recessive, and X-linked recessive.

name: Ptosis-upper ocular movement limitation-absence of lacrimal punctum syndrome
clinical_summary: Ptosis-upper ocular movement limitation-absence of lacrimal punctum syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a distinct set of facial anomalies and ocular symptoms present from infancy or neonatal age. This condition exhibits features such as microretrognathia, hypoplasia of the maxilla, and sloping forehead.

name: Frontonasal dysplasia-alopecia-genital anomalies syndrome
clinical_summary: Frontonasal dysplasia-alopecia-genital anomalies syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a combination of prenatal and neonatal abnormalities, including developmental anomalies of the frontonasal area, hypogonadism, dental abnormalities, and brachycephaly. It typically presents with autosomal recessive inheritance and is associated with features such as cryptorchidism and small scrotum, as well as a broad philtrum.

name: 2q23.1 microdeletion syndrome
clinical_summary: 2q23.1 microdeletion syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by distinct facial features, including an open mouth and coarse facial features, present from infancy or neonatal age. Key physical characteristics include microcephaly, brachycephaly, and malar flattening.

name: 8q12 microduplication syndrome

The cause of the syndrome is unknown, with no clear pattern of inheritance identified.

name: Diffuse cutaneous systemic sclerosis
clinical_summary: Diffuse cutaneous systemic sclerosis is a rare disease characterized by a complex range of symptoms including xerostomia, carious teeth, skin abnormalities, muscle weakness, arthritis, and flexion contractures. It typically affects adults and has a multigenic/multifactorial inheritance pattern.

name: Limited cutaneous systemic sclerosis
clinical_summary: Limited cutaneous systemic sclerosis is a rare, adult-onset disease characterized by skin abnormality, pigmentation changes, and gastrointestinal symptoms. It exhibits a multigenic/multifactorial inheritance pattern.

name: Joubert syndrome with ocular defect
clinical_summary: Joubert syndrome with ocular defect is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by congenital symptoms such as intellectual disability, ataxia, hypotonia, and global developmental delay. Affected individuals often present with retinal dystrophy and oculomotor apraxia, typically manifesting in infancy or the neonatal period.
