name: Microduplication Xp11.22p11.23 syndrome
clinical_summary: Microduplication Xp11.22p11.23 syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by features such as delayed speech and language development, precocious puberty, and intellectual disability. Onset typically occurs in infancy or neonatal stages, often accompanied by symptoms like seizures and a hoarse voice, with a complex inheritance pattern of X-linked dominant.

name: 17p13.3 microduplication syndrome
clinical_summary: 17p13.3 microduplication syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a cluster of facial and physical features, including a narrow mouth, hypertelorism, high forehead, low-set ears, and wide nose. This condition typically manifests in infancy or in the neonatal period, with a short neck also being a notable feature.

name: Combined immunodeficiency due to DOCK8 deficiency
clinical_summary: Combined immunodeficiency due to DOCK8 deficiency is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by chronic otitis media, recurrent respiratory infections, and increased circulating IgE levels, presenting in infancy or at birth. Affected individuals commonly experience atopic conditions, including asthma and atopic dermatitis.

name: Xeroderma pigmentosum-Cockayne syndrome complex
clinical_summary: Xeroderma pigmentosum-Cockayne syndrome complex is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a combination of neurological and ophthalmological symptoms, including hydrocephalus, microcephaly, hearing impairment, and visual disorders such as retinopathy, nystagmus, and optic atrophy. This complex typically presents in infancy or neonatal stage.

name: Joubert syndrome with renal defect
clinical_summary: Joubert syndrome with renal defect is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a combination of nephropathy, oculomotor apraxia, intellectual disability, ataxia, hypotonia, and global developmental delay. It presents in infancy or neonatal period with these severe symptoms.

name: Juvenile primary lateral sclerosis
clinical_summary: Juvenile primary lateral sclerosis is a rare neurodegenerative disease characterized by progressive muscle weakness, spasticity, and spastic gait, typically presenting in childhood. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern.

name: 1q44 microdeletion syndrome
clinical_summary: 1q44 microdeletion syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by physical abnormalities, including a thin vermilion border, microcephaly, epicanthus, hypertelorism, and micrognathia. The condition typically presents in infancy or the neonatal period.

name: 4q21 microdeletion syndrome
lab_findings: decreased insulin-like growth factor binding protein 3 (igfbp3)
clinical_summary: 4q21 microdeletion syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by physical features including full cheeks, hypertelorism, short philtrum, broad forehead, low-set ears, and short neck, typically presenting in infancy or at birth. The inheritance pattern of this condition is currently unknown.

name: Hypohidrotic ectodermal dysplasia
inheritance: Autosomal dominant, Autosomal recessive, X-linked recessive
clinical_summary: Hypohidrotic ectodermal dysplasia is a rare congenital disorder characterized by multiple systemic symptoms including nephrotic syndrome, abnormal dentition, xerostomia, and facial abnormalities. Its age of onset is neonatal and it follows a pattern of inheritance that includes autosomal dominant, autosomal recessive, and X-linked recessive modes.

name: 15q11q13 microduplication syndrome
clinical_summary: 15q11q13 microduplication syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by symptoms such as intellectual disability, delayed speech and language development, and compulsive behaviors, typically presenting in infancy or neonatal stages. Clinical features of this condition may also include global developmental delay, hypotonia, and attention deficit hyperactivity disorder.

name: 6p22 microdeletion syndrome
clinical_summary: 6p22 microdeletion syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by neonatal to infancy onset of hydronephrosis, hydrocephalus, epicanthus, low-set ears, overfolded helix, and short neck. The inheritance pattern of this disorder is unknown.

name: 2q32q33 deletion syndrome
clinical_summary: 2q32q33 deletion syndrome is a rare condition characterized by various craniofacial anomalies, including cleft palate, high palate, thin vermilion border, micrognathia, high forehead, and low-set ears. Onset of symptoms typically occurs in infancy or at birth.

name: 2q31.1 microdeletion syndrome
clinical_summary: 2q31.1 microdeletion syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by intellectual disability, global developmental delay, and distinct facial anomalies, including microcephaly, long philtrum, micrognathia, and low-set ears. Onset typically occurs in infancy or neonatal period, with an uncertain inheritance pattern.

name: 1q21.1 microdeletion syndrome
lab_findings: * low imipramine binding ratio of alpha 1 antitrypsin
clinical_summary: 1q21.1 microdeletion syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by high palate, microcephaly, and other distinct facial features, typically presenting at birth or in infancy. This disorder is inherited in an autosomal dominant manner.

name: Autosomal recessive Stickler syndrome
clinical_summary: Autosomal recessive Stickler syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by early childhood onset of sensorineural hearing impairment, eye abnormalities, and skeletal anomalies, including epiphyseal dysplasia and facial features such as a flat face. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern.

name: Isolated aniridia
clinical_summary: Isolated aniridia is a rare genetic disorder characterized by the absence or severe hypoplasia of the iris, leading to visual impairment and associated symptoms such as nystagmus and reduced visual acuity. It is typically inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern and presents in the neonatal period.

name: Beta-thalassemia major
clinical_summary: Beta-thalassemia major is a rare, severe form of anemia characterized by pallor, hypochromic microcytic anemia, and failure of hemoglobin A production. It typically presents in infancy with autosomal recessive inheritance.

name: Usher syndrome type 1
clinical_summary: Usher syndrome type 1 is a rare autosomal recessive disease characterized by progressive sensorineural hearing impairment and rod-cone dystrophy leading to visual loss. It presents in infancy or neonatal stages with morphological abnormalities in the cochlea and an abnormal electroretinogram, progressing to scotoma and further visual impairment.

name: Usher syndrome type 2
clinical_summary: Usher syndrome type 2 is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by profound sensorineural hearing impairment and progressive vision loss due to retinal dystrophy. It typically presents in infancy or neonatal period with abnormal findings of the inner ear and retina.

name: Classical-like Ehlers-Danlos syndrome type 1
clinical_summary: Classical-like Ehlers-Danlos syndrome type 1 is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by symptoms such as sensory neuropathy, thin skin, and muscle weakness. It typically presents in childhood, infancy, or even neonatally, with features of hyperextensible skin and bruising susceptibility.

name: Dermotrichic syndrome
clinical_summary: Dermotrichic syndrome is a rare condition characterized by neonatal onset of abnormal vertebral morphology, proportionate short stature, and multiple cutaneous and nail abnormalities. Associated symptoms include depressed nasal bridge, ichthyosis, nail dystrophy, and hyperconvex toenail, though the inheritance pattern is unknown.

name: Familial gestational hyperthyroidism
clinical_summary: Familial gestational hyperthyroidism is a rare autosomal dominant disorder characterized by thyrotoxicosis with diffuse goiter and associated symptoms including goiter, weight loss, diarrhea, and hand tremor. Affected individuals typically present with thyroid hyperplasia in adolescence or adulthood.

name: Symptomatic form of HFE-related hemochromatosis
clinical_summary: HFE-related hemochromatosis is a rare autosomal recessive disorder, characterized by hyperpigmentation of the skin, increased circulating ferritin concentration, and abnormal iron homeostasis. Key symptoms include abdominal pain and hepatomegaly, presenting in adulthood.

name: Zebra body myopathy
clinical_summary: Zebra body myopathy is a rare disorder characterized by neonatal onset of neck muscle weakness, torticollis, and global developmental delay. It may also present with decreased fetal movement and distal muscle weakness.

name: Kidney tubulopathy-dilated cardiomyopathy syndrome
clinical_summary: This rare, inherited disorder primarily affects children, characterized by dilated cardiomyopathy and associated electrolyte disturbances, including hypokalemia, hypercalciuria, hypocalcemia, hypomagnesemia, and hypermagnesiuria. Inheritance occurs in an autosomal dominant pattern.

name: Familial hyperthyroidism due to mutations in TSH receptor
clinical_summary: Familial hyperthyroidism due to mutations in TSH receptor is a rare genetic disorder characterized by goiter, weight loss, diarrhea, hand tremor, and thyroid hyperplasia. It follows an autosomal dominant inheritance pattern and can affect individuals of all ages, with a notable association with being small for gestational age.

name: Cono-spondylar dysplasia
clinical_summary: Cono-spondylar dysplasia is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a combination of skeletal abnormalities, including hypertelorism, low-set ears, and brachydactyly, which present from an antenatal stage through infancy and neonatal periods. The condition involves a range of anomalies affecting the skeletal system and associated facial features.

name: Bazex syndrome
clinical_summary: Bazex syndrome is a rare condition characterized by palmoplantar keratoderma, nail dystrophy, and yellow nails alongside other symptoms. It typically presents in adults with a combination of hyperkeratosis, parakeratosis, and a neoplasm.

name: Tetrasomy 5p syndrome
clinical_summary: Tetrasomy 5p syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by symptoms such as seizure, global developmental delay, and cerebellar hypoplasia, among others, that typically manifest in the antenatal or neonatal period.

name: Autoimmune hemolytic anemia-autoimmune thrombocytopenia-primary immunodeficiency syndrome due to TPP2 deficiency
clinical_summary: This rare disease, inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern, is characterized by hemolytic anemia, thrombocytopenia, and immunodeficiency due to TPP2 deficiency, often presenting with various symptoms including hemiparesis and splenomegaly in childhood or infancy.

name: Monosomy 18q syndrome
clinical_summary: Monosomy 18q syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a range of physical anomalies, including micropenis, mandibular prognathia, and macrotia, among others. This condition typically presents with antenatal or neonatal onset and exhibits an autosomal dominant inheritance pattern.

name: Chylomicron retention disease
clinical_summary: Chylomicron retention disease is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by elevated liver enzymes, failure to thrive, and growth delay. It presents with symptoms such as retinopathy, steatorrhea, and fat malabsorption, typically manifesting in infancy or childhood.

name: Contractures-developmental delay-Pierre Robin syndrome
clinical_summary: Contractures-developmental delay-Pierre Robin syndrome is a rare condition characterized by delayed speech and language development, motor delay, and distinctive facial features, including glossoptosis, cleft palate, micrognathia, and overfolded helix. The age of onset is antenatal or neonatal, although inheritance patterns are currently unknown.

name: Distal duplication 17q syndrome
clinical_summary: Distal duplication 17q syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by symptoms including global developmental delay, intellectual disability, cryptorchidism, vesicoureteral reflux, wide mouth, and cleft palate, which manifest at a neonatal age. The inheritance pattern of this disorder remains unknown.

name: 20q11.2 microdeletion syndrome
clinical_summary: 20q11.2 microdeletion syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by distinctive facial features, intrauterine growth retardation, and hearing impairment. It presents with symptoms from the antenatal or neonatal period, with no known familial inheritance pattern.

name: Distal deletion 1q syndrome
clinical_summary: Distal deletion 1q syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by short stature, thin vermilion border, and congenital malformations. The condition presents neonatally with symptoms including microcephaly, epicanthus, round face, and hypertelorism.

name: Isolated sedoheptulokinase deficiency
clinical_summary: Isolated sedoheptulokinase deficiency is a rare autosomal recessive disease characterized by symptoms including inguinal hernia, renal insufficiency, and macrocephaly. It manifests antenatally or neonatally, presenting with distinctive craniofacial features and renal abnormalities.

name: Autosomal dominant myopia-midfacial retrusion-sensorineural hearing loss-rhizomelic dysplasia syndrome
clinical_summary: Autosomal dominant myopia-midfacial retrusion-sensorineural hearing loss-rhizomelic dysplasia syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by multiple congenital anomalies including cleft palate, micrognathia, narrow chest, rhizomelic dysplasia, and micromelia. This autosomal dominant disorder affects individuals at birth, presenting with symptoms such as upper airway obstruction.

name: Evans syndrome
key_symptoms: Neutropenia in presence of anti-neutropil antibodies
clinical_summary: Evans syndrome is a rare autoimmune disorder characterized by the simultaneous presence of autoimmune hemolytic anemia and immune thrombocytopenia, manifesting with symptoms such as petechiae, epistaxis, jaundice, bruising susceptibility, and pallor. The condition can affect individuals in both adulthood and childhood, without a known inheritance pattern.

name: Immune thrombocytopenia
key_symptoms: Liver kidney microsome type 1 antibody positivity
clinical_summary: Immune thrombocytopenia is a rare disease characterized by thrombocytopenia, accompanied by symptoms such as petechiae and purpura. It can also occur with increased risk of arterial thrombosis and is associated with the presence of anti-glutamic acid decarboxylase and anti-thyroid peroxidase antibodies.

name: Primary biliary cholangitis
clinical_summary: Primary biliary cholangitis is a rare disease characterized by symptoms such as hyperpigmentation of the skin, cirrhosis, and conjugated hyperbilirubinemia, among others. It affects individuals across a range of ages, including adolescents, adults, and the elderly, and is believed to have a multigenic/multifactorial or unknown inheritance pattern.

name: Atopic keratoconjunctivitis
clinical_summary: Atopic keratoconjunctivitis is a rare disease characterized by symptoms including keratitis, dry skin, and keratoconjunctivitis sicca. Clinical manifestations also include corneal opacity, abnormal eyelid morphology, and blepharitis.

name: Myasthenia gravis
clinical_summary: Myasthenia gravis is a rare disease characterized by muscle weakness, which can present in various forms, including ptosis and ophthalmoparesis. Abnormalities of the thymus are also commonly associated with this condition, affecting individuals of all ages.

name: Carpenter syndrome
clinical_summary: Carpenter syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a combination of tall stature, macrocephaly, oxycephaly, narrow face, and epicanthus, often presenting at birth or in early childhood. It is primarily inherited in an autosomal recessive manner and may also be associated with cryptorchidism.

name: CAMOS syndrome
clinical_summary: CAMOS syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by renal insufficiency, nephrotic syndrome, and neurological symptoms including seizures, spasticity, dysarthria, and brain atrophy, typically presenting in infancy or at birth. The disorder affects renal and neurological systems with severe impact.

name: X-linked microcephaly-growth retardation-prognathism-cryptorchidism syndrome
lab_findings: no other confirmed lab findings for this rare disease.
clinical_summary: This rare neurodevelopmental disorder, X-linked microcephaly-growth retardation-prognathism-cryptorchidism syndrome, presents with a range of congenital malformations and developmental delays. Characterized by an X-linked recessive inheritance pattern, it primarily affects males with antenatal and neonatal onset of symptoms.

name: COG2-CDG
clinical_summary: COG2-CDG is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by intellectual disability, decreased liver function, and severe neurological deterioration in infancy. Key features of the condition include abnormal facial shape, cerebral atrophy, and hypoplasia of the corpus callosum.

name: 3p25.3 microdeletion syndrome
clinical_summary: 3p25.3 microdeletion syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by significant delays in motor and speech development, often present from infancy. It is also marked by distinct facial features and abnormal repetitive behaviors, such as those seen in infancy and early childhood.

name: Stargardt disease
clinical_summary: Stargardt disease is a rare genetic disorder characterized by macular degeneration, central scotoma, and color vision defect, often accompanied by abnormal foveal and choroid morphologies. It is associated with visual-evoked potential abnormalities and can be inherited in an autosomal dominant or recessive pattern, typically manifesting from childhood to adulthood.

name: Estrogen resistance syndrome
clinical_summary: Estrogen resistance syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by hypoplasia of the uterus, tall stature, and primary amenorrhea. Key features include increased circulating gonadotropin levels, osteopenia, and osteoporosis, manifesting in adolescence.

name: Primary hypergonadotropic hypogonadism-partial alopecia syndrome
clinical_summary: Primary hypergonadotropic hypogonadism-partial alopecia syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by neonatal onset of reproductive and craniofacial abnormalities, including cryptorchidism, thin upper lip vermilion, and abnormal eyebrow morphology. Affected individuals often present with primary amenorrhea, infertility, and impotence later in life.

name: Keppen-Lubinsky syndrome
clinical_summary: Keppen-Lubinsky syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a distinct set of physical and developmental abnormalities, including microcephaly, intellectual disability, and hyperreflexia, typically evident at neonatal age. Key physical features include an open mouth, short philtrum, and micrognathia.

name: Stiff person spectrum disorder
clinical_summary: Stiff person spectrum disorder is a rare neurological disorder characterized by intermittent painful muscle spasms, emotional lability, anxiety, hyperhidrosis, falls, and abnormal EMG results. It affects individuals across various age groups, including infancy, childhood, adolescence, adulthood, and old age.

name: Progressive supranuclear palsy
key_symptoms: Neuronal loss in central nervous system
clinical_summary: Progressive supranuclear palsy is a rare neurodegenerative disease characterized by supranuclear gaze palsy, dysphagia, postural instability, and an unsteady gait progressing to falls. It predominantly affects adults and the elderly.

name: Laminin subunit alpha 2-related congenital muscular dystrophy
clinical_summary: Laminin subunit alpha 2-related congenital muscular dystrophy is a rare disorder characterized by muscular dystrophy, hypotonia, and metabolic/homeostasis abnormalities. It presents antenatally, in infancy, or neonatally with symptoms including motor delay, muscle weakness, and weak cry, and is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern.

name: Carney complex
key_symptoms: Atypical nevi in non-sun exposed areas
clinical_summary: Carney complex is a rare, autosomal dominant disorder characterized by the early onset of multiple endocrine symptoms, including pigmented micronodular adrenocortical disease, ovarian cysts, and thyroid abnormalities. Affected individuals often present with multiple lentigines in infancy or neonatal stages.

name: Aromatase deficiency
clinical_summary: Aromatase deficiency is a rare disease characterized by osteopenia, osteoporosis, growth delay, obesity, bone pain, and delayed epiphyseal ossification, and affects individuals of all ages. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern, indicating a genetic deficiency that can be passed from parents to offspring if both parents are carriers.

name: Familial median cleft of the upper and lower lips
clinical_summary: Familial median cleft of the upper and lower lips is a rare condition characterized by neonatal onset of a median cleft lip, associated with abnormalities in the mandible, midface morphology, and maxilla, often complicated by diastema. The mode of inheritance remains unknown.

name: Lissencephaly type 3-metacarpal bone dysplasia syndrome
key_symptoms: Partial agenesis of the corpus callosum
clinical_summary: Lissencephaly type 3-metacarpal bone dysplasia syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by various neurological abnormalities including lissencephaly and cerebellar hypoplasia. This condition is associated with microcephaly, facial edema, pachygyria, and partial agenesis of the corpus callosum.

name: 7p22.1 microduplication syndrome
clinical_summary: 7p22.1 microduplication syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a spectrum of physical and developmental abnormalities, manifesting in infancy or neonatal age. Clinical features include cryptorchidism, abnormal kidney morphology, macrocephaly, hypertelorism, and abnormal outer ear anatomy, with impaired speech and language development.

name: Autosomal recessive frontotemporal pachygyria
lab_findings: hypotrophied brain with pachygyria on imaging
normal to low white blood cell count
clinical_summary: Autosomal recessive frontotemporal pachygyria is a rare disease characterized by infancy-onset global developmental delay, delayed speech, and other neurological symptoms. The condition is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern and presents with distinct features such as pachygyria and hypotonia.

name: Woodhouse-Sakati syndrome
lab_findings: Decreased response to growth hormone stimulation test
clinical_summary: Woodhouse-Sakati syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a distinctive set of physical and hormonal abnormalities, including hypoplasia of the uterus, micropenis, and hypogonadism, typically presenting in adolescence or childhood. This condition is further complicated by additional features such as hypothyroidism and delayed puberty.

name: Mitochondrial DNA-related cardiomyopathy and hearing loss
clinical_summary: This rare disease is characterized by the combination of sensorineural hearing impairment, ataxia, gait disturbance, muscle weakness, slurred speech, and hypertrophic cardiomyopathy. Symptoms may present in adults or children, indicating a variable age of onset.

name: Birdshot chorioretinopathy
clinical_summary: Birdshot chorioretinopathy is a rare disease characterized by inflammatory and structural abnormalities of the eye, including retinal thinning, macular hole, epiretinal membrane, and enlarged blind spot. It most commonly affects adults and the elderly.

name: Multiple myeloma
clinical_summary: Multiple myeloma is a rare disease characterized by osteopenia, pathologic fracture, nephrotic syndrome, nephropathy, pallor, and anemia in adults. It typically presents with systemic complications, without a known familial inheritance.

name: Herpes simplex virus stromal keratitis
clinical_summary: Herpes simplex virus stromal keratitis is a rare condition characterized by reduced visual acuity and herpetiform corneal ulceration, often accompanied by symptoms such as deep anterior chamber and corneal stromal edema. It can affect individuals of all ages.

name: Transaldolase deficiency
clinical_summary: Transaldolase deficiency is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by severe early onset symptoms, including cirrhosis, hepatosplenomegaly, thrombocytopenia, anemia, and abnormalities in glutamine metabolism.

name: Congenital hypothyroidism due to maternal intake of antithyroid drugs
clinical_summary: Congenital hypothyroidism due to maternal intake of antithyroid drugs presents with symptoms such as goiter, elevated circulating thyroid-stimulating hormone concentration, and decreased circulating thyroxine level. This rare condition often manifests in infancy or the neonatal period with associated symptoms including macroglossia and abdominal distention.

name: LIPE-related familial partial lipodystrophy
clinical_summary: LIPE-related familial partial lipodystrophy is a rare genetic disorder characterized by polycystic ovaries, insulin-resistant diabetes mellitus, and other secondary metabolic effects. It typically presents in adulthood with an autosomal recessive inheritance pattern.

name: CIDEC-related familial partial lipodystrophy
clinical_summary: CIDEC-related familial partial lipodystrophy is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by insulin-resistant diabetes mellitus, polycystic ovaries, and other metabolic dysregulations. Symptoms typically begin in adolescence.

name: Short stature, Brussels type
clinical_summary: Short stature, Brussels type is a rare disease characterized by the combination of growth delay, abnormal facial shape, short stature, and associated anatomical anomalies such as horseshoe kidney and microretrognathia, often accompanied by macrocephaly. The age of onset occurs in infancy or neonatal period.

name: Neovascular glaucoma
clinical_summary: Neovascular glaucoma is a rare condition characterized by visual loss, associated with various ocular abnormalities including iris and retinal changes. It primarily affects adults and the elderly, presenting with symptoms such as iris neovascularization and retinopathy.

name: Developmental and speech delay due to SOX5 deficiency
clinical_summary: This rare disease is characterized by developmental and speech delay resulting from a deficiency in the SOX5 gene, presenting with various physical manifestations including hyperplasia of the maxilla and strabismus. It typically affects individuals from infancy, with symptoms such as delayed speech and language development and strabismus observed early in life.

name: Idiopathic pulmonary arterial hypertension
key_symptoms: Edema of the dorsum of feet
clinical_summary: Idiopathic pulmonary arterial hypertension is a rare disease characterized by elevated pulmonary artery pressure and increased pulmonary vascular resistance, leading to right ventricular hypertrophy and potential symptoms such as syncope. It affects individuals of all ages and has no known inheritance pattern.

name: Eiken syndrome
clinical_summary: Eiken syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by epiphyseal dysplasia and associated physical abnormalities including broad palms and abnormal fingertips, often presenting in infancy or neonatal age. It follows an autosomal recessive inheritance pattern.

name: Leukoencephalopathy with bilateral anterior temporal lobe cysts
lab_findings: mri findings of bilateral anterior temporal lobe cysts
elevated serum very long chain fatty acids
clinical_summary: Leukoencephalopathy with bilateral anterior temporal lobe cysts is a rare neurological disorder characterized by symptoms including a Babinski sign and functional motor deficit, present from infancy or neonatal age, often resulting in intellectual disability and global developmental delay. The underlying cause and inheritance pattern of this condition remain unknown.

name: Cardiomyopathy-hypotonia-lactic acidosis syndrome
clinical_summary: Cardiomyopathy-hypotonia-lactic acidosis syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by neonatal onset of hypertrophic cardiomyopathy, hypotonia, metabolic acidosis, and elevated lactate levels due to abnormal mitochondrial function. It is a severe and often fatal condition typically associated with significant morbidity from infancy.

name: GNE myopathy
clinical_summary: GNE myopathy is a rare inherited disease characterized by muscle weakness, rimmed vacuoles, and mildly elevated creatine kinase, typically presenting in adolescence or adulthood. The condition shows both autosomal dominant and autosomal recessive inheritance patterns.

name: Autosomal recessive spastic paraplegia type 57
clinical_summary: Autosomal recessive spastic paraplegia type 57 is a rare, inherited disorder characterized by symptoms including spastic paraplegia, optic atrophy, and spasticity that typically presents in infancy. Clinical manifestations include inability to walk, abnormal Babinski sign, and abnormality of the Achilles tendon.

name: PPARG-related familial partial lipodystrophy
key_symptoms: Loss of subcutaneous adipose tissue in limbs
clinical_summary: PPARG-related familial partial lipodystrophy is a rare disease characterized by the loss of subcutaneous adipose tissue in limbs, insulin-resistant diabetes, and associated metabolic abnormalities. It typically presents in adulthood with an autosomal dominant inheritance pattern.

name: Infantile nephropathic cystinosis
clinical_summary: Infantile nephropathic cystinosis is a rare disease characterized by renal tubular dysfunction, ocular symptoms such as corneal crystals and photophobia, and systemic findings including failure to thrive and growth delay. It typically presents in infancy and follows an autosomal recessive inheritance pattern, often resulting in metabolic acidosis.

name: RIN2 syndrome
clinical_summary: RIN2 syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by distinctive facial features including coarse facial features, long philtrum, and downslanted palpebral fissures. Additional symptoms include abnormal lip morphology, gingival overgrowth, and high palate.

name: Pseudoprogeria syndrome
clinical_summary: Pseudoprogeria syndrome is a rare congenital disorder characterized by microcephaly, distinctive bird-like facies, and other physical abnormalities such as a convex nasal ridge, joint stiffness, thin skin, and glaucoma. The disease onset typically occurs in infancy or at the neonatal stage, with an unknown inheritance pattern.

name: Insulin autoimmune syndrome
clinical_summary: Insulin autoimmune syndrome is a rare adult-onset condition characterized by insulin-resistant diabetes mellitus, often associated with autoimmune and inflammatory symptoms. It is not an inherited disorder, typically affecting adults and the elderly.

name: Acrocephalopolydactyly
clinical_summary: Acrocephalopolydactyly is a rare autosomal recessive disease characterized by developmental abnormalities in infancy or neonatal onset, including abnormalities of the mouth and head. Common features of the disease include oxycephaly, epicanthus, hypertelorism, and a depressed nasal ridge.

name: AKT2-related familial partial lipodystrophy
clinical_summary: AKT2-related familial partial lipodystrophy is a rare, autosomal dominant disorder characterized by a subset of symptoms including acanthosis nigricans, hepatic steatosis, hypertriglyceridemia, hepatomegaly, decreased serum leptin, and increased intraabdominal fat. It typically presents in adulthood.

name: Atypical juvenile parkinsonism
clinical_summary: Atypical juvenile parkinsonism is a rare disease characterized by a range of symptoms including inability to walk, slowed slurred speech, intellectual disability, seizures, and hyporeflexia, typically presenting in adolescence or adulthood. The disease's inheritance pattern is autosomal recessive.

name: Friedreich ataxia
clinical_summary: Friedreich ataxia is a rare, autosomal recessive neurodegenerative disease characterized by adolescent or childhood onset of symptoms including areflexia of lower limbs, scoliosis, urinary bladder sphincter dysfunction, abnormal EKG, and sensory axonal neuropathy leading to falls.

name: 3MC syndrome
clinical_summary: 3MC syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by hearing impairment, ocular and facial anomalies, and limb abnormalities. It manifests prenatally or at birth with symptoms including hearing impairment and physical deformities such as ptosis and radioulnar synostosis.

name: Distal 16p11.2 microdeletion syndrome
clinical_summary: Distal 16p11.2 microdeletion syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by distinctive facial features and severe retinal dystrophy, typically presenting antenatally or at birth. Individuals with this condition often exhibit features such as a narrow mouth, low anterior hairline, and prominent nasal bridge.

name: Paternal uniparental disomy of chromosome X syndrome
clinical_summary: Paternal uniparental disomy of chromosome X syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a combination of physical features including micropenis, short neck, wide intermamillary distance, shield chest, and short metacarpal appearing at birth. The underlying inheritance pattern of this condition is currently unknown.

name: FGFR2-related bent bone dysplasia
clinical_summary: FGFR2-related bent bone dysplasia is a rare disease characterized by a combination of craniofacial abnormalities and developmental issues, with symptoms including gingival overgrowth, hypertelorism, and micrognathia. This condition is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern and manifests antenatally or neonatally.

name: Dent disease
clinical_summary: Dent disease is a rare, X-linked recessive disorder characterized by renal insufficiency and associated complications such as renal tubular atrophy, proteinuria, and renal phosphate wasting. It typically presents in childhood.

name: Familial isolated hyperparathyroidism
clinical_summary: Familial isolated hyperparathyroidism is a rare, autosomal dominant disorder characterized by the combination of parathyroid adenoma, leading to elevated calcium levels and subsequently causing secondary manifestations such as nephrocalcinosis, chondrocalcinosis, osteopenia, hypophosphatemia, and hypercalciuria. It typically manifests in adulthood.

name: AL amyloidosis
clinical_summary: AL amyloidosis is a rare disease characterized by a range of systemic symptoms including abnormal kidney function and cardiac abnormalities, presenting primarily in adults. Common manifestations of the disease include nephrotic syndrome, proteinuria, and hypertrophic cardiomyopathy, often accompanied by fatigue.

name: Wild type ATTR amyloidosis
clinical_summary: Wild type ATTR amyloidosis is a rare disease primarily affecting adults and the elderly, characterized by symptoms of congestive heart failure, cardiomyopathy, and cardiac events. These symptoms often include pleural effusion, abnormal electrocardiogram findings, and abnormal pulmonary interstitial morphology.

name: Congenital hyperinsulinism due to HNF4A deficiency
clinical_summary: Congenital hyperinsulinism due to HNF4A deficiency is a rare disease characterized by neonatal or infantile onset of hyperinsulinemic hypoglycemia, proteinuria, agitation, hyperhidrosis, and pallor. It is an autosomal dominant disorder.

name: 15q overgrowth syndrome
clinical_summary: 15q overgrowth syndrome is a rare condition characterized by a cluster of congenital anomalies including horseshoe kidney, hydronephrosis, and coarse facial features. Symptoms present antenatally, in infancy, and in the neonatal period.

name: Insulin-resistance syndrome type B
clinical_summary: Insulin-resistance syndrome type B is a rare condition characterized by hyperinsulinemia, insulin resistance, and other associated symptoms such as glucose intolerance and acanthosis nigricans. It occurs in various age groups including adolescent, adult, childhood, and the elderly, with an unclear inheritance pattern.

name: Pituitary gigantism
clinical_summary: Pituitary gigantism is a rare disorder characterized by excessive growth hormone secretion, leading to symptoms such as tall stature, coarse facial features, and mandibular prognathia. It typically manifests in adolescence, childhood, or infancy, with autosomal dominant and X-linked dominant inheritance patterns.

name: Overgrowth-macrocephaly-facial dysmorphism syndrome
clinical_summary: Overgrowth-macrocephaly-facial dysmorphism syndrome is a rare disease characterized by tall stature, macrocephaly, and facial dysmorphic features, including a thick lower lip vermilion and thin upper lip vermilion. This condition is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern, with symptoms present at birth or manifesting in infancy or neonatal stages.

name: Rapid-onset childhood obesity-hypothalamic dysfunction-hypoventilation-autonomic dysregulation syndrome
lab_findings: Decreased response to growth hormone stimulation test
clinical_summary: Rapid-onset childhood obesity-hypothalamic dysfunction-hypoventilation-autonomic dysregulation syndrome is a rare condition characterized by various physical and behavioral anomalies including macrocephaly, hypertelorism, and autistic behavior. It is associated with infancy or childhood onset and can present with additional symptoms such as sensorineural hearing impairment, anteverted nares, and decreased lacrimation.

name: Isolated permanent neonatal diabetes mellitus
key_symptoms: Contractures of the joints of the lower limbs
clinical_summary: Isolated permanent neonatal diabetes mellitus is a rare disease characterized by early onset diabetes and various congenital anomalies, including arthrogryposis and distinctive facial features. It can be inherited in an autosomal dominant or autosomal recessive pattern, with symptoms present at birth.

name: Growth delay due to insulin-like growth factor type 1 deficiency
clinical_summary: Growth delay due to insulin-like growth factor type 1 deficiency is a rare disease characterized by microcephaly, hearing impairment, and behavioral abnormalities. It is typically inherited in an autosomal recessive manner and presents with symptoms starting in utero or in infancy.

name: Lysosomal acid lipase deficiency
clinical_summary: Lysosomal acid lipase deficiency is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by symptoms including jaundice, xanthomatosis, and hepatic failure. The disease can present at various ages, from infancy to adulthood.

name: Duplication of the pituitary gland
clinical_summary: Duplication of the pituitary gland is a rare disease characterized by multiple craniofacial abnormalities, including wide mouth, abnormal tongue, cleft palate, brachyturricephaly, microcephaly, and retrognathia. The disease typically presents in infancy or at birth, with unknown inheritance pattern.

name: Ovarian hyperstimulation syndrome
clinical_summary: Ovarian hyperstimulation syndrome is a rare disease characterized by a combination of ovarian cyst formation, hirsutism, ascites, and gastrointestinal symptoms such as nausea and vomiting, typically presenting in adolescents and adults. The exact cause is unclear, and this condition does not appear to have a familial inheritance pattern.

name: Ovarian fibroma
clinical_summary: Ovarian fibroma is a rare disease characterized by abnormal ovarian growth, associated with symptoms such as abdominal pain, and potentially life-threatening complications including ascites and peritonitis. The age of onset is typically in adulthood.

name: Cataract-growth hormone deficiency-sensory neuropathy-sensorineural hearing loss-skeletal dysplasia syndrome
lab_findings: Decreased response to growth hormone stimulation test
clinical_summary: This rare autosomal recessive syndrome, manifest in infancy or childhood, presents with symptoms of cataract, progressive sensorineural hearing impairment, narrow mouth, thick eyebrow, and skeletal dysplasia. Growth hormone deficiency and sensory neuropathy are also characteristic features of this disease.

name: Intellectual disability-seizures-macrocephaly-obesity syndrome
clinical_summary: This rare disease, characterized by intellectual disability, seizures, macrocephaly, and obesity, presents with a range of distinctive physical features including macroglossia and hypertelorism, typically beginning in infancy or the neonatal period. Symptoms also include hearing impairment and astigmatism, contributing to the syndrome's overall complex manifestations.

name: 1p21.3 microdeletion syndrome
clinical_summary: 1p21.3 microdeletion syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by macrocephaly, distinctive facial features, and astigmatism. Its exact inheritance pattern and age of onset are currently unknown.

name: Trisomy 18p syndrome
clinical_summary: Trisomy 18p syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by distinct facial features such as a thin vermilion border, sloping forehead, and underdeveloped nasal alae. The disorder can manifest at various ages, from infancy to adulthood.

name: Developmental delay-language impairment-dopa responsive dystonia-parkinsonism syndrome due to 2q24 microdeletion
clinical_summary: A Developmental delay-language impairment-dopa responsive dystonia-parkinsonism syndrome is a rare disorder due to a 2q24 microdeletion. Characterized by various physical and behavioral abnormalities including cleft palate, abnormal iris morphology, and hand clenching.

name: Distal Xq28 microduplication syndrome
clinical_summary: Distal Xq28 microduplication syndrome is a rare, X-linked recessive disorder characterized by multiple congenital anomalies, including short lingual frenulum, thick lower lip, and high palate. Symptoms of this condition, which typically present in infancy or at birth, also include open mouth and microcephaly.

name: Benign schwannoma
lab_findings: Abnormality of peripheral nervous system electrophysiology
clinical_summary: Benign schwannoma is a rare disease characterized by abnormal morphology of the cranial nerve and presence of schwannoma. It typically affects adults and the elderly with manifestations including vestibular schwannoma and peripheral schwannoma.

name: SHOX-related short stature
clinical_summary: SHOX-related short stature is a rare genetic disorder characterized by short stature, micrognathia, high palate, short neck, obesity, and short foot, with symptoms typically presenting in infancy or neonatal period. This autosomal dominant disorder leads to variable growth and development impairments.

name: X small rings syndrome
clinical_summary: X small rings syndrome is a rare condition characterized by a distinct pattern of facial features, including thin upper lip vermilion, coarse facial features, epicanthus, hypertelorism, long philtrum, and protruding ears. It manifests antenatally or neonatally and its inheritance pattern is currently unknown.

name: Intellectual disability-obesity-brain malformations-facial dysmorphism syndrome
clinical_summary: This rare disease is characterized by intellectual disability, obesity, brain malformations, and distinct facial features. Clinical manifestations of this syndrome include hypertelorism, epicanthus, round face, brachycephaly, microcephaly, and thin upper lip vermilion, typically presenting at infancy or neonatally.

name: Autosomal dominant hyperinsulinism due to Kir6.2 deficiency
key_symptoms: Excessive insulin response to glucagon test
clinical_summary: Autosomal dominant hyperinsulinism due to Kir6.2 deficiency is a rare disease characterized by hyperinsulinemic hypoglycemia, resulting in various glycemic disturbances in infancy and neonatal periods. This autosomal dominant condition presents with hyperinsulinemia, hypoketotic hypoglycemia, fasting hypoglycemia, increased C-peptide levels, and an excessive insulin response to glucagon test.

name: Autosomal dominant hyperinsulinism due to SUR1 deficiency
key_symptoms: Excessive insulin response to glucagon test
lab_findings: Decreased circulating free fatty acid level
clinical_summary: Autosomal dominant hyperinsulinism due to SUR1 deficiency is a rare disease characterized by recurrent episodes of fasting hypoglycemia and hyperinsulinemia, with an autosomal dominant mode of inheritance. The disease typically manifests in infancy or early childhood.

name: Deafness-hypogonadism syndrome
lab_findings: Abnormality of the middle ear ossicles
Abnormality of the internal auditory canal
clinical_summary: Deafness-hypogonadism syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by progressive hearing impairment and hypergonadotropic hypogonadism, leading to delayed puberty. It presents in infancy or neonatal stage with symptoms including conductive hearing impairment, progressive sensorineural hearing impairment, and stapes ankylosis.

name: Chondroectodermal dysplasia with night blindness
clinical_summary: Chondroectodermal dysplasia with night blindness is a rare disease characterized by micrognathia, otitis media, platyspondyly, delayed skeletal maturation, osteopenia, and osteoporosis, presenting in infancy or neonatally. The inheritance pattern of this disease is currently unknown.

name: Insulinoma
clinical_summary: Insulinoma is a rare disease characterized by the production of excessive insulin, leading to hypoglycemia and associated symptoms such as atypical behavior and seizures. It can affect individuals of all ages and is not inherited.

name: Progressive non-fluent aphasia
lab_findings: high levels of tau protein in csf
clinical_summary: Progressive non-fluent aphasia is a rare disease characterized by mental deterioration, frontotemporal dementia, memory impairment, and aphasia, often accompanied by thickened nuchal skin fold and frontotemporal cerebral atrophy. Onset typically occurs in adulthood, with a multigenic/multifactorial inheritance pattern.

name: Deficiency in anterior pituitary function-variable immunodeficiency syndrome
clinical_summary: A rare disease caused by deficiency in anterior pituitary function presents with a variable immunodeficiency syndrome, affecting individuals mainly in childhood, with symptoms including recurrent infections and life-threatening hypoglycemia, often leading to severe complications. The disorder is inherited in an autosomal dominant manner.

name: LMNA-related cardiocutaneous progeria syndrome
clinical_summary: LMNA-related cardiocutaneous progeria syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by absent eyelashes, cardiovascular complications including congestive heart failure and coronary artery atherosclerosis. This autosomal dominant condition typically presents in childhood and affects multiple systems, including the heart and skin.

name: Behavioral variant of frontotemporal dementia
clinical_summary: Behavioral variant of frontotemporal dementia is a rare disease characterized by atypical behavior, hyperorality, restlessness, aggressive and inappropriate behavior. It typically affects adults with autosomal dominant inheritance.

name: Hyperinsulinism due to UCP2 deficiency
key_symptoms: Excessive insulin response to glucagon test
lab_findings: Decreased circulating free fatty acid level
clinical_summary: Hyperinsulinism due to UCP2 deficiency is a rare genetic disorder characterized by severe, recurrent episodes of hypoglycemia. It typically presents in infancy or early life and is associated with an autosomal dominant inheritance pattern.

name: Non-insulinoma pancreatogenous hypoglycemia syndrome
clinical_summary: Non-insulinoma pancreatogenous hypoglycemia syndrome is a rare condition characterized by severe hypoglycemia, often presenting with symptoms such as hyperinsulinemia, hyperhidrosis, pallor, lethargy, and potentially coma. This syndrome primarily affects adults and can be inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern.

name: Hyperinsulinism due to HNF1A deficiency
key_symptoms: Excessive insulin response to glucagon test
lab_findings: Decreased circulating free fatty acid level
clinical_summary: Hyperinsulinism due to HNF1A deficiency is a rare condition characterized by severe hypoglycemia and hyperinsulinemia in infancy. It is an autosomal dominant disorder resulting in life-threatening hypoglycemia and other metabolic abnormalities, such as increased C-peptide and decreased free fatty acids.

name: Obesity due to SIM1 deficiency
clinical_summary: Obesity due to SIM1 deficiency is a rare autosomal recessive disorder, characterized by early onset hyperinsulinemia, developmental delays, and distinctive cardiovascular symptoms including hypotension and postural tachycardia. Affected individuals typically present with obesity, polyphagia, and associated cardiovascular impairments from infancy or early neonatal period.

name: Dysbetalipoproteinemia
clinical_summary: Dysbetalipoproteinemia is a rare, inherited disorder characterized by abnormalities in lipoprotein metabolism, resulting in elevated levels of cholesterol and triglycerides in the blood. It presents with a range of symptoms including skin abnormalities, hypertriglyceridemia, and lipemia retinalis.

name: Hypercholesterolemia due to cholesterol 7alpha-hydroxylase deficiency
clinical_summary: Hypercholesterolemia due to cholesterol 7alpha-hydroxylase deficiency is a rare, semi-dominant disorder characterized by hypercholesterolemia, cholestasis, and hepatic steatosis. It presents with increased LDL cholesterol concentration, hypertriglyceridemia, and macrovesicular hepatic steatosis primarily in adults.

name: Familial aortic dissection
key_symptoms: Cystic medial necrosis of the aorta
clinical_summary: Familial aortic dissection is a rare disease characterized by cystic medial necrosis of the aorta and associated cardiovascular manifestations. It can occur at any age, with presentation featuring a range of symptoms including cardiomegaly and aortic root aneurysm.

name: Short stature due to GHSR deficiency
clinical_summary: Short stature due to GHSR deficiency is a rare disease characterized by symptoms including delayed skeletal maturation, growth delay, short stature, hypoglycemia, ketosis, and vomiting, typically presenting in infancy or neonatal age. The condition exhibits autosomal dominant and autosomal recessive inheritance patterns.

name: Leukoencephalopathy-palmoplantar keratoderma syndrome
clinical_summary: Leukoencephalopathy-palmoplantar keratoderma syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by onset in childhood, presenting with a range of neurological symptoms including dementia, muscle weakness, and tetraparesis. These symptoms are often accompanied by distinctive EEG patterns and specific physical features such as slurred speech and pseudobulbar signs.

name: Thyrotoxic periodic paralysis
clinical_summary: Thyrotoxic periodic paralysis is a rare adult-onset condition characterized by muscle symptoms including muscle spasm, stiffness, and fatigue, often in association with hyperglycemia.

name: Combined pituitary hormone deficiencies, genetic forms
lab_findings: Decreased response to growth hormone stimulation test
inheritance: Autosomal dominant, Autosomal recessive, X-linked recessive
clinical_summary: Combined pituitary hormone deficiencies, genetic forms is a rare disease characterized by hypogonadotropic hypogonadism, a decreased response to growth hormone stimulation test, and other symptoms including depressed nasal ridge and infertility. It typically presents in infancy or neonatal stages with inheritance patterns of autosomal dominant, autosomal recessive, and X-linked recessive.

name: Isolated follicle stimulating hormone deficiency
clinical_summary: Isolated follicle stimulating hormone deficiency is a rare genetic disorder characterized by hypogonadotropic hypogonadism and delayed sexual development. It is primarily diagnosed in adolescents and adults with an autosomal recessive inheritance pattern.

name: Eosinophilic gastroenteritis
clinical_summary: Eosinophilic gastroenteritis is a rare disease characterized by gastrointestinal symptoms including eosinophilia, anemia, leukocytosis, vomiting, diarrhea, and dysphagia. It may affect individuals of all ages and does not follow a specific genetic inheritance pattern.

name: Non-acquired panhypopituitarism
lab_findings: Decreased response to growth hormone stimulation test
clinical_summary: Non-acquired panhypopituitarism is a rare condition characterized by hypogonadotropic hypogonadism, growth delay, and other endocrine deficiencies, often presenting with depressed nasal ridge and infertility. It can manifest in individuals of all ages, with autosomal recessive and X-linked recessive inheritance patterns.

name: Central neurocytoma
clinical_summary: Central neurocytoma is a rare disease characterized by the presence of an intracranial cystic lesion with abnormal cellular phenotype, often affecting the lateral ventricle, vision, and causing symptoms such as depression and ataxia. It can occur at various ages, specifically in adolescence, adulthood, and childhood.

name: Kikuchi-Fujimoto disease
clinical_summary: Kikuchi-Fujimoto disease is a rare disorder characterized by cervical lymphadenopathy and variable combinations of oral ulcers, skin rash, pruritus, cutaneous photosensitivity, and alopecia. Its inheritance pattern is unknown.

name: Cogan syndrome
clinical_summary: Cogan syndrome is a rare disease characterized by symptoms including tinnitus, keratitis, photophobia, abnormal vestibular function, vertigo, and reduced visual acuity. This condition can manifest across various age groups, including adolescents, adults, and children.

name: Functioning gonadotropic adenoma
clinical_summary: Functioning gonadotropic adenoma is a rare condition characterized by symptoms including abnormality of the menstrual cycle, visual impairment, and anterior hypopituitarism, typically presenting in adults.

name: Familial hypoaldosteronism
clinical_summary: Familial hypoaldosteronism is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by adrenal insufficiency, resulting in decreased circulating aldosterone levels, which lead to life-threatening electrolyte imbalances. It manifests with symptoms such as hyperkalemia, hypotension, hyponatremia, and failure to thrive, typically presenting at various ages including infancy, neonatal, adolescent, and adult stages.

name: Primary hyperaldosteronism-seizures-neurological abnormalities syndrome
clinical_summary: Primary hyperaldosteronism-seizures-neurological abnormalities syndrome is a rare condition characterized by seizures, global developmental delay, and various neurological abnormalities. It is identified early in infancy or neonatal period, often presenting with symptoms such as hypokalemia and ventricular hypertrophy.

name: Vulvovaginal gingival syndrome
lab_findings: Abnormality of tumor necrosis factor secretion
clinical_summary: Vulvovaginal gingival syndrome is a rare disease characterized by the combination of abnormalities, including oral ulcers, genital abnormalities, and inflammatory skin symptoms such as pruritus, parakeratosis, and erythema. It primarily affects adults and has no known inheritance pattern.

name: Isaacs syndrome
clinical_summary: Isaacs syndrome is a rare condition characterized by a combination of autonomic symptoms, including hyperhidrosis and weight loss, along with neurological manifestations such as fasciculations, myokymia, and muscle spasms. This condition can be identified by abnormal electroencephalogram (EEG) readings, and it may develop at any age.

name: Acute adrenal insufficiency
inheritance: Autosomal recessive, Not applicable, X-linked dominant
clinical_summary: Acute adrenal insufficiency is a rare condition characterized by hyperpigmentation of the skin, muscle weakness, and failure to thrive. It presents with weight loss, diarrhea, nausea, and vomiting in patients of all ages.

name: Addison disease
clinical_summary: Addison disease is a rare endocrine disorder characterized by chronic symptoms including gastrointestinal disturbances such as abdominal pain, nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. These symptoms often occur in conjunction with hypotension.

name: Acquired generalized lipodystrophy
clinical_summary: Acquired generalized lipodystrophy is a rare disease characterized by insulin resistance, hyperinsulinemia, and insulin-resistant diabetes mellitus, often associated with hepatic steatosis, cardiomyopathy, and autoimmunity. It can affect individuals of all ages, with its onset not influenced by genetic inheritance.

name: Adrenocortical carcinoma with pure aldosterone hypersecretion
clinical_summary: Adrenocortical carcinoma with pure aldosterone hypersecretion is a rare disease characterized by muscle weakness, hypokalemia, and metabolic alkalosis. It presents with increased circulating cortisol and glucocorticoid-insensitive primary hyperaldosteronism, typically affecting individuals of all ages.

name: Myoclonic epilepsy of infancy
lab_findings: EEG with irregular generalized spike and wave complexes
clinical_summary: Myoclonic epilepsy of infancy is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by intellectual disability and seizures, including generalized myoclonic and bilateral tonic-clonic seizures, in infancy. It may also be associated with attention deficit hyperactivity disorder and aggressive behavior.

name: Hyperparathyroidism-jaw tumor syndrome
clinical_summary: Hyperparathyroidism-jaw tumor syndrome is a rare autosomal dominant disorder characterized by hypophosphatemia, nephrocalcinosis, and elevated parathyroid hormone levels. It is often associated with abnormalities in parathyroid morphology and additional non-parathyroid manifestations, such as uterine leiomyoma.

name: Generalized glucocorticoid resistance syndrome
inheritance: Autosomal dominant, Autosomal recessive, Not applicable
clinical_summary: Generalized glucocorticoid resistance syndrome is a rare disease characterized by symptoms including hirsutism, hypertension, and an elevated urinary cortisol level. It is associated with all ages and has an autosomal dominant and autosomal recessive inheritance pattern.

name: Maternal uniparental disomy of chromosome 9 syndrome
clinical_summary: Maternal uniparental disomy of chromosome 9 syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a distinct constellation of physical features, including a long face, facial asymmetry, micrognathia, low-set ears, and short neck, typically presenting prenatally or in the neonatal period. Myopia may also be a component of this condition.

name: Paternal uniparental disomy of chromosome 5 syndrome
clinical_summary: Paternal uniparental disomy of chromosome 5 syndrome is a rare condition characterized by symptoms including renal duplication, abnormally large globe, global developmental delay, generalized hypotonia, polyhydramnios, and secundum atrial septal defect. The condition typically presents antenatally or in early infancy with these distinctive features.

name: Severe congenital hypochromic anemia with ringed sideroblasts
clinical_summary: Severe congenital hypochromic anemia with ringed sideroblasts is a rare disease characterized by anemia, reticulocytopenia, and increased circulating ferritin concentration in infancy or at the neonatal stage. This condition is associated with decreased transferrin saturation and additional complications such as azoospermia and hypogonadism.

name: Congenital dyserythropoietic anemia type III
lab_findings: Abnormality of cells of the erythroid lineage
clinical_summary: Congenital dyserythropoietic anemia type III is a rare disorder characterized by anemia, distinctive abnormalities in red blood cell morphology, and elevated levels of iron and bilirubin. It may arise in individuals of all ages and can be inherited in either an autosomal dominant or autosomal recessive pattern.

name: Ring chromosome 7 syndrome
clinical_summary: Ring chromosome 7 syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a distinctive set of physical anomalies present at birth. It affects individuals with manifestations such as hypospadias, hypogonadism, brachycephaly, and microcephaly.

name: Classic glucose transporter type 1 deficiency syndrome
clinical_summary: Classic glucose transporter type 1 deficiency syndrome is a rare disease characterized by symptoms including progressive microcephaly, seizures, ataxia, and global developmental delays. It typically presents in infancy or the neonatal period with varying patterns of inheritance including autosomal dominant and autosomal recessive.

name: N syndrome
clinical_summary: N syndrome is a rare X-linked recessive disorder characterized by various congenital anomalies including cryptorchidism, hypospadias, and visual impairment in individuals presenting in infancy or neonatality. Its symptoms include complex ocular features such as megalocornea and abnormal eyelid morphology, often associated with intellectual disability.

name: Pineocytoma
clinical_summary: Pineocytoma is a rare tumor that typically presents in adults and the elderly with symptoms such as hydrocephalus, nausea and vomiting, and increased intracranial pressure. Affected individuals may also experience headache, memory impairment, and abnormal eyelid morphology.

name: Papillary tumor of the pineal region
clinical_summary: Papillary tumor of the pineal region is a rare disease characterized by symptoms such as hydrocephalus, diplopia, nausea, headache, and memory impairment. This tumor is most often identified in individuals of all ages, without a known familial pattern.

name: Hemolytic anemia due to red cell pyruvate kinase deficiency
lab_findings: Reduced red cell pyruvate kinase level
clinical_summary: Hemolytic anemia due to red cell pyruvate kinase deficiency is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by chronic hemolytic anemia and splenomegaly. It typically presents in infancy or neonatal stage with symptoms including anemia, reticulocytosis, and unconjugated hyperbilirubinemia.

name: Familial cerebral cavernous malformation
clinical_summary: Familial cerebral cavernous malformation is a rare disease characterized by seizures, cerebral hemorrhage, and other symptoms including headache, hemangioma, and muscle weakness, often presenting in individuals of all ages. It follows an autosomal dominant inheritance pattern, indicating a strong genetic component.

name: Laryngotracheoesophageal cleft type 4
lab_findings: Abnormal form of the vertebral bodies
clinical_summary: Laryngotracheoesophageal cleft type 4 is a rare disease characterized by severe congenital malformations, including respiratory and cardiac anomalies, often presenting with life-threatening symptoms from infancy or birth. Affected individuals often manifest respiratory insufficiency, laryngomalacia, and other developmental anomalies.

name: Craniometadiaphyseal dysplasia, wormian bone type
clinical_summary: Craniometadiaphyseal dysplasia, wormian bone type is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by neonatal onset and marked by high palate, macrocephaly, and skeletal abnormalities. Common symptoms include parietal bossing, wide anterior fontanel, malar flattening, and micrognathia.

name: Spondyloepiphyseal dysplasia-craniosynostosis-cleft palate-cataracts-intellectual disability syndrome
clinical_summary: Spondyloepiphyseal dysplasia-craniosynostosis-cleft palate-cataracts-intellectual disability syndrome is a rare disease characterized by a combination of facial dysmorphism and skeletal abnormalities. It is a neonatal-onset condition that is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern.

name: Spondyloepiphyseal dysplasia-brachydactyly-speech disorder syndrome
clinical_summary: This rare disease, Spondyloepiphyseal dysplasia-brachydactyly-speech disorder syndrome, manifests with multiple abnormalities including cryptorchidism, wide mouth, and abnormal palate morphology as early as infancy or neonatal stage. The inheritance pattern of this condition is currently unknown.

name: Osteoporosis-oculocutaneous hypopigmentation syndrome
clinical_summary: Osteoporosis-oculocutaneous hypopigmentation syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by visual impairment, abnormal retinal morphology, and skeletal abnormalities including osteoporosis and platyspondyly, often presenting in infancy or at birth. This autosomal recessive disorder also involves oculocutaneous hypopigmentation although, it is described here but the exact symptom is missing.

name: Primary hyperoxaluria type 1
clinical_summary: Primary hyperoxaluria type 1 is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by recurrent kidney stone formation, kidney damage, and systemic metabolic complications, including metabolic acidosis and anemia. Affected individuals of all ages may exhibit symptoms such as dysuria, nephrocalcinosis, nephrolithiasis, hyperoxaluria, and anemia.

name: Hypergonadotropic hypogonadism-cataract syndrome
clinical_summary: Hypergonadotropic hypogonadism-cataract syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by delayed puberty, decreased fertility, and increased circulating gonadotropin levels. It also presents with secondary complications such as primary amenorrhea, osteoporosis, and delayed skeletal maturation.

name: Amoebic keratitis
lab_findings: Abnormality of the posterior segment of the globe
clinical_summary: Amoebic keratitis is a rare disease characterized by abnormal cornea morphology, ocular pain, and other symptoms such as photophobia, punctate opacification of the cornea, and abnormal corneal epithelium morphology. It can occur in individuals of all ages.

name: Thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura
clinical_summary: Thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura is a rare disease characterized by thrombocytopenia, reticulocytosis, microangiopathic hemolytic anemia, dyspnea, generalized muscle weakness, and seizures, occurring in individuals of all ages. The condition is inherited in an autosomal recessive or multifactorial manner.

name: Anonychia with flexural pigmentation
key_symptoms: Axillary and groin hyperpigmentation and hypopigmentation
clinical_summary: Anonychia with flexural pigmentation is a rare genetic disorder characterized by nail abnormalities (anonychia) and skin discoloration (hypermelanotic macule). It presents with distinctive physical features and can be inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern.

name: Hypogonadotropic hypogonadism-retinitis pigmentosa syndrome
clinical_summary: Hypogonadotropic hypogonadism-retinitis pigmentosa syndrome is characterized by symptoms including decreased fertility, primary amenorrhea, delayed puberty, anterior hypopituitarism, and osteoporosis. The age of onset is unknown, with inheritance pattern also being unidentified.

name: Autosomal dominant Charcot-Marie-Tooth disease type 2F
clinical_summary: Autosomal dominant Charcot-Marie-Tooth disease type 2F is a rare condition characterized by reduced tendon reflexes, distinctive foot deformities, and a progressive peripheral axonal neuropathy leading to debilitating gait disturbances. It is inherited in an autosomal dominant manner and can present in various age groups, including adolescent, adult, and elderly individuals.

name: Osteochondritis dissecans
clinical_summary: Osteochondritis dissecans is a rare disease characterized by joint swelling, stiffness, and limited mobility. Common symptoms include arthralgia and osteopenia, with abnormalities often appearing in the knee.

name: AA amyloidosis
clinical_summary: AA amyloidosis is a rare disease characterized by nephropathy, renal amyloidosis, and proteinuria, often resulting in hypotension and nephrotic syndrome. It can affect individuals across a wide age range, from childhood to adulthood, and does not have a known familial inheritance pattern.

name: Radiation proctitis
clinical_summary: Radiation proctitis is a rare disease characterized by inflammation of the rectum, typically presenting in adults, with symptoms including abnormality of the rectum, colitis, and rectal abscess. The disease is not heritable and involves abnormalities of the gastrointestinal vasculature and connective tissue.

name: Spinocerebellar ataxia type 3
clinical_summary: Spinocerebellar ataxia type 3 is a rare autosomal dominant disorder characterized by a range of neurological symptoms. This condition presents with various physical and motor impairments, including delayed speech and language development, proptosis, and ophthalmoplegia.

name: Spinocerebellar ataxia type 17
clinical_summary: Spinocerebellar ataxia type 17 is a rare disorder characterized by progressive ataxia, gait disturbance, and various movements such as torticollis and blepharospasm, as well as cognitive and behavioral decline. This autosomal dominant condition affects individuals of all ages and may also present with spasticity.

name: Paroxysmal exertion-induced dyskinesia
clinical_summary: Paroxysmal exertion-induced dyskinesia is a rare disease characterized by a range of symptoms including choreoathetosis, dystonia, paroxysmal dyskinesia, intellectual disability, seizures, and torsion dystonia. Symptoms can manifest in various age groups, including adolescence, adulthood, childhood, and infancy, with some forms exhibiting autosomal dominant inheritance.

name: Maternal uniparental disomy of chromosome 6 syndrome
clinical_summary: Maternal uniparental disomy of chromosome 6 syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by multiple congenital anomalies including inguinal hernia, hydrocele testis, cleft palate, cleft upper lip, and facial dysmorphia. Symptoms of this condition begin to manifest either before birth or in early infancy.

name: Maternal uniparental disomy of chromosome 1 syndrome
key_symptoms: Delayed closure of the anterior fontanelle
clinical_summary: Maternal uniparental disomy of chromosome 1 syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a distinct set of physical and developmental features, including smooth philtrum, hearing impairment, and nystagmus in infancy and neonatal stages. The syndrome also involves conditions such as cataract, autism, and a single transverse palmar crease.

name: Paternal uniparental disomy of chromosome 6 syndrome
clinical_summary: Paternal uniparental disomy of chromosome 6 syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a combination of clinical features including cryptorchidism, labial hypertrophy, macroglossia, gingival overgrowth, and high palate. This disorder becomes apparent in the antenatal or neonatal period.

name: Mandibuloacral dysplasia
key_symptoms: Loss of subcutaneous adipose tissue in limbs
lab_findings: Increased adipose tissue around the neck
clinical_summary: Mandibuloacral dysplasia is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by distinct facial features and other systemic abnormalities manifesting in infancy or neonatal periods. Key symptoms include high palate, full cheeks, micrognathia, narrow nose, and increased adipose tissue around the neck, which may be accompanied by hyperinsulinemia.

name: Paternal uniparental disomy of chromosome 1 syndrome
clinical_summary: Paternal uniparental disomy of chromosome 1 syndrome is a rare condition characterized by symptoms including proteinuria, enlarged kidney, strabismus, progressive visual loss, photophobia, and abnormality of dental enamel. Onset of the syndrome typically occurs in infancy or the neonatal period.

name: Maternal uniparental disomy of chromosome 4 syndrome
clinical_summary: Maternal uniparental disomy of chromosome 4 syndrome is a rare condition characterized by rod-cone dystrophy, pigmentary retinopathy, and other neurological and psychological symptoms. It typically presents with delayed speech and language development, depression, and abnormalities of the nervous system in individuals with antenatal or neonatal onset.

name: Placental insufficiency
clinical_summary: Placental insufficiency is a rare disease characterized by intrauterine growth retardation and hypoxemia, typically presenting with a small placenta and abnormal umbilical cord blood vessels. This condition results in babies being small for gestational age, often with proportionate short stature.

name: Pulmonary fibrosis-hepatic hyperplasia-bone marrow hypoplasia syndrome
clinical_summary: Pulmonary fibrosis-hepatic hyperplasia-bone marrow hypoplasia syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by multiple organ system abnormalities, including portal hypertension, pulmonary fibrosis, and bone marrow hypocellularity. Key symptoms include hepatosplenomegaly, nodular regenerative hyperplasia of the liver, and thrombocytopenia.

name: Kleefstra syndrome due to 9q34 microdeletion
clinical_summary: Kleefstra syndrome due to 9q34 microdeletion is a rare genetic disorder characterized by distinctive craniofacial features, intellectual disability, and hypotonia, often presenting in infancy or neonatal period. Key features include everted lower lip vermilion, brachycephaly, hypertelorism, anteverted nares, and hypotonia.

name: Microphthalmia with linear skin defects syndrome
clinical_summary: Microphthalmia with linear skin defects syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by anophthalmia, microphthalmia, sclerocornea, and congenital diaphragmatic hernia, often associated with hyperpigmentation or abnormality of skin pigmentation. This X-linked dominant disorder typically presents in the neonatal period.

name: Microcephaly-lymphedema-chorioretinopathy syndrome
clinical_summary: Microcephaly-lymphedema-chorioretinopathy syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by neonatal onset microcephaly and variable features including eye and vision abnormalities, lymphedema, myopia, and edema. This autosomal dominant disorder presents with congenital symptoms and inherited traits.

name: Neu-Laxova syndrome
clinical_summary: Neu-Laxova syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by prenatal onset symptoms including microcephaly, intrauterine growth retardation, and physical anomalies such as ichthyosis and a sloping forehead. Pachygyria, a rare form of brain development, is also a primary feature.

name: Mietens syndrome
clinical_summary: Mietens syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by strabismus, Nystagmus, Intellectual disability, and global developmental delay. The condition typically affects individuals in infancy or neonatal stages, and is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern.

name: Blepharophimosis-intellectual disability syndrome, SBBYS type
clinical_summary: Blepharophimosis-intellectual disability syndrome, SBBYS type, is a rare condition characterized by various physical abnormalities and joint hypermobility. It is usually present at birth, with an autosomal dominant inheritance pattern.

name: Ear-patella-short stature syndrome
key_symptoms: Atresia of the external auditory canal
clinical_summary: Ear-patella-short stature syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by a combination of joint hypermobility, abnormal outer ear, and craniofacial abnormalities including retrognathia, micrognathia, and microcephaly. It manifests antenatally or neonatally and can be inherited via an autosomal dominant or autosomal recessive pattern.

name: Chronic inflammatory demyelinating polyneuropathy
clinical_summary: Chronic inflammatory demyelinating polyneuropathy is a rare disease characterized by segmental peripheral demyelination/remyelination and various neurological symptoms. Symptoms of this disease may include decreased nerve conduction velocity, areflexia, unsteady gait, paresthesia, and somatic sensory dysfunction.

name: Baraitser-Winter cerebrofrontofacial syndrome
clinical_summary: Baraitser-Winter cerebrofrontofacial syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by coarse facial features, wide mouth, and other distinct craniofacial anomalies. It is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern.

name: Ichthyosis follicularis-alopecia-photophobia syndrome
inheritance: Autosomal dominant, Not applicable, X-linked recessive
clinical_summary: Ichthyosis follicularis-alopecia-photophobia syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by symptoms including photophobia, intellectual disability, seizures, and hair abnormalities, typically presenting in infancy or neonatal period.

name: Nicolaides-Baraitser syndrome
clinical_summary: Nicolaides-Baraitser syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by distinctive facial features, including a wide mouth, everted lower lip vermilion, thin vermilion border, microcephaly, and a smooth philtrum, resulting in a triangular face shape. This condition typically presents in infancy or at birth and is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern.

name: Filippi syndrome
lab_findings: i couldn't find any information on filippi syndrome.
clinical_summary: Filippi syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by cryptorchidism and microcephaly in infancy or at birth. It exhibits a wide nose, prominent nasal bridge, and underdeveloped nasal alae as distinct features.

name: Wild type ABeta2M amyloidosis
lab_findings: Decreased amplitude of sensory action potentials
clinical_summary: Wild type ABeta2M amyloidosis is a rare disease characterized by axonal loss and constrictive neuropathy, presenting with pain, dysesthesia, and decreased sensory action potential amplitude. Other manifestations may include bone cysts.

name: Hemophilia B
clinical_summary: Hemophilia B is a rare X-linked recessive disorder characterized by bleeding manifestations such as hematuria, poor wound healing, and spontaneous and recurrent epistaxis. It often presents in childhood, infancy, or neonatal stages with symptoms including prolonged bleeding and clotting times.

name: Autosomal semi-dominant severe lipodystrophic laminopathy
clinical_summary: Autosomal semi-dominant severe lipodystrophic laminopathy is a rare disease characterized by a combination of micrognathia, narrow nasal ridge, and diabetes mellitus. Onset typically occurs in adulthood, with additional symptoms including insulin resistance, xanthomatosis, and hypertriglyceridemia.

name: GM2 gangliosidosis, AB variant
clinical_summary: GM2 gangliosidosis, AB variant is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by early-onset neurodegeneration and various neurological manifestations, including dystonia, hyperreflexia, and exaggerated startle response. This condition typically presents in infancy with symptoms of cerebral atrophy and developmental regression.

name: Duchenne muscular dystrophy
clinical_summary: Duchenne muscular dystrophy is a rare X-linked recessive disorder that primarily affects males in childhood, manifesting with delayed speech, motor, and global developmental delays. It may also lead to specific learning disabilities, flexion contractures, and cardiomyopathy.

name: Bronchopulmonary dysplasia
clinical_summary: Bronchopulmonary dysplasia is a rare disease characterized by abnormal lung morphology, prematurity, and respiratory distress in infancy. Affected individuals often present with symptoms such as emphysema and dyspnea.

name: Meningioma
key_symptoms: Chromosomal breakage induced by ionizing radiation
clinical_summary: Meningioma is a rare disease characterized by an intracranial meningioma, chromosomal breakage, and increased sensitivity to ionizing radiation. It is associated with symptoms such as hypogonadotropic hypogonadism, amenorrhea, and impotence.

name: Pyruvate dehydrogenase E3 deficiency
