clinical_summary: Pyruvate dehydrogenase E3 deficiency is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by childhood onset of generalized hypotonia, vomiting, lactic acidosis, and neurodevelopmental delay. It is often associated with seizures and elevated circulating lactate concentration.

name: PRKAR1B-related neurodegenerative dementia with intermediate filaments
clinical_summary: PRKAR1B-related neurodegenerative dementia with intermediate filaments is a rare, autosomal dominant neurodegenerative disorder manifesting in adults, characterized by symptoms including frontotemporal dementia, motor deterioration, and memory impairment. Common manifestations include abnormal behavior, motor decline, memory loss, and short attention span due to abnormal neuron morphology.

name: Congenital glaucoma
inheritance: Autosomal dominant, Autosomal recessive, Not applicable
clinical_summary: Congenital glaucoma is a rare condition characterized by glaucoma, corneal abnormalities, and ocular enlargement in infancy or neonatal age. Manifestations may include photophobia, blepharospasm, and other related ocular features.

name: Medulloblastoma
clinical_summary: Medulloblastoma is a rare and malignant brain tumor characterized by delayed cranial suture closure, ataxia, lethargy, and other neurological symptoms such as abnormal cranial nerve morphology, dysmetria, and nausea and vomiting. It occurs in individuals of all ages and does not have a known familial inheritance pattern.

name: Walker-Warburg syndrome
clinical_summary: Walker-Warburg syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by significant neurological impairment, including areflexia, muscle weakness, and specific learning disability, along with distinct brain malformations such as pachygyria, cerebellar hypoplasia, and lissencephaly, often present at birth or in infancy.

name: Ullrich congenital muscular dystrophy
lab_findings: Increased variability in muscle fiber diameter
clinical_summary: Ullrich congenital muscular dystrophy is a rare disease characterized by symptoms including abnormal palate morphology, generalized hypotonia, and spinal rigidity in infancy or at birth. It is inherited through both autosomal dominant and autosomal recessive patterns of inheritance.

name: Drug-induced lupus erythematosus
clinical_summary: Drug-induced lupus erythematosus is a rare condition characterized by the presence of symptoms including hematuria, petechiae, pericardial effusion, pericarditis, thrombocytopenia, and anemia. This condition can affect individuals of all ages and is not inherited.

name: Hereditary angioedema type 1
key_symptoms: Edema of the dorsum of hands
clinical_summary: Hereditary angioedema type 1 is a rare, autosomal dominant disorder characterized by recurrent episodes of severe facial and potentially life-threatening abdominal edema. Symptoms can also include urticaria, abnormal metabolism/homeostasis, paresthesia, and intestinal edema.

name: POEMS syndrome
clinical_summary: POEMS syndrome is a rare disease characterized by hypogonadism, abnormality of the endocrine system, visceromegaly, lymphoproliferative disorder, increased circulating antibody level, and abnormality of skin physiology in adult and elderly patients. The inheritance pattern of POEMS syndrome is unknown.

name: 46,XX difference of sex development-skeletal anomalies syndrome
clinical_summary: 46,XX difference of sex development-skeletal anomalies syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by ambiguous genitalia, skeletal anomalies including dislocated radial head and deformed humerus, and primary amenorrhea. It appears to be distinct from other difference of sex development syndromes due to its unique combination of sex and skeletal anomalies.

name: Progressive encephalopathy-severe neurodegeneration-lipodystrophy syndrome
clinical_summary: Progressive encephalopathy-severe neurodegeneration-lipodystrophy syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder manifesting in early life with features including hyperinsulinemia, insulin resistance, and generalized lipodystrophy. This condition is characterized by progressive psychomotor deterioration and a significant reduction in adipose tissue, including both subcutaneous and intraabdominal deposits.

name: Infantile systemic hyalinosis
clinical_summary: Infantile systemic hyalinosis is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by infancy-onset symptoms, including polycystic ovaries, gingival overgrowth, macrocephaly, coarse facial features, short neck, and adrenal gland abnormalities. It affects individuals from birth through infancy and is often inherited.

name: Frasier syndrome
key_symptoms: Gonadal dysgenesis with female appearance, male
clinical_summary: Frasier syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by proteinuria, focal segmental glomerulosclerosis, primary amenorrhea, and hypergonadotropic hypogonadism. It is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern and affects individuals across various age groups, including childhood, adolescence, and adulthood.

name: Occipital horn syndrome
clinical_summary: Occipital horn syndrome is a rare X-linked recessive disorder characterized by symptoms including large fontanelles, delayed cranial suture closure, and abnormal skull morphology, which typically present in infancy, childhood, or at birth. Additionally, affected individuals often exhibit intellectual disability and other physical abnormalities, such as hyperextensible skin and a distinctive facial appearance.

name: Arterial tortuosity syndrome
clinical_summary: Arterial tortuosity syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by the gradual dilatation of arteries, often leading to severe cardiovascular complications such as congestive heart failure and aortic aneurysms. This condition typically presents in childhood or infancy and follows an autosomal recessive inheritance pattern.

name: Progeria-short stature-pigmented nevi syndrome
clinical_summary: Progeria-short stature-pigmented nevi syndrome is a rare disease characterized by a combination of physical anomalies, including hypospadias, micropenis, and bird-like facies. The disease typically manifests in infancy or neonatal stage, but its exact inheritance pattern is currently unknown.

name: Autosomal dominant Robinow syndrome
clinical_summary: Autosomal dominant Robinow syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by distinct facial features and limb abnormalities present at birth. It is marked by symptoms such as hypertelorism, wide nasal bridge, and brachydactyly, which manifest in infancy or neonatal period.

name: Desmosterolosis
clinical_summary: Desmosterolosis is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by distinct facial features, ventriculomegaly, and recurrent seizures. It typically manifests in infancy or antenatal stage with symptoms such as posteriorly rotated ears and short nose.

name: Oculoectodermal syndrome
clinical_summary: Oculoectodermal syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by a combination of eye and skin abnormalities, including abnormal conjunctiva morphology and generalized skin hyperpigmentation. It typically presents in infancy with features such as limbal dermoid, agenesis of the corpus callosum, and absent septum pellucidum.

name: Secondary syringomyelia
lab_findings: Hyperintensity of MRI T2 signal of the spinal cord
clinical_summary: Secondary syringomyelia is a rare condition characterized by the presence of a cyst-like structure within the spinal cord, often presenting with gait disturbance, paresthesia, decreased taste sensation, and dysphonia. Diagnostic features include increased CSF protein concentration and hyperintensity of MRI T2 signal of the spinal cord, typically affecting adults.

name: Adenohypophysitis
clinical_summary: Adenohypophysitis is a rare disease characterized by panhypopituitarism, amenorrhea, blurred vision, impotence, increased circulating prolactin concentration, and pallor in adolescents and adults with unknown inheritance. Clinical manifestations of this condition typically occur in late adolescence or adulthood.

name: Panhypophysitis
clinical_summary: Panhypophysitis is a rare condition characterized by symptoms including central diabetes insipidus, panhypopituitarism, and abnormality of the posterior pituitary, as well as hormonal and reproductive-related symptoms such as amenorrhea, blurred vision, and impotence. The age of onset for panhypophysitis can occur in any stage of life, including adolescence, adulthood, childhood, and old age, with unknown inheritance patterns.

name: Perlman syndrome
clinical_summary: Perlman syndrome is a rare condition characterized by tall stature, macrocephaly, and distinct facial abnormalities, including an open mouth, retrognathia, and a round face. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern, typically presenting antenatally, in infancy, or neonatally.

name: Sheehan syndrome
clinical_summary: Sheehan syndrome is a rare condition characterized by panhypopituitarism, manifesting in symptoms such as decreased libido, oligomenorrhea, and secondary hormone deficiencies, including cortisol and growth hormone.

name: Lenz-Majewski hyperostotic dysplasia
clinical_summary: Lenz-Majewski hyperostotic dysplasia is a rare disease characterized by a combination of cranial and skeletal abnormalities, including joint hypermobility, large fontanelles, and delayed cranial suture closure. It is an autosomal dominant disorder typically presenting in the antenatal, infancy, or neonatal period.

name: Osteopathia striata-cranial sclerosis syndrome
clinical_summary: Osteopathia striata-cranial sclerosis syndrome is a rare condition characterized by distinctive bone abnormalities, including thickened calvaria and facial hyperostosis, and high iliac wings. It is inherited in an X-linked dominant pattern and typically presents with abnormal bone morphology from an early age, even in utero.

name: Focal facial dermal dysplasia type IV
key_symptoms: Fragmented elastic fibers in the dermis
clinical_summary: Focal facial dermal dysplasia type IV is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by microcephaly, short chin, and presence of a hemangioma among other symptoms. It manifests antenatally/neonatally, often with complications such as intracranial hemorrhage and hemiparesis.

name: Interstitial cystitis
clinical_summary: Interstitial cystitis is a rare disease characterized by the functional abnormality of the bladder and associated urinary symptoms. Key symptoms include urinary urgency, nocturia, and abnormalities of the bladder and genital system.

name: Autosomal dominant optic atrophy and cataract
clinical_summary: Autosomal dominant optic atrophy and cataract is a rare genetic disorder characterized by progressive optic atrophy and cataract formation, leading to symptoms of reduced visual acuity, central scotoma, and nystagmus. The disease typically presents in childhood and is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern.

name: Encephalocraniocutaneous lipomatosis
clinical_summary: Encephalocraniocutaneous lipomatosis is a rare disease characterized by a combination of neurological symptoms including hypertonia, cerebral atrophy, and rigidity. It also presents with various congenital anomalies such as absent septum pellucidum and tricuspid valve prolapse.

name: Obesity due to leptin receptor gene deficiency
clinical_summary: Obesity due to leptin receptor gene deficiency is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by various somatic and hormonal abnormalities, manifesting in early childhood or infancy. It is associated with a range of symptoms including gynecomastia, primary amenorrhea, and hyperinsulinemia.

name: Obesity due to congenital leptin deficiency
lab_findings: Decreased proportion of CD4-positive T cells
clinical_summary: Obesity due to congenital leptin deficiency is a rare, autosomal recessive condition, manifesting in childhood with symptoms such as hyperinsulinemia, polyphagia, and primary amenorrhea, alongside physical characteristics including decreased CD4-positive T cells and gynecomastia. Treatment may be necessary to manage the condition's various complications.

name: Lynch syndrome
lab_findings: increased cag repeat length in msh2
clinical_summary: Lynch syndrome is a rare, autosomal dominant disorder characterized by gastrointestinal symptoms in adults, including weight loss, constipation, and abdominal pain. The condition often progresses to colon cancer and can also result in gastrointestinal hemorrhage and malabsorption.

name: Sarcoidosis
clinical_summary: Sarcoidosis is a rare, potentially life-threatening disease characterized by systemic inflammation and granulomatous formations. Its clinical presentation varies widely, often manifesting with symptoms such as uveitis, joint swelling, and hematologic abnormalities like thrombocytopenia and leukopenia.

name: Vernal keratoconjunctivitis
clinical_summary: Vernal keratoconjunctivitis is a rare disease characterized by abnormal morphology of the cornea, conjunctiva, and sclera, often accompanied by photophobia, keratoconjunctivitis, and allergic conjunctivitis. It typically affects children.

name: Familial Alzheimer-like prion disease
clinical_summary: Familial Alzheimer-like prion disease is a rare, autosomal dominant condition characterized by adult-onset symptoms such as atypical behavior and specific learning disability. Affected individuals exhibit a range of neurocognitive and behavioral manifestations, including anxiety, depression, and sleep abnormalities.

name: Adenylosuccinate lyase deficiency
lab_findings: elevated adenylosuccinate (also known as succinylaminoimidazole carboxamide ribotide or sai)
clinical_summary: Adenylosuccinate lyase deficiency is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by distinct facial abnormalities, including a thin upper lip vermilion, smooth and long philtrum, low-set ears, brachycephaly, and microcephaly, typically presenting in childhood or infancy.

name: Wilson disease
clinical_summary: Wilson disease is a rare, inherited disorder characterized by a range of neurological and psychiatric symptoms, including gait disturbance, depression, and aggressive behavior. It can present at various ages, from childhood to adulthood, due to its autosomal recessive inheritance pattern.

name: Brain dopamine-serotonin vesicular transport disease
clinical_summary: Brain dopamine-serotonin vesicular transport disease is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by infancy or neonatal onset of hypomimic face, abnormal eye movements, ptosis, hyperhidrosis, ataxia, and dysarthria.

name: Pituitary apoplexy
clinical_summary: Pituitary apoplexy is a rare but life-threatening condition characterized by sudden onset of a thunderclap headache and possibly other symptoms such as bitemporal hemianopia and diplopia. It can manifest with systemic complications including hypertension and hypogonadism.

name: Pituitary dermoid and epidermoid cysts
clinical_summary: Pituitary dermoid and epidermoid cysts are rare, age-related conditions affecting adults and the elderly, causing a range of symptoms including anterior hypopituitarism, hypogonadism, and amenorrhea. Common complications of the disease include nausea and vomiting, avascular necrosis, and neoplasm of the anterior pituitary.

name: Late-onset isolated ACTH deficiency
lab_findings: inhibited adrenocorticotropin hormone response to crh
clinical_summary: Late-onset isolated ACTH deficiency is a rare disease characterized by adult onset of lethargy, muscle weakness, and failure to thrive, accompanied by symptoms such as weight loss, diarrhea, nausea, and vomiting. It is typically identified in adults without a familial inheritance pattern.

name: Tangier disease
clinical_summary: Tangier disease is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by Hypertriglyceridemia, Hypocholesterolemia, and associated signs such as Ectropion, Dry skin, Hepatosplenomegaly, and Abdominal pain. It typically presents in infancy, childhood, adolescence, or adulthood.

name: Severe X-linked mitochondrial encephalomyopathy
lab_findings: Increased variability in muscle fiber diameter
clinical_summary: Severe X-linked mitochondrial encephalomyopathy is a rare genetic disorder characterized by delayed speech and language development, muscle weakness, and sensory neuropathy. It presents in infancy or neonatally, primarily affecting boys due to its X-linked recessive inheritance pattern.

name: VIPoma
clinical_summary: VIPoma is a rare disease characterized by the development of a pancreatic neoplasm leading to hypokalemia, secretory diarrhea, and other metabolic disturbances. The symptoms include weight loss, normochromic anemia, and diabetes mellitus, typically affecting individuals of all ages.

name: Glycine encephalopathy
lab_findings: Abnormal metabolic brain imaging by MRS
clinical_summary: Glycine encephalopathy is a rare genetic disorder characterized by seizures, hypotonia, and abnormal EEG readings with burst suppression, often presenting in infancy or neonatally. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern, also featuring hyperglycinemia and hypoplasia of the corpus callosum.

name: American trypanosomiasis
clinical_summary: American trypanosomiasis is a rare disease characterized by edema, pallor, skin rash, cardiomyopathy, splenomegaly, and thromboembolism. It can affect individuals of all ages, with an unknown genetic basis.

name: RFT1-CDG
clinical_summary: RFT1-CDG is a rare disease characterized by infancy-onset multisystemic involvement, including hearing impairment, visual impairment, failure to thrive, arthrogryposis multiplex congenita, microcephaly, and abnormal bleeding, inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern.

name: SSR4-CDG
clinical_summary: SSR4-CDG is a rare genetic disorder characterized by distinctive facial features, including a wide mouth, macrotia, strabismus, and widely spaced teeth, often presenting in infancy or the neonatal period. It is inherited in an X-linked recessive pattern, typically affecting males more frequently.

name: SLC35A1-CDG
clinical_summary: SLC35A1-CDG is a rare disease characterized by thrombocytopenia, decreased total neutrophil count, abnormal bleeding, and other complications such as giant platelets and subcutaneous hemorrhage. This disease typically presents in infancy or in the neonatal period.

name: Chronic myeloid leukemia
lab_findings: Abnormality of blood and blood-forming tissues
clinical_summary: Chronic myeloid leukemia is a rare disease characterized by abnormal blood and blood-forming tissue development, leading to various hematologic abnormalities, including splenomegaly and thrombocytopenia or thrombocytosis. It affects individuals of various ages, from infancy to adulthood.

name: Kaposi sarcoma
clinical_summary: Kaposi sarcoma is a rare adult-onset disease characterized by the development of various cutaneous and systemic lesions, including a neoplasm of the skin and abnormal retinal morphology. It presents with symptoms such as hypermelanotic macules, abnormality of the lower limb, and hemangioma.

name: PPoma
clinical_summary: PPoma is a rare disease characterized by a neoplasm of the pancreas or small intestine, causing weight loss, diarrhea, nausea, and vomiting, often presenting with an abnormal abdomen morphology. It typically affects adults and does not have a known familial inheritance pattern.

name: Pseudoleprechaunism syndrome, Patterson type
clinical_summary: Pseudoleprechaunism syndrome, Patterson type is a rare condition characterized by abnormal skeletal and skin features, including abnormal odontoid process, metaphyseal sclerosis, and generalized bronze hyperpigmentation. The age of onset is antenatal or neonatal, with unknown inheritance patterns.

name: Colchicine poisoning
lab_findings: Abnormality of blood and blood-forming tissues
clinical_summary: Colchicine poisoning is a rare condition characterized by metabolic acidosis, dehydration, leukocytosis, and respiratory distress among its symptoms. It can affect individuals of all ages.

name: AICA-ribosiduria
clinical_summary: AICA-ribosiduria is a rare genetic disorder characterized by unique physical features and seizures, with symptoms present from the prenatal period through infancy and childhood. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern, suggesting both copies of the gene must be mutated for the disorder to manifest.

name: Glucagonoma
clinical_summary: Glucagonoma is a rare disease characterized by a neoplasm of the pancreas, typically presenting in adults with symptoms such as glossitis, diabetes mellitus, a skin rash, pruritus, and weight loss.

name: Malaria
lab_findings: Abnormality of blood and blood-forming tissues
clinical_summary: Malaria is a rare disease characterized by a combination of severe symptoms, including anemia, acute kidney injury, and fever. Common symptoms also include nausea, vomiting, headache, and morphological central nervous system abnormalities.

name: Leishmaniasis
clinical_summary: Leishmaniasis is a rare disease characterized by abnormal oral cavity morphology, splenomegaly, and organomegaly, as well as hematological abnormalities including pancytopenia. It presents with recurrent fever, abnormal bleeding, and can affect individuals of all ages.

name: GRFoma
clinical_summary: GRFoma is a rare disease characterized by elevated circulating growth hormone concentration, coarse facial features, and abdominal abnormalities. Affected individuals typically present with symptoms such as weight loss, diarrhea, nausea, and vomiting in adulthood.

name: Somatostatinoma
lab_findings: elevated pancreatic and duodenal somatostatin levels
clinical_summary: Somatostatinoma is a rare endocrine disorder characterized by symptoms including diabetes mellitus, weight loss, diarrhea, nausea and vomiting, constipation, and anorexia, primarily affecting adults and the elderly. The inheritance pattern of the disease is currently unknown.

name: RHYNS syndrome
clinical_summary: RHYNS syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive disease characterized by a range of systemic symptoms including kidney and eye disorders. Affected individuals often present with symptoms in utero or early in life, including nephronophthisis, rod-cone dystrophy, ptosis, skeletal and liver abnormalities, and hypopituitarism.

name: 7q11.23 microduplication syndrome
clinical_summary: 7q11.23 microduplication syndrome is a rare condition characterized by delayed speech and language development, along with various physical features including posteriorly rotated ears, brachycephaly, high palate, and thin upper lip vermilion. The disorder typically manifests in infancy or neonatally, potentially with significant developmental and physical impact.

name: Hepatic veno-occlusive disease
clinical_summary: Hepatic veno-occlusive disease is a rare condition characterized by severe liver dysfunction, with symptoms including jaundice, ascites, and abdominal pain. It can also involve complications such as renal insufficiency, respiratory failure, and hepatomegaly.

name: Mercury poisoning
clinical_summary: Mercury poisoning is a rare disease characterized by symptoms such as hypertension, seizure, confusion, dystonia, tremor, and tachycardia, which can affect individuals of all ages regardless of genetic predisposition.

name: Sickle cell anemia
clinical_summary: Sickle cell anemia is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by hemolytic anemia, recurrent infections, pain, retinopathy, osteoporosis, and jaundice. It can affect individuals of all ages, regardless of their age, and is typically passed down through generations in an autosomal recessive pattern.

name: Pelviscapular dysplasia
key_symptoms: Stenosis of the external auditory canal
clinical_summary: Pelviscapular dysplasia is a rare Autosomal recessive disease characterized by a combination of severe craniofacial anomalies, including low-set ears, abnormal skull base, hypertelorism, and abnormal pinna morphology, often accompanied by hearing impairment. Symptoms typically manifest in infancy or early childhood and can lead to short stature.

name: Ring chromosome 13 syndrome
clinical_summary: Ring chromosome 13 syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by microcephaly, intellectual disability, growth delay, and abnormal facial features. It is typically present at birth and is associated with various congenital anomalies, including hypospadias and bifid scrotum.

name: Monosomy 13q34 syndrome
clinical_summary: Monosomy 13q34 syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by intellectual disability, growth delay, and distinct physical features, typically present from birth. Common symptoms include global developmental delay, obesity, and microcephaly, with variable severity.

name: Urocanic aciduria
clinical_summary: Urocanic aciduria is a rare, severe neurodegenerative disorder characterized by ataxia, dysarthria, and other symptoms from infancy or neonatal stages. It follows an autosomal recessive inheritance pattern.

name: 20q13.33 microdeletion syndrome
clinical_summary: 20q13.33 microdeletion syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by delayed speech and language development, intellectual disability, and growth deficiencies. It presents with neonatal onset and includes symptoms such as hypotonia, limb bone abnormalities, and being small for gestational age.

name: Microtriplication 11q24.1 syndrome
lab_findings: i couldn't find enough reliable information about this condition.
however, i may be able to suggest other related conditions or similar disorders that might help.
clinical_summary: Microtriplication 11q24.1 syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by distinctive facial features, including coarse facial features, hypertelorism, smooth philtrum, short philtrum, posteriorly rotated ears, and a wide nose, apparent in childhood. The inheritance pattern of this condition is currently unknown.

name: Pancreatic hypoplasia-diabetes-congenital heart disease syndrome
clinical_summary: Pancreatic hypoplasia-diabetes-congenital heart disease syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by neonatal insulin-dependent diabetes, congenital heart defects, and intellectual disability. It is an autosomal dominant condition that can affect individuals of all ages.

name: SERKAL syndrome
clinical_summary: SERKAL syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a combination of congenital anomalies, including genitourinary, orofacial, cardiac, and adrenal abnormalities. It presents with a range of symptoms from birth, including abnormality of the penis, hypospadias, orofacial cleft, congenital diaphragmatic hernia, adrenal gland abnormalities, and a ventricular septal defect.

name: Lennox-Gastaut syndrome
lab_findings: EEG with focal sharp slow waves
clinical_summary: Lennox-Gastaut syndrome is a rare neurological disorder characterized by intellectual disability, encephalopathy, and atypical behavior presenting early in life. It is diagnosed through distinctive EEG findings, including focal sharp slow waves, often coupled with other symptoms such as aggressive and autistic behavior.

name: Amyotrophic lateral sclerosis
inheritance: Autosomal dominant, Autosomal recessive, Not applicable
clinical_summary: Amyotrophic lateral sclerosis is a rare neurodegenerative disease characterized by generalized muscle weakness, motor neuron atrophy, and neurodegeneration in adults. Key symptoms also include xerostomia, atypical behavior, and emotional lability.

name: Symphalangism with multiple anomalies of hands and feet
clinical_summary: Symphalangism with multiple anomalies of hands and feet is a rare condition characterized by symphalangism of the 4th and 5th fingers, along with macrocephaly. It also presents with reduced proximal interphalangeal joint space, absent distal interphalangeal creases, and brachydactyly, typically manifesting in infancy or neonatally.

name: Hypoinsulinemic hypoglycemia and body hemihypertrophy
clinical_summary: Hypoisulinemic hypoglycemia and body hemihypertrophy is a rare disorder characterized by hypoglycemia and hemihypertrophy, presenting with symptoms such as gynecomastia, truncal obesity, and hypoketotic hypoglycemia in infancy and neonatal stages. It is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern.

name: Kyphoscoliotic Ehlers-Danlos syndrome due to FKBP22 deficiency
clinical_summary: Kyphoscoliotic Ehlers-Danlos syndrome due to FKBP22 deficiency is a rare disease characterized by severe infancy-onset symptoms, including sensorineural hearing impairment, hyperextensible skin, motor delay, joint hypermobility, pes planus, and poor head control. It follows an autosomal recessive inheritance pattern.

name: Multiple endocrine neoplasia type 4
clinical_summary: Multiple endocrine neoplasia type 4 is a rare condition characterized by abnormalities in the endocrine system, often manifesting as hyperparathyroidism and associated electrolyte imbalances. The disease typically presents in adults with symptoms such as hypercalcemia and elevated circulating parathyroid hormone levels.

name: Monosomy 22 syndrome
clinical_summary: Monosomy 22 syndrome is a rare condition characterized by distinctive facial features including posteriorly rotated ears, open mouth, high palate, thin vermilion border, prominent occiput, and retrognathia. These physical abnormalities typically present at birth (neonatal age).

name: Spondyloepimetaphyseal dysplasia, aggrecan type
clinical_summary: Spondyloepimetaphyseal dysplasia, aggrecan type is a rare genetic disorder characterized by infancy or neonatal onset of mandibular prognathia, brachydactyly, and other systemic features. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern.

name: CHST3-related skeletal dysplasia
clinical_summary: CHST3-related skeletal dysplasia is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a range of severe skeletal abnormalities, including Brachydactyly, hypertelorism, and scoliosis. Affected individuals typically present with distinctive physical characteristics from birth, including a barrel-shaped chest and waddling gait.

name: Congenital fibrinogen deficiency
clinical_summary: Congenital fibrinogen deficiency is a rare disorder characterized by bleeding manifestations, including micropenis, gingival bleeding, and bruising susceptibility, often accompanied by eye abnormalities such as developmental cataract and microphthalmia. It can manifest at any age and may be inherited in either an autosomal dominant or autosomal recessive pattern.

name: Atypical hemolytic uremic syndrome
lab_findings: Abnormality of blood and blood-forming tissues
inheritance: Autosomal dominant, Autosomal recessive, Not applicable
clinical_summary: Atypical hemolytic uremic syndrome is a rare disease characterized by acute kidney injury, microangiopathic hemolytic anemia, and other symptoms including proteinuria, hematuria, thrombocytopenia, and abnormality of blood and blood-forming tissues. It affects individuals of all ages with varying modes of inheritance, including autosomal dominant and autosomal recessive patterns.

name: Sialuria
clinical_summary: Sialuria is a rare disease characterized by a distinct set of facial features present from infancy, including coarse facial features, thin upper lip vermilion, epicanthus, hypertelorism, smooth philtrum, and low-set ears. It is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern.

name: KCNQ2-related developmental and epileptic encephalopathy
clinical_summary: KCNQ2-related developmental and epileptic encephalopathy is a rare condition characterized by symptoms such as global developmental delay, epilepsy, and abnormal cerebral white matter morphology, typically presenting in infancy or at birth. It is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern.

name: Post-traumatic pituitary deficiency
lab_findings: Decreased response to growth hormone stimulation test
clinical_summary: Post-traumatic pituitary deficiency is a rare condition characterized by hypogonadotropic hypogonadism, decreased response to growth hormone stimulation test, and other symptoms that occur after traumatic injury.

name: Primary sclerosing cholangitis
clinical_summary: Primary sclerosing cholangitis (PSC) is a rare, chronic liver disease characterized by cholestasis, abnormal biliary tract morphology, cirrhosis, and hepatic fibrosis. It is marked by the presence of autoimmunity and can lead to portal hypertension.

name: Emanuel syndrome
clinical_summary: Emanuel syndrome is a rare condition characterized by global developmental delay and distinct physical anomalies, including cryptorchidism, micropenis, and hypogonadism. These features appear either before birth or at birth, with an unknown pattern of inheritance.

name: Tetrasomy 9p syndrome
clinical_summary: Tetrasomy 9p syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by distinct facial features such as hypertelorism, deep-set eyes, and micrognathia. The age of onset is during fetal development or in the neonatal period, with inheritance patterns remaining unknown.

name: X-linked adrenoleukodystrophy
clinical_summary: X-linked adrenoleukodystrophy is a rare, inherited disorder characterized by varying degrees of visual impairment and behavioral issues in individuals of different ages. Its X-linked dominant inheritance pattern indicates that it primarily affects males.

name: Autosomal recessive hypophosphatemic rickets
clinical_summary: Autosomal recessive hypophosphatemic rickets is a rare disease characterized by renal phosphate wasting, growth delay, and skeletal dysplasia, typically presenting in childhood or infancy. Symptoms also include sensorineural hearing impairment and delayed tooth eruption, leading to bone pain in affected individuals.

name: Craniopharyngioma
clinical_summary: Craniopharyngioma is a rare disease characterized by a neoplasm of the anterior pituitary, often presenting as a cerebral calcification and intracranial cystic lesion. The condition can cause various endocrine disturbances, including hypopituitarism and hypogonadotropic hypogonadism, due to its effect on the enlarged pituitary gland.

name: Solar urticaria
clinical_summary: Solar urticaria is a rare disease characterized by the occurrence of urticaria (hives) after exposure to sunlight, accompanied by symptoms such as edema, pruritus, and potentially syncope or nausea.

name: Becker muscular dystrophy
clinical_summary: Becker muscular dystrophy is a rare genetic disorder characterized by the presence of elevated circulating creatine kinase concentration, myoglobinuria, myalgia, exercise intolerance, and gait disturbance. It typically presents in childhood but can occur in any age group due to its X-linked recessive inheritance pattern.

name: Nelson syndrome
clinical_summary: Nelson syndrome is a rare condition characterized by an increased circulating cortisol level and typically affects adults and the elderly, often resulting in visual impairment and other symptoms.

name: Giant axonal neuropathy
clinical_summary: Giant axonal neuropathy is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by childhood or infancy onset of generalized muscle weakness and joint hypermobility, as well as distinct physical features such as pili canaliculi. Affected individuals often exhibit hypomyelination of the CNS, diffuse axonal swelling, and areflexia.

name: Inhalational anthrax
clinical_summary: Inhalational anthrax is a rare disease characterized by symptoms that may include fever, epigastric auras, fatigue, confusion, vomiting, and abnormality of the sweat gland, affecting individuals of all ages. The condition is not hereditary.

name: Branchioskeletogenital syndrome
clinical_summary: Branchioskeletogenital syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by various congenital anomalies, including micropenis, abnormal dentition, and cranial abnormalities. It is also associated with other physical deformities such as a bifid uvula and short skull (brachycephaly).

name: Focal facial dermal dysplasia type II
key_symptoms: Curved linear dimple below the lower lip
clinical_summary: Focal facial dermal dysplasia type II is a rare disorder characterized by specific facial abnormalities that manifest antenatally or at birth, including curved linear dimples, low-set ears, and distichiasis. This autosomal dominant disorder presents with a distinct combination of symptoms, such as Bitemporal forceps marks, trichiasis, and sparse eyebrows.

name: Calciphylaxis
clinical_summary: Calciphylaxis is a rare disease characterized by secondary hyperparathyroidism, cutaneous manifestations, and abnormal metabolic and vascular changes, often in patients with stage 5 chronic kidney disease. It typically presents with symptoms such as cutis marmorata and hyperphosphatemia in adults.

name: Homozygous familial hypercholesterolemia
clinical_summary: Homozygous familial hypercholesterolemia is a rare genetic disorder characterized by precocious atherosclerosis, hypertension, and hepatic steatosis, leading to increased risks of sudden cardiac death and myocardial infarction. It manifests at birth due to autosomal dominant or recessive inheritance patterns.

name: Autoimmune hypoparathyroidism
clinical_summary: Autoimmune hypoparathyroidism is a rare disease characterized by hypocalcemia and hyperphosphatemia due to autoimmune destruction of the parathyroid glands. The condition is marked by a range of symptoms including hypercalciuria, paresthesia, and cataract formation, often presenting in adolescence, adulthood, or childhood.

name: Pseudohypoparathyroidism type 1A
key_symptoms: Low urinary cyclic AMP response to PTH administration
clinical_summary: Pseudohypoparathyroidism type 1A is a rare, autosomal dominant condition characterized by hypocalcemia, hyperphosphatemia, and elevated circulating parathyroid hormone levels. Clinical features also include short stature, round face, and an abnormal urinary cyclic AMP response to parathyroid hormone administration, with symptoms typically presenting in infancy, childhood, or neonatal stages.

name: Pseudohypoparathyroidism type 1B
key_symptoms: Low urinary cyclic AMP response to PTH administration
clinical_summary: Pseudohypoparathyroidism type 1B is a rare disorder characterized by hypocalcemia, hyperphosphatemia, and elevated parathyroid hormone levels. It is marked by distinct facial features and is typically inherited in an autosomal dominant manner.

name: Pseudohypoparathyroidism type 1C
key_symptoms: Low urinary cyclic AMP response to PTH administration
clinical_summary: Pseudohypoparathyroidism type 1C is a rare autosomal dominant genetic disorder characterized by hypocalcemia, hyperphosphatemia, elevated parathyroid hormone levels, and pituitary resistance to thyroid hormone in the setting of a normal or low urinary cyclic AMP response to PTH administration. Additional physical features include full cheeks, typically presenting in childhood, infancy, or neonatal stages.

name: Auricular abnormalities-cleft lip with or without cleft palate-ocular abnormalities syndrome
key_symptoms: Incomplete cleft of the upper lip
clinical_summary: Auricular abnormalities-cleft lip with or without cleft palate-ocular abnormalities syndrome is a rare condition characterized by deep philtrum, intestinal malrotation, kyphosis, and other abnormalities, typically presenting from antenatal to neonatal periods. The inheritance pattern is currently unknown.

name: Autosomal recessive primary microcephaly
clinical_summary: Autosomal recessive primary microcephaly is a rare genetic disorder characterized by microcephaly, growth delay, and global developmental delay, among other distinct physical features such as a thin upper lip vermilion, sloping forehead, and upsloping palpebral fissure. It manifests antenatally and presents neonatally, following an autosomal recessive inheritance pattern.

name: Primary hepatic neuroendocrine carcinoma
clinical_summary: Primary hepatic neuroendocrine carcinoma is a rare disease characterized by symptoms such as ascites, weight loss, diarrhea, nausea, anorexia, and dyspnea in adults. Its pathogenesis does not have a known familial or hereditary component.

name: Gallbladder neuroendocrine tumor
clinical_summary: Gallbladder neuroendocrine tumor is a rare disease characterized by intermittent jaundice, cholecystitis, ascites, weight loss, nausea, and anorexia in adults. The cause of this disease is unknown and its inheritance pattern is also not established.

name: Bronchial neuroendocrine tumor
clinical_summary: Bronchial neuroendocrine tumors are rare masses that present with symptoms such as weight loss, anorexia, pneumonia, dyspnea, asthma, and hemoptysis in individuals of various age groups. The pathogenesis of this condition appears to be non-familial and non-inherited.

name: Adrenocortical carcinoma
clinical_summary: Adrenocortical carcinoma is a rare disease characterized by abnormality of reproductive system physiology, irritability, anxiety, diabetes mellitus, hypertension, and hyperaldosteronism. It primarily affects children and is not heritable.

name: Neuroendocrine tumor of anal canal
clinical_summary: Neuroendocrine tumor of the anal canal is a rare disease characterized by weight loss, abdominal pain, and other systemic symptoms, often occurring in adults and the elderly. Symptoms may include weight loss, constipation, abdominal pain, chronic lymphadenopathy, elevated liver enzymes, and tenesmus.

name: Ulbright-Hodes syndrome
clinical_summary: Ulbright-Hodes syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive condition characterized by a distinct set of congenital anomalies, including cryptorchidism, penile abnormalities, and craniofacial anomalies. Affected individuals typically present at birth with these features.

name: Spastic paraplegia-nephritis-deafness syndrome
clinical_summary: Spastic paraplegia-nephritis-deafness syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by proteinuria, nephropathy, sensorineural hearing impairment, intellectual disability, spasticity, and gait disturbance. It is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern.

name: Otodental syndrome
clinical_summary: Otodental syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by progressive sensorineural hearing impairment and oral manifestations, including carious teeth, abnormal dental pulp, and gum abnormalities. This autosomal dominant disorder typically presents in childhood, with associated dental anomalies and hearing impairments.

name: Pili torti-onychodysplasia syndrome
clinical_summary: Pili torti-onychodysplasia syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a range of congenital anomalies. Onset typically occurs in infancy or neonatally, presenting with cleft palate, skin abnormalities, and absence of eyelashes.

name: Pelvis-shoulder dysplasia
clinical_summary: Pelvis-shoulder dysplasia is a rare disease characterized by multiple skeletal anomalies presenting in infancy or neonatal period. It is associated with other anomalies including ambiguous genitalia, hydronephrosis, microglossia, cleft palate, and hydrocephalus, and follows an autosomal dominant inheritance pattern.

name: DPM3-CDG
lab_findings: Decreased sialylation of O-linked protein glycosylation
clinical_summary: DPM3-CDG is a rare disease characterized by decreased sialylation of O-linked protein glycosylation presenting with symptoms such as muscle weakness and intellectual disability. It is an autosomal recessive disorder with childhood onset.

name: COG4-CDG
clinical_summary: COG4-CDG is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by abnormal protein N- and O-linked glycosylation, resulting in severe neurological manifestations such as microcephaly, nystagmus, and irritability. It presents in infancy or neonatal period, often with a distinctive craniofacial appearance including a sloping forehead.

name: COASY protein-associated neurodegeneration
key_symptoms: Eye of the tiger anomaly of globus pallidus
clinical_summary: COASY protein-associated neurodegeneration is a rare, autosomal recessive neurodegenerative disorder characterized by a spectrum of neurological symptoms including compulsive behaviors, Parkinsonism, and gait disturbance, typically manifesting in childhood. Key morphological changes are observed in the caudate nucleus.

name: Spinocerebellar ataxia type 37
clinical_summary: Spinocerebellar ataxia type 37 is a rare genetic disorder with adult onset, characterized by symptoms including abnormal conjugate eye movement, sensorineural hearing impairment, and gait disturbance. The disorder is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern, often manifesting with fall risk, cogwheel rigidity, and horizontal nystagmus.

name: Severe combined immunodeficiency due to adenosine deaminase deficiency
key_symptoms: Absence of lymph node germinal center
clinical_summary: Severe combined immunodeficiency due to adenosine deaminase deficiency is a rare disease characterized by failure to thrive, recurrent upper respiratory tract infections, and immunological deficiencies including lymphopenia. It typically presents in infancy or neonatal stage with symptoms such as sinusitis, otitis media, and diarrhea.

name: X-linked intellectual disability-acromegaly-hyperactivity syndrome
clinical_summary: This rare syndrome, inherited in an X-linked recessive pattern, presents with symptoms of intellectual disability, acromegaly, hyperactivity, spinal arachnoid cysts, and distinct physical features such as triangular faces and macroorchidism. Affected males typically exhibit symptoms, which manifest in childhood.

name: Glycogen storage disease with severe cardiomyopathy due to glycogenin deficiency
clinical_summary: This rare disease, caused by glycogenin deficiency, leads to severe cardiomyopathy with associated symptoms such as ventricular fibrillation, ventricular hypertrophy, palpitations, vertigo, and exertional dyspnea in childhood. It inherits in an autosomal recessive pattern, indicating a child must inherit a defective gene from each parent.

name: Yunis-Varon syndrome
key_symptoms: Aplasia of the distal phalanges of the hand
Aplasia of the distal phalanx of the hallux
clinical_summary: Yunis-Varon syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by aplasia of the distal phalanges, hypospadias, micropenis, and other congenital anomalies. It presents with characteristic physical deformities, including aplasia of the distal phalanges and proximal phalanx of the hallux, and craniofacial abnormalities such as a short upper lip.

name: Glioblastoma
clinical_summary: Glioblastoma is a rare disease characterized by a multigenic/multifactorial inheritance pattern and affecting individuals of all ages, presenting with a variety of symptoms including fatigue and abnormal nervous system physiology.

name: Idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis
clinical_summary: Idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis is a rare disease characterized by pulmonary fibrosis and abnormal pulmonary interstitial morphology. It often presents with symptoms such as exertional dyspnea, gastroesophageal reflux, and bronchiectasis, and primarily affects adults.

name: Cenani-Lenz syndrome
clinical_summary: Cenani-Lenz syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by the presence of complex limb anomalies, notably absent or abnormal fingernails, syndactyly, and oligodactyly. These features often become apparent at birth or in infancy.

name: Isotretinoin-like syndrome
key_symptoms: Atresia of the external auditory canal
clinical_summary: Isotretinoin-like syndrome is a rare, developmental disorder characterized by multiple congenital anomalies including microtia, inguinal hernia, cleft palate, hydrocephalus, microcephaly, and micrognathia. It typically presents within the first few months of life with symptoms appearing in infancy or neonatal stages.

name: Non-progressive predominantly posterior cavitating leukoencephalopathy with peripheral neuropathy
clinical_summary: This rare disease is characterized by progressive leukoencephalopathy, predominantly in the posterior regions, as well as associated peripheral neuropathy, and multiple systemic complications. It is an autosomal recessive disorder typically presenting in childhood.

name: Cardiocranial syndrome, Pfeiffer type
key_symptoms: Contracture of the proximal interphalangeal joint of the 2nd finger
inheritance: Autosomal dominant, Autosomal recessive, Not applicable
clinical_summary: Cardiocranial syndrome, Pfeiffer type is a rare disorder of infancy and neonatal onset characterized by distinctive physical features including posteriorly rotated ears, deep palmar crease, and contractures.

name: Renpenning syndrome
clinical_summary: Renpenning syndrome is a rare X-linked recessive disorder characterized by severe intellectual disability, microcephaly, and cachexia. Affected individuals typically display features such as skeletal muscle atrophy, severe short stature, and hypospadias in infancy or neonatal onset.

name: Cardiospondylocarpofacial syndrome
lab_findings: Abnormal form of the vertebral bodies
clinical_summary: Cardiospondylocarpofacial syndrome is a rare autosomal dominant disorder characterized by a combination of conductive hearing impairment, mitral valve abnormalities, and characteristic physical malformations including brachydactyly and a high, narrow palate. The syndrome can manifest in individuals of all ages.

name: Deafness-genital anomalies-metacarpal and metatarsal synostosis syndrome
clinical_summary: Deafness-genital anomalies-metacarpal and metatarsal synostosis syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by congenital anomalies, including sensorineural hearing impairment and genital abnormalities such as hypospadias. Affected individuals often present with distinct facial features and skeletal abnormalities in infancy or neonatally.

name: Deafness-enamel hypoplasia-nail defects syndrome
clinical_summary: Deafness-enamel hypoplasia-nail defects syndrome is a rare condition characterized by childhood onset of intellectual disability and multiple physical anomalies including nail defects. This autosomal recessive disorder typically presents in childhood with additional symptoms such as pili torti and external genital hypoplasia.

name: Deafness-small bowel diverticulosis-neuropathy syndrome
clinical_summary: Deafness-small bowel diverticulosis-neuropathy syndrome is a rare condition characterized by sensorineural hearing impairment, gastrointestinal symptoms including malabsorption, chronic diarrhea, and duodenal ulcer, as well as neurological symptoms such as hemiplegia, presenting in childhood with unknown inheritance.

name: Long chain 3-hydroxyacyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency
clinical_summary: Long chain 3-hydroxyacyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by symptoms including photophobia, hypoglycemia, and abnormal electroretinogram, typically presenting in infancy or neonatal period. If left untreated, this condition may lead to severe complications such as visual loss and exotropia.

name: Trigonocephaly-short stature-developmental delay syndrome
lab_findings: * microcephalic frontal lobe atrophy on brain imaging
clinical_summary: Trigonocephaly-short stature-developmental delay syndrome is a rare condition characterized by trigonocephaly, global developmental delay, short stature, inguinal hernia, broad secondary alveolar ridge, and high palate, with inheritance pattern unknown.

name: Nasopalpebral lipoma-coloboma syndrome
clinical_summary: Nasopalpebral lipoma-coloboma syndrome is a rare autosomal dominant disorder characterized by multi-systemic abnormalities including ophthalmological and respiratory manifestations. Affected individuals typically exhibit a range of symptoms, including abnormal eyelash morphology, cataract, and recurrent upper respiratory tract infections, often presenting from a neonatal age.

name: Thyrocerebrorenal syndrome
lab_findings: Abnormality of the musculature of the limbs
clinical_summary: Thyrocerebrorenal syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder affecting children, characterized by renal insufficiency, nephritis, and various neurological manifestations including sensorineural hearing impairment, seizures, myoclonus, and slurred speech.

name: Autoimmune pulmonary alveolar proteinosis
lab_findings: Abnormality of the upper respiratory tract
clinical_summary: Autoimmune pulmonary alveolar proteinosis is a rare disease characterized by intraalveolar phospholipid accumulation, causing symptoms such as cyanosis, clubbing, and restrictive ventilatory defect leading to dyspnea. The age of onset varies from childhood to adulthood.

name: PHAVER syndrome
lab_findings: Abnormal form of the vertebral bodies
clinical_summary: PHAVER syndrome is a rare disease characterized by a combination of symptoms including posteriorly rotated or low-set ears, and various congenital anomalies, typically presenting from infancy to neonatal period. This condition is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern.

name: Autosomal recessive Kenny-Caffey syndrome
clinical_summary: Autosomal recessive Kenny-Caffey syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by hypocalcemic seizures, hypocalcemia, and congenital hypoparathyroidism in childhood. Other notable symptoms include microcephaly, delayed cranial suture closure, and full cheeks.

name: Autosomal dominant Kenny-Caffey syndrome
key_symptoms: Cortical thickening of long bone diaphyses
Stenosis of the medullary cavity of the long bones
clinical_summary: Autosomal dominant Kenny-Caffey syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by delayed cranial suture closure, short stature, and long bone abnormalities, among other features. Key symptoms include cortical thickening, medullary cavity stenosis, hypertelorism, and hypermetropia, which typically manifest in childhood.

name: Neuromyelitis optica spectrum disorder
key_symptoms: Neuronal loss in central nervous system
clinical_summary: Neuromyelitis optica spectrum disorder is characterized by a range of symptoms including visual loss, central nervous system neuronal loss, and peripheral demyelination. This rare disorder can affect individuals of varying ages, with a multifactorial inheritance pattern.

name: Posterior cortical atrophy
lab_findings: reduced parietal gray matter on mri
clinical_summary: Posterior cortical atrophy is a rare disease characterized by a combination of cognitive and motor symptoms, including confusion, anxiety, and ataxia, often presenting in adulthood. Key symptoms include oculomotor apraxia, dyscalculia, and alexia, but the mode of inheritance remains unknown.

name: Acyl-CoA dehydrogenase 9 deficiency
clinical_summary: Acyl-CoA dehydrogenase 9 deficiency is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a range of symptoms including generalized hypotonia, hepatic steatosis, and failure to thrive. Affected individuals often present with symptoms in childhood, infancy, or shortly after birth and may also experience encephalopathy, congestive heart failure, and hypertrophic cardiomyopathy.

name: Rigid spine syndrome
clinical_summary: Rigid spine syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by symptoms including neck muscle weakness, generalized hypotonia, respiratory insufficiency, scoliosis, myopathy, and spinal rigidity. It typically presents in infancy or neonatal stage.

name: Mesomelia-synostoses syndrome
clinical_summary: Mesomelia-synostoses syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by abnormal humerus morphology, among other skeletal and craniofacial abnormalities, typically evident at birth or in early infancy. It is inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern.

name: Mutilating hereditary sensory neuropathy with spastic paraplegia
key_symptoms: Distal sensory impairment of all modalities
clinical_summary: Mutilating hereditary sensory neuropathy with spastic paraplegia is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by childhood or infancy onset of spasticity, distal sensory impairment, and impaired pain sensation, leading to progressive motor dysfunction. Clinical manifestations include gait disturbance, hyperreflexia, and spastic paraplegia.

name: Acrootoocular syndrome
key_symptoms: Atresia of the external auditory canal
clinical_summary: Acrootoocular syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by congenital anomalies including microcephaly, low-set ears, and other facial abnormalities. It typically presents in infancy or neonatally and is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern.

name: Autosomal recessive omodysplasia
clinical_summary: Autosomal recessive omodysplasia is a rare disease characterized by distinct facial and skeletal features, including long philtrum, abnormal ear positions, and anteverted nares, typically presenting in infancy or early neonatal life. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern.

name: Arachnodactyly-abnormal ossification-intellectual disability syndrome
clinical_summary: Arachnodactyly-abnormal ossification-intellectual disability syndrome is a rare disease characterized by developmental anomalies including delayed cranial suture closure, arachnodactyly, and intellectual disability. Symptoms present in infancy or neonatal stages, including micrognathia, downslanted palpebral fissures, and shallow orbits.

name: Systemic capillary leak syndrome
lab_findings: normal to low c3 complement levels
clinical_summary: Systemic capillary leak syndrome is a rare disease characterized by leukocytosis, pedal edema, pancreatitis, weight loss, diarrhea, and abdominal pain in adults. It does not have a known familial inheritance pattern.

name: Toxic epidermal necrolysis
clinical_summary: Toxic epidermal necrolysis is a rare disease characterized by severe skin blistering and accompanied by various systemic symptoms such as weight loss, thrombocytopenia, and decreased total neutrophil count. It affects individuals of all ages.

name: Acrofacial dysostosis, Palagonia type
clinical_summary: Acrofacial dysostosis, Palagonia type is a rare disorder characterized by a set of congenital anomalies including low anterior hairline, micrognathia, and low-set or posteriorly rotated ears. Infants afflicted with this condition typically exhibit features such as sparse eyelashes and oligodontia.

name: CHIME syndrome
clinical_summary: CHIME syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by distinctive facial features and tall stature, with symptoms typically presenting in childhood. The condition manifests with a combination of features, including hypertelorism, epicanthus, short philtrum, brachycephaly, and abnormal dentition.

name: Inverted duplicated chromosome 15 syndrome
clinical_summary: Inverted duplicated chromosome 15 syndrome is a rare genetic disorder presenting with symptoms such as autistic behavior, generalized hypotonia, joint hypermobility, drooling, abnormal repetitive mannerisms, and hyperactivity from a neonatal age of onset. The pathogenesis of this disorder is currently unknown due to a lack of applicable inheritance patterns.

name: Sporadic pheochromocytoma/secreting paraganglioma
clinical_summary: Sporadic pheochromocytoma/secreting paraganglioma is a rare disease characterized by episodic paroxysmal anxiety, among other symptoms. It primarily affects adolescents, adults, and rarely children, with no known familial tendency or inheritance pattern.

name: Non-functioning paraganglioma
clinical_summary: Non-functioning paraganglioma is a rare disease characterized by symptoms such as paraganglioma, episodic hyperhidrosis, hypertensive retinopathy, cerebral hemorrhage, dysphonia, and weight loss, affecting individuals across various age groups.

name: Hereditary pheochromocytoma-paraganglioma
clinical_summary: Hereditary pheochromocytoma-paraganglioma is a rare disorder characterized by the presence of paraganglioma, pheochromocytoma, and associated renal manifestations such as proteinuria and glomerulosclerosis. It typically presents in childhood and follows an autosomal dominant pattern of inheritance.

name: Kleefstra syndrome
clinical_summary: Kleefstra syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by distinct facial features and delayed speech and language development. It presents with a range of physical abnormalities, including those noted at birth.

name: Malignant atrophic papulosis
clinical_summary: Malignant atrophic papulosis, an autosomal dominant disease, presents with symptoms of dermal atrophy, telangiectasia of the skin, and various gastrointestinal issues. This condition characteristically affects adult individuals.

name: Mutilating palmoplantar keratoderma with periorificial keratotic plaques
inheritance: Autosomal dominant, Not applicable, X-linked recessive
clinical_summary: Mutilating palmoplantar keratoderma with periorificial keratotic plaques is a rare disorder characterized by thickened skin and abnormal skin morphology. It often presents with additional symptoms such as anhidrosis, palmoplantar hyperhidrosis, and abnormal fingernail morphology, typically in infancy, childhood, or at birth.

name: Male hypergonadotropic hypogonadism-intellectual disability-skeletal anomalies syndrome
lab_findings: Abnormal form of the vertebral bodies
clinical_summary: Male hypergonadotropic hypogonadism-intellectual disability-skeletal anomalies syndrome is a rare disease characterized by hypogonadism, decreased fertility, intellectual disability, and distinctive physical features such as small scrotum and gynecomastia. The exact inheritance pattern is currently unknown.

name: Hydrocephaly-tall stature-joint laxity syndrome
clinical_summary: Hydrocephaly-tall stature-joint laxity syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by hydrocephalus, tall stature, and joint laxity, typically manifesting in infancy. It encompasses various physical features, including arachnodactyly and adducted thumb, with joint hypermobility being a prominent symptom.

name: Hydrocephalus-costovertebral dysplasia-Sprengel anomaly syndrome
clinical_summary: Hydrocephalus-costovertebral dysplasia-Sprengel anomaly syndrome is a rare condition characterized by congenital anomalies including hydrocephalus, mandibular prognathia, and Sprengel anomaly. The onset typically occurs in the neonatal period with symptoms including macrocephaly and facial dysmorphia.

name: Johnson neuroectodermal syndrome
key_symptoms: Atresia of the external auditory canal
clinical_summary: Johnson neuroectodermal syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by childhood onset of hypogonadism, conductive hearing impairment, and craniofacial abnormalities, including alopecia, sparse hair, and facial asymmetry. It is inherited in an autosomal dominant manner.

name: IVIC syndrome
clinical_summary: IVIC syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by hearing impairment, joint stiffness, severe short stature, and several physical anomalies including hypoplasia of the radius and triphalangeal thumb. It typically presents with symptoms at birth or in utero.

name: Microvillus inclusion disease
lab_findings: Abnormality of small intestinal villus morphology
clinical_summary: Microvillus inclusion disease is a rare autosomal recessive disorder presenting in infancy or neonatal stages, characterized by global developmental delay, nephrocalcinosis, pruritus, metabolic acidosis, dehydration, and chronic diarrhea. Early clinical manifestations often include severe gastrointestinal and systemic complications.

name: NPHP3-related Meckel-like syndrome
clinical_summary: NPHP3-related Meckel-like syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by multicystic kidney dysplasia, renal dysplasia, Dandy-Walker malformation, and abnormalities of the pancreas, presenting in childhood. The prenatal presentation may also include polyhydramnios and oligohydramnios.

name: Pallister-Hall syndrome
clinical_summary: Pallister-Hall syndrome is a rare congenital disorder characterized by multiple congenital anomalies, including posteriorly rotated ears, Downslanted palpebral fissures, and other physical defects. It begins at various stages of development, from before birth to infancy.

name: Hennekam syndrome
clinical_summary: Hennekam syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a unique combination of physical and developmental symptoms, including intellectual disability. Key features of the syndrome include hypertelorism, low-set ears, wide nasal bridge, and delayed teething, often accompanied by lymphedema.

name: Isolated arrhinia
clinical_summary: Isolated arrhinia is a rare condition characterized by the lack of a fully formed nose at birth, presenting with multiple associated craniofacial anomalies and respiratory distress due to nasal atresia. This condition is typically identified in the prenatal or neonatal period.

name: Shprintzen-Goldberg syndrome
clinical_summary: Shprintzen-Goldberg syndrome is a rare multisystem disorder characterized by joint hypermobility, characteristic craniofacial abnormalities, and multiple other associated features. It exhibits autosomal dominant inheritance and typically presents ante natally, in infancy, or at birth.

name: MOMO syndrome
clinical_summary: MOMO syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by distinct facial features and cranial abnormalities, primarily presenting in infancy. Key physical characteristics include brachycephaly, macrocephaly, and epicanthus, often accompanied by thickened lower and upper lip vermilion and a high palate.

name: Lethal Kniest-like dysplasia
lab_findings: absent or delayed ossification of epiphyses
clinical_summary: Lethal Kniest-like dysplasia is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by several congenital anomalies including cleft palate, macrocephaly, and short ribs, with symptoms evident from the neonatal period. Affected individuals exhibit multiple skeletal and craniofacial abnormalities, often resulting in a poor prognosis.

name: Melnick-Needles syndrome
clinical_summary: Melnick-Needles syndrome is a rare X-linked dominant disorder characterized by a distinct set of craniofacial abnormalities. It typically presents in childhood with manifestations including joint hypermobility, delayed cranial suture closure, and distinctive facial features.

name: Melorheostosis
clinical_summary: Melorheostosis is a rare disease characterized by abnormalities of the skeletal system, often presenting with symptoms like lymphedema, joint stiffness, bone pain, and skeletal dysplasia. It can affect individuals of all ages with no known inheritance pattern.

name: Marshall-Smith syndrome
clinical_summary: Marshall-Smith syndrome is a rare autosomal dominant disorder characterized by multiple congenital anomalies and intellectual disability. It presents in infancy or at birth with joint hypermobility, retrognathia, anteverted nares, proptosis, and thin skin.

name: Juberg-Hayward syndrome
clinical_summary: Juberg-Hayward syndrome is a rare condition characterized by microcephaly, intrauterine growth retardation, severe short stature, hypospadias, and an abnormal kidney formation (horseshoe kidney). It manifests antenatally or neonatally and has a potential autosomal dominant or autosomal recessive pattern of inheritance.

name: Kapur-Toriello syndrome
clinical_summary: Kapur-Toriello syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a combination of orofacial cleft, ophthalmic anomalies, and distinct ear malformations. It manifests in infancy or at birth, often with symptoms such as microphthalmia and retinal coloboma.

name: Mitochondrial neurogastrointestinal encephalomyopathy
clinical_summary: Mitochondrial neurogastrointestinal encephalomyopathy is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by a combination of neurological and gastrointestinal symptoms, including external ophthalmoplegia, vomiting, dysphagia, and abdominal pain. This condition affects individuals across the age spectrum, including adolescents, adults, and children, with variable severity and onset.

name: Chronic graft versus host disease
clinical_summary: Chronic graft versus host disease is a rare disease characterized by a range of symptoms including poor wound healing, skin thickening, and recurrent corneal erosions. The exact inheritance pattern is unknown, with symptoms occurring in all age groups.

name: Pterygium colli-intellectual disability-digital anomalies syndrome
clinical_summary: Pterygium colli-intellectual disability-digital anomalies syndrome is a rare disorder presenting with a combination of intellectual disability, brachycephaly, and unique digital and facial anomalies. It manifests in infancy and can be inherited in an autosomal dominant or X-linked dominant pattern.

name: Müllerian derivatives-lymphangiectasia-polydactyly syndrome
clinical_summary: Müllerian derivatives-lymphangiectasia-polydactyly syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by the presence of inguinal hernia, cryptorchidism, hydronephrosis, and Müllerian duct anomalies such as abnormal uterus and vaginal atresia, often associated with high palate. The age of onset is both antenatal and neonatal, with unknown inheritance pattern.

name: Arnold-Chiari malformation type I
lab_findings: mri brain findings of c1 and c2 spondylolisthesis or cervical kyphosis
clinical_summary: Arnold-Chiari malformation type I is a rare condition characterized by small posterior fossa and associated symptoms such as headache, neck pain, tinnitus, and nystagmus that can occur primarily in infancy or neonatal stages.

name: Restrictive dermopathy
clinical_summary: Restrictive dermopathy is a rare disease characterized by distinct facial features including a narrow mouth, submucous cleft hard palate, and hypertelorism, which present at birth typically in an antenatal or neonatal stage. The disease can be inherited through autosomal dominant and autosomal recessive patterns.

name: Wrinkly skin syndrome
clinical_summary: Wrinkly skin syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by progressive microcephaly, high and wrinkled skin, and multiple congenital anomalies including inguinal hernia and cryptorchidism, often present at birth or in infancy. This autosomal recessive condition results in various craniofacial abnormalities such as epicanthus and hypertelorism.

name: Distal deletion 15q syndrome
clinical_summary: Distal deletion 15q syndrome is a rare disorder characterized by intrauterine growth retardation and associated physical anomalies, including cryptorchidism, hypospadias, and micropenis. Symptoms typically present antenatally or in the neonatal period.

name: Turner syndrome due to structural X chromosome anomalies
clinical_summary: Turner syndrome due to structural X chromosome anomalies is a rare disease characterized by abnormal ovarian function, short neck, and delayed puberty among other skeletal anomalies. Symptoms present at various stages, from antenatal to childhood and infancy.

name: Monosomy X syndrome
clinical_summary: Monosomy X syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by various physical and reproductive abnormalities, including abnormalities of the ovary and delayed puberty. It affects individuals from an early age, often presenting symptoms from infancy to childhood.

name: Mosaic monosomy X syndrome
clinical_summary: Mosaic monosomy X syndrome is a rare genetic disorder presenting with multiple symptoms including abnormal ovarian development and delayed puberty. Affected individuals often exhibit short stature, osteopenia, and characteristic abnormalities of the neck and sternum.

name: De Barsy syndrome
clinical_summary: De Barsy syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by a combination of hypernasal speech, foot deformities, premature rupture of membranes, and other skeletal anomalies, including Wormian bones. It is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern and primarily affects individuals from birth.

name: Hemangioblastoma
clinical_summary: Hemangioblastoma is a rare vascular tumor characterized by symptoms such as nausea and vomiting, headache, and vertigo. It typically manifests in adults as a cerebellar hemangioblastoma, retinal capillary hemangioma, or intracranial cystic lesion.

name: Thymic neuroendocrine tumor
clinical_summary: Thymic neuroendocrine tumor is a rare disease characterized by abnormality of the mediastinum, lymphadenopathy, and increased cortisol and ACTH levels, often presenting with weight loss in adults and the elderly. The underlying cause and inheritance pattern of this disease are unknown.

name: Wiedemann-Rautenstrauch syndrome
lab_findings: Decreased response to growth hormone stimulation test
clinical_summary: Wiedemann-Rautenstrauch syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by various ocular and auricular abnormalities. Its symptoms, including low-set ears and cataract, are typically present from birth or detected antenatally.

name: Congenital enterocyte heparan sulfate deficiency
clinical_summary: Congenital enterocyte heparan sulfate deficiency is a rare condition characterized by symptoms such as weight loss, diarrhea, and hypoalbuminemia presenting in infancy or neonatal age. The disorder involves abnormality in polysaccharide metabolism, leading to edema and protein-losing enteropathy.

name: Hyperinsulinism due to short chain 3-hydroxylacyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency
clinical_summary: Hyperinsulinism due to short chain 3-hydroxylacyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency is a rare metabolic disorder characterized by severe hyperinsulinemic hypoglycemia. It is an autosomal recessive condition typically presenting in infancy or neonatal stages with symptoms including lethargy, confusion, and hepatic steatosis.

name: Meacham syndrome
clinical_summary: Meacham syndrome is a rare, autosomal dominant disorder characterized by a combination of congenital abnormalities including ambiguous genitalia, vaginal atresia, and congenital diaphragmatic hernia. Affected individuals typically present with these anomalies in infancy or neonatal stages.
